content: Save Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera Roll | Help CenterSkip to main contentSave Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera RollIf you'd like to save every photo to your Camera Roll as well as in CompanyCam— here's how: April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This setting is available on all plans.Permission Role:All User Permissions Roles can access this setting. Platform Availability:This setting is only available in the mobile app. This setting is User-specific and only affects your device. If you want others in your company to use this setting, make sure they enable it.In the appTap on your User Icon at the top of the page.Next, tap Settings.Within your settings, select the Camera section.Tap to turn on, Save Images to Device.Note: Every photo will save to your camera roll that is taken in CompanyCam until this is disabled. Don't want to save every photo you take in your camera roll except a select few? Check out our help guide on how to save photos!Related ArticlesOpen to the Camera ViewUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using LiDAR to Measure Photos | Help Center
content: Using LiDAR to Measure Photos | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing LiDAR to Measure PhotosWant to reduce manual measuring and speed up your workflow? Use LiDAR mode to provide more accurate job site documentation. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:LiDAR Mode can be used by all permission roles.Platform Availability:LiDAR Mode is only available on the mobile app.LiDAR is only available for iOS users. Please NoteLiDAR measurements are approximate and best used for estimating. For best results, we recommend standing between 3-15 feet away from the object you're measuring. It's also helpful to avoid transparent or reflective surfaces. On the webMeasure photosOpen a Project and click on the Photos Tab. If a photo has LiDAR data, you'll see a Measure Icon in the bottom right-hand corner of the photo. Click on the Photo to open it. Click on the Curved Line Icon to open the photo editor. Within the editor, click on the Measure Icon within the toolbar and click on the area you'd like to measure. After you've added your measurements, click Done. Once you've finished adding annotations, click on the green Checkmark Button to save. In the appEnable LiDAR ModeTap on your User Icon.Tap on the Settings Button.Choose Camera under the Preferences section.Scroll down to the bottom of the page and enable LiDAR Mode.Take photos using LiDARTap on the Camera Icon as you normally would when taking a photo.Choose the Project you'd like to take photos in.Once in the capture tool, tap on the Measure Button.If prompted, tap Start Measuring.Stand 3-15 feet away from the object you're measuring and take photos as normal. Once you've finished, tap Done.Measure PhotosAfter you've taken your photos, you'll see a Measure Icon next to each photo you took when LiDAR Mode was enabled. To measure your photo, tap on the Curved Line Icon to open the photo editor.Next, tap on the Measure Icon and tap on the area you'd like to measure. Once measured, tap on the green Checkmark Icon.Add any additional annotations (if needed), then tap Save.FAQsWhat iPhone models are compatible with LiDAR Mode? iPhone Pro and Pro Max devices that have a LiDAR sensor are compatible. This sensor is included on the iPhone 12 Pro/Pro Max and newer Pro/Pro Max models. What iPad models are compatible with LiDAR Mode? iPad Pro devices with a LiDAR sensor are compatible. This includes:iPad Pro 13-inch (M4) and 11-inch (M4)iPad Pro 12.9-inch (4th generation) and lateriPad Pro 11-inch (2nd generation) and later Can Android devices use LiDAR Mode? No. Android devices are not compatible with LiDAR Mode. Did this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Take Photos in the Mobile App | Help Center
content: Take Photos in the Mobile App | Help CenterSkip to main contentTake Photos in the Mobile AppJust downloaded the app and ready to snap some photos? Here’s how to get started in CompanyCam! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All User Permission Roles can take photos in the mobile app.Restricted Users can only take photos in the Projects they've created or have been assigned to. Platform Availability:Taking photos is only available in the mobile app. In the appTake PhotosTap the Camera Icon at the bottom of the screen.Open an existing Project, or you can create a new one. Tap the Capture Button. When finished, tap Done. Camera SettingsWhen taking photos in CompanyCam, you can adjust your Image Quality and Aspect Ratio to best fit your needs. From the Home ScreenTap on your User Icon at the top of the Home Screen.Tap the Settings Icon.Select Camera.Make your adjustments as needed.From the CameraWhile in the Camera view, tap the Settings Icon in the top right. Select All Camera Settings.Make your adjustments as needed.From here, you’ll be able to adjust two main options: Image Quality and Aspect Ratio. These settings let you balance file size, photo detail, and framing based on what matters most for your work. FAQs What Image Quality settings are available in the app? There are four different image quality settings available within your app settings:Normal – Smallest image size, recommended if you are concerned about data usage. High – Balanced option for good quality and reasonable file size. Super Fine – Better quality, best for everyday use. Ultra – Highest quality, recommended when capturing the best possible image matters most. What Image Aspect Ratios are available in the app? There are two Aspect Ratios available to choose from within the app:4:3 – Standard photo ratio, captures more vertical space. 16:9 – Wide format, good for landscape-style photos.Do I need to enable camera permissions in my device's settings? Yes. Make sure your device’s camera permissions are enabled for the CompanyCam app; otherwise, you won’t be able to capture photos directly. Can I take photos outside of a Project? No. You’ll need to be inside a Project to take or upload photos. If you don’t already have one set up, check out this guide: Create a New Project. Is the Camera Control button on newer iPhone models compatible with the mobile app? Yes! With the iPhone 16 series, Apple introduced a Camera Control button on the side of the device. In the app, this button allows you to take photos as well as control the zoom and exposure settings. When taking photos:Double-tap the Camera Control Button to open the camera settings overlay.With the overlay open, you can double-tap the Camera Control Button to switch between Zoom and Exposure.Slide up or down on the Camera Control to adjust your selected setting. Single-tap the Camera Control Button to take a photo. Please note that any changes you make to Camera Settings will only apply to new photos you take. Existing photos in your Projects won’t be affected.Related ArticlesSave Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera RollCreate a Before & After PhotoUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Accepting a Project Collaboration | Help Center
content: Accepting a Project Collaboration | Help CenterSkip to main contentAccepting a Project CollaborationProject Collaboration allows you and another company to share a Project you're both working on. Once sent and invite, here's how to accept: April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:Accepting a Project Collaboration is available on all plans. Permission Roles:To accept a Project Collaboration, you need to have the Admin, Manager, or Standard role.Platform Availability:Accepting a Project Collaboration is available on both the web and mobile apps.​On the webAccepting a Project Collaboration without an accountIf your Project Collaboration was sent via email, check your inbox for an invite. If you're not seeing it right away, you may need to check your spam or junk folders. After selecting the link, it will redirect you to a page with the option to sign up. From here, you'll select Sign Up - It's Free. Next, enter your information, and then click Create Company. Accepting a Project Collaboration with an active accountIf your Project Collaboration was sent via email, check your inbox for the invitation. If you're not seeing it right away, you may need to check your spam or junk folders. When you open the link, you will see an option to Accept and Start Collaboration. In the appAccepting a Project Collaboration without an active accountIf your Project Collaboration invitation was sent via text, tap on the link you received. Note: Cell phone carriers may be blocking the links for collaboration invites when sent via text because the messages are being detected as Spam. If you're having trouble receiving links via Text, we recommend reaching out to the User who sent the invitation initially and asking them to send the invitation via Email as a workaround.After opening the link, you'll be prompted to Download CompanyCam if you haven't installed the app yet.Once downloaded, open the app and tap Allow Paste if prompted.Next, tap Sign Up a New Company to create an account. Fill out the form with your Company's information and then tap on Get Started to accept the Collaboration and get started on the Project.Accepting a Project Collaboration with an active accountIf your Project Collaboration invitation was sent via text, tap on the link you received. Note: Cell phone carriers may be blocking the links for collaboration invites when sent via text because the messages are being detected as Spam. If you're having trouble receiving links via Text, we recommend reaching out to the User who sent the invitation initially and asking them to send the invitation via Email as a workaround.If you aren't already logged in to your account, tap Sign In to Accept the Invitation after opening the link.Next, tap Accept and Start Collaborating. Re-accepting a Collaboration invite after a Collaboration has endedIf your Collaboration has ended, the owning company of the Project will need to generate a new Collaboration Invitation and share it with you.Once you have a new invitation link, you will want to accept it by following the same steps as listed above. After accepting the new invitation, you will see two versions of the Project. You will see the old, ended Collaboration Project (denoted by a Collaboration Ended Badge) as well as the new Collaboration Project. Note: You will want to upload photos and interact with the newly created Project, not the Project with the Collaboration Ended badge.Accessing the ProjectCollaborators will be able to:Add photos - Upload photos directly from your computer, take them in CompanyCam, or upload them from your phone's camera roll!View photos - View all photos in the entire Project, even photos added before you were added as a Collaborator. You can also share any photos in the Project with clients, subcontractors, or others.Comment - View or leave comments on the project for your team or the collaborating team to view for thorough transparency and communication. Want to invite another company to collaborate? Here's how! FAQsDo Collaboration invites expire? Yes, invites will expire after 7 days. If you have not accepted the invite before the 7 days, the inviting company will need to send another invitation from that Project. If you need access to multiple Projects, you will need multiple invites — one from each Project — to access those projects from your account. Do I need a CompanyCam account to accept the Collaboration invite? Yes, this feature requires a User to have a CompanyCam account. You do not have to have an active account before accepting a Collaboration invitation, but we do recommend downloading the app before accepting the invite. Related ArticlesManage and Remove CollaboratorsHow to Add Guest Access on ProjectsAdd Project CollaboratorsAccepting a User InvitationGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Add Project Collaborators | Help Center
content: Add Project Collaborators | Help CenterSkip to main contentAdd Project CollaboratorsWorking on a Project with another company? The Project Collaboration feature lets you invite them to collaborate on Projects with you! April 22, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:Sending Collaboration invitations is available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options. Permission Roles:To invite Collaborators, you need to have the Standard, Manager, or Admin role. If you don't have one of those roles, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Sending Collaboration invitations is available on both the web and the mobile app.Please NoteThe information that can be shared between Project Collaborators is: PhotosVideos (Collaborators on Pro, Premium, and Elite Plans can upload videos)Edits & annotations on the photosCommentsProject ConversationProject TasksBoth the Owning Company and Collaborator can create and interact with each other's Project Tasks.Completed Tasks will show the user info on who checked off the Task.Tasks cannot be assigned to Collaborators.ChecklistsBoth the Owning Company and Collaborator can create a Checklist and interact with each other's Checklists to complete Fields.Completed Fields will show the user info on who checked off the Field.Checklists cannot be assigned to Collaborators.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Open the Project that you want to collaborate on.Click on the expandable Sidebar Icon on the right side if it's collapsed.​ Under the Collaborators tab on the far right panel, click on the + Circle Icon. If you haven’t invited a Collaborator to this Project yet, click Invite a Company to Collaborate. Once you click Invite, a link will be generated for you to either Copy to Clipboard and paste the link externally, or you can click Send via Email and send the link to the company you’d like to collaborate with! Note: You’ll want to send a new invite to each company if you’d like to add more than one collaborator to the Project. ** The link will expire after seven days, so if a collaborator has not opened the link within that time period, you’ll need to invite them to the Project again.** Once you’ve shared the invite, you can either choose to invite more Collaborators or return to your Project by clicking No, I’m Done. You’ll know Collaborators have accepted your invite by looking at the Collaborators section in the Project. Each Collaborator added to the Project will be assigned a color. When the Collaborators from that company add photos or comments, their assigned color will show up to identify what they’ve added to the Project. In the appProject with no CollaboratorsOpen the Project you’d like to invite Collaborators to.Tap on the Menu Icon at the top right corner of the Project Screen.Scroll down and select + Collaborators.On the next screen, tap on + Collaborator.Tap on Share Invite to copy the link or begin sending in a text or email.Project with existing Collaborators Open the Project you’d like to invite Collaborators to.Tap on the Menu Icon at the top right corner of the Project Screen.Scroll down to the Collaborators section and select + Add.Select + Invite a Company.Select + Collaborator.Click on Share Invite to copy the link or begin sending in a text or email.** The invite will expire after 7 days, and a new invite will need to be sent out after 7 days have passed. FAQsWhat information is not shared between Collaborators? Project Description, Documents, Reports, Pages, Tags, and Project Labels are not shared between Collaborators. Can a Collaborator be added to more than one Project? Yes, a Collaborator can be invited to more than one Project. You will need to generate and share a new link for each Project individually. Is there a limit to the number of Collaborators I can add to a Project? No. As of right now, there is no limit to the number of Collaborators you can invite to a Project. However, you will need to generate and share a new link with each Collaborator since invitations cannot be reused. If I collaborate with another company and delete a photo, is it also deleted on my Collaborator's side? Yes. If you collaborate with another company and delete a photo while the Collaboration is active, the photo will be deleted for both you and your Collaborator. If photos were accidentally deleted, they can be recovered within the Project Trash. Can I merge a Project that has an active Collaboration? No. Projects that are part of an active Collaboration cannot be merged. You can only merge a Project once the Collaboration has ended. Can I move photos between Projects if they're part of an active Collaboration? No. You cannot move photos between Projects that are part of an active Collaboration. To move photos, you will need to end the Collaboration. Once the Collaboration has ended, you'll be able to move photos between the two Projects. Can Collaborators view the Files I add to a Project? No. Collaborators cannot view the Files you add to a Project. The Files you upload are only seen by Users on your account. Can I view a list of all Projects I have with a Collaborator? No. There isn't a feature that'll allow you to view a list of all Projects you have with a Collaborator. Projects with Collaborations would need to be opened manually one by one. You can tell if a Project is part of a Collaboration on the Project Feed if there is a Building Icon on the Project Card. Can Collaborators on the Basic Plan upload videos? No. Collaborators on the Basic Plan cannot upload video to a Project.​Can I add a Collaborated Project to a Project Group? Yes, you can add a Collaborated Project to a Project Group, but it depends on whether you're the owning or the invited company within the collaboration.Owning Company: The company that created the collaboration invitation can add the Project to a Project Group.Invited Company: The company that was invited to the collaboration cannot add the Project to a Project Group. Can I invite multiple Collaborators in a single invite? No, only one company can be invited to collaborate at a time. If you need multiple Collaborators, you'll need to send out multiple invites. Related ArticlesManage and Remove CollaboratorsMerging ProjectsHow to Add Guest Access on ProjectsAccepting a Project CollaborationCreate and Manage Project GroupsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Manage and Remove Collaborators | Help Center
content: Manage and Remove Collaborators | Help CenterSkip to main contentManage and Remove CollaboratorsSharing Projects via Collaboration is easy, ending them should be too. Manage access in CompanyCam with these steps. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options. Permission Roles:To manage and remove Collaborators, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Managing Collaborators can be done on both the web and the mobile app.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Open the Project that has Collaborators you'd like to manage or remove.Click on the expandable Sidebar Icon to the right if it's collapsed.​ Click on the Collaborators option to the right. Your Project Collaborators page will show you all active Collaborators on the Project. Click the Three Dot Icon next to the Collaborator you'd like to manage or remove. Select End Collaboration if you'd like to remove the collaborating company completely from the Project. In the appOpen the Project whose Collaborators you'd like to manage or remove.Tap on the Menu Icon at the top right of the page. Scroll to the bottom of the Menu until you get to the Collaborators section. Tap on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the Collaborator's name that you'd like to remove.Tap End Collaboration to remove the collaborating company from the Project. FAQsWhat happens to a project after a collaboration ends?When a collaboration ends, the project history stays intact for both parties:All photos and comments your collaborator added will remain in your project.Your collaborator retains a personal copy of the project in their account, including everything added during the collaboration.Keep in mind that once the collaboration ends, changes are no longer shared. Your collaborator can continue adding content to their copy of the project, but you won't see those updates — and they won't see any changes you make on your end. Can I re-invite a collaborator after ending a collaboration?Yes. You can re-invite a previous collaborator to any project at any time. When you do, a new copy of the project is created in your collaborator's account. The original collaborated project in their account will display an "Ended Collaboration" badge, making it easy to distinguish between the two. Related ArticlesMove PhotosAccepting a Project CollaborationAdd Project CollaboratorsCreate and Use Project TemplatesCreate and Manage Project GroupsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Communicate with Your Team | Help Center
content: Communicate with Your Team | Help CenterSkip to main contentCommunicate with Your TeamKeeping in touch with your team is crucial! Learn about the different ways to communicate with your team within CompanyCam. Updated this weekTable of contentsCompanyCam gives you 2 ways to communicate with your team directly in the app: Project Conversations and Photo Comments. Use Project Conversations to keep a running thread tied to the job, and Photo Comments to leave feedback or notes on a specific photo.On the webProject ConversationsLog in to CompanyCam and open the Project your team is working on.Click the expandable Side Bar Icon on the right side of the screen.Click the Comment Box in the Project Conversation panel. Click the @ Icon or type @ to @mention a group or individual team member to notify someone. Mentioned team members are notified based on their notification settings.Type your comment and click Post. Note: If a message was written in a different language than your device settings, toggle between the original and translated text by clicking See Original or See Translated.Adding a Photo CommentLog in to CompanyCam and open the photo you'd like to comment on.Click on the Comment Box to leave a note under the Comments section. Click on the @ Icon or type @ on your keyboard when commenting to @mention groups or individual team members within your message. Team members who are @mentioned will receive a notification depending on their notification settings. Type your comment and click Post. In the appProject ConversationOpen the Project you and your team are working on.Tap on the Message Icon at the bottom left.Click the @ Icon or type @ to @mention a group or individual team member to notify someone. Mentioned team members are notified based on their notification settings.Type your message and tap Post. Adding a Photo CommentOpen the photo you'd like to comment on.Tap the Comment Icon.Click the @ Icon or type @ to @mention a group or individual team member to notify someone. Mentioned team members are notified based on their notification settings.Type your comment and tap Post. FAQsCan I edit Project Conversations or Photo Comments? No, Project Conversations and Photo Comments cannot be edited or deleted after they are published. Can I @mention Collaborators? No, Collaborators cannot be @mentioned in a Project. What messages can I translate? Translations are available for Photo Comments and Project Conversations. Comments left in any language are translated into either English or Spanish, depending on the User's default language settings. 💡 Need more help? Contact our Support Team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesCreate and Modify User GroupsHow to Use TranslationsIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetGetting Started with CompanyCamManaging your Google Business Profile in Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Add Guest Access on Projects | Help Center
content: How to Add Guest Access on Projects | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Add Guest Access on ProjectsCollect additional photos for your project by adding subcontractors or homeowners with Guest Access! April 22, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on specific plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:To allow Guest Access, you need to have the Admin, Manager, or Standard role. If you don't have one of those roles, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Guest Access invites can be sent on both the web and mobile apps.On the webSign in to CompanyCam on a web browser.Open the Project you want to add Guest Access to.Click the Share button in the upper right corner of the page. Click Add Guest Access from the menu, then click on Add Guest Access in the dialog box that pops up. Click the Add Guest Access button.​ Type in the email address(s) of those you'd like to add for Guest Access. Add any additional information, choose an Access Expiration Date (if applicable), and add a message if you'd like. Toggle access to allow the guest to view all photos if needed and then click Add Guest. The person receiving Guest Access will receive an email and/or text message with a link to the Project. They will need to tap the link, verify their email address, and then begin adding photos to the Project! In the appOpen the Project you want to add Guest Access to.Tap on the Contact Button.Enter the Contact Name, Phone Number, Email, and toggle on Guest Access.Tap Add Contact to send the link. The person receiving Guest Access will receive an email and/or text message with a link to the Project. They will need to tap the link, verify their email address, and then begin adding photos to the Project!FAQsIs there a fee for using Guest Access? No, there's no cost to give someone Guest Access, and they can add unlimited photos to your Project and make unlimited comments. Does the person I'm inviting need to have the CompanyCam app installed? No, they don't. Guests can only access the Project through the link sent to them. What information can they see through Guest Access? You can choose if the person getting Guest Access can see the images you've added to the Project. This can only be done on the web. Can I send multiple Guest Access invites? Yes, however, it's different based on the platform. You can only create one Guest Access invite on the mobile app. You can log in on the web to create additional Guess Access invitations. Can I add Guest Access for a Project Contact that has already been added to the Project? Yes. You can add Guest Access for an existing Project Contact by following these steps. Can people with Guest Access view photos within the Project? Yes! People with Guest Access can view Project Photos as long as the share setting is enabled. You can enable or disable this option in the web app. Here's how:To enable/disable the Guest's permission to view all photos within the Project, open the Project. Click Share, then Add Guest Access. Click the Three Dots in the corner of the Guest's card and select Edit. The option to view all photos within the Project is at the bottom of the section. Toggle on/off to give or remove access, and then click Save Guest to save the changes. If you are working on multiple projects with someone, inviting them to your company account may be the best option for accessibility. Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosUsing CompanyCam with Guest AccessAccepting a Project CollaborationAdd Project CollaboratorsGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using CompanyCam with Guest Access | Help Center
content: Using CompanyCam with Guest Access | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing CompanyCam with Guest AccessFollow the steps below to add Photos and/or Notes with Guest Access. April 10, 2026Table of contentsPlease NoteTo access the Project, you must always access via the link either in your text message or email. You'll only be able to upload photos and add notes to photos. There isn't a way to communicate within the Projects; any responses to the notes won't be displayed.EmailClick the Project Link in the email that you received.​ Enter your email address to verify yourself for access to the Project. ​Choose Add Photos. Once your Photos are added you can click Add Notes to add any additional details to the photos as needed. Text MessageTap on the Project Link in the text message that you received. Enter your email address and tap Verify.Tap Add Photos or Add Note as needed.Interested in finding out what CompanyCam is all about? Go here to start your own account!Related ArticlesHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusHow to Add Guest Access on ProjectsUsing Tags in CompanyCamGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Use Translations | Help Center
content: How to Use Translations | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use TranslationsTasks and Comments will auto-translate from any language into either English or Spanish, depending on your default language settings. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on specific plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:All permission roles can use Translations.Platform Availability:Translations are available on both the web and the mobile app.On the webHow to Translate Project TasksLog in to CompanyCam and open a Project.Click on the expandable Side Bar Icon on the right side if it's collapsed. In the Tasks section, click See Original or See Translation. How to Translate Project ConversationLog in to CompanyCam and open a Project.Click on the expandable Side Bar Icon on the right side if it's collapsed. In the Project Conversation, click See Original or See Translated. How to Translate PagesLog in to CompanyCam and open a Project.Open the Page you'd like to translate. Click the Share button, and you can either copy the link or view the page. Open the shared Page link in a web browser, and click on the Drop-down Menu under Language. Select your preferred language to translate the Page. Currently, we offer English and Spanish. How to Translate Photo CommentsLog in to CompanyCam and open a Project.Open the photo you'd like to view the comment on.Click See Original or See Translated. In the appHow to Translate Project ConversationOpen the Project you and your team are working on.Select the Message Icon next to the Menu button. Select See Original or See Translation.How to Translate Photo CommentsOpen the Project you and your team are working on.Open the photo you'd like to comment on.Tap on the Comment Icon.Select See Original or See Translation.How to Translate PagesOpen the Project you're working on.Open the Page you'd like to translate. Tap the Three Dots Icon. From the dropdown, tap Share Link.Open the shared Page link in a web browser, and tap the Dropdown Menu under Language. Select your preferred language to translate the Page. Currently, we offer English and Spanish. FAQsWhat can be translated in CompanyCam? Translations are available for Photo Comments, Project Conversations, and Project Tasks. Comments left in any language are auto-translated into either English or Spanish, depending on the User's default language settings. Shared Pages Weblinks can also be translated. These links will not auto-translate based on your browser's language settings. Rather, there will be a Language options toggle to switch between languages. Is there a limit to Page Translations? Yes, Users can generate up to 5 Page Translations within five minutes. Once the limit is reached, you'll see an error message and be asked to refresh your Page. Related ArticlesHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Create and Edit Project TasksCommunicate with Your TeamUsing CompanyCam in Spanish: FAQGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: API and Custom Integrations | Help Center
content: API and Custom Integrations | Help CenterSkip to main contentAPI and Custom IntegrationsCompanyCam's open API and documentation make it easy to create custom integrations! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:To integrate, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Creating a Custom Integration can only be done in the web app.Please NoteWhile we're happy to help in any way we can, please note that our Support Team isn't developers. Any troubleshooting will need to be done from the User's end. We recommend working with someone who has experience setting up APIs or Custom Integrations to ensure everything is set up correctly and runs smoothly.Open API OverviewIf you're looking for a place to build a custom integration for your users or publish an integration to the public for all CompanyCam customers to see, you've found the right place! CompanyCam makes it easy for developers to get up and running and start building amazing things. Like our contractors, we value our developers' time, so we've provided you with a ton of resources to make it dead simple to start developing with CompanyCam. Check out our Getting Started page to begin today. If you're looking to partner with CompanyCam and launch a public integration with all CompanyCam customers, please check out our guide to Partnering with CompanyCam. Developer AccountsYou need a CompanyCam account in order to begin developing with CompanyCam. If you already have a CompanyCam account, creating a developer account is optional. If you need a developer account, you can create one by signing up here. Core APIOur primary API is where you can get under the hood and work with the core CompanyCam data. It allows you to read, create, update, and delete most CompanyCam entities like Projects and Photos. We designed it as a REST-based API for easy integration into any solution. Here are a few examples of how it's used:Creating projects from your CRMSyncing photos to your job management softwareSubscribing to a webhook when a photo is taggedGet started today! Important FeaturesOAuth 2.0The authorization code grant type is used to obtain both an access token and a refresh token and is optimized for confidential clients. Since this is a redirection-based flow, the client must be capable of interacting with the resource owner's user-agent (typically a web browser) and capable of receiving incoming requests (via redirection) from the authorization server.WebhooksYou can use webhooks to subscribe to certain events when they occur. Webhooks provide a way to respond to real-time events as they happen without having to constantly poll the API. When an event occurs that matches your webhook scope, a POST HTTP request will be sent to your webhook URL.Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupIntegrate CompanyCam + Followup CRMIntegrate CompanyCam + Builder PrimeIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateIntegrate CompanyCam + ZohoDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extension | Help Center
content: How to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extension | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionAdd the CompanyCam Chrome extension to use your CompanyCam photos wherever you need! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startYou must have an active account with CompanyCam. The extension is not available on Basic. Upgrade your plan to take advantage of this feature.To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.​ Please NoteKeyboard Shortcuts: Users can now batch-select multiple photos at once using the Shift Key and selecting the batch of photos they would like. 'CMD+click' will hold your selection and select more photos. What is the CompanyCam Chrome extension? The CompanyCam Chrome Extension allows you to upload your CompanyCam photos to almost any website that allows you to add photos. Just download the Chrome extension, and you will have access to photos from any Project to drop into most sites! Setup How to add Extensions to Edge Browser instead of Chrome ​How to add Extensions to Brave Browser instead of ChromeNavigate the CompanyCam Extension in the Chrome Web Store via this link.Once at the CompanyCam Extension in the Chrome Web Store, click Add to Chrome. Select Add extension. Once you've added the extension, click on the CompanyCam Icon that appeared once the Extension was installed, and then Sign In With OAuth. Enter your email address and password.Choose Authorize.How it worksAfter you've installed and signed into the extension, it's time to head to a website where you'd like to upload photos. If you do not see the extension at the top right of the Browser window, then click on the Extensions Icon and pin CompanyCam so that it's immediately accessible. The next steps will have to be done for each website you want to upload CompanyCam photos.Once at the website, click on the Extension Icon and enable it. Then, head to the attachments section in your software where you would normally upload photos. Click on Select a File or the relevant button to normally select photos from your device. From here, the extension should open up for you automatically. Search for and open your Project, and then select the photos you want to upload and choose Upload. Confirm the upload if the website prompts you to. Photos are now part of that record or have been uploaded.Repeat this process for each website where you'd like to upload CompanyCam photos! Need Support?​If you need support or have any issues with the extension, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! title: Integrate CompanyCam + AccuLynx | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + AccuLynx | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + AccuLynxIntegrate with AccuLynx to have Photos, Files, Tags, and Videos sync to AccuLynx in real time! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You need to have an active account with CompanyCam and AccuLynx to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To integrate, you need to have the Admin or Manager permission set in CompanyCam and the correct permissions in AccuLynx. If you don't have that role in CompanyCam, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:You must enable the AppConnections add-on in order to connect CompanyCam to your AccuLynx account. Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is AccuLynx? AccuLynx is the leading, all-in-one roofing software designed to streamline every aspect of a roofing business. Since 2008, thousands of roofing companies have turned to their award-winning software to save time, simplify production, and sell more profitable jobs. SetupEnable App Connections in the Market Tab if you haven't already. You can start by going to the Market and selecting Add-Ons. Click on the AppConnections by AccuLynx box. Click Enable. Toggle Enable AppConnections and follow any additional prompts. Once enabled, you will have the ability to enable integrations. To integrate with CompanyCam, navigate to the Market Tab in AccuLynx and select App Connections. Select the CompanyCam Integration. Click Get Connected.​ Select Authorize Connection, and sign in to CompanyCam if prompted. This integration offers a historical sync option. If you select Yes, CompanyCam Projects will be created for AccuLynx jobs that meet the following criteria: Touched within the last 30 daysAssigned to a representativeHave a milestone status of Lead, Prospect, or Approved Select Yes or No based on your preference, then click Connect to proceed. That's it! You are now connected. If you need to update or reconnect your integration, you can select Manage Connection. How to Disconnect the IntegrationOpen Acculynx and log in to your account.In the toolbar at the top of the page, click on Market. Click on AppConnections. Click on CompanyCam. Click on Manage Connection. Click to enable Yes, Remove Connection.Click on Remove Connection. How it worksThis integration allows CompanyCam and AccuLynx to communicate automatically. To ensure a CompanyCam Project is created, you must create a Lead or Job in AccuLynx and assign it to a representative. If a Lead or Job is not assigned, a CompanyCam Project will not be created. Once the Lead or Job is assigned, a corresponding CompanyCam Project will be created automatically, and any photos, photo descriptions, videos, files, and tags captured in that project will sync back to AccuLynx in real time. How to Create a Project in CompanyCam from an Assigned Lead or Job in AcculynxCreate a new Lead or Job in AccuLynx and make sure it’s assigned to a user.​If the Lead or Job is created without an assignment, it will NOT generate a CompanyCam Project. Once your Lead information is added and an assignment is provided, you can hit Save New Lead/Job.​​ Once you add your Lead/Job and assign it in AccuLynx, a matching CompanyCam Project will generate. Assigned Users will also carry over to the matching Project. If the User is set up on both platforms and their username/email matches in both software. You can tell it is from AccuLynx because of the Label titled "AccuLynx" and an additional Label showing what stage the entry is in, Lead, Prospect, Approved, etc. ​At this point, you are ready to capture content in this Project and have it sync back to your AccuLynx entry. How to Sync Photos, Photo Descriptions, Videos, Files, and Tags from an Integrated CompanyCam ProjectTake photos and videos, and add Photo Descriptions and Tags as needed. Photo Descriptions will continue to sync throughout the entire lifecycle of the photo, whether they are added at the time of capture or updated later. You will see the photos and videos you took in the Photos Section of your AccuLynx entry, as well as any tags applied. Clicking into the Photos tab will present all the content for that entry, including the tags that were applied to those photos/videos inside of CompanyCam. ​​Syncing CompanyCam Files to AccuLynx To add Files, click + Add Files within the Project in CompanyCam. ​ When they finish uploading, you will be able to find and view them within the Documents section of Acculynx in the tied record. Syncing a Completed Checklist from CompanyCam as a File to AccuLynxOnce your Checklist is finished, select Export to PDF. Make sure to toggle on Add to Saved Files and select Export. Once you export, you will see that the Checklist shows up in the Files section of CompanyCam and the Document section of the AccuLynx entry.​ Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to the AccuLynx Support team! ​​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + Roof.LinkIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetIntegrate CompanyCam + SRS Roof HubUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Albiware | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Albiware | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + AlbiwareIntegrate CompanyCam + Albiware to manage your job site documentation more easily than ever before! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Albiware in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don’t have that role, ask an Admin on your company’s account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability: Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Albiware?Albi is an all-in-one restoration management platform that integrates everything you need. Built by restorers who understand the industry, Albi gives you clear visibility into key metrics so you can focus on growth and serving your clients. Intuitive, user-friendly, and powerful—Albi has you covered. ​SetupBefore connecting with Albiware, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam.Log in to CompanyCam on a web browser, and from there, click User Icon > Access Tokens from the menu.After you've created an Access Token, log in to your Albiware Account, go to your Settings, and click Integrations. There, you will see the CompanyCam Integration option. Click Connect. A sidebar window will appear. You will need to input your Admin email address and Access Token. Your Admin email address must match in both CompanyCam and Albiware. A blue pop-up will appear in the upper right corner of your screen, indicating you are now successfully integrated. How it worksNow that you are successfully connected to CompanyCam, you'll want to decide how the integration creates Projects. To do this, click on the blue Configure Button and click Settings. You'll have three options to choose from. These options will determine when new Projects are created in CompanyCam. Once you've decided on the configuration you'd like to use, click Save. ​​Option 1: Everytime Project Created: This option will create a Project in CompanyCam automatically when a Project is created in Albiware. Option 2: Certain Statuses: This will create a Project in CompanyCam once your Project in Albiware reaches a certain status(es). Keep in mind you can select more than one. Example: Project 123 is In Production. Option 3: Certain Dates: This will create a Project in CompanyCam once a specific date(s) is completed. Example Project-123 Arrived on Site. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out via the live chat at the bottom right of the website. Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + GetTheReferralIntegrate CompanyCam + SumoQuoteIntegrate CompanyCam + Job-DoxIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Aspire (Powered by MindCloud) | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Aspire (Powered by MindCloud) | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Aspire (Powered by MindCloud)Integrate CompanyCam + Aspire (Powered by MindCloud) to automate and manage your job site documentation easier than before! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You will need to have an active account with both CompanyCam and Aspire, as well as a MindCloud subscription, to connect the integration. Permission Roles:To integrate, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need. Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Aspire (Powered by MindCloud)? MindCloud is a software company that builds and maintains custom integrations between your software and other platforms. They help teams, automators, and businesses eliminate manual data entry by connecting their tools and automating workflows. With a low-code platform and optional white-glove support, MindCloud makes it easy to automate, scale, and grow without complexity. SetupThe following link is a prebuilt MindCloud invitation. When you join through this link, you'll automatically have access to the 4 prebuilt workflows included within this integration. Please see the video below for setup instructions: How it worksThis integration includes 4 prebuilt workflows that sync Projects, Users, and job data between Aspire and CompanyCam. All workflows come fully configured. Users can simply connect their platforms and run the recipes as-is or customize them as needed within MindCloud. Users can toggle the 4 following workflows (recipes) on or off:Every time an Opportunity is created in Aspire, a new Project is created in CompanyCam.Assigned and updated Users are automatically updated in CompanyCam.New photos and Files added to CompanyCam are automatically uploaded to the associated Aspire Opportunity.When you complete an Opportunity in Aspire, Projects are automatically archived in CompanyCam. Need Support?This integration was developed and is supported by MindCloud. If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to MindCloud's Support Team. The best way to access MindCloud Support is via their Slack Community. You can also visit https://docs.mindcloud.co/docs for more information. Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + Beam | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Beam | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamIntegrate CompanyCam + Beam to manage your jobs easier than ever! Updated over a month agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.To integrate, you must have an active account with CompanyCam and Beam.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Beam? Beam offers a full toolkit of financial software and services to automate your back office and help your construction business make more money in less time. Beam’s software & card help your team estimate, invoice, manage expenses, budget, pay bills, and get paid faster—all in one place. With fast online payment, high APY checking accounts, and an unlimited 1% cash-back card, your team will save time and earn more money on Beam. Beam supports general contractors and subcontractors in both residential and commercial construction. SetupLog in to Beam and click the Settings button next to your company name in the top navigation bar.​ Select Integrations.Click Connect next to CompanyCam. This will take you to the CompanyCam sign-in screen to enter your credentials. Once connected, you'll see a Linked tag next to the name on Beams side. All set! You’re now connected! Please note: Once connected, Beam will import your CompanyCam projects from the last 90 days and check for a match in Beam based on project name and address. If a match is found, projects link automatically. If not, Beam creates a new one. This same process applies to all new projects going forward.How it worksCompanyCam + Beam Workflow VideoWhen a Project is created in CompanyCam, a matching Beam Project is automatically created. Any Photos and Files added to the CompanyCam Project will sync back to the CompanyCam tab within the corresponding Beam Project. From there, you can directly attach those Photos and Files to Estimates, Invoices, and Expenses, making it easy to bring your field documentation into your financial workflows. Additionally, users can now link or unlink Projects between Beam and CompanyCam. This means you can manually connect an existing Beam Project to a CompanyCam Project (or disconnect it) if needed, giving you more control over which Projects sync content.In CompanyCam, click the + sign in the left-hand corner bar, then select Create Project.Enter a Project Name and Address, then click Create Project. This will generate the Project in CompanyCam and automatically create a matching Project in Beam. Please note: Include a full address when creating your project. Beam only imports CompanyCam projects that have an address, so projects with only coordinates will not be imported. It may also take up to 5 minutes for the Beam project to appear. After the Project is created, return to CompanyCam and upload Photos and Files to the Project.The uploaded Photos and Files will automatically sync to the matching Beam Project and can be found in the CompanyCam tab on the left-hand side. Within this section, you’ll also see a View Project in CompanyCam option, which will take you directly to the Project in CompanyCam. Once Photos and Files have synced, you can create an Estimate, Invoice, Bill, or Expense within the Beam Project, and then click Add from CompanyCam to attach the synced Photos and Files. Linking a CompanyCam Project to a Beam ProjectOpen the Beam Project you want to link.Click the CompanyCam section, then select Manage Links. Find the CompanyCam Project you want to link and click Link.Search for the Beam Project you want the CompanyCam Project to connect to, then click Save. Once saved, open the Beam Project. The CompanyCam Photos and Files will now appear in the CompanyCam tab, along with a Link to Project in CompanyCam option. Linking or Unlinking Projects via SettingsClick the Settings icon in Beam.Select Integrations, then click Manage Connection. To link a CompanyCam Project, click Edit next to the Beam Project you want to connect, then select the desired Beam Project. Click Save. To unlink a CompanyCam Project, click Edit next to the Beam Project it is currently linked to, click X to remove the link, then click Save. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to their live chat in the bottom right of their site! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProIntegrate CompanyCam + ProcoreIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

With automated costing based on supplier pricing data, the patented estimating platform transforms what used to be static quotes into fully interactive point of sale experiences for homeowners in minutes, winning more jobs and saving hours of time every day. Save more time, win more jobs, and get paid faster with Bolster. Customers can also choose to enable Project syncing so any Projects updated in either platform will be updated in the other. title: Integrate CompanyCam + ClientTether | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + ClientTether | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + ClientTetherUnlock the power of visual engagement with our seamless integration between CompanyCam and ClientTether! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and ClientTether in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:You must have Admin privileges in both CompanyCam and ClientTether to integrate. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is ClientTether?ClientTether is the sales automation & CRM platform of choice for home service franchise systems. Their tools streamline franchise development, & unit-level operations, sales, & growth with leading text, call, & email automation, integrated quoting, payment processing, online reviews, & world-class services to ensure each client succeeds. ClientTether is recognized as a top 2 technology supplier in the franchise industry by Entrepreneur Magazine and as a categorical leader in client satisfaction and ease of use by G2, Capterra, GetApp, and Software Advice. SetupThis integration is an Add-On feature in ClientTether and must be activated before the integration tab is visible. Please visit the Add-On Support Page to learn how to activate Add-Ons. After CompanyCam integration has been activated, a new Settings Tab will become visible. Click on that new tab to continue with the setup process. Setup Instructions Before connecting with ClientTether, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam.Log in to CompanyCam on a web browser, and from there, click User Icon > Access Tokens from the menu.After you've created your Access Token, copy and paste it into the Integration Settings in ClientTether. Optional: Project Naming Convention​This is a field that can be used to auto-generate Project Names if you choose to create a new Project from within ClientTether. Use ClientTether Tokens from the dropdown list to the right or type in some text. For example: if I wanted Projects that start in ClientTether to be named with the initials of CT_ and then the address of the property where the work would be done, and finally followed by today’s date, then in the Project Naming convention field you would enter:CT_{{client.address}}_{{tokens.today}}. Remember, the tokens will come from the Token dropdown menu to the right. When you select the token by name, the value is copied to the clipboard so that all you have to do is paste it into the naming convention field. How it worksOnce the integration is connected and turned on, you will be able to create a new CompanyCam Project or link an existing Project to a Proposal in ClientTether. After your entries are linked, you will be able to upload photos from the tied CompanyCam Project to this Proposal. Once photos are uploaded into ClientTether, you will be able to view the images on the Long Quote in ClientTether! ​​​Link to a Demo on how to utilize the integration​Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to ClientTether Support.​ ​Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + ConstructionClock | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + ConstructionClock | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + ConstructionClockManage your job sites and stay on task easier than ever using the CompanyCam + ConstructionClock integration! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and ConstructionClock to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:You must have Admin privileges in CompanyCam and the correct Permissions in ConstructionClock to integrate. What is ConstructionClock? ConstructionClock is an automated time-tracking app dedicated to construction. Leave your phone in your pocket, and ConstructionClock will automatically clock your crew in when they arrive at the job site and clock them out when they leave. It even tracks travel time spent in between projects, so you don’t miss a thing. Never ask your crew for their hours again. Never forget to clock in again. By connecting ConstructionClock to your CompanyCam account, the Projects you create will automatically sync between both apps, along with the photos assigned to them.​SetupGo to your Construction Clock Settings and select Connected Software.Tap Sync Account next to CompanyCam. Log in to your CompanyCam account, and that's it! You are now connected. How it worksCreate your Project in either ConstructionClock or CompanyCam. A matching Project will be generated in either software once it's added in one. Add Photos to your ConstructionClock Project as needed, and those photos will sync back to CompanyCam. Your CompanyCam-linked Project will include any photos taken in ConstructionClock and vice versa. Photos captured in the CompanyCam Project, as well as any edits, will sync back to the ConstructionClock Project. In the ConstructionClock Project, there will be a button to easily swap back to your CompanyCam-tied Project. title: Integrate CompanyCam and Contractor+ | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam and Contractor+ | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + Contractor+ to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Contractor+ in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.​ What is Contractor+? Contractor+ is one field service management solution that powers everything you do. A complete field service management app solution that helps you manage your client relationships and work productively as a team. All your estimates, invoices, client contracts, post-inspections, and everything else you need to run your business are accessible in one dashboard. Setup How to link your CompanyCam account to Contractor+Click Settings at the bottom of the main menu.​ Choose CompanyCam from the settings menu.​ Click on the Connect to CompanyCam button.​ Log in to your CompanyCam account and & authorize the connection. That's it! You're connected! How it worksHow to automatically sync photos from Contractor+ to CompanyCamThis is all automated! When you take photos in Contractor+, they'll automatically sync to your CompanyCam account under the relevant Project. If a Project in CompanyCam doesn't already exist for the service address, it will be created automatically. How to manually add photos from CompanyCam to Contractor+This works on Estimates, Invoices, Post-Inspections & Jobs. Anywhere and everywhere you can attach photos inside Contractor+, you have the option to select photos from CompanyCam.Click + Add Photos from any Estimate, Invoice, Post-Inspection, or Job. Select Add Photos From CompanyCam. Select the photo(s) you'd like to add to the report you're currently working on, and when you're done, click Add CompanyCam Photos. That's it! Your selected photos will be added where you need them.Note: Contractor+ will only display photos related to the same service address (which is the project address in CompanyCam). You can also further filter the photos list by selecting specific tags when browsing for photos to add. How to use Tags in CompanyCam to automatically link photos to their related Projects in Contractor+From the CompanyCam Project Feed, open your current Project. Select the photo(s) you'd like to tag, and from the Actions Menu at the top, click on Tag. Add Tags relevant to the selected photo(s). For example, if you want a photo to attach to an estimate, add the exact estimate number i.e., "EST-000002" as a Tag. The same goes for invoices, post-inspections, and jobs. When you're finished adding your tag(s), click Tag Photos. It's that easy! Your photos will automatically attach to the relevant report(s) in Contractor+.Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, head to the Contractor+ support page to start a ticket or begin a live chat.​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + One Click ContractorIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + SalesProIntegrate CompanyCam + Contractor's CloudIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Contractor's Cloud | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Contractor's Cloud | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Contractor's CloudIntegrate CompanyCam + Contractor's Cloud to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and Contractor's Cloud to integrate.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.​SetupBefore connecting with Contractor's Cloud, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam.Log in to CompanyCam on a web browser, and from there, click User Icon > Access Tokens from the menu.After you've created your Access Token, log in to Contractor's Cloud.Under Dashboard, click Corporate Dashboard and scroll on the left side to the bottom under Integrations, and click CompanyCam. Post that token you grabbed from CompanyCam here and save. After that, you are good to go! NOTE: If you want to automatically create a Project in CompanyCam once the lead is entered in Contractors Cloud, check the box. How it worksYou can choose to link an existing CompanyCam Project to an active Lead in Contractor's Cloud, or alternatively, you can create new CompanyCam Projects from Leads within Contractor's Cloud! Photos captured in CompanyCam can be attached to a Lead in Contractor's Cloud or synced automatically! FAQsWhat can I do if I need to reconnect CompanyCam photos with the correct Lead in Contractor's Cloud? Below are some steps highlighting how you can reconnect your photos with the correct Lead in Contractor's Cloud:Log in to your Contractor's Cloud account and choose the correct Lead.Click on the Photos/ Files Icon. Click Change Project. Search for Last Name, click on the correct Last Name, and click OK to confirm. Images will now be uploaded into this entry! Need Support?If you have any issues with the integration, you can reach out to the Contractor's Cloud team, and they can assist! You can reach them here. Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlow | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlow | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlowIntegrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlow to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and ContractorFlow to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:You must have Admin or Manager privileges in CompanyCam and the proper permissions in ContractorFlow to integrate the two. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is ContractorFlow? ContractorFlow helps home improvement contractors accelerate the growth of their business and improve profitability by reducing deal time and increasing average deal size. ContractorFlow provides powerful reporting and analytics in real time allowing owners to manage their business from anywhere in the world. With CFlow’s efficiency gains, employees get to focus on generating revenue and offer a better customer experience. Streamline your Leads, Sales, and Production flow in one program. The Contractor Flow app is built on Salesforce, the #1 CRM-Platform in the world. Leverage the power of Salesforce without having to recreate the wheel and leverage thousands of integratable tools for your business.SetupTo set up the integration, please get in touch with ContractorFlow support at [email protected]. They can complete this process for you. How it works The CompanyCam and ContractorFlow integration enables users to create an Opportunity in ContractorFlow and subsequently create a Project in CompanyCam by clicking the drop-down arrow at the top right-hand corner and then clicking Create as Project in CompanyCam. Once this is done, a CompanyCam Project will be created with the information of the Opportunity. The Opportunity in ContractorFlow should then show the address of the Opportunity under the CompanyCam Project Tab. This will confirm that a Project has been successfully created in CompanyCam. The CompanyCam Project Tab in ContractorFlow allows you to open the CompanyCam Project and view the Photos and Files uploaded to the Project in CompanyCam. After establishing this connection, you can upload Photos and Files to the CompanyCam Project, which will sync back to the Opportunity on the ContractorFlow website under the CompanyCam Project Tab. Need Support?If you need help with integration or encounter any issues, reach out to ContractorFlow support at [email protected]. Want to make workflows even easier? Fields not supported by the CompanyCam API will not transfer.Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected]​Want to make workflows even easier? No, only photos will sync back to Contractor Foreman from CompanyCam. title: Integrate CompanyCam + Dropbox | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Dropbox | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxEasily create a backup of your CompanyCam content in Dropbox with this powerful Integration! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have active accounts with both CompanyCam and Dropbox.Permission Roles:You must have Admin or Manager privileges in both CompanyCam and the correct permissions in Dropbox. If you are on a Personal Dropbox Plan, anyone can integrate. If you are using Dropbox Team, then anyone who is a part of the shared folder can integrate. If you don't have this permission, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission. Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Dropbox?Dropbox is a cloud storage service that allows users to store, sync, and share files online, offering features to access and collaborate on content from any device! Please NoteThis Integration will only sync new Projects created after integrating with Dropbox. There is not currently a Historic Sync available at this time.​You can move any Dropbox folders as needed within your Dropbox account. For example, if you need to move your Dropbox Folder that has all your CompanyCam Projects, you can just select the gear option and choose "Move."If you are setting up a Dropbox Business account with CompanyCam, you can copy your Dropbox Business Team Folder link and paste this into the CompanyCam integration field and then hit Connect. If you are using a Dropbox Personal account, no share link is required for setup; just choose Connect.​ Setup Start in CompanyCam. Under Resources, select the Integrations Tab. Click into the Dropbox Card. Click Connect. Copy and paste a shared team folder link if you'd like to create and sync to a Dropbox Business team account. Leave this field blank if you'd like to create and sync to your Dropbox Personal account. Then click Connect. That's it! You should now be connected to Dropbox. How it works ​Once the integration is connected, a CompanyCam root folder will be synced to Dropbox. Anytime a new Project is created in CompanyCam, a new subfolder will be added automatically. Photos, videos, and Files added to CompanyCam will sync to the matching Dropbox record.Once Integrated, you should see that a new main "CompanyCam Projects" folder is created in Dropbox. This is where your subfolders for your Projects will go. Those sub-folders will include photos, videos, and Files from the tied CompanyCam Project. If you set up a Business Shared folder, this is where your "CompanyCam Projects" folder would be located. Next, create CompanyCam Projects as normal. When you create a new Project in CompanyCam, a matching subfolder will be added to your Root CompanyCam Folder in Dropbox. The CompanyCam Project will include a Dropbox Label showing you that it is integrated with Dropbox. When photos, videos, and Files are added to CompanyCam, these will sync to the subfolder tied to Dropbox. ​​​FAQsCan I sync previously created CompanyCm Projects to Dropbox? No. After setting up the integration, newly created Projects will sync to Dropbox. Projects created before setting up the integration will not sync. Do I need to pay any additional fees to utilize the Dropbox integration? No, there are no other fees associated other than being on a paid CompanyCam plan and having the proper plan in Dropbox! Will each User on the account need to set up the integration? Only one Admin will need to set up the integration. Once connected, you can move the Parent folder if needed to give everyone access, and any User's content will sync. Need Support?​If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadIntegrate CompanyCam + Google DriveIntegrate CompanyCam + Microsoft SharePointDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Estimate Rocket | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Estimate Rocket | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Estimate RocketIntegrate CompanyCam + Estimate Rocket to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and Estimate Rocket to integrate.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Estimate Rocket?Estimate Rocket is the proven growth system for contracting companies that are ready to take their business to the next level. From Job & Client Management, Power CRM Tools, Team Collaboration, and more! SetupPlease see the documentation linked below to integrate CompanyCam + Estimate Rocket: ​ Integration Instructions and Documentation How it worksCompanyCam + Estimate Rocket Overview Anytime a Project in Estimate Rocket is created, a Project will also be created in CompanyCam. Once created, you can go into the created CompanyCam Project, add your photos and edits, and they will sync back automatically to that entry in Estimate Rocket. In Estimate Rocket, you can select the green plus near photos to immediately jump back into that CompanyCam project to snap more photos. Add your Project in Estimate Rocket. The Estimate Number and address will pull over to CompanyCam as a new Project. Upload your photos to the Project in CompanyCam. In real time, these photos will pop back over into your Estimate Rocket entry! Selecting the Green Plus Button in Estimate Rocket will bring you back to the CompanyCam Project in a new tab on the computer or bring you to the CompanyCam app on a mobile device, making it easy to add more photos!Creating New CompanyCam Projects from Existing Entries in Estimate RocketWant to start a CompanyCam Project and document your job site for an entry that was already added in Estimate Rocket? No problem! You can create a fresh CompanyCam Project, whether the Estimate Rocket entry is listed as No Status, An Estimate has already been scheduled, or the Estimate has already been completed! All you need to do is update the action inside of Estimate Rocket to create this Project. If there is No Status: Select Schedule Estimate under Actions. Schedule your Estimate in Estimate Rocket. Under Actions that should show as "Complete Estimate" inside of Estimate Rocket, and then you can check your CompanyCam account to see a freshly created Project! If the Estimate has been Scheduled: Select Unschedule Estimate. Schedule the Estimate in Estimate Rocket. A Project will be created in CompanyCam! If the Estimate has been Completed: Choose Accept Work in the Estimate Rocket Entry. A Project for this entry will be available in CompanyCam! Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to Estimate Rocket support at [email protected].​ ​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + SumoQuoteIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProIntegrate CompanyCam + busybusyIntegrate CompanyCam + DripJobsIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

content: Integrate CompanyCam + FieldPulse | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + FieldPulseIntegrate CompanyCam + FieldPulse to manage your documentation easier than before!
