What is FieldPulse? Serving 50+ field service industries, FieldPulse is the #1 rated and most awarded field service management software for businesses looking to scale. Using Field Service Management (FSM) software, field service businesses can optimize their processes to improve customer satisfaction, increase operational efficiency, and grow revenue. With best-in-class customer support and simple pricing, business owners can trust that FieldPulse will be their partner every step of the way.​SetupPlease see the documentation linked below to integrate:Integration Instructions and Documentation How it worksThe CompanyCam + FieldPulse integration allows users to create Customers in FieldPulse and have a Project be created in CompanyCam. If the address already exists, the FieldPulse customer will be linked to that Project in CompanyCam.The address is the key link, so if multiple of the same address exist in FieldPulse, photos would sync to both.When photos, videos, and PDFs are added to a Project in CompanyCam, they will automatically sync to the Customer record in FieldPulse that has the matching address. Users can add CompanyCam files to customers and Projects/Jobs/Estimates/Invoices. This is accomplished by:On mobile, go to the record of your choosing, tap the Take Photo in CompanyCam Button, which launches the CompanyCam app. Pictures are taken and then synced back to files on that record in FieldPulse.On the FieldPulse web app, CompanyCam files will appear on the Files tab of records.Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to FieldPulse support at [email protected] or check out their direct support options. ​Want to make workflows even easier? content: Integrate CompanyCam + Followup CRM | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Followup CRMIntegrate CompanyCam + Followup CRM to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Followup CRM in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. This is slightly different than some integrations but will allow for user-specific documentation between both services. What is Followup CRM?FollowUp is the best customer relationship management software, specially designed for and preferred by Construction Industry Pros, Contractors, and builders. Followup CRM helps you get off spreadsheets and streamline your construction sales and bidding process.​SetupLog in to FollowUp and click on My Settings. Select the Integrations Tab and then choose Connect to CompanyCam. Once this is connected, you will see this status change, and you can select Disconnect if you ever need to remove the integration! How it worksOnce integrated, create a new Project in FollowUp CRM Under File Data.Enter the Project name, address, and any other information needed, and save.Once created, click on the Create CompanyCam option at the top of the Followup CRM entry.This will generate a CompanyCam Project. The button will update from Create CompanyCam to View CompanyCam. Clicking this will take you to the Project in CompanyCam.Now when you take Photos in this matching CompanyCam Project photos will sync back to the Followup CRM entry under Documents! Another important feature of this integration is that you can pull Projects that are already created in CompanyCam over to Followup CRM. To do this, create a New Project in CompanyCam, and you will see a matching record created in Followup CRM! The ability to create Projects is useful; however, care should be taken not to create duplicates if possible. If you happen to create duplicate Projects, you can Merge Projects and then delete the duplicate Project in Followup CRM if needed. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to Followup CRM support at [email protected] or hop into their live support chat for immediate assistance. GAF Project streamlines the entire sales process — integrating lead management, digital measurement, inspection, presentation, estimating, proposals, e-signature, and payments — into one easy-to-use digital tool. With one-click estimates and ready-to-use content, GAF Project means you’re always ready for business — from anywhere — and ready to help protect what matters most. SetupLog in to GAF Project and click on Setup.Scroll down to the Integrations Tab.Find CompanyCam and click Authorize.​ You will be redirected to CompanyCam to authenticate your information.Sign in to CompanyCam using your email address and password. Then click Sign In.After you sign in to CompanyCam, you will be redirected back to the GAF Project's Integration Tab.On the Integrations tab, you will now see a list of Users.The system will automatically link up users between GAF Project and CompanyCam where the email addresses match.You can manually match users by clicking the pencil button to match other users.​ How it works Jobs are automatically synced from CompanyCam with all of the appropriate information (Name, Address, etc.). As updates are made on the CompanyCam side, those things are automatically updated in the GAF Project. Photos are automatically pulled from CompanyCam and added to the Photos Tab in GAF Project. title: Integrate CompanyCam + GetTheReferral | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + GetTheReferral | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + GetTheReferralIntegrate CompanyCam + GetTheReferall to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and GTR in order to integrate both platforms.Permission Roles:To start integrating the platforms, you must have Admin privileges in both services. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Get the Referral? Consumers have never been more actively engaged with the brands they use in their everyday lives and are more willing than ever to talk about and refer them to. Average Americans look at their phones a staggering average of 96 times a day. With adults spending 2 hours and 54 minutes a day on their phone, 86% of that time in apps, it pays to have a branded mobile app dedicated to getting referrals, managing them, and paying out rewards. This is where GetTheReferral provides tremendous value for the businesses that we serve. We'll show you how easy it is to put your branded mobile app in the hands of your customers right from the point of sale to help streamline, automate, and amplify the referral process. The results speak for themselves, with GTR customers generating a 22X return on their investment on average: SetupBefore connecting with GetTheReferral, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam.Log in to CompanyCam on a web browser, and from there, click User Icon > Access Tokens from the menu.After you've created your Access Token, log in to GetTheReferral.In the left-hand menu, click Integrations > CompanyCam and paste the token into the Auth Token field.Congratulations! Your integration between CompanyCam and GTR is complete! How it worksYour customer will download the Your Brand app and create an account to become your advocate:GetTheReferral will automatically create a Project in CompanyCam with the advocate's name.CompanyCam's User (e.g., your project manager on site) will locate the advocate's Project in the CompanyCam app and take the pictures.Your advocate will get push notifications when pictures are uploaded to their project and will access the pictures in the Your Company app.The GTR account administrator can also see the pictures in GTR dashboard.Need Support?If you have any trouble with this integration, please reach out to the support team at Get The Referral.​​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your Integration up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + SumoQuoteIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + Job-DoxIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlowDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + GiddyUp | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + GiddyUp | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + GiddyUpIntegrate CompanyCam + GiddyUp to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startYou must have an active account with both CompanyCam and GiddyUp in order to integrate the two services.Integrations are not available on Basic. Upgrade your plan to take advantage of this feature.To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Photos will only sync to GiddyUp if the user login from your CompanyCam account matches the login in GiddyUp. What this basically means is that for your CompanyCam photos to appear in GiddyUp, the user must exist in both systems with the same email address. What is Giddyup? GiddyUp has everything you need to scale your business, with the ability to configure settings to suit your bottom line. Cloud-based CRMThe automated and streamlined workflow is configured for your operations, improving productivity, so that you can get back to what you do best.​CanvasserA mobile solution that allows you to take command of door-to-door sales, capture leads, schedule appointments, and track progress in real time.​Customer ConnectCustomer Connect gives your customers access to their own portal. This platform lets customers see information on their project team, retrieve documents, and allows customers to view photos of job progress. Setup Go to your Setup page in GiddyUp. Click on Integration under General on the left side. Click on the CompanyCam Logo. Click the Authorize button.Sign in to your CompanyCam account.Select Authorize.Click Save & Close.How it worksOpportunities in GiddyUp will sync to CompanyCam, but not the other way around. Opportunities that you create in GiddyUp will instantly sync to CompanyCam, but Projects that you create in CompanyCam will not sync back to GiddyUp. Photos will sync from CompanyCam to GiddyUp, but not the other way around. The photos your company takes in CompanyCam will instantly be available in GiddyUp, but photos that you take or upload in GiddyUp will not sync to CompanyCam. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, please reach out to [email protected]. Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + Google Calendar | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Google Calendar | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Google CalendarWith this integration, you will be able to create Google Calendar events from CompanyCam! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Google Calendar in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:This integration is only available via the web version of CompanyCam. Please NoteYou may want to set up a Calendar specifically for work-related events to use with this integration. All Events created in Google Calendar under the calendar you set up will create Projects in CompanyCam if that is the workflow you use. ​This Integration allows you to pick between two different workflows:You can create a new Google Calendar Event from an existing CompanyCam Project.You can have a new CompanyCam Project created from a new Google Calendar Event. You won't want to set up both flows, as that will cause duplicate CompanyCam Projects. ​Need a way to have some events sync to CompanyCam as Projects but not all? Try creating a new Calendar and only using this Calendar for when you want to create matching CompanyCam Projects. Steps here What is Google Calendar?Google Calendar is a powerful time management and Calendar tool. Easily create and edit events and help get everyone on the same page with Google Calendar! SetupThe steps below are only for the workflow to create new Projects in CompanyCam when you add a new Event in Google Calendar. If you just need to create a new event from an existing Project, you can refer to this section to learn how to use it! ​How to set up creating a CompanyCam Project from a Google Calendar EventIn CompanyCam, under the Resources section, click on the Integrations Tab. Click on the Google Calendar Card. Once inside the integration's card, click Connect. Next, we will need to grab the Calendar ID from the Calendar(s) that you'd like to create Projects from. To locate your Calendar ID, open Google Calendar, go to My Calendars, and find the calendar you want to use. Click the three-dot icon next to that calendar, then choose Settings and sharing from the menu. Scroll down to the Integrate Calendar section and locate the Calendar ID. Copy this ID, as you will need to paste it into the integration steps within CompanyCam. Note: The Calendar ID can either be an email address or a sequence of letters and numbers similar to the screenshot below. An email address will appear if it's your default calendar and the sequence ID will appear if it is a calendar you've created. Paste the Code(s) back into the Calendar ID's box in the Integration Setup page and hit Save. OR After you grant Google access to integrate, you are good to go! Keep in mind that multi-calendar support is available. Note: When entering multiple Calendar IDs, separate them with commas without spaces, like this: CalID1,CalID2 instead of CalID1, CalID2. Including spaces after commas may cause issues with project creation. How to Disconnect the IntegrationIn the left-hand navigation menu, click on the Integrations Tab under the Resources section. Find the Google Calendar integration card using the search bar or by scrolling. Click on the card, then select Disconnect. How can I create a new Calendar in Google Calendar to use as my Calendar to trigger new Events as CompanyCam Projects? Look for the Other Calendars section on the left sidebar in Google Calendar. Click the + Icon next to the Other Calendars Section. Select Create New Calendar from the dropdown menu. Enter a name for your new calendar (e.g., "Work Projects," "Family Events") and an optional description. Click the Create Calendar button to finalize. How it worksYou will have the option to either create Events in Google Calendar from a CompanyCam Project or create a Project in CompanyCam when a Google Calendar event is added. You will not want to utilize both workflows, as that would cause duplicate Projects in your CompanyCam account. We will cover both options below! ​How to create an event in Google Calendar from a CompanyCam ProjectThere is no setup necessary with this particular workflow. Create a CompanyCam Project and add any relevant Contact Info. Next, click on the 3 Dots Icon on the top right and click Send to Google Calendar. This will take you to Google Calendar and prefill the event with the Project info. Make any changes if necessary and then click Save. Now you are good to go! This event will also have the Project contact info and a URL link out to the CompanyCam Project for easy reference. How to create a CompanyCam Project from a Google Calendar Event​This workflow will require you to complete the Setup steps at the top of this article. Create your event in Google Calendar. The CompanyCam Project title will be the Event name, and the address will be the Location added to the event. Add the name and location, and then click Save. A matching CompanyCam Project will be generated with the event name and location. You can tell the Project came from Google Calendar as it has a yellow Google Calendar Label attached. The Project Linkout to CompanyCam will be available in the linked event in Google Calendar for quick access to the Project. Need Support?​If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, please reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + Google DriveIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Google Drive | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Google Drive | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Google DriveEasily create a back up of your CompanyCam content in Google Drive with our powerful Integration! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.To utilize this integration, you'll want to ensure you're on a plan in CompanCam and the correct plan in Google Drive. You must have active accounts with both CompanyCam and Google Drive.Permission Roles:You must have Admin or Manager privileges in CompanyCam and the correct permissions in Google Drive. If you or another User needs their permissions updated in CompanyCam here is how.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Google Drive? ​Google Drive, part of Google Workspace, lets you securely store, intelligently organize, and collaborate on files and folders from anywhere, on any device!​With Google Drive, you can safely store and access your files anywhere. Edit and store 100+ file types, including PDFs, Office files, videos, and more! ​ Please NoteYou can move the CompanyCam Folder to a shared drive as needed once the Parent folder is initially created. Select the 3 dots to the right of the folder name, select "Organize", and then "Move."​This integration does not currently have a historical sync available. Only new Projects created after integrating will sync!Setup​Start in CompanyCam. Under Resources, select the Integrations Tab. Find the Google Drive card and select it. Select Connect. Hit Save. Next, it will have you choose which Google account you're looking to integrate with. Choose the correct one in your case and then hit Allow. That's it! You should now be connected to Google Drive. How to Disconnect the IntegrationIn the lower-left corner, click on the Integrations Tab. Find the Google Drive integration card using the search bar or by scrolling. Click on the card, then select Disconnect. That’s it! Your integration has been disconnected. How it works When you first connect the integration, this will create a folder called "CompanyCam." From here, any new Projects added to CompanyCam will create a subfolder in Google Drive under your main folder.​​PLEASE NOTE: YOU CAN MOVE THIS PARENT FOLDER TO A SHARE DRIVE OR WHEREVER YOU NEED IN GOOGLE DRIVE. ​Then photos, videos, and Files will sync to that subfolder created from CompanyCam! If the subfolder is moved to another location, new content will still sync back to the subfolder in Google Drive. Once Integrated, you should see that a new main "CompanyCam" folder is created. This is where your subfolders for your Projects will go. Those sub-folders will include photos, videos, and Files from the tied CompanyCam Project. Next, simply work on your CompanyCam projects as normal! When you create a new Project in CompanyCam, a matching subfolder will be added to your main CompanyCam Folder in Google Drive. When photos, videos, or Files are added in CompanyCam, they will then sync to that subfolder tied to Google Drive. FAQsCan I sync previously created CompanyCam Projects to Google Drive? No, after setting up the integration, newly created Projects will sync to Google Drive. Projects created before setting up the integration will not sync. Do I need to pay any additional fees to utilize the Google Drive integration? No, there are no other fees associated other than being on a paid CompanyCam plan and having the proper plan in Google Drive. Does every User on the account need to set up the integration? Only the Admin needs to integrate! Once connected, you can move the Parent folder to a shared drive if needed to give everyone access, and any User's content will sync to that shared drive in Google Drive! ​Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadIntegrate CompanyCam + Google CalendarIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxIntegrate CompanyCam + Microsoft SharePointDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + HeyPros | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + HeyPros | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + HeyProsSync your CompanyCam Project to a HeyPros Work Order and ensure all photos subcontractors send you are instantly uploaded to CompanyCam! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active paid account with both CompanyCam and HeyPros in order to integrate the two services. Permission Roles:You must have Admin privileges in CompanyCam and the correct permissions in HeyPros to integrate. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is HeyPros? In today's fast-paced construction industry, efficiency, clarity, and streamlined operations are paramount. Enter HeyPros, a game-changing platform designed exclusively for the construction world to revolutionize the way you manage your subcontractor network and work orders. HeyPros acts as a centralized hub, enabling businesses to seamlessly connect with subcontractors, track compliance, dispatch work orders swiftly, and keep an eye on every project's real-time progress. With a focus on optimizing workflows, HeyPros is committed to ensuring that every stakeholder works in harmony, paving the way for a smoother project lifecycle. SetupWithin HeyPros, go to the left-hand side and click Settings. Under Settings, select Integrations.Next, click Configure within the CompanyCam Tab and sign in to your CompanyCam account if you are not already signed in. Once you log in, you will be connected! You can confirm that the two are integrated if the status changes from Configure to Configured.​How it worksOnce integrated, create a new CompanyCam Project.Once your Project is created, you can go to HeyPros and begin building your Work Order for this job. Fill out the necessary information for your Work Order and then toggle on Link to CompanyCam Project.​ After turning the toggle on, you can select the matching Project from the Dropdown. If photos or Documents have already been added for that Project, you can select them with the checkmark in the top right of the photos. Once you choose your Project and photos you'd like to tie to this work order, you can click Continue in the bottom right. ​​ Next, you can add your Contractors to the Work Order as normal and click Send to. From there, the Contractor will be notified of that Work Order! Contractors can add their photos to this Work Order by adding them from their device or the camera directly in the Work Order chat. Once photos are uploaded to the Work Order, those Photos will sync back to the CompanyCam Project. Tags will also be added to the photos showing that they were taken in HeyPros, as well as the name of the Contractor who took them. Once an invoice has been created you can attach photos in HeyPros to this Invoice. Once the Invoice has been approve,d the Photos will attach to the matching CompanyCam Project! Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, please reach out to the HeyPros team at [email protected]​ ​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + AtlasPro+Integrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + Contractor ForemanIntegrate CompanyCam + IssueIDDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Housecall Pro | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Housecall Pro | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall Pro to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! Updated over a week agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You need to have an active account with CompanyCam and Housecall Pro to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To integrate, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Housecall Pro? Founded in 2013, Housecall Pro helps home service professionals thrive. They offer an industry-leading SaaS operating platform combined with modern financial services and supporting business solutions, helping Pros run all aspects of their business. Housecall Pro is a cloud-based field service management app for service professionals to automate workflows and streamline technician dispatch.​Please NoteEach Address added under a Customer in HCP will have a matching CompanyCam Project entry. Photos and videos for every Estimate or Job in Housecall Pro that have been added for that Address will be found under the Address Project in CompanyCam. There will not be multiple Projects for Jobs at that Address.You can not sync photos or videos back to Jobs created before the integration was set up. A new Estimate or Job must be created to send Files, photos, and videos back to Housecall Pro. By default, the active Estimate or Job that the Project will sync photos and videos to is the most recently created one. If you would like your CompanyCam Project to sync photos and videos to another entry for an address, you can select the "On My Way" option for that Estimate or Job, and the photos will sync to that entry.If an Estimate or Job is created before an address is added under the Customer, this can cause syncing and duplicate issues. Always create your Customer and address first before creating the Estimate or Job for that client.Videos over 100MB in size may not sync over to Housecall Pro. SetupOpen CompanyCam and under Resources, click on the Integrations Tab. Find the Housecall Pro card. Select this and then click Connect. You have the option to sync Primary Contacts from HouseCall Pro into CompanyCam by checking the box, as well as if you want content from CompanyCam to be synced to HCP Estimates, Jobs, or both! After you decide, click Save. You can also enable the automatic assignment of Users from HouseCall Pro into CompanyCam. Note: The User's email in Housecall Pro must match the email in CompanyCam for this to work. Users assigned to the corresponding Housecall Pro Customer will overwrite any Users assigned to the Project in CompanyCam. Users who were previously assigned to the Project in CompanyCam will be unassigned. If you have not signed in to Housecall Pro, there will be a prompt telling you to sign in to your account. That's all! You are now ready for smooth documentation in Housecall Pro using CompanyCam. You will find that the "Connect Integration" changes to "Connected" when the integration is operating properly. Once the integration is set up, you can select the HCP card to Edit or Disconnect as needed. How to Disconnect the IntegrationOpen CompanyCam and log in to your account.In the lower-left corner under Resources, click on the Integrations Tab. Find the Housecall Pro integration card using the search bar or by scrolling. Click on the card, then select Disconnect. How it worksWhen you create a new customer in Housecall Pro, a matching project is created in CompanyCam. Once you add an Estimate or Job to that customer, the project updates to sync with that specific entry in HCP. From there, any photos, videos, or Files you upload to the CompanyCam project will automatically appear in the attachments section of the active Estimate or Job in Housecall Pro. If you have an existing customer that was created before setting up the integration and does not have a linked project in CompanyCam, you can trigger this by going to an existing Estimate or Job and clicking "On My Way." This will create a new CompanyCam project linked to that customer’s Estimate or Job. The system will automatically sync to the most recent active Estimate or Job. If you want to manually set which one is active, you can do so by clicking "On My Way" in HCP. Just allow a few minutes for everything to sync. Follow the steps below to get everything set up properly.Start by creating a new customer and address in Housecall Pro. Once this is done, a matching project will automatically be created in CompanyCam with the label "Housecall Pro Customer." Note: At this stage, Files, photos, and videos will not sync yet, as there is no active Estimate or Job. After the customer is set up, create a new Estimate or Job under that same customer. This will automatically update the label on the CompanyCam project to either "Housecall Pro Estimate" or "Housecall Pro Job." This label change enables the syncing functionality. Once the Project Label has changed to "Housecall Pro Estimate" or "Housecall Pro Job", you can start uploading your content to the CompanyCam project. Anything you add, such as photos, videos, or Files, will automatically appear under the Attachments section in the active Estimate or Job in HCP. A direct link to the CompanyCam project will also be included for easy access. Make sure uploads are under 500 MB to sync successfully. If you don’t see the files or the CompanyCam link right away in HCP, give it a minute and refresh the page. They should appear shortly.For Existing Customers Without Linked ProjectsIf the customer was created before the integration setup and does not have a linked CompanyCam project, open an existing Estimate or Job for that customer and click "On My Way." This will trigger a new CompanyCam project to be created and linked to that customer’s Estimate or Job. Redirect Uploads to a Different Estimate or JobBy default, the most recently created Estimate or Job will be treated as the active one that receives uploads. If you want to manually choose which one is active and switch which Estimate or Job your photos, videos, and Files sync to, you’ll need to open the specific Estimate or Job under the correct customer in HCP and click the "On My Way" option. This sets the Estimate or Job as the active one for syncing uploads.After selecting "On My Way," please wait about five minutes before uploading any photos to ensure everything has time to sync properly. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your FSM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam + Estimate RocketDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + HOVER | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + HOVER | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam + Hover to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and have a membership plan with Hover in order to integrate the two platforms.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Hover?​ Hover transforms photos of any property into an accurate, interactive 3D model. Thousands of contractors are using HOVER to put together accurate estimates and show homeowners what different materials and colors will look like in and outside of their home! SetupOpen CompanyCam and under Resources, click on the Integrations Tab. Locate the Hover card and click on it to open. Click Connect in the top right-hand corner. Select the option to Sync Primary Contacts to CompanyCam, then click Save. This will cause Hover to open. Sign in to your Hover account (if necessary). Click Allow. After clicking Allow, you will see its status change to Connected. After setting up the integration, you'll see options to Edit or Disconnect on the card to the right, so you can make changes if you need to. How it works​This integration works with both single and multi-structure Projects! You’ll start the process by creating an interior or exterior project in the Hover app and capturing the required photo set (at least 8 photos). Once those photos are captured, a CompanyCam Project is automatically created. This means the Project exists earlier in your workflow, so you can begin adding notes, checklists, and other job site documentation right away. Photos captured in Hover for this Project will sync to your CompanyCam Project as soon as they’re available. Any interior or exterior measurement reports, along with additional structure documents, will also sync back to the matching CompanyCam Project under the Files Tab. If you add additional structures inside the Hover Project, they’ll appear within the same CompanyCam Project. Each structure will generate its own File in the Files Tab, keeping everything organized without needing to create additional projects. On the webOn the web, you can open the Hover Project and invite a Homeowner or Teammate to add extra Photos to your Project.In Hover, open your Project and click Share at the top right. Select Homeowner or Pro (Teammate), fill in their Email, Name, and Phone Number, and add a Message if you like. Once everything’s filled out, click Send. In the appOpen the Hover app and hit the + at the bottom to start a new interior or exterior Project.Select either Interior measurements or Exterior measurements.Type in the Property Name and Address, pick a Deliverable, then click Get Started.You’ll be prompted to snap up to 8 photos of the property in order to generate the report.After taking the 8 photos, you can click Add Photo to include extra photos for your records (these won’t affect the 3D model), or click Continue to move on.Click Finish.You’ll see the Project appear in Hover, which will automatically create the CompanyCam Project while the report is being generated.While the report is generating, you can open the Hover Project in CompanyCam.This lets you and your crew add photos or other content to the CompanyCam Project while the report is being generated.Once the Report is generated, you’ll find it in the Documents section of the CompanyCam Project under Files.​Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat!​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam + REISIntegrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + SumoQuoteIntegrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlowDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Inspect.Properties | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Inspect.Properties | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Inspect.PropertiesAI-powered property reports: weather, measurements, onsite photo docs, and estimates April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Inspect.Properties in order to integrate the two services. Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Each user needs to set up their own OAuth connection. It can’t be enabled for the whole account. Please NoteEach User needs to set up their own OAuth connection. It cannot be enabled for the whole account. What is Inspect.Properties? Inspect.Properties provides roofers, adjusters, and property professionals with a complete package of inspection and claims documentation. Their platform unifies four core components—roof measurements, historical weather reports, onsite photo documentation, and material estimates—into structured, professional reports that can be trusted by both contractors and carriers. ​By combining these inputs, they eliminate the inefficiencies of piecemeal tools and deliver one streamlined workflow: order measurements and weather histories before stepping on-site, capture and upload inspection imagery in the field, and receive a finished report with AI-structured evidence and clear annotations. The result is a defensible claims package that shortens onsite time, reduces back-office effort, and strengthens the credibility of every submission. SetupPlease see the documentation linked below to integrate:Integration Instructions and Documentation How it WorksBy connecting your CompanyCam and Inspect.Properties accounts, any photos uploaded in either platform will flow bi-directionally. Roof measurements, weather data, estimates, and synced photos are then compiled into structured reports, which are stored in the Files Tab of the corresponding project in CompanyCam. From there, users can choose whether to share reports organization-wide or keep them at an individual level. If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or call the dedicated account phone support given upon signup. title: Connecting CompanyCam to Jobber | Help Center
content: Connecting CompanyCam to Jobber | Help CenterSkip to main contentConnecting CompanyCam to JobberConnect CompanyCam and Jobber to automatically sync job site photos, videos, and files with your Requests, Quotes, and Jobs. Updated todayTable of contents Jobber is a business management platform for small home service businesses. Connect it to CompanyCam, and every new Client, Request, Quote, or Job in Jobber creates a matching Project automatically, so your job site photos, videos, and files live right alongside your Jobber records. This article covers connecting the two. For how the sync works once you're set up, see Using the CompanyCam + Jobber Integration.This integration is available on select plans and is only available on the web app.Setting Up the IntegrationNote: You must have Admin or Manager permissions in both CompanyCam and Jobber to set up this integration. In the left-hand navigation, under Resources, click Integrations. Find the Jobber card and click it.Click Connect to begin the integration process.A settings pop-up will appear. If you want your Jobber client contact information to sync to the Contact Info section of your CompanyCam Projects, check the box next to Sync Primary Contacts to CompanyCam. Note: Sync Primary Contacts to CompanyCam only applies to new Clients created after the setting is enabled. Existing Projects will not be updated retroactively with contact information from Jobber. Click Save.You'll be prompted to allow CompanyCam access to your Jobber account. If you aren't already signed in to Jobber, enter your credentials to authorize the connection. Once connected, the Jobber card will display "Connected" on your Integrations page.Note: CompanyCam will automatically import up to 1,000 of the most recent active Clients from Jobber and create linked Projects. You'll see these appear in CompanyCam shortly after connecting.Setting Up Auto Assign UsersThe Auto Assign Users feature automatically assigns team members in CompanyCam based on who's assigned in Jobber. For Requests, the user designated to execute the Request is synced over. Assignment is not available at the Client stage — it applies only after a Request, Quote, or Job has been created.Go to Integrations and find the Jobber card.Click Edit on the Jobber card.Check the box next to Auto Assign Users. Click Save.Keep these things in mind when using Auto Assign Users:Email addresses for users in Jobber must match their email addresses in CompanyCam exactly for automatic assignment to work.Users must be active on both platforms to receive automatic assignment.The Assigned User setting must be enabled in CompanyCam at the account level. This is managed by an Admin or Manager.Users assigned through Jobber will overwrite any existing users already assigned to the corresponding CompanyCam Project.Note: If all users are removed from a Jobber entry at once, CompanyCam will not unassign any of them — existing assignments stay in place. Only individual unassignments sync over. Users will need to be manually unassigned in CompanyCam if all were removed at once.Creating a Custom Field to Link CompanyCam Projects in JobberNote: You must be on Jobber's Connect or Grow plan to use custom fields.Adding a custom property field in Jobber automatically populates a direct link to the corresponding CompanyCam Project. This lets you open a CompanyCam Project directly from a Quote, Job, or Invoice page in Jobber. Click the gear icon in the top right of your Jobber home page, then click Settings.In the left sidebar, click Custom Fields.Under Properties, click + Add Field.Fill in the custom field details exactly as shown in the screenshot below. Set the Field Type to Text and name it exactly CompanyCam Project, then click Create Custom Field.Important: The custom field must be named exactly CompanyCam Project (with a space between the two words). Any variation will prevent the link from populating automatically.Disconnecting the IntegrationIf you run into issues or want to refresh the connection, you can disconnect and reconnect the integration at any time from the Integrations page. Note: Disconnecting does not delete your CompanyCam Projects. Reconnecting will resume syncing for active Clients and Jobs.FAQWill the custom field work for entries that already exist in Jobber?No. The custom field will only populate for new Quotes, Jobs, and Invoices created in Jobber after the field has been set up. Existing entries will not be updated automatically.Can I connect more than one Jobber account to CompanyCam?No. CompanyCam supports a one-to-one connection with Jobber, meaning only one Jobber account can be connected to your CompanyCam account at a time.💡 Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetConnecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitanUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Job-Dox | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Job-Dox | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Job-DoxIntegrate CompanyCam + Job-Dox to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! May 6, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Before integrating, you'll need to have an active account with Job-Dox and CompanyCam.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Job-Dox? Job-Dox is a fully functional project management software system that is mobile and user-friendly. With our project management tools, you can effectively keep track of emails, phone calls, and texts while still achieving important goals in regard to your business. SetupGenerate an Access Token Before connecting with Job-Dox, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam. Log in to CompanyCam on a web browser, and from there, click User Icon > Access Tokens from the menu.Now that you've copied your Access Token, we will want to paste it into Job-Dox. To do this, you will first log into your Job-Dox account and click the App Drawer Icon near the top right. Click Settings. Once you are in the Settings section, click the CompanyCam Button on the left. Your menu options may look different than this screenshot, based on what features you have turned on. Click the Checkbox to enable the integration. Enter your CompanyCam email address (username) and the Access Token that you generated in your CompanyCam account. Click Save. Validate your integrationOnce you click Save, Job-Dox will attempt to validate the Access Token. If there is a problem with the information you provided, the status will update to "Failed" and you can try again after double-checking your username and access token. Otherwise, it will say "Active". To test the integration, go create a new project in Job-Dox. If you go to the Photos section of Job-Dox, you will see a new Album entitled CompanyCam. Opening that album will also give you a link to that Project in CompanyCam in the top-right corner. How it worksWith the Job-Dox and CompanyCam Integration, you can have a matching CompanyCam Project get created automatically when you create a Job-Dox Project! Easily view your linked Project in Job-Dox in the photo section of Job-Dox. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach their team here.​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + SuccessWareIntegrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration SoftwareIntegrate CompanyCam + KnowifyDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusCreate Projects in CompanyCam from Jobs or Contacts in JobNimbus, Then Sync photos and Files back to JobNimbus from CompanyCam! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.To integrate CompanyCam with JobNimbus, you’ll need active accounts on both platforms.Permission Roles:You need Admin or Manager privileges in both CompanyCam and JobNimbus, and the email address used for your Admin or Manager account must match in both platforms when setting up the integration.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is JobNimbus?​JobNimbus is a CRM and project management software wrapped up in one application. The platform is an end-to-end solution designed to help contractors (roofing and construction professionals) streamline their communication and better manage their teams. From customer contacts to scheduling, boards to lead acquisition, jobs to payments, JobNimbus is the place to accurately update and track all company information. SetupGo to JobNimbus, click your User Profile in the top-right, then select Settings. Scroll down to the Integrations section and click on API. Click on New API Key in the top right corner. Then, scroll down to the bottom of the token list, click the empty (white) field, and choose CompanyCam. After that, click Select Profile and pick Admin from the list, then hit Save. Just a heads-up, the profile you select should match the Permission Set you have in JobNimbus. Once the key is created, make sure to copy it by clicking Copy to the right of the new key at the bottom of the list. You can double-check that it’s the right one by confirming the creation date. Open CompanyCam and click on the Integrations option within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Locate the JobNimbus Card and click on it to open. Click Connect. Paste the API Key you copied from JobNimbus into the API Key section. Enter the Admin Email Address from JobNimbus. NOTE: The email entered here must be the same one tied to your JobNimbus account that you logged in with when you created your access token. You can click on your profile in JobNimbus to confirm the email you are logged in with. Select to sync Contacts or Jobs from JobNimbus to CompanyCam, and then click Save. You're not done yet. Just a few more steps! Next, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam. To do this, you'll click on your User Icon > Access Tokens. Note: DO NOT checkmark the option "This token will ONLY be used to read data from CompanyCam", as this will make the token Read-Only and prevent the integration from syncing information. After you've created your Access Token, return to your JobNimbus account and go to Settings. From there, select CompanyCam from the menu on the left under the Integrations section. NOTE: If you don’t see CompanyCam in the list, go to your account Settings, click Features under the General section, and toggle on CompanyCam under the 3rd Party Features section. Paste the Access Token from your CompanyCam account into the CompanyCam API Token section and select either Contacts or Jobs. Remember, your selection must match the one you made in Step 10. Click Save. Once the integration is completed, you will see its status change to Connected. After the integration is set up, you will notice Edit and Disconnect options to the right of the integration, allowing you to make changes if needed. How it worksYou can create new Contacts or Jobs in JobNimbus, which will automatically trigger the creation of a new CompanyCam Project labeled either "JobNimbus Contact" or "JobNimbus Job." Any photos or Files added to this integrated project will instantly sync back to the corresponding Contact or Job under the Photos or Documents section. Additionally, user and sales rep assignments in Contacts and Jobs will now sync to Projects under the Project Users section in CompanyCam. This means when you assign a user or sales rep to a Contact or Job in JobNimbus, they will automatically be assigned to the corresponding Project in CompanyCam and will appear in the Project Users section within the project. Please note that this feature only applies to newly created Contacts or Jobs, not previously created ones. To ensure smooth syncing, users must be set up on both platforms and have the same email address linked to their accounts. Create a CompanyCam Project and sync content from a JobNimbus Contact.Once integrated, you can either search for an existing JobNimbus Contact or create a new one. If creating a new contact, be sure to fill out the First Name, Last Name, and Address, and assign the desired Sales Representatives and Users. If you don’t want to assign any users, you can leave that field blank. Then click Save & Continue to complete the Contact setup. OR Once created, you should see a matching CompanyCam Project created with a JobNimbus Contact Label attached. If you assigned a Sales Representative or User to the Contact, you’ll see them listed in the Project Users section inside the Project in CompanyCam. NOTE: If you unassign Users in CompanyCam, it will not be reflected in JobNimbus. At this point, you can take photos and upload Files to this Project as usual. Once uploaded to this Project, they will sync to the matching Contact. You can view the synced Photos and Files within the JobNimbus Contact, in the Photos and Documents sections. Create a CompanyCam Project and sync content from a JobNimbus JobOnce it’s integrated, you can start by creating the Job in JobNimbus. You’ll be prompted to either create a new Contact or search for an existing one. If you’re creating a new Contact, make sure to fill in the First Name, Last Name, and Address, and assign any Sales Reps or Users you want. If you don’t want to assign anyone, you can leave that field blank. Then just click Save & Continue to finish setting up the Contact. Then click Create Job. Once the Job is created, you’ll see a matching CompanyCam Project automatically generated with the JobNimbus Job Label attached. If you assigned a Sales Representative or User to the Job, you’ll see them listed in the Project Users section inside the Project in CompanyCam. NOTE: If you unassign Users in CompanyCam, it will not be reflected in JobNimbus. At this point, you can take photos and upload Files to this Project as usual. Once uploaded to this Project, they will sync to the matching Job. You can view the synced photos and Files in JobNimbus from the Photos and Documents sections! Photos Section: Document Section: FAQsWhat does it mean to sync the primary contact in this integration? Syncing primary contacts to CompanyCam allows the main contact from JobNimbus to carry over to the corresponding Project in CompanyCam when it’s created. Need Support?If you need help with the integration or encounter any issues, please reach out to CompanyCam's support team. You can email us at [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVERConnecting CompanyCam to JobberIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVER | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVER | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVER to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.If you are a customer of JobNimbus and HOVER, you can take advantage of a powerful three-way integration between CompanyCam and those services!Permission Roles:You need to have the Admin permissions role in CompanyCam and the necessary permissions in JobNimbus and HOVER to set up the integrations. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.SetupThe first thing that you need to do is make sure all three integrations are set up properly. Here are links to the documentation on how to set each one up:How to set up the integration with JobNimbusHow to set up the integration with HOVER JobNimbus Documentation on how to set up JobNimbus and HOVER - instructions for how to send a HOVER order are included here.How it worksAfter you have all three set up, here is the optimal workflow:Create a Contact or Job in JobNimbus (depending on how you have it integrated); this will automatically create a CompanyCam Project. Order the HOVER in JobNimbus - Instructions for this are in the JobNimbus article.Go to the HOVER app and look for the order that's waiting to have photos taken, and take the required 8 photos or more.Submit the HOVER order in HOVER.Once the order is submitted and the processing ends, the photos will be pushed to the CompanyCam Project that was created in Step 1.​Once the report is done processing, it will be pushed to the Files Tab within the CompanyCam Project.All content is synced back to JobNimbus. You can expect the photos to be synced back to CompanyCam shortly after they are taken. The report will be synced to CompanyCam, then JobNimbus.Your team can take photos in CompanyCam within the same Project, and they will be synced back to JobNimbus, keeping everything in one JobNimbus record. If you want your photos to sync back to JobNimbus, please make sure to never manually create a Project within CompanyCam. If a Project is manually created in CompanyCam, it will not be connected to the integration, and photos won't sync. Need Support?​If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupIntegrate CompanyCam + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam + busybusyIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + JobTread | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + JobTread | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTread to easily manage your jobsites! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and JobTread to integrate.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is JobTread? JobTread provides end-to-end construction management software to help jobs-based businesses more efficiently manage all of their processes, from pre-construction through to project completion. The platform creates a central location to manage jobs, teams, schedules, documents, photos, finances, customers, vendors, sub-contractors, and more all in one place. Its budget-first approach creates financial transparency at every stage of a project, mitigating risk and ensuring the project stays on time and on budget. Get organized, complete your projects on time, and increase your profits with JobTread. SetupNavigate to your JobTread Settings and click on the Integrations Tab.Select the CompanyCam Integration.In the CompanyCam integration section, click on the Create Link Button and follow the on-screen prompts or instructions that appear.How it worksWhenever you create a Job in JobTread, a corresponding Project will be automatically generated in CompanyCam. The Project will include primary contact, locations, and coordinates associated with the Job.As long as you have matching file Tags in both systems, any Tags you attach to photos in CompanyCam will also be attached to the files when copied to JobTread. Whenever a Project is edited in CompanyCam, the file will be updated in JobTread. This includes any markup on photos or name and description changes on non-photo/video files. By following these step-by-step instructions and understanding how the integration functions, you can successfully set up the JobTread and CompanyCam integration and take advantage of their combined capabilities for efficient project management and file organization.​Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + ProcoreIntegrate CompanyCam + KnowifyIntegrate CompanyCam + ConstructionClockIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamIntegrate CompanyCam + MarketSharpDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Leap | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Leap | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + Leap to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and Leap to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Leap?Leap is an all-in-one cloud-based contractor’s business platform that is flexible, easy to use, and highly intelligent. Yielding the quickest time-to-value ratio, Leap allows contractors and their teams to meet their business, strategic, and personal goals in a simple, easy-to-adopt format. The integration connects Leap Jobs to CompanyCam Projects. You can then select the photos and Files you want to save to a Leap folder, and they can be used for estimates and invoices in Leap. SetupBefore connecting with Leap, you'll need to generate an Access Token from CompanyCam.Log in to CompanyCam on a web browser, and from there, click User Icon > Access Tokens from the menu.After you've created your Access Token, log in to Leap, click in the upper right corner to locate the menu, and choose Settings. Navigate to your Company Settings and click Partnerships/Integrations. Under the CompanyCam Tab, click Connect. Paste the Access Token that you copied in CompanyCam and click Connect. That's it! Now, the photos you take in CompanyCam will automatically be available to view, use, and save to Jobs in Leap! How it works Create a CompanyCam Project automatically when a Job is created When adding a Job to a Lead, Prospect, or Customer, make sure that the CompanyCam option is checked. When you save the Job, a Project with the Customer's name and address and Job Name will be created. Create a CompanyCam Project after a Job is createdGo into the Job that you'd like to link.Click View All to the right of Recent Photos and Documents.​ Select the two arrows icon and then Sync.​ Confirm that you'd like to sync the Job with CompanyCam.​​Link an existing CompanyCam Project to a Job in LeapGo into the Job that you'd like to link.Choose View All to the right of Recent Photos and Documents.​ Select the Two Arrows Icon and then Link.​ Choose which CompanyCam Project you'd like to link to this Job and then hit Proceed. Use Photos and Documents in LeapOnce you have a Leap Job and CompanyCam Project linked, you'll be able to save those photos to a job folder.Click View in the Photos & Documents section of a Job with a linked Project. Here you will see all the Project photos from CompanyCam. To save them to this specific Job, you would select the 3 Dots Icon in the bottom right corner of the Photo and then select Save In Job.​ Note:If you'd like to use the photos in an estimate or invoice, make sure to Save In Job, then choose the Photos folder, which saves them there.Photos will be available to send as needed in Leap once they are added to that folder.​Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected]. Do you want to make your workflows smoother? Now that you have your CRM up and running, take a look at our other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + Job-DoxIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProIntegrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration SoftwareIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Markate | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Markate | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateIntegrate CompanyCam Markate to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Markate in order to integrate the two servicesYou can utilize the Integration during your CompanyCam Trial at no additional cost. Following that period, the Integration is an additional $10 a month for unlimited users added to your monthly Markate bill. Permission Roles:You must have Admin privileges in both CompanyCam and Markate to integrate. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.​What is Markate? ​Markate is a software that helps businesses with CRM, field service management, sales, and marketing automation. It allows users to efficiently manage all their business schedules and keep their operations running using any device. Users can track all expenses, store receipts, and record mileage on one dashboard. ​SetupLog in to Markate on the web. Under More, click Connectors. Find the CompanyCam card. Once selected, click Connect to CompanyCam, and that's it! Now your CompanyCam photos can be accessed in Markate at any time. How it worksOnce integrated, upload photos in CompanyCam to an Estimate, Invoice, or Work Order. Photos will not sync automatically. Look for the section on the Markate entry towards the bottom titled "CompanyCam Photos". From here, you have the option to select a CompanyCam Project to add Photos from or create a completely new Project in CompanyCam for it! Up to 100 Photos can be added from CompanyCam to your entry in Markate. This gives you access to your full cloud-based Photo library in CompanyCam for any Markate entry!Once integrated, find the CompanyCam Photos Section under your Estimate, Invoice, or Work Order. Select Attach CompanyCam Photos. Choose either Select from existing Project or Create a new Project. Selected Photos from CompanyCam will be available in this Markate Entry! Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or call 480-360-1360. Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + Microsoft SharePoint | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Microsoft SharePoint | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Microsoft SharePointEasily create a copy of your CompanyCam Projects and content in Sharepoint automatically with this powerful integration! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have active accounts with both CompanyCam and SharePoint.Integrations require specific plan levels. Ensure that you’re on a plan in CompanyCam and the correct plan in SharePoint to utilize this integration.Permission Roles:You must have Admin or Manager privileges in CompanyCam and the correct permissions in SharePoint. It is recommended that a SharePoint Admin integrate so you have access to all subfolders that you may wish to sync content back to. If you or another User needs their permissions updated in CompanyCam, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission. Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Microsoft SharePoint? Microsoft SharePoint is a cloud-based service that helps teams of all sizes collaborate and manage their documents! With Microsoft SharePoint on your PC, Mac, or mobile device, you can:Build intranet sites and create pages, document libraries, and lists.Add web parts to customize your content.Show important visuals, news, and updates with a team or communication site.Discover, follow, and search for sites, files, and people across your company.Manage your daily routine with workflows, forms, and lists.Sync and store your files in the cloud so anyone can securely work with you.Catch up on news on the go with the mobile app. Please NoteThis Integration will only sync new Projects created after integrating with SharePoint. There is not currently a Historic Sync available at this time.​You can move any SharePoint folders as needed within your account. For example, if you need to move your CompanyCam Folder, you can select the gear option and choose Move to.​​​ SetupStart in CompanyCam and select the Integrations Tab within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. ​ Open the Microsoft SharePoint Online Card. Click Connect in the top right. Then once more in the next screen. At this point, you need to select the Site and Library within SharePoint to which you'd like your content to sync. This is the path where Projects and their content will sync. Once you choose those, hit Save. Once that saves, you are now connected to SharePoint! A CompanyCam Project root folder will be created in SharePoint, and new Projects and content that you add in CompanyCam will sync there in SharePoint automatically! How it worksOnce you integrate, a CompanyCam Root Folder will be created in SharePoint. Anytime a new Project is created in CompanyCam, a new subfolder will be created here automatically. Those sub-folders will include photos, videos, and Files from the tied CompanyCam Project.Once Integrated, you should see that a new main CompanyCam folder is created in SharePoint where you set up the integration to sync. This is where your subfolders for your Projects will go. Those sub-folders will include photos, videos, and Files from the tied CompanyCam Project.​​​Next, create CompanyCam Projects as normal. When you create a new Project in CompanyCam, a matching subfolder will be added to your Root CompanyCam Folder in SharePoint. The CompanyCam Project will include a "Microsoft SharePoint Online" Label showing you that it is integrated. When photos, videos, and Files are added in CompanyCam, these will sync to SharePoint under either the Photos or Documents section of the Project subfolder. ​ The Photos folder will show you all the photos and videos for the matching Project. Files in CompanyCam will sync to the Documents subfolder. ​ FAQsCan I sync previously created Projects to SharePoint, or only those created after I set up the integration? The integration will only work with newly created Projects after setting up the integration. Do I need to pay any additional fees to utilize the SharePoint integration? No, there are no other fees associated other than being on a paid CompanyCam plan and having the proper plan set up in SharePoint. Will every User on the account need to set up the integration? No, only one Admin or Manager will need to set up the integration for the account. Just make sure they have Admin access in SharePoint so that they can access the Folders your team needs to set up. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadIntegrate CompanyCam + Google DriveIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + monday.com | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + monday.com | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + monday.comCustomize your Workflows with CompanyCam and monday.com to make your documentation more efficient than ever! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have active accounts with both CompanyCam and monday.com. Ensure that you’re on a plan in CompanyCam and the correct plan in monday.com to utilize this integration.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is monday.com? monday.com is a global software company that builds products people love to manage the core of their work. Their product suite can adapt to the needs of diverse industries and use cases within one powerful platform. More than 250,000 customers are reimagining how work gets done, driving greater efficiency, and scaling like never before. For more information, visit monday.com. How-To-VideoSetupIn CompanyCam, click Integrations within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Find the Monday.com card and open it. Click Connect in the top right corner. If the CompanyCam app is not already installed in your monday.com account, a pop-up will appear. Click Save to start the installation process. Please note: If the app is already installed, you’ll be taken directly to Step 6. You’ll be redirected to the monday.com website, where you can choose to grant access to either All Workspaces or Specific Workspaces. After making your selection, click Install. Please note: that if you’re not an Admin in monday.com, a banner will appear, and you won’t be able to continue. Next, you’ll be taken to authorize the integration. Click Authorize to continue. After authorizing, you’ll return to the setup screen in CompanyCam. Select the workspace you want and click Save. Keep in mind you can connect to only one workspace at a time. Once saved, you can link an existing monday.com board to that workspace. Click the Link Board to get started. You can link multiple boards simultaneously. This will take you to the board mapping process. Before starting, ensure your monday.com board has these required columns: Title/Name (Customer Name)PersonStatus (Event Trigger)AddressFilesPlus any additional columns you'd like included In Step 1, select the Board you want to start mapping. Please note: We will pull in the 500 most recently created boards into our board selector. If you have more than 500 boards in your account or cannot find the one you need, please reach out to support. Step 2 will then appear, allowing you to begin the mapping and event trigger process. Keep in mind that the event trigger is what pushes the integration to create a new Project in CompanyCam from your board columns. Please note: Photos, Videos, and Files will only sync if a destination item is selected and set to Files, and the board includes a Files column. Once you’ve chosen all required options, click Create. Your integration is now connected! To link additional boards, click Link Board and repeat steps 7 to 9. How it worksThis integration allows you to choose which monday.com boards sync with CompanyCam! Customizable field mapping allows you to choose which monday.com columns populate CompanyCam Project fields (e.g., Project name,Person, address, status). Flexible triggers allow you to decide what events in monday.com create new CompanyCam Projects. ​Then, once you capture content in CompanyCam, you can choose if they want to sync CompanyCam Photos/Videos, Files, or both back to the corresponding monday.com item. A deep Project link can be added to the monday.com item for one-click access to the CompanyCam Project, and visual updates appear in real-time back in monday.com! Log in to your monday.com account and navigate to your integrated workspace. Select the Board you linked, either from the left-hand menu or directly in the workspace. To create a new customer, click either New Customer Name, + Add Customer Name, or the custom field name used on your board. This will create a new contact (or row) on your linked board, allowing you to enter the necessary information. Be sure to complete all required fields you configured in the mapping settings. Once you’ve entered all the required information, be sure to set your event trigger (e.g., Status = Working on it). This trigger is required for the system to automatically create a new Project in CompanyCam with the Project Label Monday.com. Without this trigger, the Project will not be created. You can then open the Project in CompanyCam and start uploading Photos, Videos, and Files. As long as your board has a Files column, the uploaded items will sync back to that column in Monday.com. Please Note: Any changes made later to the Name/Title, Person, or Address columns in monday.com will automatically update the corresponding information in the linked Project in CompanyCam. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier?
