title: Integrate CompanyCam + NiceJob | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + NiceJob | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + NiceJobIntegrate CompanyCam and NiceJob to level up your team's marketing efforts! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and NiceJob to integrate.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.​What is NiceJob? NiceJob is the all-in-one reputation marketing software for busy and budget-conscious business owners. It puts review generation, referrals, and social media content on autopilot. Use it alongside CompanyCam to show your customers the great work you did and get more reviews! ​SetupGo to the Apps page in NiceJob's Product Settings: https://app.nicejob.com/settings/product/appsClick Connect on the CompanyCam App listing.Log in to your CompanyCam account when prompted.​(Note: The integration only works when the initial connection is done through an account with Manager or Admin access.)You are connected! How it worksCompanyCam + NiceJob Demo:Automatically send review invites with photosConnect CompanyCam with your CRM to automate the process of sending review invites with photos! CompanyCam connects with the following NiceJob app integrations: Jobber, Housecall Pro, HubSpot, JobTread, QuickBooks, ServiceMinder, Markate. It can also connect with any app that is available through Zapier.Connect NiceJob to your CRM on the Apps page: https://app.nicejob.com/settings/product/appConnect your CRM to CompanyCam through CompanyCam’s Integrations page: https://app.companycam.com/integrations ​(Important: Check the 'Sync Primary Contacts to CompanyCam' box before connecting. This will be unchecked by default.)ORConnect NiceJob to your CRM through Zapier (see help article here).Connect your CRM to CompanyCam through Zapier.Once your CRM is connected to both NiceJob and CompanyCam you can start triggering automatic review invites with photos!For example:You create an entry in your CRM for a new customer. A new Project in CompanyCam will be made for that customer if one hasn't already been created.Upload photos to the Project in CompanyCam.Tag your best photo of the work you have done for your customer in CompanyCam with 'NJReview'. All tagged photos will be imported to the NiceJob Stories page: https://app.nicejob.com/stories.Complete the job/proposal/invoice in your CRM.NiceJob automatically enters your customer into the Get Reviews campaign with the most recently tagged photo from that customer's CompanyCam Project. Send review invites to customers manually No connected CRM? You can still use CompanyCam to easily import your best photos to the NiceJob Stories page and trigger review invites from there!Create a new project in CompanyCam.Upload photos to the Project.Tag your best photo of the work you have done for your customer in CompanyCam with 'NJReview'. All tagged photos, as well as the customer's contact information will be imported to the NiceJob Stories page: https://app.nicejob.com/stories.Click Get a review under the story. If your customer's contact information was saved in the CompanyCam Project, then the invite will go out in one click to that customer. If not, you just need to add your customer's contact information to enrol them into the Get Reviews campaign with the most recently tagged photo from your CompanyCam Project.Note: Remember to update your customer contact details in CompanyCam for each project so that we receive the correct customer information for each photo Share your reviews with photos to your website and social media feedsOnce you receive a new review from a customer invited through the CompanyCam integration, it will be linked to their imported photos in your Stories page. If you have our Stories widget installed on your website (see installation instructions here), your reviews and photos will be automatically published together straight to your website! This helps drive your website conversion rate from new visitors. Your reviews and photos can also be shared together to your social media sites. You can either choose for this to be done automatically or through manual selection (see how here). This will enhance your social media posts to boost engagement. Data NiceJob will send or receivePeople (customer name, email, and phone number)PhotosNeed Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or go to https://help.nicejob.com/en/. Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + One Click Contractor | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + One Click Contractor | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + One Click ContractorIntegrate CompanyCam + One Click Contractor to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account for CompanyCam and One Click Contractor for you to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.What is One Click Contractor?Designed by and for home remodeling contractors, One Click Contractor's all-in-one sales platform makes measuring, estimating, and selling incredibly simple. Create estimates in minutes, present professional proposals, and reduce time spent selling. SetupLog in to One Click Contractor and navigate to Administration in the top left corner.Scroll down to the Integrations Tab. Find CompanyCam and click Authorize.You will be redirected to CompanyCam to authenticate your information and Sign In.After you sign in to CompanyCam, you will be redirected back to the One Click Contractor's Integration Tab.On the Integrations Tab, you will now see a list of Users.The system will automatically link up users between One Click Contractor and CompanyCam where the email addresses match.You can manually match users by clicking the Pencil Button to match other users. How it worksYou can utilize the integration in a few different ways. Photos taken in a linked CompanyCam Project will sync back to the Job in One Click! Those workflows are detailed below:Create a Project in CompanyCam when a Job is Created in One Click: When checked, the system will automatically create a project in CompanyCam when a job is created in One ClickCreate a Job in One Click Contractor from a Project in CompanyCam: When checked, the system will look at newly created projects in CompanyCam and create a corresponding job in One Click. Reassign a Project in CompanyCam when Updated in One Click Contractor: When a job in One Click is reassigned to a different user, the integration will update CompanyCam to reflect the new user as an “assigned user”.Important details about this integrationJobs are automatically synchronized from CompanyCam with all of the appropriate information (Name, Address, etc.) As updates are made on the CompanyCam side, those things are automatically updated as it runs. Photos are automatically pulled from CompanyCam and added to the Photos Tab in One Click Contractor. Note: This integration could cause duplicate records if you are integrating other third-party programs (specifically MarketSharp) into One Click Contractor. Do you have questions about records or this integration? Email Rachel at [email protected]! Are you looking to make workflows even easier? If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.​What is PaintScout?PaintScout is built for Painting contractors to succeed. Painting contractors have special needs, like prep times, paint volumes, work orders, colors, measuring by area, and so much more. PaintScout makes implementing all these variables a breeze. When you have a proposal that is easy to understand and sells your company, your close rate goes up. PaintScout Web Proposals do just that and are easy to set up and send. Also, when you have the ability to give your team clear work orders, the job will get done right the first time. With PaintScout, you can give your crew clear and detailed work orders to set them up for success. Simple tools for better customer experience. PaintScout has tools like adding options, showing pictures and videos, customized pricing, grouping areas together, and substrate view. PaintScout allows you to show your customer a more tailored estimate to suit their needs.​SetupPlease see the documentation linked below to integrate: ​Integration instructions and documentation. How it worksThis integration brings selected photos from CompanyCam Projects directly into PaintScout Estimates. Choose between original and annotated photos. They will be placed in the area or line item that you added them to. Photos will also be available in the media section of the estimate at the bottom. title: Integrate CompanyCam + Pipedrive | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Pipedrive | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + Pipedrive to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and Pipedrive to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. Please NoteThe first time you integrate, your active Deals will sync over to CompanyCam as Projects for a historical sync. This way, you can start using the integration right away. What is Pipedrive? Pipedrive is the easy-to-use, #1 user-rated CRM tool. Get more qualified leads and grow your business. Pipedrive helps users visualize their sales processes and get more done. They knew from experience that in sales, as in life, you can’t control your results, but you can control your actions. So, they created Pipedrive around activity-based selling, a proven approach that’s all about scheduling, completing, and tracking activities.​​SetupLog in to CompanyCam and click on the Integrations Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. Open the Pipedrive V2 card on the integrations page and click Connect. Next, you'll choose which address the integration will use to create a Project.Deal: Creates a Project in CompanyCam using the address from the Deal in Pipedrive.Person: Creates a Project in CompanyCam using the address from the Person on the Deal in Pipedrive. Optional: At this point, you also can choose if you'd like a CompanyCam Project to be created once a Deal hits a certain stage. This will be pasted into the Optional box in CompanyCam. If you only want a Project to be created in CompanyCam when a Pipedrive Deal moves to a specific stage(s), make sure to list the names of the stages exactly in the Stage integration option. For example, if you only want a project to be created when a Deal moves to the "Proposal Made" or "Negotiations Started" stages, set the text box value to: Proposal Made, Negotiations Started Paste your Internal ID from Pipedrive into the Optional box in CompanyCam. Click Save to continue setting up the integration. After clicking save, you'll be prompted to log into your Pipedrive account if you're not already signed in. Next, click Allow and Install. You'll know that the integration is connected when a green 'Connected' label appears. How it worksDeals in Pipedrive will create CompanyCam Projects automatically. A link-out to the CompanyCam Project will be available in the Pipedrive Deal for easy access to photos and all Files added in CompanyCam, which will sync over to the Pipedrive Deal. The address used to create a Project in CompanyCam will be determined based on the setting chosen within your integration settings (see Step 3 of the Setup instructions above).Create a Deal in Pipedrive. Find the matching Project in CompanyCam. Take photos and upload Files as normal. A CompanyCam link-out will be available in the Pipedrive Deal for easy access to photos. Files uploaded to CompanyCam will show up within the History Section of the Deal within the Files Tab. Need Support?If you need help with the integration or encounter any issues, please reach out to CompanyCam's support team. You can email us at [email protected] or reach out to our live chat!​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that your CRM is up and running, check out other integration opportunities! title: Integrate CompanyCam + Procore | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Procore | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + ProcoreIntegrate CompanyCam + Procore to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Procore to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:You must have Admin privileges in both CompanyCam and Procore. If you don't have that, your Admin can update your permissions. Here is what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Procore?Procore is the all-in-one construction management software built to help you finish quality Projects safely, on time, and within budget.SetupIn CompanyCam, click on the Integrations option under Resources in the left-hand navigation menu. Look for the Procore card under the different Integration options and open it. ​ Select Connect. Select Continue to link your Procore account to CompanyCam. Once you continue, select Log in with Procore. This will then take you to Procore, where you will select Allow. Confirm the Procore company you set up with the CompanyCam integration. That's it! You will now be connected. You should see the card updated and a Disconnect option. If you ever need to turn the integration off, you can select this. Note: When you first connect the integration, CompanyCam will sync over any active entries in Procore as CompanyCam Projects. Then, moving forward, all Projects added to Procore will create new CompanyCam Projects. How it worksAnytime you add a new Project in Procore, a new Project in CompanyCam will be created automatically. This CompanyCam Project will also have a Procore Label attached. If you add photos or Files to the CompanyCam Project, they will automatically go back into the Procore Project under Documents. Create a Project in Procore. ​ Once a Project is generated in Procore, a matching CompanyCam Project will be created automatically.​ Capture photos and upload Files to this matching CompanyCam Project and see those sync back to the Procore Project under the Documents Tab in real time! title: Integrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks Online | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks Online | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineUtilize the CompanyCam + QuickBooks Online integration to manage your jobs easier than ever! Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have active accounts with both CompanyCam and QuickBooks Online. Ensure that you’re on a plan in CompanyCam and the correct plan in QuickBooks Online to utilize this integration.Permission Roles:To integrate, you must have Admin privileges in both CompanyCam and the correct permissions in QuickBooks Online. If you or another User needs their permissions updated in CompanyCam, here is how.You need to either have the Admin or Manager permissions in order to sync photos to QuickBooks Online.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. Please NoteThis integration is only available for QuickBooks Online. The desktop version of QuickBooks will not work with this integration. Need to reconnect your integration to the newest version? Follow these instructions:Navigate to the Integrations tab within your account and open the connected integration card listed under “QuickBooks Legacy (Deprecated).” Click the “Upgrade” button to begin the transition to the updated integration.Once the upgrade is complete, the integration name will update to “QuickBooks Online,” and the previous Legacy Integration card will automatically disappear.​There are no changes to the current functionality of the integration at this time. What is QuickBooks Online? QuickBooks Online is a cloud-based financial management software. It's designed to slash the time you spend managing your business finances by helping you with tasks such as creating estimates and invoices. Tracking sales and cash flow.​SetupIn the left-hand navigation menu, click on the Integrations option under Resources. Find the QuickBooks Online Card and select it. Hit Connect in the top right corner. Now the integration is almost complete, just follow the steps to link your two accounts by selecting Continue.Continue signing in with your account and following any additional prompts. Once you finish logging in with QuickBooks Online that's it! You are now connected. ​​Note: You can choose whether you would like to hide Invoices and Estimates synced over to CompanyCam as Files from Restricted and Standard Users with the settings toggle. You can also choose to sync Payments from CompanyCam back to QuickBooks Online ​How it works When a new Customer or Sub-Customer is created in QuickBooks Online, a matching Project will now be automatically created in CompanyCam.Projects created from Customers will use the Customer’s Display Name and Shipping Address.Projects created from Sub-Customers will use the Sub-Customer’s Display Name (even if it differs from the parent’s name) and the Sub-Customer’s Shipping Address — not the parent’s mailing or billing address.Each new Project in CompanyCam will include a “QuickBooks Customer” label to indicate it was created through the QuickBooks Online integration.If you create a new Estimate or Invoice for a Customer or Sub-Customer that hasn’t yet synced to CompanyCam, this will also trigger a new Project to be created.Invoices and Estimates added in QuickBooks Online for this Customer will be available in the Files section of CompanyCam. Payments that have reached "Paid" status in CompanyCam will sync back to the matching QuickBooks Customer and be found under transactions. ​From there, you can check out our Signatures feature to fill these invoices or estimates out directly in CompanyCam. Projects with Invoices will have an additional label “Invoice Attached." Projects with Estimates will have an additional "Estimate Attached" label. ​A copy of the link to the CompanyCam Project will be available in the Notes section for that customer in Quickbooks online. Photo syncing is only available for the higher tiers in QuickBooks Online. Photos won't sync from QuickBooks Online to CompanyCam automatically but manually syncing is available on both the web and mobile app. You will need to be a Manager or Admin in CompanyCam in order to sync Photos. ​​You can follow the step-by-step guide below to see the integration in action:Create a Customer in QuickBooks Online. Go to New in the top left corner and select Add Customer and Save. The name, address, email, and phone number will sync to the created CompanyCam Project. Please note: Sub-Customers can be synced as new Projects as well. Once your Customer is created, you can create Invoices or Estimates for that created Customer and Save. Once saved, those Estimates and Invoices will sync to the Files section of the matching Project. Invoice: b. Estimate: ​ Once that information is saved, you should see a matching CompanyCam Project. Any Invoices or Estimates added for this customer in QuickBooks will be available in the Files section and a Label titled "Invoice Attached" and or "Estimate Attached." Note: If there is no dollar amount added to the invoice or estimate, the invoice won't sync to CompanyCam. In the QuickBooks Project, there will be a copy of a CompanyCam Project link available to put in your browser, and you will have quick access to your integrated Project using that. You can highlight the copy, right-click the deep link, and select Go To, and it will take you directly to the Project. Syncing Photos From CompanyCam to QuickBooks OnlineOn the webOnce a Project is created, you can now manually sync photos from CompanyCam to QuickBooks Online. This can be done using the Action menu on the Project Screen.To sync photos, you'll want to make sure photos are uploaded to a Project created by the QuickBooks Online integration.Select the photos you want to be synced over (click on the Square Icon next to the date to select all photos for that date or the Square Icon in the upper left corner of each photo). ​Keep in mind you will need to be an Admin or Manager in CompanyCam to use this feature. Select the Action Menu and select Send Photos to QuickBooks Online. A pop-up will appear, allowing you to select whether you want the photos to sync to the Customer Record, Estimate, or Invoice. Photos synced over will be available in the Attachment section under Customer Details in the Customer Record and at the bottom of the Invoice or Estimate in the Attachment Section. Note: If the sync fails, you'll receive a notification letting you know that the sync did not go through.On the appNote: Your device will need to have CompanyCam App Version: 9.3.8 in order to sync photos to QuickBooks Online.Once a Project is created, you can now manually sync photos from CompanyCam to QuickBooks Online. This can be done using the Action menu on the Project Screen.To sync photos you'll want to make sure photos are uploaded to a project created by the QuickBooks Online integration.Select the photos you want to sync over.Tap on the Three Dots in the bottom right corner of the screen.Select Send Photos to QuickBooks Online.Photos synced over will be available in the Attachment section under Customer Details in the Customer Record and at the bottom of the Invoice or Estimate in the Attachment Section. Note: If the sync fails, you'll receive a notification letting you know that the sync did not go through.How to sync Payments to QuickBooks OnlinePlease Note: You must enable Payment syncing in the QBO integration settings and have an active Stripe account AND Payments enabled. Request a Payment as normal and complete the workflow. ​​ Once your customer has completed their Payment and the status has changed to "Paid," the Payment will sync back to QuickBooks Online automatically under the transactions of the linked Customer. ​​The transaction number will be the Stripe transaction for reference and will be viewable from the transactions tab! FAQsCan I request Signatures on the Estimates and Invoices added to my CompanyCam Project from the QuickBooks Online integration? Yes. When the integration syncs a File to your Project in CompanyCam, you can utilize the Signatures Feature to collect a signature. Please keep in mind that the Signatures Feature is available on select plans in CompanyCam. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + REIS | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + REIS | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + REISIntegrate CompanyCam + REIS to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and REIS in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is REIS? A great deal of effort was given to creating a customizable tool that works the way you want, quickly. REIS was developed by Restoration professionals who wanted to help solve the problem of 1-2 hours of office time after a hard day's work just to create a professional-looking estimate or invoice.​Restoration Estimating & Invoicing Solutions (REIS) provides a comprehensive solution that helps restoration companies easily generate estimates and submit invoices. Pricing is provided by Craftsman Data and is among the best in the industry. However, users can create their own price lists if they choose. The goal was to create a system that is easy to use and has complete flexibility to allow the user to customize to their liking. REIS specializes in improving the speed and accuracy of estimating for your business operations with these features:Call Report FormsScope FormsEstimates and InvoicesEstimate/Invoice SummariesContract FormsDry LogsMoisture MapsPsychometric ReportEquipment Inventory and TrackingForm BuilderRemote SignaturesReview RequestsCustomization of formsSetupLog in to your company's main CompanyCam account.​Click on the Team Tab located in the left-hand navigation menu.​ Click on the + Invite Users located at the top right corner. ​ Fill in all Invite New User information.Click on the Review Invite Button, located at the bottom. Once each User invited receives their invitation email, they will create a login and password. Note: Each User in CompanyCam will have their own login email and password. This can be used on the CompanyCam Website and/or Mobile App.Link CompanyCam to each REIS user accountEach User will log in to their REIS account.Click on the Settings Tab, located in the top dashboard. Click on the Integrations Tab, located in the left dashboard.​ Click CompanyCam Authorization. Fill in your individual CompanyCam email address and password.​ Select the Remember Me Box. (This will make it easier, so you do not have to log in each time you go into CompanyCam through the REIS program)Click on the blue Sign In Button.Once you do this, you will see CompanyCam Linked No, change to Linked. How it Works Note: You must have Owner/Insured Name and Project Location Address filled in.Create a new Project in REIS, or open a previously created project.Click on the CompanyCam Logo, located in the right dashboard. ​a. On a desktop or laptop, a new tab will appear opening the CompanyCam website.​b. On a tablet, a new tab will appear opening the CompanyCam website. It will also give you the option to open the CompanyCam APP if it has been uploaded to your tablet. Note: When integrating with CompanyCam, Users must allow pop-up Windows for REIS and CompanyCam in their Internet browsers. If it is the first time clicking on the CompanyCam Logo inside a Project, it will create a new Project in CompanyCam automatically.The CompanyCam Project that you are working on will be pulled up automatically in the new tab.Add photos to a Project anytime through REIS by clicking on the CompanyCam Logo while inside any Project, or add photos to a Project anytime through the CompanyCam APP on your tablet or smartphone.When inside any REIS project, and you send an email, there will be an option to attach the link to CompanyCam photos & videos for that project.Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected].​​Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your Integration up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + LeapIntegrate CompanyCam and Contractor+Integrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

Email [email protected] with the subject line: “{your company name} | CompanyCam Integration” and paste your Token in the body.​ How it worksThe Rilla and CompanyCam integration syncs all the photos your sales reps take in the field directly into your virtual ride-along, allowing you to not only hear but also see what your rep is seeing in order to coach them to the fullest. This integration weaves together the audio from Rilla with the visuals from CompanyCam to make monitoring, giving feedback on, and improving field sales team's close rates easier. title: Integrate CompanyCam + Roof.Link | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Roof.Link | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Roof.LinkIntegrate CompanyCam + Roof.Link to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Roof.Link in order to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two initially, you need to have the Admin or Manager role in CompanyCam. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Once integrated, every Roof.Link user on the account will be able to connect the integration individually to get access. Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Roof.Link?Roof.Link is a software for Roofing Companies built by roofers. It was created to be one system capable of managing every aspect of large roofing companies. Create Estimates, Orders, and Electronic Contracts. Manage your business by using the interactive map tools and dashboards within the platform.​Roof.Link allows you to have everything in one place so your employees can focus on roofing rather than organizing. It will help you grow your company with powerful reporting and automation features.​SetupOnce the Roof.Link Admin initially sets up the integration. All Users will be able to individually link their Roof.Link account to CompanyCam Login to Roof.Link.​ Go to your Profile in the top right corner and select Profile & Settings. In your Personal/ Company settings, find the tab Integrate CompanyCam on the left-hand side and select it. Select Click to connect CompanyCam. Select Authorize. Sign in to CompanyCam if prompted, and you are now connected! You can verify it's connected with the green checkmark next to the CompanyCam integration tab. To make any changes to how you want your workflow to function with this integration, you can go to the CompanyCam Settings Tab and update how you want it to work. ​Roof.Link to Company Cam ManualYou will have a manual Checklist item in your Prospect or Job Checklist that will require you to press "Sync Now" to push the Project from Roof.Link to Company Cam. Projects will never push from Company Cam to Roof.Link. Roof.Link to Company Cam AutomaticThis option will automatically push all Roof.Link leads to Company Cam as soon as you create the lead in Roof.Link. Projects will never push from Company Cam to Roof.Link. Company Cam to Roof.Link Creating a Project in Company Cam will push the lead to Roof.Link. Creating a lead in Roof.Link WILL NOT push a Project to Company Cam. Both Directions Manual Pushing a lead from Roof.Link to Company Cam will require manually clicking "Sync Now" on your Roof.Link job checklist. However, creating a Project in Company Cam will automatically push to Roof.Link as a new lead. Both Directions Automatic This is the default option. Creating a new lead in Roof.Link will create the Project in Company Cam automatically, and creating a Project in Company Cam will push to Roof.Link as a new lead. Both directions automatically. How it worksCreating or editing a Project or Lead in CompanyCam or Roof.Link will push the updates to the other system. Creating, editing, or deleting photos in Company Cam will create, edit, or delete the same photos in Roof Link. Making comments on the Company Cam Project will create Job Notes on the Roof Link Prospect. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out using the Contact Support option under the User Menu at the top right of the Roof.Link website. You can also reach out using the Live Chat option at the bottom right of the screen. Want to make workflows even easier? SetupWe've included Setup instructions on the links below. Please reach out if you have questions or issues.Lightning Setup VideoClassic (or Improveit360 without Lightning) Setup VideoPackage Install LinkAdding a second (or more) CompanyCam ConnectionCommon Errors and FAQsCommon ErrorsI've been an existing user of the package, and my visualforce page or link now returns an error. This is likely due to a Hyperforce update from Salesforce and affects the backend for urls. Please try updating any formula references to your visualforce pages to the below example format, and reach out to us at Support if that doesn't resolve your issue.The new format should be like "INSERT_YOUR_DOMAIN_HERE.vf.force.com/apex/Companycam_Project_[object]?id="I received an installation error related to 'Post Install Script'.The Post-Install script in the package is attempting to send an email to our Support Team notifying them to add your org to our Allowlist. You need to enable email sends from your org (and sandbox if applicable) to complete this step.I received an installation error related to the 'Orders object'.You need to enable the Orders object in your Salesforce org and create a Record Type as well. The Setup Video above goes through the specific details if you need them.FAQsCan this be installed in a sandbox? Yes! You just need to append the package details [/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg0000000S9L] to your sandbox domain. As an example [NOT an active package location]: https://devorg_example.lightning.force.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg0000000S9L​Can this be installed in Custom Objects or FSL? Per the videos above, this integration can be installed in any objects required. The Org setup requirements will change depending on whether you use Lightning vs Classic pages. Can multiple CompanyCam accounts connect to a single Salesforce org? Yes, this is supported in Lightning using custom metadata records and some automation in your org to set the value within the record. Please see the short video above for details. Why do I receive an error when trying to authenticate? If the code is related to a bad 'Redirect URI', then our team has not completed the Allowlist update on our end yet. We already received an email notice to get it added, but feel free to chat with us quickly as well! Are Pages, Checklists, Reports, etc., going to be added to the visualization component? We've got several things on our roadmap, but not all of them are available in API connections yet. We'll be updating here and via communications when things change. Need Support?​If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your CRM up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupIntegrate CompanyCam + improveit 360Integrate CompanyCam + ProcoreIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlowDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

content: Integrate CompanyCam + SalesPro | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + SalesProIntegrate CompanyCam + SalesPro to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! What is SalesPro? SalesPro lets you do all your contracts and bids digitally. That means you can say goodbye to the printer and fax machine when you're estimating, financing, and invoicing. And now that we can integrate with them! You can add CompanyCam photos to proposals, contracts, and customer communication to make sure clients understand the scope of work before you start, as it's being done, and long after it's complete.SalesPro is a sales solution for contractors in many fields. Contractors use SalesPro to help homeowners make decisions. SalesPro is transforming the home improvement industry as the first-ever end-to-end contractor sales software application, digitizing every stage of the in-home sales process, including estimating, financing, contracting, and real-time communication. Created by seasoned home improvement professionals, the SalesPro application helps businesses eliminate errors and increase efficiency by simplifying and automating the day-to-day sales process. SetupPlease refer to these setup instructions.​How it worksThis integration allows you to insert CompanyCam photos for an Estimate, Proposal, or Contract in SalesPro.Select Add New +. Choose CompanyCam. Search for the Project with the photos you need. Select the Photos you want to bring into SalesPro and choose Done. You can select edited photos that have already been marked up. You can edit photos in SalesPro as you build your document if there is something you'd like to highlight. Photos will be imported into that SalesPro document and will be viewable as an entire page. title: Using the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan Integration | Help Center
content: Using the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan Integration | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationLearn how the CompanyCam and ServiceTitan integration works, including how Projects are created, how photos sync, and how archiving works. Updated over a week agoTable of contents Before getting started, make sure you've already connected your CompanyCam and ServiceTitan accounts. Once your CompanyCam and ServiceTitan accounts are connected, the integration handles the heavy lifting. Projects are created automatically from ServiceTitan jobs, photos, and files sync back in real time, and completed jobs can archive themselves. Your office and field stay on the same page without anyone having to chase documentation. On the webHow Projects are createdWhen a qualifying ServiceTitan job hits the trigger you've configured (Dispatched or Scheduled), CompanyCam automatically creates a matching Project. It can take 2 to 5 minutes to appear in your Project feed. Each generated Project includes:The ServiceTitan job numberA Project label showing the job type (if applicable)Any users assigned to the appointment in ServiceTitan, as long as their email addresses match between the 2 systemsIf your team has Project Groups turned on in your integration settings, each new Project also lands in a Project Group that matches its ServiceTitan location, so jobs stay sorted by location. See Connecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitan to turn this on.Important: Users assigned to the ServiceTitan appointment will overwrite any users already assigned to the matching Project in CompanyCam. Any users previously assigned in CompanyCam will be removed. Access the Project from ServiceTitanOnce the Project is created, a link to it appears in the ServiceTitan job summary. Anyone on your team can click it to jump directly to the CompanyCam Project, on both the web and mobile app. Add photos, videos, and filesUpload content to the CompanyCam Project as you normally would. Photos, videos, and files sync back to the ServiceTitan job automatically and appear under Job History.Note: The file size limit for content syncing to ServiceTitan is 30 MB. Files larger than 30 MB won't sync. Archive Projects when jobs are completeIf you've enabled auto-archive in your integration settings, CompanyCam will automatically archive a Project when the linked job is marked Complete, On Hold, or Canceled in ServiceTitan. No manual steps needed. If you don't have auto-archive turned on, you can still manually archive Projects in CompanyCam at any time. See Archiving and Restoring Projects for details.FAQsHow long does it take for a Project to appear after a job is dispatched?Projects typically appear in CompanyCam within 2 to 5 minutes of the trigger in ServiceTitan.What information syncs from ServiceTitan to CompanyCam?When a qualifying job triggers the integration, CompanyCam creates a Project with the job number, job-type label, and assigned users. After that, photos, videos, and files from the Project sync back to the ServiceTitan job under Job History.What happens if a user's email doesn't match between systems?Only users whose email addresses match in both CompanyCam and ServiceTitan will be assigned to the Project automatically. If someone isn't being assigned, check that their email is the same in both systems.Can I sync jobs that were already in progress when I connected the integration?Yes. Email [email protected], and we can run a manual sync to pull in any jobs that were already active at the time of setup.What file types sync from CompanyCam to ServiceTitan?Photos, videos, and files all sync to the ServiceTitan job under Job History, as long as each file is under 30 MB.💡 Need more help?Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetConnecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitanUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Connecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitan | Help Center
content: Connecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitan | Help CenterSkip to main contentConnecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitanLearn how to connect your CompanyCam and ServiceTitan accounts so jobs and Projects sync automatically. Updated this weekTable of contents ServiceTitan is a cloud-based software platform built to power trades businesses, giving contractors the tools to run their back office and deliver a better customer experience. Connecting it to CompanyCam means Projects are created automatically from ServiceTitan jobs, so your crew can document work without any extra steps. Once you're connected, take a look at how the integration works day-to-day, so your team knows what to expect. On the webConnect your accountsNote:ServiceTitan is only available to connect on the web.ServiceTitan is available on select plans.You must have Admin or Manager permissions in CompanyCam and the appropriate permissions in ServiceTitan to set up this integration.Log in to CompanyCam > click Integrations in the left-hand navigation under Resources.Select the ServiceTitan card.Click Connect in the top-right corner. Paste your Tenant ID from ServiceTitan into the Tenant ID field in CompanyCam. To find your Tenant ID in ServiceTitan: go to Settings > Integrations > API Application Access. Your Tenant ID appears in the top-right corner of that page. You can also navigate directly to the API Application Access page in ServiceTitan. Copy the Tenant ID and paste it into CompanyCam, then click Save. You'll see a confirmation message that your request is being processed. This typically takes up to 1 business day. Once the confirmation screen updates, return to ServiceTitan > Settings > Integrations > API Application Access and click Edit on the CompanyCam integration card.Copy your Client ID and Client Secret from that screen.Paste both into the corresponding fields in CompanyCam and click Save.Once saved, you'll see a Disconnect button in the top-right corner, which means the integration is active. Configure your settingsAfter connecting, you can choose how the integration behaves for your team. These settings are available on the ServiceTitan integration page in CompanyCam. Project creation trigger: Choose when a CompanyCam Project is created: when a job is Dispatched or when it's Scheduled in ServiceTitan. Pick whichever matches how your team works.Important: If you switch to the Scheduled trigger after already using the integration, jobs already in a scheduled state won't automatically get Projects created for them. Email [email protected], and we can run a manual sync to capture those jobs.Business Unit and Job Type filters: Filter which ServiceTitan jobs trigger a Project in CompanyCam. You can filter by Business Unit, Job Type, or both. This is useful if only certain parts of your business need to sync to CompanyCam.Important: If the Business Units dropdown shows "Missing scope," you'll need to update the CompanyCam integration to the latest version in ServiceTitan's API Application Access settings. Once updated, your business units will populate within 10 minutes or after the next ServiceTitan job is created.Auto-archive on job completion: Check this box if you want CompanyCam to automatically archive a Project when its linked ServiceTitan job is marked complete. This keeps your Project feed clean without any manual cleanup. Organize Projects into Project Groups: Check this box to automatically sort your synced Projects. When it's on, we create a Project Group for each ServiceTitan location and sync that location's Projects into the matching group, so jobs stay organized by location. Note: This setting is available on select plans only. Reconnect the integrationIf you ever need to reconnect, click Disconnect, then paste your Tenant ID and click Save. Then re-enter your Client ID and Client Secret to complete the reconnection. To learn how Projects are created and how photos sync once you're set up, see Using the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan Integration. FAQsWho can set up the ServiceTitan integration?You need Admin or Manager permissions in CompanyCam and the appropriate access in ServiceTitan. If you don't have that, ask your account Admin to connect it.Does this integration work on mobile?The integration can only be connected on the web. Once connected, the sync works regardless of where photos and files are uploaded from.Can I filter which ServiceTitan jobs create Projects in CompanyCam?You can filter by Business Unit, Job Type, or both. Set this up in the integration settings after connecting.What if my Business Units aren't showing up?If the Business Units dropdown shows "Missing scope," update to the latest version of the CompanyCam integration in ServiceTitan's API Application Access settings. Your business units will populate within 10 minutes or after the next job is created in ServiceTitan. 💡 Need more help?Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProConnecting CompanyCam to JobberUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + Siro.ai | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Siro.ai | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Siro.aiFace-to-face. It's your business. Siro is the recording app for in-person sales and service April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You must have an active account with CompanyCam and Siro.ai to integrate.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Siro.ai Siro is an application on mobile devices that records, transcribes, and analyzes all in-person conversations. Siro’s mission is to make careers in field sales the most accessible path to financial freedom. Siro's AI is trained on millions of field sales conversations (and growing daily) to show leadership what's working in the field and help identify areas of improvement. Setup How it worksSiro’s integration with CompanyCam simplifies the job for owners and managers by providing a clear, reliable record of each job. With onsite photos automatically synced into Siro’s system, both leaders and field personnel gain a visual and audio record of every interaction, helping reps and technicians improve. Simply hit record in Siro, and watch photos seamlessly attach to each conversation. What can you expect?Ensure you have end-to-end visibility with not only photos taken at the job, but also where reps could have upsold the customer.Protect yourself from liability by recording every field interaction and capturing photo evidence.Unify your tech stack by being able to easily jump between your two sources of truth – Siro & CompanyCam.FAQsHow do I set up this integration? To set up this integration and provision your account, you'll have your Siro Admin log in to CompanyCam and open the Integrations Tab within the left-hand navigation menu. Next, find and open the Siro.ai card and click on the website jumplink to open Siro.ai and connect the integration. Is the Siro integration legal? Yes, 11 states require consent to record. Thousands of techs seamlessly use Siro in these states simply by asking for permission to record. How does Siro's AI work? Siro’s AI is trained on millions of face-to-face sales conversations, allowing Siro to pinpoint key moments within conversations and to suggest improvements. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected]. Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + Smartsheet | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Smartsheet | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetIntegrate CompanyCam + Smartsheet to sync job site photos and files directly with your projects. Gain real-time visibility, reduce admin work, and keep your team on the same page effortlessly! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startYou must have an active account with both CompanyCam and Smartsheet in order to integrate the two services.You must have Admin or Manager privileges in both CompanyCam and Smartsheet to set up the integration. If you don’t have that role, ask an Admin on your company’s account for permission; here’s what they’ll need.Integrations are not available on Basic plans. Upgrade your plan to take advantage of this feature. What is Smartsheet?Smartsheet is the modern work management platform that connects the people, technologies, and goals of the organization by empowering teams to plan, manage, scale, and report on work with greater speed and consistency. SetupPlease NoteBefore you get to the mapping step, your Smartsheet needs to have the right rows in place. Row names do not need to match exactly since you will map them to the correct CompanyCam fields during setup, but the row types do matter. If your sheet is missing the right structure, the mapping dropdowns will have nothing to pull from. At a minimum, your sheet should include the following rows:Project Name — Text row used to populate the CompanyCam project nameAddress — Text row for the project addressStatus — Dropdown row used to set and trigger project status changes (e.g. In Progress, Complete)Crew — Contact row to assign team members. For the user assignment to sync correctly, the crew member must be an active user in both platforms and have the same email address in each.Create in CompanyCam — Dropdown row with a "Create" option, used as the trigger to push a new project into CompanyCamCompanyCam Project Link — Text or URL row where the auto-generated project link will appear once the project is createdLog in to CompanyCam and, in the left-hand navigation menu under Resources, select Integrations. Look for the Smartsheet card, select it, and then click Connect. A pop-up will appear where you can choose whether to check the box to sync photos and files to Smartsheet. Note: If this box is not checked, no photos or files will be synced. Once you’ve made your selection, click Save to complete the setup. You will be taken to the Smartsheet website, where you can either create a new Smartsheet account or sign in to an existing one. Click on the option that applies to you. Click Allow. You will then be taken back to CompanyCam. After granting access, you’ll return to the setup screen in CompanyCam. You can now begin linking your Smartsheet sheets. Click Link Sheet to get started. This will take you to Step 1 of the Smartsheet Data Mapping process, where you’ll start by typing and selecting the Smartsheet sheet you want to connect. Step 2 will then appear, allowing you to begin the mapping and event trigger process. Keep in mind that the event trigger is what pushes the integration to create a new Project in CompanyCam from your board columns. Once you’ve chosen all required options, click Create. Your integration is now connected! To link additional sheets, click Link Sheet and repeat steps 7 to 10. How it worksThis integration allows you to choose which Smartsheet sheets sync with CompanyCam! During setup, start typing the name of a sheet and a list of matching sheets will appear for you to select from. Customizable field mapping allows you to choose which Smartsheet columns populate CompanyCam Project fields (e.g., Project name, address, status). Flexible triggers allow you to decide what events in Smartsheet create new CompanyCam Projects, such as a status change to "In Progress," a new item being added, or a specific column update. You can also set archiving triggers, so when a project is marked "Complete" in Smartsheet, the corresponding CompanyCam Project is automatically archived. Then, once you capture content in CompanyCam, you can choose to sync CompanyCam photos and files back to the corresponding Smartsheet row as attachments. A deep Project link can be added to the Smartsheet row for one-click access to the full CompanyCam Project, and visual updates appear in near real-time, keeping your Smartsheet rows up to date with the latest job site documentation! Log in to your Smartsheet account and open the workspace where your linked Sheet lives. Select the Sheet you linked in this integration. To create a new project, start by adding the Name, Address, Crew, and Status to the designated columns in your Sheet. Once the required information has been entered, navigate to the Create in CompanyCam column and select Create from the dropdown to trigger a new Project to be created in CompanyCam. Please note: The trigger must be set in order for a Project to be created in CompanyCam. The new CompanyCam Project will be created with the Project Label Smartsheet and reflect the Status you've set (e.g., Smartsheet - In Progress, or any other Status you've configured). Please note that it can take up to 5 minutes for the Project to appear in CompanyCam. Once the Project has been created, a Project Link will automatically appear in the CompanyCam Project Link column of your Sheet, giving you one-click access to the Project in CompanyCam. The Crew member(s) selected in your Sheet will automatically be assigned to the Project in CompanyCam under the Project Users section, as long as they are active users in both platforms and share the same email address. Any Photos and Files uploaded to the CompanyCam Project will automatically sync back to the corresponding row in Smartsheet and appear under the Attachments section of that row. Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + SumoQuote | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + SumoQuote | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + SumoQuoteIntegrate CompanyCam + SumoQuote to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.This integration requires you to have both a CompanyCam and a SumoQuote account.Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission. Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is SumoQuote? SumoQuote is a powerful proposal integration that allows users to create custom proposal templates and quick proposals on the fly. By connecting your CompanyCam and SumoQuote accounts, you can easily add any photo from CompanyCam to your SumoQuote reports. Simply link to your SumoQuote estimates in the correct CompanyCam Project, and now you have access to add whatever photos from the project to your report. Setup Go to your Projects page in SumoQuote. Click on Configuration and then Integrations. Select the CompanyCam Card in the Integrations options. Authorize the connection between CompanyCam and SumoQuote. That's it! You are now connected. You can manage your connection from the Connected Apps section. How it works​Once you have integrated CompanyCam and SumoQuote, you can use your CompanyCam photos for your SumoQuote Reports. Here's how to link your SumoQuote estimates to the proper CompanyCam project:To link a new SumoQuote Report to a CompanyCam Project, click the green Create New Project Button. Next, select the Link to a CompanyCam Project option from the menu. You can also link to a CompanyCam Project from an existing SumoQuote Report. You'll need to click the CompanyCam Icon and it should pull up Projects with the matching address. You can also search for Projects. You can now use your CompanyCam photos in your SumoQuote Reports. A Project will need to be linked to the SumoQuote entry. Once a Project is linked, you'll see the option to add photos from CompanyCam. If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to SumoQuote support.​​Want to make workflows even easier? title: Integrate CompanyCam + Teamup | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Teamup | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupIntegrate CompanyCam + Teamup to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.To integrate, you must also be on the Business Plan in Teamup. Permission Roles:To start integrating the two, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Teamup? Teamup simplifies how groups share plans, schedule events, and communicate statuses. You can also color-code calendars for teams, projects, and bookable resources. Create a Teamup event, and that event's information will be available in the CompanyCam mobile app to create a Project. Add photos, and they will be synced to the original even in Teamup. Please NoteCompanyCam and Teamup will sync every 15 minutes.Setup Go to your Teamup Calendar.Select the blue Menu Icon at the top right, then Settings. Select Sharing. Create a new link by clicking Create Link.​ Name it whatever you'd like - The CompanyCam Integration will work if you're not able to think of something.Give CompanyCam permission to make changes - Set it to Modify under basic permissions. Click Save.Copy the ID after https://teamup.com/. That is your TeamUp Calendar Link. Don't hit the copy icon, this will copy the entire URL You just want the ending of the link. Head over and sign in to CompanyCam.Select Integrations from within the left-hand menu. Find the TeamUp Integration and select it. Click Connect. Paste the ID into the Calendar field.Click Save. How it worksCreate events as usual in your Teamup Calendar. When CompanyCam Users are in the field, they'll open their mobile app and tap on the blue Camera Icon on their home screen.Next, they will tap the Teamup Tab. This will display all Teamup events for the current day.Tap on the correct Teamup Event, and a CompanyCam Project will be created automatically.Take photos in this integrated Project. Your photos will sync back to the connected event in Teamup.If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! Want to make workflows even easier? Now that your CRM is up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbus + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam + busybusyIntegrate CompanyCam + ProcoreIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

content: Integrate CompanyCam + Workiz | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + WorkizIntegrate CompanyCam + Workiz to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! What is Workiz? Workiz lets you customize documents, track calls, turn voicemails into jobs in one click, and even understand which ad sources are profitable and which just aren't working out. How it works When you create a Job in Workiz, the integration will create a Project in CompanyCam. CompanyCam will pull the address and the Workiz Job ID to create the Project in CompanyCam. The photos you take in CompanyCam will then sync back to your Workiz Job under the Attachments Section. title: Integrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration Software | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration Software | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration SoftwareIntegrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration Software to manage your job site documentation easier than ever before! If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is Xcelerate? Xcelerate Restoration Software is the ultimate job management platform that drives growth and success for restoration contractors. Xcelerate is dedicated to helping contractors achieve their goals through their comprehensive software and personalized support. title: Integrate CompanyCam + Zapier | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + Zapier | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + ZapierUse Zapier to connect CompanyCam to thousands of other tools! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You need to have an active account with CompanyCam and Zapier to integrate the two services.Permission Roles:To integrate, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser.What is Zapier?Zapier is an automation platform that connects CompanyCam to over 4,000 other Apps. There's a good chance that Zapier already connects software that's part of your existing business workflows. SetupZapier's workflow is quick and intuitive. Use data from a trigger to accomplish automated tasks in your workflow to save time and make fewer mistakes in data entry. Once you've connected your CompanyCam account for a Trigger or an Action, you will be able to use that account for more Zaps without signing in to CompanyCam. In Zapier a workflow automation is called a "Zap". Zaps are built with one Trigger which starts the automation and then you add one or multiple Actions as tasks to be accomplished. Learn more about CompanyCam integrations powered by Zapier. How it worksCreating a Zap with a CompanyCam TriggerGet started at Zapier.com by logging in or creating a new account.Once in your Zapier Home select the + Create button and select Zaps. Name your Zap for easy management.Search for CompanyCam and select CompanyCam.​ Select your Trigger event from the list (Triggers occur when Zapier receives data from CompanyCam) and click Continue. Click Sign in to CompanyCam. You'll be prompted to sign in to CompanyCam and Authorize Zapier to have access to your CompanyCam account. If you are signed in to your CompanyCam account, you'll automatically be signed in to connect your account to Zapier.If you have already connected your CompanyCam account in a different Zap, then you'll simply need to select the account that you'd like to use for this Zap.​Click the Test Trigger Button and then Continue. If the test is successful, you will see data from a recent trigger event. This will inform you what data you have to work with in the following Action Steps. Zapier will then prompt you to Choose an Action App.There are thousands of choices. Start typing in the search field to choose an app. Examples: Pipedrive, Hubspot, Zoho, Monday.com Google Sheets, and Google Drive. Use the search function in the Action step to search for Apps that you want to automate. Depending on the Action that you've selected, the steps that follow will depend on how that app is connected with Zapier. Click on the data fields and then choose the CompanyCam Trigger and any data that you use in that Action step. You'll see an example of how this works in the example below.Creating a Zap with a CompanyCam ActionSelect and set up the Trigger.Select the Action Tab. Search for CompanyCam. ​ Choose the action event, then Continue.​ Click Sign in to CompanyCam. You'll be prompted to sign in to CompanyCam and Authorize Zapier to have access to your CompanyCam account. If you are signed in to your CompanyCam account, you'll automatically be signed in to connect your account to Zapier.If you have already connected your CompanyCam account in a different Zap, then you'll simply need to select the account that you'd like to use for this Zap. Then set up the Action by filling in the fields with data from the Trigger.​ When you've selected or entered in Data for what you'd like to pass along, choose Continue.Then select the Test step Button if you are satisfied with what the output looks like. If the test is successful, select Publish. Once the Zap is turned on, test to see if adding data from the trigger adds new data to CompanyCam as expected.That's it! Feel free to create more zaps for workflows you would like to automate. ​Need Support?If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to [email protected] or reach out to our live chat! You can also contact Zapier support for any other workflow or setup questions as you build your Zap. Want to make workflows even easier? Now that you've got your Zaps up and running, check out other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineIntegrate CompanyCam + NiceJobIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Retrieve an API Key in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Retrieve an API Key in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Retrieve an API Key in CompanyCamFind your CompanyCam API key and use it to securely connect third-party tools or custom integrations. May 6, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:To retrieve an API key, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Retrieving an API key can only be done in the web app.On the webLog in to CompanyCam on a web browser.Click on your User Icon and choose Access Tokens. Click New Token. From there, select the integration you're looking to connect from the drop-down menu and click Create Token. If your supported integration is not listed, select N/A. Note: Do not select “This token will ONLY be used to read data from CompanyCam.” Choosing this will make the token read-only and prevent the integration from syncing information properly. Copy the Access Token you just created.That’s it! Your API key is ready to use. Paste the copied Access Token into your integration to complete the setup.Building your own integration? Check out our API and Custom Integrations guide to see what’s possible. Or check out our other integration opportunities!Related ArticlesAPI and Custom IntegrationsIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + SuccessWareIntegrate CompanyCam + AlbiwareIntegrate CompanyCam + ClientTetherDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Integrate CompanyCam + SRS Roof Hub | Help Center
content: Integrate CompanyCam + SRS Roof Hub | Help CenterSkip to main contentIntegrate CompanyCam + SRS Roof HubIntegrate CompanyCam + SRS Roof Hub to bring job site photos and project context directly into Roof Hub. Empower your team to work faster, create more accurate estimates, and stay aligned—all without switching between tools! April 22, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.You will need to have an active account with both CompanyCam and SRS Roof Hub to connect the integration.Permission Roles:To integrate, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Integrations cannot be connected in the mobile app. You must set them up using a web browser. What is SRS Roof Hub? SRS Distribution is a leading distributor of roofing and building materials, dedicated to serving professional contractors across the United States. With a strong network of independent brands and over 440 locations nationwide, they provide unparalleled access to high-quality products, expert support, and innovative solutions. Set upPlease note:The integration is one-way: CompanyCam → Roof HubThere is no automatic syncing or storage of photos in Roof HubPhotos remain in CompanyCam unless they are explicitly imported into Estimator Log in to SRS Roof Hub.Navigate to the Integrations page on Roof Hub. Locate the CompanyCam card, and click connect. This will then redirect you to the CompanyCam-specific integration page to authenticate your account. How it worksThis integration allows you to connect your CompanyCam account to Roof Hub and access your projects and photos directly within your Roof Hub workflows—without duplicating data. Once connected, Roof Hub pulls in your CompanyCam projects, which can be viewed through the CompanyCam integration card for easy access to job context.When you’re ready to create a proposal, you can use the photo picker to select and upload photos directly from CompanyCam. The integration is one-way, from CompanyCam to Roof Hub, and project data is pulled on demand. Photos remain stored in CompanyCam by default and are not automatically synced or duplicated.If you choose to import photos into Roof Hub Estimator, only the selected photos are downloaded and stored in Roof Hub to support estimating and proposal workflows. Need Support?This integration was developed and is supported by SRS Roof Hub. If you need support integrating or have any issues with the integration, reach out to SRS Roof Hub's Support Team at [email protected]. Want to make workflows even easier?
