title: Using the CompanyCam + HubSpot Integration | Help Center
content: Using the CompanyCam + HubSpot Integration | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing the CompanyCam + HubSpot IntegrationLearn how Projects, Files, and Checklists sync between CompanyCam and HubSpot, and how to set up your HubSpot forms. Updated this weekTable of contentsOnce CompanyCam and HubSpot are connected, new Deals and Contacts create Projects, and your Files sync back automatically. This article covers how the sync works, how to use Deals and Contacts, how to send a Checklist, and how to set up your HubSpot forms. New here? Start with Connecting CompanyCam to HubSpot.This feature is only available on the web app. How the integration worksAny new HubSpot Deal or Contact that matches your trigger settings creates a matching Project in CompanyCam. The two stay linked from there. A Universal Link to the Project shows up in the Notes section of the Deal or Contact, so anyone can jump to the Project from either side. Any Files you upload to the Project sync to the Attachments section of the linked Deal or Contact, keeping the documentation in one place. Note: Only Files uploaded to the Project sync to HubSpot. Photos and Checklists don't sync directly. To send a Checklist, see Exporting a Checklist to HubSpot below.Using DealsIn HubSpot, select the Pipeline, click Add deals, then click Create New.Fill out these fields before you click Create:Deal Name so it's easy to spot in CompanyCam.Pipeline matching the one you chose during setup.Deal Stage matching your trigger stage, if you set one.Associate Deal with a Contact that has a saved address. CompanyCam pulls the property address from that Contact. Without it, the Project is created with no address. Open CompanyCam and find the new Project under your recent Projects. Capture Photos and upload Files as usual.Back in HubSpot, open the Deal. The Universal Link in the Notes section takes you straight to the Project on web or mobile. Files you upload to the Project sync to the Attachments section automatically. Using ContactsIn HubSpot, go to Contacts, click Add Contacts, then click Create New.Fill out these fields before you click Create:First and Last Name so the Contact is easy to spot in CompanyCam.Email, required by HubSpot to create the Contact.Address so CompanyCam can populate the Project's property address.Lifecycle Stage matching your trigger stage, if you set one. Open CompanyCam and find the new Project under your recent Projects. Capture Photos and upload Files as usual. The contact info syncs into the Contact Info section of the Project.Back in HubSpot, open the Contact. The Universal Link in the Notes section takes you straight to the Project on web or mobile. Files you upload to the Project sync to the Attachments section automatically.Exporting a Checklist to HubSpotChecklists don't sync automatically, but you can send one over as a PDF.Open a Checklist in your Project, click the three dots, and select Export to PDF.Toggle on Add to Saved Files, then click Export.The PDF saves to CompanyCam and syncs to the Attachments section of the linked Deal or Contact.Adding the Associate Deal With section to the Create Deal formNote: These steps are performed in HubSpot's web settings and require Admin access in HubSpot.If the Associate Deal with section isn't there when you create a Deal, add it.In HubSpot, click the Settings icon.Under Data Management, click Objects.In the Select an Object dropdown, choose Deals.On the Setup tab, click Customize the 'Create Deal' form. Click Add Associations. Select both Contacts and Companies.Click Save and Close.Adding address fields to the Create Contact formNote: These steps are performed in HubSpot's settings and require Admin access in HubSpot.If the address fields aren't there when you create a Contact, add them.In HubSpot, click the Settings icon.Under Data Management, click Objects.In the Select an Object dropdown, choose Contacts.On the Setup tab, click Customize the 'Create Contact' form.Click Add Properties. Select Contact Information.Select these address fields: City, Postal Code, State/Region, and Street Address.Click Save and Close.FAQ'sWhy was my Project created without an address?A Project is created without an address when the Deal has no associated Contact with a saved address, or when the address fields are missing from your HubSpot form. Add the fields with the steps above.Why is the Associate Deal With section missing?The Associate Deal with section can be added to your Create Deal form in HubSpot's settings. See Adding the Associate Deal With above.Why don't I see address fields on the Contact form?Address fields can be added to your Create Contact form in HubSpot's settings. See Adding address fields above.What creates a new Project in CompanyCam?A new Project is created by any HubSpot Deal or Contact that matches the trigger stage you set during setup. Leave the stage blank, and every new Deal or Contact creates one.Do Photos sync to HubSpot?Photos don't sync to HubSpot. Only Files uploaded to the Project sync, and they appear in the Attachments section of the linked Deal or Contact.Do Checklists sync to HubSpot?Checklists don't sync automatically. Export the Checklist as a PDF, and it syncs to the Attachments section. See Exporting a Checklist to HubSpot above. 💡 Need more help? Contact our Support team— we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Connecting CompanyCam to HubSpot | Help Center
content: Connecting CompanyCam to HubSpot | Help CenterSkip to main contentConnecting CompanyCam to HubSpotLearn what HubSpot is and how to connect it to CompanyCam so that new Deals and Contacts automatically create Projects. Updated this weekTable of contentsHubSpot is a CRM for managing your marketing, sales, and customer records in one place. Connect it to CompanyCam, and every new Deal or Contact can spin up a matching Project automatically, so your job-site documentation lives right alongside the customer record. This article covers connecting the two. For how the sync works once you're set up, see Using the CompanyCam + HubSpot Integration.This feature is only available on the web app. Connecting HubSpotImportant: Connect to the newest version, HubSpot V2. Search for HubSpot V2 under All Integrations > open the card > click Connect > follow the setup instructions.You'll need an active CompanyCam account and a HubSpot account to connect the two.Note: You need Admin permissions in both CompanyCam and HubSpot to set up this integration.Log in to CompanyCam > open Integrations (under Resources in the left-hand menu).Find the HubSpot card and click Connect. In the pop-up, choose what creates a new Project: Deals or Contacts. Most teams pick Deals, since they track a job through a pipeline stage. Click Save. Sign in to HubSpot, or create an account, when the HubSpot window opens.Pick the HubSpot account you want to connect, then click Choose Account.Click Connect App. HubSpot sends you back to CompanyCam.Optionally, use the dropdown to pick the exact Deal stage or Contact lifecycle stage that should create a Project. Leave it blank, and every new Deal or Contact creates one, no matter the stage.Click Save.Note: CompanyCam connects to one HubSpot account at a time. Once you're connected, head to Using the CompanyCam + HubSpot Integration to see how Projects, Files, and Checklists sync.FAQ'sCan I connect more than one HubSpot account?CompanyCam connects to one HubSpot account at a time.Can I set up the integration on mobile?Integrations can only be set up on the web app, not in the mobile app.Do I need a HubSpot account to connect with CompanyCam?A HubSpot account is required to connect. You can sign in to an existing one or create a new one during setup.💡 Need more help? Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusConnecting CompanyCam to JobberIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadConnecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitanDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using the CompanyCam + Jobber Integration | Help Center
content: Using the CompanyCam + Jobber Integration | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationLearn how photos, videos, and files sync between CompanyCam and Jobber across Requests, Quotes, Jobs, and Invoices. Updated todayTable of contentsBefore getting started, make sure you've already connected your CompanyCam and Jobber accounts. Once your accounts are connected, the integration handles the heavy lifting. Projects are created automatically from Jobber properties, and photos, videos, and files sync back as your work progresses, so your office and field stay on the same page without anyone having to chase documentation.This feature is only available on the web app. How the Integration WorksIn Jobber, a Client can have one or more Properties, which are the addresses associated with that Client. Each Property syncs as a separate CompanyCam Project. When you see "Jobber Property," "Jobber Request," "Jobber Quote," or "Jobber Job" as a Project label in CompanyCam, that label reflects the most recent active entry for that Property in Jobber. When you add a new property for a Client in Jobber, CompanyCam automatically creates a matching Project. The two stay linked from there. Photos, videos, and files you add to the CompanyCam Project sync to the Internal Notes section of the corresponding Request, Quote, or Job in Jobber. Note: Syncing can take up to 5 minutes. If the Jobber record remains a Client only and no Request, Quote, or Job has been created, photos, videos, and files added to the CompanyCam Project won't sync.Adding a Client and Starting a ProjectIn Jobber, click Create in the left-hand navigation and select Client. Enter the client's First Name, Last Name, Property Address, and any other details, then click Save Client. Saving the client triggers a new Project to be created in CompanyCam with the label "Jobber Property." Note: At this stage, photos, videos, and files added to the CompanyCam Project won't sync back to Jobber yet. You'll need to create a Request, Quote, or Job first. To create a Request, Quote, or Job, open the client's page, click the ...More Actions button in the top right corner, and select the option you want to create. Once created, the CompanyCam Project label updates to reflect the entry — "Jobber Request," "Jobber Quote," or "Jobber Job." Photos, videos, and files added to the Project will now sync to that entry in Jobber. Viewing Synced Photos in JobberIn Jobber, open the Request, Quote, or Job for the property.Navigate to the Internal Notes section.Photos, videos, and files synced from CompanyCam will appear here as attachments.Converting Between Requests, Quotes, and JobsBy default, existing photos, videos, and files don't automatically transfer when you convert between Requests, Quotes, and Jobs. To carry content over, you'll need to link the note to the relevant entries in Jobber first. Linking your note before convertingIn Jobber, open the Request, Quote, or Job and go to the Internal Notes section.Click the pencil icon on the Notes section. Click the dropdown arrow next to "Link to related."Check the boxes for Quotes, Jobs, and Invoices.Click Save. Once saved, photos, videos, and files will carry over when you convert to the next entry in your workflow. If you have Invoices checked, that content will also appear in any invoice created from a Job that includes it.Note: You only need to complete this step if you want existing content to transfer over. If you skip it, any new photos, videos, and files added after the conversion will still sync automatically.Converting a Request to a QuoteIn Jobber, open the Request.Complete the linking steps above if you want existing content to transfer.Convert the Request to a Quote. Linked photos, videos, and files will carry over to the Quote. Converting a Quote or Request to a JobIn Jobber, open the Quote or Request.Complete the linking steps above if you want existing content to transfer.Convert to a Job. Linked photos, videos, and files will carry over to the Job. Note: Any new photos, videos, and files added after the Job is created will sync automatically regardless of the linking step.When Syncing StopsSyncing behavior is tied to whether a Job is truly closed in Jobber:If you close a Job without creating an invoice, the Job is not treated as fully closed and photos, videos, and files can still sync.If you create an invoice and close the Job (in either order), syncing stops.If a closed Job is reopened, the CompanyCam Project reconnects to that Job and any new photos, videos, and files added will resume syncing.Connecting Existing CompanyCam Projects to JobberWhen you first connect the integration, CompanyCam automatically pulls in up to 1,000 of the most recent active Clients from Jobber and creates linked Projects. If you have existing CompanyCam Projects from before the integration that you want to consolidate with those newly created Projects, there is no way to manually link them. The workaround is to merge your existing Project into the Jobber-generated one. When merging, keep the Project created by the Jobber integration to maintain the sync connection. See Merge Projects for steps. Note: Updating an existing property address or entry in Jobber will not update the linked CompanyCam Project. The address in CompanyCam will remain as it was when the Project was originally created. If the Client name is updated in Jobber, that change will push to CompanyCam and the Project name will update to match.Recurring and Multiple Jobs on the Same PropertyPhotos, videos, and files always sync to the most recent active Request, Quote, or Job for that property. If you have recurring Jobs or multiple entries for the same property, content will sync to the most recent one — not previous entries. If you close the most recent Job and reopen an older Job for that same property, any new photos, videos, and files added after reopening will sync to that reopened Job. Previously synced content will not transfer over.FAQCan I sync my existing Jobber jobs into CompanyCam?Yes. When you first connect the Jobber integration, CompanyCam automatically imports up to 1,000 of the most recent active Clients from Jobber and creates linked Projects. If you connect and find that some active Jobs are missing, contact our support team, and we can push a historical sync for up to 1,000 of the most recent active Jobs. Keep in mind, this does not include Requests or Quotes. If you have existing CompanyCam Projects you want to consolidate with newly created Jobber-connected Projects, manual linking isn't possible. See Connecting Existing CompanyCam Projects to Jobber above for the merge workaround.Why isn't my Project syncing photos, videos, and files to Jobber?Adding a Client in Jobber creates a CompanyCam Project automatically, but syncing doesn't start until a Request, Quote, or Job is created for that Client while the integration is connected. If the integration was disconnected when the Request, Quote, or Job was created, photos, videos, and files won't sync. Check that the integration is still connected from the Integrations page and that there is an active Request, Quote, or Job tied to the property.Why does my Project name keep changing?When a new Request, Quote, or Job is created for a Client in Jobber, Jobber pushes the client name to CompanyCam and the Project name updates to match. This means any manual rename you make to a Jobber-linked Project in CompanyCam may revert the next time Jobber sends an update. If you want the name to stick, update the Client name directly in Jobber instead.Why do I have duplicate Projects in CompanyCam?If a Client has more than one property in Jobber, each property creates its own CompanyCam Project. This is expected and not a duplicate. If you have two Projects for the same property address, this usually happens when a property was added to Jobber more than once, or when a Project was created manually in CompanyCam in addition to one being created through the integration. To identify which Project is linked to Jobber, check for the Project label — whichever Project has a label such as "Jobber Request," "Jobber Quote," or "Jobber Job" is the one connected to the integration. A manually created Project won't have this label. If you're still unsure, contact our support team for help.Why does an Admin or Manager appear as a Contributor on Projects created by the integration?This is expected behavior. The Admin or Manager who connected the Jobber integration will automatically appear as a Contributor on all Projects created through it. No action is needed.Can I manually sync photos, videos, and files from a CompanyCam Project to Jobber?If your Project is linked to an active Jobber Request, Quote, or Job and photos, videos, or files aren't syncing as expected, contact our support team and we can push a manual sync on our end. This is not available for Projects created manually in CompanyCam.Why didn't my photos, videos, and files transfer when I converted to a Job?Photos, videos, and files don't transfer automatically during a conversion. To carry them over, you'll need to link the note to the relevant entries before converting. See Converting Between Requests, Quotes, and Jobs above for steps.Do Pages and Checklists sync to Jobber?Not automatically. To sync a Page or Checklist, export it as a PDF and save it to your Project's Files tab. It will then sync to Jobber along with your other files.Will photos, videos, and files sync to both one-time and recurring Jobs?Yes. Photos, videos, and files always sync to the most recent active Request, Quote, or Job for that property. If you close the most recent Job and reopen an older Job for that same property, any new photos, videos, and files added after reopening will sync to that Job. Previously synced content will not transfer over.Can I delete synced photos, videos, and files in Jobber?Yes. Open the Request, Quote, or Job in Jobber, navigate to the Internal Notes section, and click the photo, video, or file you want to delete. You should see a trash icon to remove it. If you don't see the trash icon, additional permissions in Jobber may be required — check with your Jobber admin to confirm your permissions. Note: Deleting content in Jobber will not remove it from CompanyCam, and deleting content in CompanyCam will not remove it from Jobber. If you want content removed from both platforms, you will need to delete it manually in each one.💡 Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + Housecall ProConnecting CompanyCam to JobberIntegrate CompanyCam + AccuLynxUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Setting Up Marketing Suite | Help Center
content: Setting Up Marketing Suite | Help CenterSkip to main contentSetting Up Marketing SuiteConnect your Google Business Profile and social accounts to get Marketing Suite ready to use. Updated over a week agoTable of contentsMarketing Suite turns your completed Projects into professional marketing across Google, social media, and your portfolio site — automatically. Setup is a one-time process that takes about 10 minutes.Before you startPlan requirements Marketing Suite is a paid add-on ($99/month) available on any CompanyCam plan. Contact our support team or your Account Manager to get started. New accounts include a 14-day free trial.Permission roles To access Marketing Suite, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform availability Marketing Suite is only available on the web app. Connecting your Google Business ProfileImportant: To get the most out of Marketing Suite, we recommend connecting your Google Business Profile. Don’t have one yet? No problem — you can skip this step for now and jump straight to connecting your social channels. You can always connect your Google Business Profile later. Need help setting one up? Check out these step-by-step instructions for creating a Google Business Profile.Log in to CompanyCam > Marketing Suite > Dashboard, then click Connect Google to Get Started. Click Sign in with Google. Check the box to allow CompanyCam to see, edit, create, and delete your Google business listings. Click Allow. Select the Google Business Profile locations you want to include. Click Select Locations. Click Continue on the confirmation screen. Click Continue again when your portfolio is confirmed as ready. Click the + next to any social media platforms you'd like to connect to, then log in to each account when prompted. When you're finished, click Continue — or select Skip to connect your accounts later. You'll land on your Marketing Dashboard. Setup is complete. Note: You can connect multiple Google Business Profile locations in step 3. You can add or remove social accounts at any time under Marketing Suite > Settings. Connected social platformsMarketing Suite supports the following platforms. You must have login credentials for each account you want to connect.Facebook (personal page or Business page)Instagram (Business or Creator accounts)X (personal or business accounts)TikTok (all accounts)LinkedInYouTubeTo disconnect a platform, go to Marketing Suite > Settings and click Disconnect next to the account you want to remove.FAQsCan I sign up for Marketing Suite on my own?You can start your free 14-day trial at any time right from your account. Click on Marketing Suite under the Marketing tab in the left toolbar > Sign Up > Start My Free 14-Day Trial. Does Marketing Suite include a free trial?Yes. New accounts start with a 14-day free trial, then $99/month. Your billing is prorated to fit your existing CompanyCam billing cycle.Can I connect more than one Google Business Profile location?Yes. You can connect multiple locations under a single $99/month subscription. Each location is managed separately.Is Marketing Suite available on mobile? Marketing Suite is only available on the web app.Can any permission role connect social accounts?Only Admin and Manager roles can connect accounts and publish Showcases. Standard and Restricted users can still request reviews from the Project page. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + MerchyntShowcasing a Project in Marketing SuiteResponding to Reviews in Marketing SuiteCreating Posts for Socials using Marketing SuiteManaging your Google Business Profile in Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Showcasing a Project in Marketing Suite | Help Center
content: Showcasing a Project in Marketing Suite | Help CenterSkip to main contentShowcasing a Project in Marketing SuiteTurn a completed Project into social posts, a Google Business update, and a portfolio entry with one publish. Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsShowcasing a Project is at the core of the Marketing Suite workflow. CompanyCam picks your best photos, writes platform-specific captions, and generates a video reel. Once you review it, you can publish to every connected channel at once!Note:To post your Showcases to social media, you must have the Marketing Suite subscription add-on.Marketing Suite is only available on the web.Showcase your workNote: Only Admins and Managers can access the Marketing Suite.Log in to CompanyCam on the web app > Marketing > Dashboard. If no Showcases have been created, the Ready to Showcase section displays up to 6 recommended Projects to promote. Hover over a Project and click Showcase This Project. To pick a Project not in the suggested list, click Showcase a Project below it. Review the AI-generated Showcase. It includes an SEO-friendly title, a Project Description, Project Type, and curated photos selected based on aesthetic scoring. There is some customizing you can do here: Edit the description by clicking into the text box. Click Add Photos to choose your own. Click X to remove a photo. Click the Cover Photo Icon to set your cover image. To add Project Type or Materials Used, click the Arrow Icon to the right of each section to display a drop-down of options. This step is optional but improves SEO. Note: You can add, edit, and delete Project Types and Materials Used. These steps are detailed in the Manage your Showcases section. Once you have added all the photos and details you want to include, click Confirm. For Facebook and Instagram, choose Reel, Image, or Carousel. Note: All connected channels are selected by default. Click the blue checkmark to the right of any channel you don't want to publish to. To add a Call to Action on your Google Business post, click the Google Business card. Edit the caption, select a Call to Action, if needed, and click Save. To customize the social channel Reels or Videos you want to publish, click on a social channel card that offers a Reel or Video, then click Edit Reel to choose a Reel Template. Select a Reel Template from the available options. Each Template card displays a design preview and the number of photos required.Click the Arrow Icon beneath Choose Background Music to select music for your Reel.When you have finalized your selections, click Apply to All Reels. To publish the Showcase right away, click Publish Now.To schedule your Showcase to publish at a later date, click the Arrow Icon next to Publish Now, then click Schedule for Later... From here, you can select the date on the calendar. To select today's date, you can click Today. To update your Showcase publish time, click the hour and minutes fields separately. Each will reveal a dropdown of available options. Select your desired time and click Save. Click Done on the confirmation screen. Note: Publishing may take up to 20 seconds to render. Now that your Showcase is ready, share it directly to your social media channels!Manage your ShowcasesLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Showcases to view your full Showcase history. Toggle to Published to see Showcases you've already shared and which channels they were sent to. To delete a published Showcase, locate the Showcase you want to remove and click the Three Dots menu beside it, select Delete Showcase.To start a new Showcase from this page, click + Showcase in the top right. Search for the Project you want and click its card to begin. Toggle to Scheduled to see the Showcases you have scheduled to publish later. Click the Three Dots Button to Reschedule, Delete Scheduled Posts, or Delete Showcase. Toggle to Drafts to see work in progress. To edit, review, and publish this draft, click the title of the Showcase. To delete the Showcase, click the Three Dots Button, and click Delete Showcase. Manage Project TypesLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Showcases, click the Three Dots Button in the top right corner, then click Manage Project Tags. From here, you can view, create, or delete Showcase Tags like Project Types and Materials that are used to describe your Showcases. Toggle to Project Types and click Create Project Type to create a new type. Toggle to Materials and click Create Material to create a new Material. From here, a new row will appear. In the row that says Material Name, start typing. When you are finished, tap Enter on your keyboard, and the new Material will save. To delete a Project Type or Material, click the Three Dots Button to the right and click Delete. FAQsHow does CompanyCam decide which photos to include?Photos are selected using aesthetic scoring models and AI photo descriptions. The top-scoring photos are included automatically. How are Ready to Showcase Projects chosen? Projects that are deemed Ready to Showcase if no new photos have been uploaded to them in the last 7 days.Can I edit the AI-generated description?You can edit the AI-generated description by clicking into the text box on the Showcase editing screen to rewrite any part of the description before publishing.Can I post to some channels but not all?To post to select social channels, deselect any channels you don't want to post to before publishing by clicking their blue checkmarks. You can change your selection every time you publish.Can I publish a Showcase again?Once you have published a Showcase, you cannot publish it again unless you delete the original Showcase. Deleting the Showcase will not remove the posts from the social channels to which they are published, but it removes the Showcase from the CompanyCam records and allows you to publish a new Showcase for this Project. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. title: Managing your Google Business Profile in Marketing Suite | Help Center
content: Managing your Google Business Profile in Marketing Suite | Help CenterSkip to main contentManaging your Google Business Profile in Marketing SuiteKeep your business info current and upload photos to your Google Business Profile without leaving CompanyCam. Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsMarketing Suite connects to your Google Business Profile (GBP) through a one-time OAuth setup. Once connected, updates you make in CompanyCam sync to your GBP automatically — no need to log into Google separately.Plan requirements Marketing Suite add-onPermission roles Admin, ManagerPlatform availability Web onlyWhat syncs from CompanyCam to GoogleWhen you update any of the following in CompanyCam, your GBP updates automatically within 1 hour:Business hoursServices offeredService locationsBusiness descriptionContact information (phone, website, email)Business categoriesClick Save Changes after making any edits.Note: Sync is one-directional. Changes made in CompanyCam push to Google, not the other way around. Managing your Google Business ProfileLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Settings.Click Edit Profile.​ ORLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Dashboard.Click Edit Profile within Google Profile Strength.​ From here, review your Profile fields. Any incomplete fields are hurting your Profile Strength score.Upload Photos to your Google Business ProfileLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Settings.Click Edit Profile.​ ORLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Dashboard.Click Edit Profile within Google Profile Strength.​ In the Google Business Profile editor, click Upload Photos in the bottom left of the window.​ From there, select a file from your computer, from a URL, or from your CompanyCam Project Library. Once your photos are selected click, Upload Photos.Note: The Other ways to boost your profile section at the top of the panel shows quick actions like adding a logo, cover photo, at-work photos, and team photos. These are shortcuts to the same upload flow. Add a nickname for your Google Business ProfileLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Settings.Click Edit Profile.​ ORLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Dashboard.Click Edit Profile within Google Profile Strength.​ Within the Google Business Profile editor, find the Nickname box under Basic Information, type in the nickname, and click Save.​ Adding Multiple Google Business ProfilesLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Settings.Click Switch and select Add Location in the dropdown.​ ORLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Dashboard.Click your company name above Marketing Dashboard and select Add Location.​ From there, log in with the Google account associated with this location. Switching between Google Business ProfilesLog in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Settings.Click Switch and select another location in the dropdown.​ OR Log in to CompanyCam > Marketing > Dashboard.Click your company name above Marketing Dashboard and select another location in the dropdown.​ FAQsHow can I create a Google Business Profile? To get started, check out Google’s step-by-step guide for creating your profile here. Does uploading photos through CompanyCam improve my Google Profile Strength score? Yes. The Google Profile Strength score in Marketing Suite factors in whether your Google Business Profile has key photos like a logo, cover photo, team photos, and at-work photos. Uploading these photos through CompanyCam updates your Profile and can raise that score. Can I upload photos from my CompanyCam Projects to my Google Business Profile? Yes. When uploading photos to your Google Business Profile from the Edit Profile screen, you can select photos from your computer, from a URL, or from your CompanyCam Project Library. Is it an extra cost to add multiple GBP locations? No. All locations are included under a single $99/month subscription. Is there a limit on how many GBP locations I can connect? There is no limit on the number of locations you can connect. Can I view data across all locations at once? Not yet. Marketing Suite shows one location at a time. Use the location dropdown to switch between them. What's the difference between the GBP connection Marketing Suite and the Reviews feature on standard plans? On standard plans without the Marketing Suite add-on, you can connect your GBP to view reviews. With Marketing Suite, you also get two-way sync for business info, photo uploads, the ability to request new reviews, and automatic GBP posting when you publish a Showcase. Want to get the most out of Marketing Suite? Check out this guide to learn more about its features, how it works, and ways it can help you showcase your work and grow your online presence. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesHow to Request Reviews in CompanyCamSetting Up Marketing SuiteShowcasing a Project in Marketing SuiteResponding to Reviews in Marketing SuiteCreating Posts for Socials using Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Creating Posts for Socials using Marketing Suite | Help Center
content: Creating Posts for Socials using Marketing Suite | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreating Posts for Socials using Marketing SuiteTurn your CompanyCam projects into ready-to-share social media content in just a few clicks. Updated this weekTable of contentsTurn inconsistent, time-consuming marketing into a simple, streamlined workflow. Once you’ve created Showcases from your CompanyCam Projects, publish them across Facebook, Instagram, X, TikTok, LinkedIn, Google Business Profile, and YouTube—right from your account.Note:To post your Showcases to social media, you must have the Marketing Suite subscription add-on.Marketing Suite is only available on the web.Showcase your work on social media Note: only Admins and Managers can access the Marketing Suite.Click Showcases on the left-hand side panel. Click the + Showcase Icon. Select a Project you'd like to Showcase. From here, you can make changes to the Showcase Title, Description, Project Type, and Materials Used. To add photos, click the Add Photos Icon. You can rearrange photos by dragging and dropping them into your preferred order. To set a cover photo, hover over the image you’d like to feature and click the Make Cover Photo Icon. Once you've made your changes, click Confirm. Connect your socials by clicking + Connect Channel. Once you've added your socials, click the dropdown menu and select one of the following: As a ReelAs an ImageAs a Carousel Click Publish Now once you've made your selections. Schedule Social Media Posts Follow the same steps to create your Showcase on social media. Click the dropdown next to Publish Now, then click Schedule for Later. Select a date you'd like to schedule the post, then click Save. Click the new scheduled date icon at the top. Edit and manage posts Click Showcases on the left-hand side panel. Select a Showcase you want to edit. Click Edit at the top right of the page. Make your edits and click Confirm. Update your social media channels and click Publish Now or Schedule for Later. View social media analytics Click Dashboard on the left-hand side panel. From here, you should see your Marketing Dashboard, which will provide your social media analytics. Manage social media channels Click Dashboard on the left-hand side panel. Click the Channels Icon at the top. From here, you can add a new social media channel. Click Manage Channels. You can remove a social media channel by clicking Disconnect. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesCreate and Use Showcases for your Portfolio SiteSetting Up Marketing SuiteShowcasing a Project in Marketing SuiteResponding to Reviews in Marketing SuiteManaging your Google Business Profile in Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Responding to Reviews in Marketing Suite | Help Center
content: Responding to Reviews in Marketing Suite | Help CenterSkip to main contentResponding to Reviews in Marketing SuiteView and respond to your Google reviews directly from CompanyCam without logging into Google. Updated over a week agoTable of contentsMarketing Suite pulls your Google reviews into CompanyCam so you can respond and request new reviews without platform switching. Responses sync back to Google automatically.On the webNote: Marketing Suite is a paid add-on. Marketing Suite is only available on the web app.View reviewsThere are two paths to reply to Reviews:Log in to CompanyCam on the web app and click Marketing in the left navigation bar > Dashboard and scroll down to see your 4 most recent unanswered reviews at a glance. To view more, click View Reviews.Click Marketing in the left navigation bar > Reviews to view up to 50 of your most recent reviews, displaying the star rating, customer name, date, and full review text.Respond to reviewsNote: Only Admin and Manager roles can access and respond to reviews in Marketing Suite.To reply to a new Review or edit a reply you've already submitted, click Reply/Edit Reply on that review. When finished, click Send Reply/Update Reply.Multiple locationsIf you have more than one Google Business Profile connected, use the location dropdown at the top of the page to switch between locations and manage reviews per location. To add another Google Business Profile, click Add Location. Request a reviewNote: Only Admin and Manager roles can request reviews through Marketing Suite on the web. Standard and Restricted users can still request Reviews through the Project page.Click Reviews under Marketing in the left navigation bar.Click Request Review in the top-right corner of the Reviews page.Copy your review link and send it directly to your customer. FAQsCan customers attach photos to a review request I send?Customers can view photos you've included in a review request, but they have to manually attach photos to their Google review themselves due to a Google limitation.Does my reply post to Google immediately?Replies sync to Google automatically once submitted. There may be a short delay before the response appears publicly on your Google Business Profile.💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesHow to Request Reviews in CompanyCamSetting Up Marketing SuiteShowcasing a Project in Marketing SuiteCreating Posts for Socials using Marketing SuiteManaging your Google Business Profile in Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Archiving and Restoring Projects | Help Center
content: Archiving and Restoring Projects | Help CenterSkip to main contentArchiving and Restoring ProjectsWant to keep a Project you've completed, but tired of popping up in your feed? Learn more about Archiving below. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:To Archive or restore Projects, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Archiving and restoring Projects is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webArchiving Log in to CompanyCam.Open the Project you’d like to Archive.Click on the Three Dots Icon in the upper right of the screen. Click Archive Project. After a Project has been Archived, you'll be able to access the Project by opening the Archived Tab within your Project Feed. RestoringLog into CompanyCam from the web or a mobile browser.Toggle to Archived on the Show Bar to search for the Project. Open the Project you’d like to restore.Click Unarchive. In the appArchivingOpen the mobile app.Open the Project you’d like to Archive.Tap on the Menu Icon in the upper right of the screen.Scroll to the bottom of the page and tap Archive Project.After a Project has been Archived, you'll be able to access the Project by opening the Archived Tab within your Company Feed. Restoring Log in to the mobile app.Open the Project you’d like to restore.Tap on the Menu Icon in the upper right of the screen.Scroll to the bottom of the page and tap Unarchive Project.Prefer to remove a project completely? Check out how to delete Projects.Related ArticlesMove PhotosUsing Project LabelsDeleting ProjectsHow to Create and Edit Project TasksAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Use Checklist Templates | Help Center
content: Create and Use Checklist Templates | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesChecklists are an important part of CompanyCam and Checklist Templates make it easy to have masters for the checklists you use the most. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:To create and manage a Checklist Template, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Creating and using Checklist Templates is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webCreate a Checklist Template from an existing ChecklistLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Checklists Tab and open the Checklist you'd like to create a Template from. To create a Template, click the Three Dots Icon in the upper-right corner of the Checklist, then select Save List as Template. Title the Checklist Template and select Create New Template. Create a Checklist Template within the Templates LibraryClick Templates within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Navigate to Checklists, then select + Create Checklist Template. Duplicate a Checklist TemplateClick Templates within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Open the Checklist Template you'd like to duplicate. Click Duplicate Template at the top of the page. That's it! A copy of the Template now lives in your Checklist Templates section. Mark a Template Section Not ApplicableClick Templates within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Open the Checklist Template you'd like to edit. To mark a section Not Applicable, toggle the Section Required off. That's it! Now, when Users access the Template in a Project from the mobile app, they will be able to mark the section N/A.Use a Checklist TemplateLog in to CompanyCam.Open your Project and click the Checklists Tab. Click + Create Checklist and then Use Existing Template. Choose the Checklist Template you'd like to use. After selecting your Template, the Checklist will be added to your Project. In the appCreate a Checklist Template from an existing ChecklistTap on the To-Dos Tab of the Project and select the Checklist you'd like to create a Template for. Tap on the Three Dots Icon near the top right corner of the page, and select Save Checklist as Template. Title the Checklist Template and tap on Save Template. Use a Checklist Template Open the Project that you'd like to add a Checklist Template to.Tap on the To-Dos Tab. Select the black + Checklist Icon.Choose the Checklist from Template option within the menu.Select the Checklist Template you'd like to use from your Template Library. After selecting your Template, the Checklist will be added to your Project.Note: Once a Template has been created from a Checklist, any edits made to that Checklist and saved as a Template will create a new Template. It will not override the previously saved Template. Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistCreate and Use Report TemplatesHow to Access Community Checklist TemplatesCreate and Use Project TemplatesAssign a Checklist to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Use Project Templates | Help Center
content: Create and Use Project Templates | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Use Project TemplatesProjects are an important part of CompanyCam and new Project Templates make it easier to create Projects more efficiently! April 17, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesTo create Project Templates and set/remove Default Project Templates, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityManaging Project Templates is only available on the Web.On the webCreate a Project TemplateLog in to CompanyCam.Click Templates within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Select + Create Project Template. Name the Project Template and add a description. Select and add any Labels, Checklist Templates, Report Templates, Project Files, or Pages Templates that you’d like included within the Project Template. Once selected, click on Add to Project Template. Note: Changes made to your Project Template will be updated and saved automatically. Set or Remove a Default Project TemplateFrom the Templates Manager page, click on the Projects Tab. ​​Click on the Three Dots Icon on the right side of the Project Template you'd like to save as a default, and then click on Set as Default Template. ​ To remove the Default Template, click on the Three Dots Icon on the right side of the Project Template, and select Set as Default Template. Select Remove default template. ​ Using a Project TemplateClick the + Create Project button in the Project Feed. From the dropdown menu, click Use Existing Template. Select the Project Template you'd like to use and click Choose Template. Enter your Project Details (Name and Address), then Create Project. ORClick the + Create Project button in the Project Feed. From the dropdown menu, click + New Project. Enter Project Details (Name and Address) and then choose your Project Template from the Apply a Project Template dropdown menu. Select Create Project to create the Project. ​In the appUsing a Project TemplateTap on the + Icon.Tap Create Project. Type the Project Address or Confirm the Project Location.Name the Project and select Choose a Template.Select the Project Template that you'd like to use. Once you've selected your Project Template, tap on Create Project.Your Project has now been created. From here, you can start taking or uploading Project photos!Now that you've created your project, you can start capturing and uploading photos!Related ArticlesCreate a New ProjectCreate and Use Report TemplatesCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesCreate and Use Pages TemplatesCreate and Manage Project GroupsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create Text Snippets | Help Center
content: Create Text Snippets | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate Text SnippetsFinding yourself typing the same notes over and over? Let Text Snippets help you save time! Find out how to create them below. Updated this weekTable of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo use Text Snippets, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityCreating Text Snippets is available on both web and mobile apps.On the webFrom a Photo DescriptionLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to a Project with photos you need to work with. Click on a photo in the Project to view it.To the right of the photo, you'll see Description. Click Edit or click into the text area. While in the editor for the Description, click Use Text Snippet from the options. In the pop-up, click + Create New. Enter the title of the Snippet and all the details. Click Save when finished. From a ReportLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to the Report you want to work in. In the Section Summary or in a photo text box, click on the text area. Click Use Text Snippet. In the pop-up, click + Create New. Enter the title of the Snippet and all the details. Click Save when finished.From a PageLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to the Page you want to work in. When editing the Page, click on the Use Text Snippets Icon within the toolbox. In the pop-up, click + Create New. Enter the title of the Snippet and all the details. Click Save when finished. In the appFrom a Photo DescriptionOpen the CompanyCam app and navigate to one of your Projects.From within the Project, tap on a photo to view the photo. Tap Add a description.Tap Snippets to access Snippets.Tap + Create New Snippet at the bottom of the menu.Type in the title and the body of the Snippet. Tap Save when finished.From a ReportOpen the CompanyCam app and navigate to one of your Projects.Within the Project, open the Reports tab and tap into your Report.Tap into a Section Summary or a Photo Description area.Tap on Snippets.Tap + Create New Snippet at the bottom of the menu.When editing a Text Snippet, add your information, then tap Save.From a PageOpen the CompanyCam app and navigate to one of your Projects.Within the Project, open the Pages Tab and open a Page.When editing the page, tap on the Snippets Icon.Tap + Create New Snippet.Type in the title and the body of the Snippet. Tap Save when finished.After creating text snippets, you can manage your saved snippets by editing or deleting any snippets you like!Related ArticlesEdit or Delete Text SnippetsHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamUsing Tags in CompanyCamCreate a ReportCreate a ProposalDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Creating a Checklist | Help Center
content: Creating a Checklist | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreating a ChecklistChecklists are a great way to ensure you're never missing a step. Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesAdmins and Managers can create, edit, delete, and duplicate Fields and Checklists.Standard and Restricted users can only create or edit Fields on Checklists they've created.Collaborators can view Checklists and complete Fields. They can only edit Fields on Checklists they've created.Both the Owning Company and Collaborator can create a Checklist and interact with each other's Checklists to complete Fields.Completed Fields will show the User's information for the Field that was checked off.Platform AvailabilityCreating a Checklist is available on both the web and mobile apps. On the webCreate a ChecklistLog in to CompanyCam.Open the Project you'd like to make a Checklist for.Click on the Checklists Tab. Click + Create Checklist and then click + New Checklist to create a new list. Title your Checklist and click Create Checklist to start. Click + Add Field to add items to your Checklist. Title the Field, and add optional notes/instructions. To choose to require photos to complete (this is on by default), the Field and/or add reference photos, click on the Photos Required Checkbox and Add Reference Images. If you click + Question, you can choose one of the following questions to add to the Field: Yes/No (ask for a yes/no answer)Rating (ask for a 1-5 rating)Text (Ask for a text response) Multiple Choice (ask a question with multiple answer choices) To upload photos for the Field, click in an area outside of the Field for the Pencil Icon to appear and then click + Add Photos. If you have photos that have already been added to the Project, you can add them to the Checklist by clicking the Photo Icon > selecting the photos you'd like to add from the Project (click on the Square Icon in the upper left corner of the photo(s) > and then clicking Confirm in the lower right corner. Click the Circle Icon next to the Field to cross it off and mark it completed. To hide all completed Fields, toggle Hide Completed on. From the Three Dots Icon, you can open the Checklist Menu. From here, you can:Rename ListReorder FieldsCollapse All SectionsExport to PDF, and/or Export a Checklist as a PDF to your Saved FilesSave List as TemplateDuplicate the ChecklistDelete Checklist To save an exported Checklist as a PDF to your Files for quick viewing, from the Checklists menu, click on Export to PDF > toggle the Add to Saved Files on. Your File will be automatically uploaded into the Project and uploaded to any integrations that support File uploads. Click the + New Section to create a new section. Add Fields to the new Section as needed. Duplicate a ChecklistClick on the Checklists Tab and open the Checklist you're working with. Click on the Three Dots Icon in the top right of the Checklist and then choose Duplicate. You will now see a copy of the Checklist within the Checklist Tab.​ Duplicate a Checklist FieldClick on the Checklists Tab and click the Checklist you're working with. Hover over the field you would like to duplicate and click the Duplicate Icon to the right. You should now see a duplicate of that field within your Checklist. Duplicate a Checklist SectionOpen the Checklist you're working on.Click on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the section you'd like to duplicate and then click Duplicate Section. Converting a File into a ChecklistLog in to CompanyCam and open your Project.Click on the Checklists Tab. Click on + Create Checklist and then click Use a File. Click on the File you'd like to convert. You should now see your Checklist appear in the Checklist Tab labeled as the File name. ORClick on the Files Tab. Click on the Three Dots Icon next to the File you'd like to convert into a Checklist and click Create Checklist. Next, click on the Checklists Tab, and you should now see your Checklist. Converting a Page into a ChecklistClick on the Pages Tab and click on the Three Dots Icon next to the Page you'd like to convert. Click Create Checklist. Next, click on the Checklists Tab, and you should now see your Checklist. In the appCreate a ChecklistTap into the Project you'd like to make a Checklist for.Tap the To-Dos Section.Tap the + Icon.Choose Blank Checklist from the slide-up menu.Title your Checklist, and then tap Create Checklist.Tap + New Field to add items to your list.Title the Field, add optional notes/instructions, and choose if photos are required to complete the Field. If you need to take photos, add photos from the Project, or upload photos from your device to add to the Field, tap on + Photos to select from the menu.To take photos in the Checklist, tap on the Camera Icon. Otherwise, you can upload photos from your device or add photos from the Project by tapping on the + Icon.Tap the Circle Icon next to the list to cross it off and mark it as completed when the Field is finished. From the Three Dots Icon, you can open the Checklists Menu. From here, you can:Hide Completed FieldsRename ChecklistAdd SectionReorder SectionsReorder FieldsCollapse All Sections (only available if there are multiple sections within a Checklist)Export to PDFSave Checklist as TemplateDelete ChecklistNote: Add Section will add a section to the Checklist. Tap the Three Dots Icon > Add Section > Title the Section > Create New Section. Add Fields to the new Section as needed.Duplicate a ChecklistTap the To-Dos Section and open the Checklist you're working with.Tap the Three Dots Icon in the top-right corner of the page.Tap Duplicate Checklist from the slide-up menu.You will now see a copy of the Checklist within the Checklist Tab. Duplicate a Checklist FieldTap the To-Dos Tab and open the Checklist you're working with.Swipe the Field you would like to duplicate to the left until you see the Three Dots Icon appear. Tap the Three Dots Icon and then tap Duplicate Task. You should now see that the Field is duplicated within the Checklist.Duplicate a Checklist SectionOpen the Checklist you're working on.Tap on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the Section you want to duplicate.Tap Duplicate Section from the slide-up menu. Converting a Document into a ChecklistTap into the Project you'd like to make a Checklist for.Open the Documents Section.Tap the File you'd like to convert into a Checklist.Tap the Three Dot Icon in the top right of the page, then choose Create Checklist. Once the File has been converted, you will see it within the Checklists Tab inside your Project.Converting a Page into a ChecklistTap into the Project you'd like to make a Checklist for.Open the Documents Section.Tap on the Page you'd like to convert to a Checklist. Tap the Three Dots Icon at the top right corner of the Page and then tap Create Checklist. Once the Page has been converted, you will see it within the Checklist Tab inside your Project.FAQsHow many Checklists can I add to a Project? Projects can have multiple Checklists. There isn't a direct limit to the number of Checklists that can be added to a Project. Is there a way to create a Checklist Template? Yes! Our Checklist Templates feature allows you to create a Template for your commonly used Checklists so that they can be quickly added to your Projects. Are there any premade Checklist Templates available to use? Yes, there are a variety of premade Checklist Templates available within the Community Checklists Library. These are a great resource that you can use to add to your own Template Library or use as inspiration when developing your own Checklist Templates. Can I remove a Reference Image from a Field? Yes, you can remove a Reference Image from a field. Here are some steps on how to do this:Click View Reference Images within the Field. Hover over the Reference Image you'd like to remove and click on the Trash Can Icon. Click Delete, then Done. Can I complete Checklists offline? Yes! Checklist fields can be marked complete in Offline Mode. Photos cannot be added to Checklist fields until a connection is reestablished. Can I assign a Checklist to a User? Yes, you can assign a Checklist to a User. Check out our guide that'll walk you through how you can do this. Is there a character limit for Checklist Fields? Yes, Checklist Fields have a 255-character limit. Can a Walkthrough Note be converted into a Checklist? Yes! A Walkthrough can be converted into a Checklist and does not count toward the AI Action count. Can I use AI to build a Checklist? Yes, you can use AI Tools to build a Checklist. Check out our guide to learn more about how you can use AI within your Checklists. What types of Files can I convert to a Checklist? You can convert Word files(DOC/X), Excel files (XLS/X), PDFs, Images, and TXT files. Related ArticlesCreate a ReportAssign a Checklist to UsersCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamUsing AI Features for ChecklistsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Deleting Projects | Help Center
content: Deleting Projects | Help CenterSkip to main contentDeleting ProjectsNeed to delete a project from your account? Learn how below. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo delete Projects, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityDeleting Projects is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webOpen the Project you’d like to delete.Click on the Three Dots Icon in the upper right of the screen. Click Delete Project. In the appOpen the Project that you’d like to delete.Tap on the Menu Icon in the upper right corner of the screen.Tap Delete Project at the bottom of the page. Tap Delete to confirm that you'd like to delete the Project. FAQsHow can I restore a deleted Project? If you delete a Project and need it restored, please contact our Support Team.Related ArticlesHow to Delete Photos and VideosRestore Items from Project TrashHow to Create and Edit Project TasksArchiving and Restoring ProjectsAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Edit or Delete Text Snippets | Help Center
content: Edit or Delete Text Snippets | Help CenterSkip to main contentEdit or Delete Text SnippetsEditing or removing Text Snippets is easy and can be done on the web or in the app. Learn how to below! May 15, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo delete Snippets, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityEditing and deleting Text Snippets is available in both the web and mobile apps.On the webFrom a Photo DescriptionLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to one of your Projects.Click on a photo within a Project to view it. To the right of the photo, you'll see Description. Click Edit. While in the editor for the Description, click the Text Snippet Icon from the options. Scroll or search to find the Text Snippet you want to edit. Click the Pencil Icon to edit.The Pencil Icon will bring up the Text Snippet Editor. To delete the Snippet click Delete and confirm to delete. To save changes you've made click out of the Snippet and use the X to close or click Use Snippet to use it. From a ReportLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to a Report. Open the Text Snippets by clicking on the Text Snippet Icon (three lines and a star).Scroll to find or use the search bar to find the Snippet you'd like to edit, and click the Pencil Icon on the right-hand side. Make any edits you want and either exit out of the window or click Use Snippet.If you want to delete your Snippet, click Delete in the top right corner and confirm by clicking Yes, Delete.From a PageLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to a Page. Open Text Snippets by clicking on the Use Text Snippet Icon. Scroll to find or use the search bar to find the Snippet you'd like to edit, and click the Pencil Icon to the right of the Snippet.Make any edits you want, and then either out of the editor or click Use Snippet. If you want to delete your Snippet, click Delete in the top right corner and confirm by clicking Yes, Delete.In the appFrom a Photo DescriptionOpen the CompanyCam app and navigate to one of your Projects.From the Project, select a photo to view.Tap Add a description. Tap on Snippets to access your Snippets.Scroll to find or use the search bar to find the Snippet. Tap the Pencil Icon to edit. When editing a Text Snippet, add your information and then tap Save.If you want to delete your Snippet, click the Delete Icon on the right-hand side and confirm by clicking Delete.From a ReportOpen the CompanyCam app and navigate to one of your Projects and tap on the Documents Tab.Tap on the Reports Tab and open your Report.Tap into a Section Summary or a Photo Description.Tap on Snippets.Scroll to find or use the search bar to find the Snippet. Tap the Pencil Icon to edit. When editing a Text Snippet, add your information, then tap Save.If you want to delete your Snippet, click the Delete Icon on the right-hand side and confirm by clicking Delete.From a PageNavigate to one of your Projects and tap on the Documents Tab.Tap on the Pages Tab and open your Page.Tap on the Snippets Icon. Scroll to find or use the search bar to find the Snippet you'd like to edit, and tap the Pencil Icon on the right-hand side. Make any edits you want, tap Save, and it will take you back to the previous screen, where you can click Add to add the Snippet to your page. If you want to delete your Snippet, tap the Delete Icon on the right-hand side and confirm by tapping Delete.Related ArticlesEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationChange Login InformationUsing Tags in CompanyCamCreate Text SnippetsArchiving and Restoring ProjectsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Access Community Checklist Templates | Help Center
content: How to Access Community Checklist Templates | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Access Community Checklist TemplatesCommunity Checklists are a great way to access templates you're needing for jobs you're working on. Subscribe or save individual templates! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: Community Checklists are only available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options. Permission Roles: To subscribe to Community Checklists, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: Subscribing to Community Checklist Templates can only be done on the web app. ​On the webLog in to CompanyCam on a web browser.Click on your User Icon at the top of the page and select Community Checklists. Select which Partner you'd like to receive Checklists from. 4. Click Subscribe to get all the lists added to your library. You can also select the individual list you'd like to have added to your library, to do this, click on Add. Accessing subscribed TemplatesOpen the Project you'd like to add your Checklist to and click the Checklist Tab.Create a Checklist using Existing Templates. Select the Partner Checklist Template you'd like to use. FAQsWhat is the difference between Restricted and Non-restricted partners? Restricted Templates are Templates where permission must be granted by the partner before they’re available to be added to a company.Once access has been granted, the requester will receive an email telling them they’re approved, and they can then go into the community and subscribe or add Templates individually like normal. Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistAssign a Checklist to UsersHow to Manage TemplatesCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesBuilding an Advanced Checklist TemplateDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamPages is a great tool you and your team can use to help keep your notes organized! April 20, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:Creating Pages is available to all permission roles.Platform Availability:Creating Pages is available on both web and mobile apps. On the webCreate a Page within a ProjectOpen the Project that you'd like to add a Page for and click on the Pages section. Click on the + Create Page button to create a new Page. From here, your Page will open, and you can begin creating your Page from scratch by typing in the Start writing... title section. Or you can choose from existing layouts by selecting Report, Daily Log, Summary, or More Templates. The AI features Daily Log and Summary as well as Templates are only available on select plans. Note: A Table of Contents is available on the side of your Page so you can easily navigate through long documents without scrolling. You can customize your Page layout using the built-in editor. The editing tools are located at the bottom of the screen. From left to right, the tools include: Header SizeBoldItalicUnderlineColorCheckboxBulleted ListNumbered ListUse Text SnippetsInsert LinkAdd PhotosAdd TableUndoRedo Create a Page from photosOpen a Project that you've taken photos in.Within the Photos Tab, select the photos you'd like to include within a new Page. From the Actions Menu at the top of the screen, click Pages. From the Pages drop-down menu, click Create Page to generate a new Page (or click on the Summary, Daily Log, or Progress Recap option to generate a Page using AI). To begin customizing how your photos are formatted, you can click the Layout Icon to choose Photo Size, Layout, and Aspect Ratio. You can add or remove photos by clicking the Three Dots Button. Here you can also choose if you want to include Photo Descriptions by clicking on Show Photo Text. Include Photo Details from Page SettingOpen your Project, click on the Pages Tab and open a Page.At the top of the Page, click Page Settings. Use the Settings Toggles to choose which details to display on the Page, such as Capture Details, Photo Tags, and Photo Numbers. This will adjust the photo details on all photos within the Page. Note: Once you've added your photos, these details may be formatted differently based on whether you have your photos in List or Grid Layout. OR Add a Cover PageOpen the Page you'd like to add a Cover Page to.Hover over the Page and click Add Cover Page when it appears at the top of the Page. Now you can edit your Cover Page by adding a title and photo, with options to edit location, date, and company details.If you need to delete the Cover Page, hover over the body of the page so that it fills in grey. You'll see a Trash Can Icon appear below the Page title. Click the Trash Can Icon to remove the Cover Page. Share a PageOpen the Page you'd like to share. Click on Share in the upper right corner. Click Copy Link and then paste the link into an email, text message, etc. Export a Page to PDFOpen the Page you'd like to export as a PDF.Click on the Export PDF option at the top of the page. Note: When you export a Page as a PDF, the Page will be saved as a File within your Project automatically. Delete a PageOpen the Page you'd like to delete.Click on the Three Dots Icon at the top of the page. From the drop-down menu, click Delete. In the appCreate a Page in a ProjectOpen the Project that you'd like to add a Page for and tap on the + Icon in the Documents Section. Once selected, tap Page for a new blank Page to open.Start Typing to work from a blank Page or chose from another layout option by selecting Report, Walkthrough, Daily Log, or More Templates. The AI features Walkthrough and Daily Log as well as Templates are only available on select plans. After creating your Page, you can customize its layout using the built-in editor. The editing tools are located within the keyboard. From left to right, the tools include: Take a PhotoInsert a PhotoHeaderBold, Italicize, and UnderlineUse Text SnippetsCheckboxBulleted ListNumbered ListUndo (seen if you slide to show unseen options to the right)Redo (seen if you slide to show unseen options to the right)Create an AI Page from photosOpen a Project.Tap on the Select Icon to select multiple Project photos.Select the photos you'd like to include within your Page, then tap on the Three Dots Icon at the bottom of the screen.Within the slide-up menu, choose Create Page.Within the slide-up menu, choose which type of AI-generated Page you'd like to use. You can learn more about each type of AI Page here. Add Photo DescriptionsOpen a Project.Open the Documents Tab.While under the Pages section, select the Page you would like to add Photo descriptions to.Click into the Page then tap on the Add Photos Icon within the toolbar of the Page.Click to add photos. Choose the photos you want to add, then tap Next.Now that the photos have been added to the Page, tap the Three Dots Icon. Toggle on Photo Text. This ensures any photo descriptions associated with the photo are added to the Page.Include Photo DetailsOpen the Page you're working on.Tap on the Three Dots Icon at the top of the Page.From the pop-up menu, tap Page Settings.Use the Settings Toggles to decide which Photo Details you'd like to include.Adjust the Photo LayoutOpen the Page you'd like to adjust the Layout in. Click into the Photo section and tap this Layout Icon.Choose how you'd like your photos to appear.When you've made your changes, click out of the menu and your changes will be applied.Add a Cover PageOpen the Page you'd like to add a Cover Page to.Tap on the Three Dots Icon at the top of the Page.From the pop-up menu, tap Add Cover Page.Once your Cover Page is added, you can edit it as needed.If you need to delete the Cover Page, tap on the Trash Can Icon at the top of the Cover Page.Share a Page or Export a Page to PDFOpen the Page you'd like to share or export a PDF for.Tap on the Three Dots Icon at the top of the Page.From the pop-up menu, you can:Share a Page link (Copy the link and then paste it into an email, text message, etc.)Export a Page as a PDFIf you choose to export the Page as a PDF, you can: Include a Cover PageSave the Page to your Project Files within that ProjectDelete a PageOpen the Page you'd like to delete.Tap on the Three Dots Icon in the upper-right corner. From the pop-up menu, tap Delete Page and confirm. Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamUsing Tags in CompanyCamCreate a ReportCreate and Use Pages TemplatesDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Create and Edit Project Tasks | Help Center
content: How to Create and Edit Project Tasks | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Create and Edit Project TasksProject Tasks are quick-add tasks and easily viewable when looking at a Project on web or mobile! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All permission roles can create and complete Project Tasks. Only Admins and Managers can edit and delete Project Tasks.Platform Availability:Project Tasks are available on both the web and mobile apps.To access Project Tasks in Offline Mode on the mobile app, the Project will need to be open on the app before entering Offline Mode.What is the Difference between a Project Task and a Checklist?Project Tasks are great for non-repeatable, one-off tasks for a project. They can act as simpler to-dos or a punch list for specific tasks to complete on the project. Project Tasks are limited to 100 per Project. Checklists capture detailed field information — recording site conditions and step-by-step work instructions — and can be assigned to specific users.On the webHow to add a Project Task within the Project ViewLog in to CompanyCam. Open the Project that you'd like to add a Task for.Click on the collapsible Sidebar Icon to the right if it's collapsed.​ Under the Tasks section within the right-hand panel, click + New Task to create a Task. To add additional Tasks, use the Enter Key or continue selecting + New Task. Click on the Circle Icon next to the Task to mark it as completed. Completed Tasks will automatically be moved to the Show Completed section, which appears as Hide Completed when expanded. How to add a Project Task to a photo or videoLog in to CompanyCam.Open the Project that you'd like to add a Task for.Select a photo or video to open it.Select the + New Task to create a Task for the photo or video. Once the Task has been added to the photo/video, a reference photo or video will be added as a thumbnail to the general Tasks section of the Project.​ Click on the Circle Icon next to the Task to mark it as completed. How to Edit and Delete Project TasksTo edit a Task, click on the Task you'd like to make changes to by opening the photo/video or making the changes within the Tasks section on the Project page. Once you have added the edits, the Task will automatically save. To delete a Task, hover or click on the Task you'd like to delete and then click on the Trash Can Icon. Note: Once Tasks have been deleted, they cannot be restored. In the appHow to add a Project Task within the Project ViewOpen the Project you'd like to add a Task for.Tap on the To-Dos Tab within the Project screen. From there, tap on the + Icon.Next, choose Task. Enter the Task that needs to be completed and tap on the Create New Task Button. Tap on the Square Icon next to the Task to mark it as completed. How to add a Project Task from My StuffFrom the Home screen, select My Stuff.Tap the + Button at the bottom of the screen.Choose the Project you’d like to add the Task to, or create a new Project.Enter the Task details, then tap Create New Task.How to add a Project Task to a photo or videoOpen the Project you'd like to add a Task for.Select a photo or video.Tap on the Tasks Icon.Enter the Task that needs to be completed, and then tap Create New Task. Once the Task has been added to the photo/video, a reference photo or video will be added as a thumbnail to the general Tasks section of the Project. How to Edit and Delete Project TasksTo edit a Task, tap on the Task you'd like to make changes to by opening the photo/video or making the changes within the Tasks tab from the To-Dos view. Once you have added the edits, tap Save. To delete a Task, tap on the Trash Can Icon. Note: Once Tasks have been deleted, they cannot be restored. FAQsIs there a character limit for Project Tasks?Yes, there is a 250-character limit for each Project Task.Can Project Tasks be used in Project Templates?No, Project Tasks cannot be used in Project Templates. Related ArticlesCreate a New ProjectEdit or Delete Text SnippetsUpload Existing PhotosUsing Tags in CompanyCamAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Delete Photos and Videos | Help Center
content: How to Delete Photos and Videos | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Delete Photos and VideosNeed to trash a photo or video from a Project? Learn how below. Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All Users can move photos and videos to the Trash. Restricted and Standard Users can only move photos or videos to the Trash they've taken or uploaded.To restore photos from the Trash, you will need to have the Admin or Manager role.
