If you don't have that role, ask an Admin or Manager on your account to restore them for you.Platform Availability:Moving Photos and Videos to the Trash is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webDeleting a single Photo or VideoClick on the photo or video you would like to remove, and click on the Trash Icon that will appear in the upper left corner of the screen. Click Yes, Move to Trash. Deleting multiple Photos or VideosClick on the Square Icon in the upper left corner of each photo or video you want to delete. Click Actions in the menu at the top of the screen and choose Move to Trash. In the appDeleting a single Photo or VideoTap on the photo or video you would like to remove, then tap the Three Dots Icon in the lower right corner.Tap Move to Trash and confirm.Deleting multiple Photos or VideosOpen a Project and tap the Select Icon to select multiple photos or videos.From there, press and hold down on the first photo or video you'd like to select.To select the remaining photos/videos you'd like to move to the trash, tap the circle on the photo or video — selected photos/videos will show a blue checkmark.Once you're finished with your selection, tap the Three Dots Icon in the bottom right corner.Tap Move to Trash.Tap again on the pop-up to confirm.Now that you've got the project cleaned up, you're ready to quickly share a gallery, timeline, or report.Related ArticlesMove PhotosHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamDeleting ProjectsRestore Items from Project TrashHow to Create and Edit Project TasksDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Upload and Use Project Files | Help Center
content: How to Upload and Use Project Files | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Upload and Use Project FilesProject Files allows you to add commonly used files to your projects. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:To upload and manage Project Files, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Standard Users can add the existing Project Files to Projects.Platform Availability:Managing Project Files is only available on the web app.Please note: You can upload .pdf,.doc,.docx,.xls,.xlsx,.csv,.txt,.jpg,.jpeg,.png, and .numbers files. File size must be 100 MB or less.On the webHow to Upload a Project FileLog in to CompanyCam.Click Templates within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Click on the Files Tab and select + Upload File. Click and drag your File(s) into the page, and they will upload into the Template Manager. How to Add a Project File to a ProjectOpen the Project that you'd like to add a Company File to.Click on the Files Tab. Click on + Add Files and select From Saved Files. Select the Box Icon to the left of the Project File you'd like to add. Click on Add to Project, and the file(s) will immediately save to the Project. FAQsWhat file types are compatible with Project Files? The following file types are compatible: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .csv, .txt, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, and .numbers files. Is there a maximum file size I can upload for a Project File? Yes. File size must be 100 MB or less. Now that you've uploaded a Project File, here's how you can add it to a Project Template. Related ArticlesHow to Upload and Manage FilesUpload Existing PhotosMerging ProjectsCreate and Use Project TemplatesUsing the CompanyCam + ServiceTitan IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Use Map This Photo | Help Center
content: How to Use Map This Photo | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use Map This PhotoAre you wondering where a photo was taken? Map This Photo can help you locate that information. April 27, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles:This feature is available to all permission rolesPlatform Availability:This feature is available on both the web and mobile apps. On the webOpen a Project and click on the photo you'd like to view on a map. Click on the Map Icon in the right panel of the photo. Once clicked, a map view will open, and a pin will appear. Here you will be able to see information including the Project Name, Project Address, User who captured the photo, and the direction the User was facing when the photo was taken. In the appOpen a Project and select the photo you'd like to view on a map. Tap on the Map Icon at the bottom of the photo. Once tapped, a map view will open within your app, and a pin will appear. Here you will be able to see information including the Project Name, Project Address, User who captured the photo, and the direction the User was facing when the photo was taken.Related ArticlesHow to Use the Project MapPrint Photos in the Web AppHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamOrganize Photos Using TagsAdd Date/Time and GPS Coordinates Stamps to PhotosDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Use the Project Map | Help Center
content: How to Use the Project Map | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use the Project MapCompanyCam lets you view and organize Projects in many ways. Project Maps help you locate Projects using a range of search criteria. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All permission roles can view and use the Project Map.Platform Availability:The Project Map is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Map Tab in the left-hand navigation menu.​ On the Project Map, you will see your Projects marked with pins on the Map. Within the left-hand section of the Map, you'll also see Project Cards for each of your Map Pins. ​ Use controls to zoom in/out on the Map (bottom-right corner). Drag the Map to view specific areas.​ Filter by Date Range, Users, Groups, or Labels at the top of the page. Click on a Map Pin and the Project Card will appear on the left-hand side of the page.​ Click on the Project Card to open the entire Project.​ In the appLog in to the CompanyCam app.Tap on the Project Map Tab.On the Project Map, Projects will populate based on your location. You can also pinch and zoom to navigate to other areas of the Map to view other Projects.Tap on the Project Title, or if you have multiple Projects in one area, tap the option that shows up in the right area.A slide-up menu will open with the Project you selected. Tap on the Project Card to open your Project. Note: If you have multiple Projects at the same address, you'll have the option to swipe between Project cards to find the one you're looking for.After finding the project you're looking for on the Project Map, check out how to add Labels to your Projects! Project Labels can help speed up your filtering/sorting workflows when trying to find specific Projects. FAQsWhat's included in the Project Map?The Project Map gives you a visual overview of all your projects in one place. With it, you can:View all your projects on a mapFilter by date, user, or groupSearch for a project by area or exact address Do the web and mobile apps have the same filtering functionality on the Project Map?No, the experience differs between platforms:Web — Search for projects and filter by date, user, group, or label.Mobile — Browse projects by pinching and zooming to explore different areas on the map.Related ArticlesCreate a New ProjectUsing Project LabelsIntegrate CompanyCam + Project Map ItHow to Use Map This PhotoUsing Project BoardsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Merging Projects | Help Center
content: Merging Projects | Help CenterSkip to main contentMerging ProjectsAccidentally created a duplicate Project? Merging allows you to reduce or eliminate duplicated information. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To merge Projects, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: Merging Projects can not be completed in the mobile app. You will need to use a web browser on a computer or phone to merge Projects.On the webSign in to CompanyCam.Open one of the duplicate Projects.Click on the Three Dots Icon in the upper right corner of the page and then choose Merge Project.​ In the search field, start typing the name or address of the duplicate Project.A drop-down menu will show all possible Projects in your account. Click on the correct Project. Next, click on the Project you'd like to keep. Note: You can click on the box to the left or the right. You'll see the text and color change to reflect which Project will be kept and what Project will be deleted. All photos and Files from both Projects will now be saved in the Project you've selected to keep. Click Merge Projects.FAQsWhen merging Projects, will all Project information transfer over? Not all information can be moved. Only photos, Files, Reports, and Pages will be transferred when Projects are merged. Can I merge a Project that has an active Collaboration? Projects that have active Collaborations on them cannot be merged. If you're no longer working with a Collaborator on a Project, you can end the Project Collaboration and merge the Projects. After merging projects, you can continue to add photos to your project by adding new photos or uploading existing photos saved to your desktop! Related ArticlesCreate a Project without a Street AddressCreate a New ProjectIntegrate CompanyCam + GAF ProjectAccepting a Project CollaborationAdd Project CollaboratorsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Move Photos | Help Center
content: Move Photos | Help CenterSkip to main contentMove PhotosPhotos added to the wrong Project? Follow the steps below to move photos from one project to another. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo move photos, you need to have the Admin, Manager, or Standard role. If you don't have one of these roles, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform AvailabilityMoving photos is available on both the web and the mobile app.On the webLog in to CompanyCam and then open a Project.Select the photo(s) that you want to move by clicking the Square Icon in the upper left corner of each photo. Note: You can select the first photo, hold down shift, and select the last photo— this will auto-select everything in between. Next, click Actions in the menu that pops up and choose Move. Use the search box to find the Project you'd like to move the photos into.​ Select Move Photos.​ In the appOpen a Project.To the right of your photos section, tap on the Select Icon to start selecting photos.From there, press and hold to select the photo you'd like to move.If you want to move multiple photos, tap the Circle Icon in the upper left corner of each photo.After you have selected the photo(s) you want to move, tap the Three Dots Icon in the lower right corner.Choose Move to Project.Search for the Project you'd like to move your photos into. Once you select the Project, the photos will be moved.FAQsCan you move photos into or out of a Project with an active Collaboration? You cannot move photos into or out of any Project that has an active Collaboration. If you're no longer working with a Collaborator on a Project, you can end the Project Collaboration and move the photos. Looking for a way to make finding photos easier? Check out how to add tags to photos through the website to help save time looking for the photos you need!Related ArticlesHow to Delete Photos and VideosHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamUpload Existing PhotosUsing Tags in CompanyCamAdd Project CollaboratorsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Organize Photos Using Tags | Help Center
content: Organize Photos Using Tags | Help CenterSkip to main contentOrganize Photos Using TagsLooking for a better way to organize your photos? You can create custom Tags on the web or mobile app, and assign Tags to any photo. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To manage Tags, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: You can organize your photos using Tags on both the web and mobile app. ​Folders vs. TagsMany software systems utilize folders to organize content. Folders allow you to visually put the pictures away, but with folders, you are forced to choose only one category for each photo. Tags allow you to categorize your photos in multiple ways, making it easier to find exactly what you're looking for.Example Using Tags & FiltersWhen you open a Project, you can see every photo taken at this Project, nothing is hidden. By using Tags and filtering by Tags you can organize your Projects individually and your Project Feed to display only the images you need at the time. There are two ways to view tags in Projects: To show All Tags within the Project, open the Project > click on View > and then toggle On Show Tags (this option will be black when On).​ Another option, you'll be able to filter by specific Tags within a Project, to do this open the Project > click on Tags > search or select the Tags you'd like to filter by. Filtering by Tags will only display photos with that specific Tag. To filter from the Photo Feed, you'll want to click on Photos on the left sidebar > click on the Tags option > search or select the Tag(s) you'd like to filter by. You can also filter by date range, Projects, Users, and Groups. For example, when the "Start" and "Top" Tags are selected, photos with those Tags will be visible. Now that you've gotten the hang of using tags, the next step is understanding how to use Project Labels on the web and mobile App. Also, read more on how you can use tags and labels together to level up your organization.Related ArticlesCreate a Report with TagsUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosUsing Project LabelsHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamUsing Tags in CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Restore Items from Project Trash | Help Center
content: Restore Items from Project Trash | Help CenterSkip to main contentRestore Items from Project TrashSend a photo to the Trash on accident? Photos can be restored without reaching out to Support! Learn how to below. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:To restore photos that were sent to the Trash, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: Restoring Photos from the Project Trash is only available on the web app.Please NotePhotos will be deleted 90 days after they're moved to the Project Trash.On the webOpen the Project that needs to have a photo restored. Click on the Three Dots Icon in the upper-right corner of the screen. Then select View Project Trash. In the Project Trash, select which photos you'd like to restore by clicking in the upper left corner of the photo to select it. You can also choose to restore all photos from the Trash by clicking Select All. Once the photos have been selected, click Move Back to Project. Related ArticlesMove PhotosHow to Delete Photos and VideosDeleting ProjectsIntegrate CompanyCam + Xcelerate Restoration SoftwareArchiving and Restoring ProjectsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using AI Features for Pages | Help Center
content: Using AI Features for Pages | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing AI Features for PagesUse AI to quickly pull together information to share with your team! Updated over a month agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:These features are available on specific plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.The number of AI Actions available varies by plan.Permission Roles:All permission roles have access to using AI Features for Pages.Platform Availability:Using AI Features for Pages is available on both the web and mobile apps.Walkthrough Note is only available on the mobile app.What will each AI feature do?SummaryThe Summary feature allows you to add a quick summary for your photos. This tool will add an AI-generated headline callout to complement and provide context to a Page of photos from a Project. You can quickly add context to any photo selection without typing out a single word! Daily LogThe Daily Log allows you to log a day's activity all in one place. This tool will pull together your work into an easy-to-read and editable Page. This way, you can quickly share daily updates back to the office or store a log of your progress. This feature will organize photos and summarize notes into a snapshot Page of information. The Daily Log will only pull information from photos and captions from the selected date range inside your Project. Progress RecapThe Progress Recap will help you organize and summarize your Project's progress over time. This tool will put together a recap from a selection of photos and their captions into an easy-to-read, editable Page. This way, you can quickly send progress updates to managers, customers, and partners. WalkthroughThe Walkthrough feature will allow you to build a Page while you walk, talk, and take photos. This feature will help you write and format your Walkthrough automatically. This way, you can quickly take photos and notes while conducting a Walkthrough without having to type. This feature will automatically organize the photos you're taking and notes you're speaking, then convert them into an easy-to-read Page that can be shared with others.On the webSummaryOpen a Project and click on the Pages Tab. Click + Create Page. Select Summary.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Summary, then click Generate. Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Summary for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab of the Project.Daily LogOpen a Project and click on the Pages Tab. Click + Create Page. Select Daily Log. Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Daily Log, then click Generate. Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Daily Log for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab of the Project. Progress RecapOpen a Project and click on the Pages Tab. Click + Create Page. Select More Templates.Navigate to AI Templates and select Progress Recap. Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Progress Recap, then click Generate. Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a Progress Recap for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab of the Project. In the appSummaryFrom the Project screen, tap on the Documents Tab.Tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose More Templates from the available options.Navigate to the AI Templates section and select Summary.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Summary, then tap Generate.Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Summary for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab in the Documents Tab in the Project.Daily LogFrom the Project screen, tap on the Pages Tab.Under the Pages Tab, tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose Daily Log from the available options.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Daily Log, then tap Generate.Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Daily Log for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab in the Documents Tab in the Project.Progress RecapFrom the Project screen, tap on the Documents Tab.Under the Pages Tab, tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose More Templates from the available options.Navigate to the AI Templates section and select Progress Recap.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Progress Recap, then tap Generate.Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick recap for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab in the Documents Tab in the Project.WalkthroughFrom the Project screen, tap on the Documents Tab.Under the Pages Tab, tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose Walkthrough from the available options.Tap Start Walkthrough to take photos while completing your dictation.When you've finished your work, tap Done.Your Walkthrough will now be saved in the Pages Tab of your Project.FAQsCan I create a Walkthrough from my desktop? No, Walkthroughs can only be created in the mobile app. Can AI Features for Pages be used in Offline Mode? The Walkthrough feature is compatible with Offline Mode. If a Project is cached for Offline Mode, a User can create a new Page using Walkthrough, and it will process and upload once the User regains an internet connection. The Summary, Daily Log, and Progress Recap features are not compatible with Offline Mode. How long can I use a Walkthrough Note for at a time? The Walkthrough feature can record up to 15 minutes per note. Can I convert a Walkthrough Note into a Checklist? Yes, you can convert a Walkthrough Note into a Checklist. Once your Walkthrough has been saved in your Project, you can convert it to a Checklist by opening the Page > tapping on the Three-Dots Icon at the top of the screen > and selecting Create Checklist. Looking for more ways to use AI? Explore AI Features for Checklists and our Best Practices When Using AI Tools in CompanyCam. Related ArticlesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamCreate and Use Pages TemplatesUsing AI Features for ChecklistsBest Practices when Using AI Tools in CompanyCamGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using Project Labels | Help Center
content: Using Project Labels | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing Project LabelsProject Labels are a powerful way to organize your company's workflow. You can apply as many labels as you need to Projects. April 23, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To create and manage Labels, you need to have the Admin or Manager Role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Restricted Users are unable to add Project Labels to Projects they create or are assigned to.Platform Availability: Users can use Project Labels on both the web and mobile app. On the webCreate a LabelWithin the left-hand navigation menu, click on the Resources Tab to expand it and choose Labels from the dropdown. At the top of the page, click + Create Label. Next, give your Label a title, and your changes will save automatically. Edit a LabelWithin the left-hand navigation menu, click on the Resources Tab to expand it and choose Labels from the dropdown. Locate the Label you want to edit.To update the Label's title, hover your cursor over the Label and click on the Pencil Icon. To update the Label's color, click on the Color Box and select a new color. Delete a LabelWithin the left-hand navigation menu, click on the Resources Tab to expand it and choose Labels from the dropdown. Locate the Label you want to delete and click on the Three Dots Icon and choose Delete Label. Click Yes, delete to confirm. Add Labels to a ProjectOpen the Project you want to add a Label to.Below the Project Title, click Add Labels. Select the Label(s) you want to add and click Save. Remove Labels from a ProjectOpen the Project you want to remove a Label from.Below the Project Title, click Edit Labels. Click on the X Icon next to the Label and click Save. Filter by LabelsFrom the Project Feed, select Filter. Select the Label(s) you'd like to filter by.Click Apply Filter to view all Projects with the Label(s) selected. In the appCreate a LabelOpen CompanyCam and navigate to a Project you want to work on.Once you've opened your Project, tap + Label.Tap on Create New Label.When creating a new Label, add a title, choose a color, and tap Create.The Label you've added will now appear in the list of Labels.From here, you can check the new Label and add it to the Project.Edit a LabelOpen a Project you're working on and tap + Label.To edit an existing Label, tap the Pencil Icon to the right of the Label name. Edit the name and/or color, then tap on Save.Delete a LabelOpen a Project you're working on and tap + Label.To delete an existing Label, tap the Pencil Icon to the right of the Label name. Edit the name and/or color, then tap on Save or to delete a Label, tap Delete Label.Add Labels to a ProjectOpen CompanyCam and navigate to a Project you want to work on.Once you've opened your Project, tap + Label.Select the Label(s) you want to add and tap Save.Remove Labels from a ProjectOpen CompanyCam and navigate to a Project you want to work on.Once you've opened your Project, tap on the Label(s) below the Project Name.Deselect the Label(s) you want to remove and tap Save.Filter by LabelsTo open the Project Feed, tap Company under the Workspace section, then tap See All.Tap on the Projects Tab, then Filter.Tap the Labels Tab.Select the Labels you wish to filter by and tap Save.FAQsWhat are effective uses for Project Labels? Project Labels are best used to add context or attributes to individual Projects, not to group multiple Projects together. Project Labels are helpful for things like:Indicating a Job Status (e.g., Active, On Hold, Complete)Identifying the type of Project (e.g., Residential, Commercial, Warranty)Quickly filtering Projects using the mobile Sticky FilterFor organizing large jobs with multiple locations, units, or phases, Project Groups are the recommended solution. Project Groups let you bundle related Projects into one unified view and see progress across the entire job in one place. You can then use Labels and Tags for additional organization within that structure. Interested in Project Groups? Check out more information on this feature here. Related ArticlesHow to Use the Project MapUsing Tags in CompanyCamAssign Project Tasks to UsersCreate and Manage Project GroupsUsing Project BoardsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using Tags in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: Using Tags in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing Tags in CompanyCamTags help you easily organize and search for your photos. You can even add them in the camera view. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you start Plan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo edit or create Tags, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityAdding Tags to multiple photos at a time is possible on both mobile and web, but removing Tags from multiple photos at a time is only available on web.On the webCreate and edit TagsLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Tags option within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Click + Create Tag. Title your Tag and click Add Tag. To edit a Tag, hover over the Tag's title and click on the Pencil Icon when it appears. Delete a Tag, click on the Three-Dots Icon to the right of the Tag, and click Delete. Adding Tags to photosIndividual PhotosClick on any photo to open and view it.Click Add Tags on the right. Type to search or pull down the menu to search for created Tags.If you have the necessary permissions to create Tags you can also create new Tags here. Type in your Tag then select Create Tag.Once you've added all the necessary Tags, click Save Tags. Multiple PhotosSelect the photos that you wish to give the same Tag or Tags by clicking into the circle in the top left corner of the photo or by selecting a date range. Click Actions at the top of the page and choose Tag from within the drop-down menu. Once you've added all the necessary Tags, click Tag Photos. Remove Tags from photosIndividual PhotosOpen the photo you'd like to remove the Tag from.Click Add Tags on the right. Click the X at the end of the Tag you'd like to remove and click Save Tags. Multiple PhotosSelect the photos you'd like to remove Tags from by clicking into the circle in the top left corner of the photo or by selecting a date range. Click Actions at the top of the page and choose Remove Tags from within the drop-down menu.​ Select the Remove Tags button to remove the Tags from the photos you've selected. Optional: If you'd like to replace the Tags, select the toggle and select the new Tags you'd like to include on your photos. ​ Filter photos using TagsLog in to CompanyCam.Click on any Project or click Photos from the left navigation menu.Filtering from the Photos feed will allow you to search across Projects for Tags. Filtering in a Project will only show photos from that Project.Click on the Tags option > search or select the Tag(s) you'd like to filter by. Filter photos without TagsLog in to CompanyCam.Click on any Project or click Photos from the left navigation menu.Filtering from the Photos feed will allow you to search across Projects for Tags. Filtering in a Project will only show photos from that Project.Click on the Tags option > select Untagged. Using the Or/And Filter ToolLog in to CompanyCam.Open any Project or click Photos from the left Navigation Menu. Click on View and select if you'd like to filter Tags by Or or And. In the appCreate and edit TagsOpen the CompanyCam app.Navigate to a Project you want to work on and tap on a photo you want to tag.Tap the Tag Icon from the options at the bottom.Type your new Tag(s) and select Create. Tap Save when you're done.To Edit or Delete a Tag tap the Pencil Icon to the right of the Tag. Make changes to the Tag and tap Save or Delete Tag to delete.Adding Tags to photosFrom the cameraOpen CompanyCam and start a new Project or go into a Project you want to work in and open the camera to take photos.Tap on the Tag Icon within the capture tool.Type to search or scroll to find the Tag(s) you need.Tap the Tag to select it and tap Save once all the Tags are added. Selected Tags will appear over the camera view. Note: Tags will continue to be on photos until you've ended that camera session or removed the Tag.To remove the Tag from your next photo, tap the X on the Tag on the camera view or tap the Tag Icon and X on the Tags. Once removed, tap Save to apply the changes.From the ProjectIndividual photosOpen a Project that you want to work on and tap a photo to view it.Tap the Tag Icon from the options at the bottom.Type to search or pull down the menu to search for created Tags. Tap to select the Tag(s) you want to use and then tap Save to apply the Tag(s). Note: If you have the necessary permissions to create Tags you can also create new Tags here. Type in your Tag, then select Create.Once you've added all the necessary Tags, the Tag Icon will have a blue circle, and photos will now have a Tag Icon on the photo preview.Multiple photosOpen a Project that you want to work on.Tap on the Select Icon.You can tap into the circle on the photo to select individually or tap the Select Icon to the right of the date to select all the photos from that date.Tap the Three Dots Icon on the bottom blue bar, then select Tag from the menu.Type in the search bar for created Tags, then tap to select the Tag(s) you want to use. Tap Save when you're done. Note: If you have the necessary permissions to create Tags you can also create new Tags here. Type in your Tag, then select Create.Once you've added all the necessary Tag(s), photos will now have a Tag Icon on the photo preview.Remove Tags from photosFind a photo or Project you want to work in and tap the photo to view it.Tap the Tag Icon from the bottom options.Tap the X at the end of the Tag you'd like to remove and then tap Save to apply the changes.Filter photos using TagsOpen CompanyCam and navigate to a Project you want to work in. Note: Filtering from the Photos feed will allow you to search across Projects for Tags. Filtering in a Project will only show photos from that Project. Tap the Filter Icon to the right of Photos.Tap into the Tags filter and scroll to find your Tags. Select Tags by tapping the Tag(s). Tap Save to apply and view the tagged photos. The Filter Icon will have a black circle in the top right corner to indicate a filter has been applied. Using the Or/And Filter ToolOpen CompanyCam and navigate to the Project you want to work in.Tap the Filter Icon to the right of Photos.Tap into the Tags filter. Select your Tags and select if you'd like to filter your tagged photos by Or or And, then Save. FAQsWhat happens when you delete a Tag from the Company Tags? When you delete a Tag from your Company Tags, it will be removed from all photos where it was previously applied. Deleted Tags cannot be restored. If you need to use the Tag again, you’ll need to create a new one and manually apply it to your photos. The new Tag will not backfill or automatically apply to photos that had the deleted Tag. Tagging your photos isn't only helpful when using CompanyCam. You can use Tags to rename downloaded photos, too! Here's how you can save yourself time and effort by using Tags to rename your downloaded photos! Related ArticlesCreate a Report with TagsUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamIntegrate CompanyCam + AccuLynxDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using Tags to Rename Downloaded Photos | Help Center
content: Using Tags to Rename Downloaded Photos | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosLooking for a better way to keep track of photo downloads? In CompanyCam you can use Tags to rename them. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesYou will need to have the Admin, Manager, or Standard role to create Tags. If you don't have this permission, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded Photos is available on both the web and mobile apps. On the webSign in to CompanyCam on any web browser.Find the photos you want to download within your Photos Feed or in a Project.Select at least 2 photos (renaming will not work with a single photo) by clicking the Square Icon in the top-left corner of the photo or mass-selecting photos by clicking on the square to the left of the date. (Optional) If you need to tag your photos now, you can click Actions at the top of the screen and click Tag. This will tag every photo. Click Actions at the top of the screen and select Download. Click Download Modified. Your downloaded photos should show up in your Downloads folder in a .zip file. When you unzip the file and open it to view your photos, each photo should now be named as its tag from CompanyCam.​ FAQsDo Photos need to be tagged before selecting them, or can I tag Photos after selecting them? Before starting, your photos can already be tagged, or you can bulk tag your photos after selecting them. Do Photos uploaded to CompanyCam retain their previous naming convention? Photos uploaded to CompanyCam will not retain any previous naming conventions. If you require photos to have a specific name, the steps above will help. Related ArticlesCreate a Before & After PhotoHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamUsing Tags in CompanyCamOrganize Photos Using TagsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Create an Invoice Using Pages | Help Center
content: How to Create an Invoice Using Pages | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Create an Invoice Using PagesNeed to create an Invoice for your client? Our Pages feature can help! April 21, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options. Permission Roles: This is an account-level feature and is available to all permission roles.Platform Availability:This feature is available on both the web and mobile apps. On the webLog in to CompanyCam and open the Project you'd like to create an Invoice for.Click on the Pages Tab and select + Create Page. A blank Page will generate. From here, you can click More Templates to search for our pre-made Invoice Templates. Within the Example Templates Tab, type "invoice" in the search bar to filter and choose one of the available pre-made Invoice Example Templates. After you've selected an Invoice Example, a Template Preview will be available. Click Use Template to add it to your Project. Once the Page has been created, you can use the editor to customize the layout of the Invoice. The editing tools are listed at the bottom of the Page. In order from left to right, they are: Header SizeBoldItalic UnderlineColorCheckboxBulleted ListNumbered ListUse Text SnippetsInsert LinkAdd PhotosAdd TableUndoRedo After you've created your Invoice, you can share it with your client by clicking the Share Button at the top of the Page or export the Invoice to PDF by clicking Export PDF. When you export a Page as a PDF, the Page will be saved as a File within your Project automatically. This way, you can request Signatures for your Pages or sync your Pages to any integration that supports File syncing.Want to create a general Invoice to use across multiple Projects? Save it as a Pages Template!Related ArticlesHow to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamIntegrate CompanyCam + Google DriveIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Use Pages Templates | Help Center
content: Create and Use Pages Templates | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Use Pages TemplatesPages are a useful tool in CompanyCam, and Pages Templates make it easier to quickly add your commonly used Pages to multiple Projects. Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Role:To create and manage a Pages Template, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.All permission roles can apply a Pages Template to a Project. Platform Availability:Managing Pages Templates is only available on the Web. On the webCreate a Pages TemplateOpen the Project you're working on.Click on the Pages Tab and open the Page you'd like to create a Template for. To create the Template, click on the Three Dots Icon in the upper-right corner of the Page, then click Save as New Template. Title your new Template and click Save. Create a Pages Template within the Templates LibraryClick Templates within the left-hand navigation menu under Resources. Navigate to Pages, then select + Create Page Template.​ Using a Pages TemplateOpen your Project and click on the Pages Tab, then click + Create Page. From here, a new blank Page will open. Once your new Page is open, you can choose from recommended AI-generated Templates like Report, Daily Log, or Summary. Select More Templates to see other options.Note: To create a blank Page without using a Template, you can begin editing the Page, and the Template options will disappear. Here you can view your recently used Templates and filter by Your Company Templates, Example Templates, and AI Templates. Click on the Template you'd like to use. After making a selection, you'll see a preview of the Template. Click Use Template. A Table of Contents stays pinned on the side so you can jump around long documents without scrolling. In the appCreate a Pages TemplateOpen a Project and tap on the Documents Section.Tap on the Pages Tab and select the Page you'd like to create a Template for.Tap on the Three Dots Icon in the top right corner of the Page. From the menu, tap Save Page as Template.Give your Template a title and tap Save. Using a Pages TemplateOpen the Project and tap on the Documents Section.Tap on the + Button at the bottom of the page. Tap the Page option from the slide-up menu. A new Page will open. Here, you can choose to use the Report or Daily Log AI Template, or for more options, select More Templates.In the Templates Library, you can filter by Your Company Templates, Example Templates, and AI Templates. Tap on the Pages Template you want to use. You will be directed to a preview of the Template you selected. Review and tap Use. FAQsCan videos be saved within a Pages Template? No. Videos cannot be saved within a Pages Template. Interested in using AI features for Pages? Here is a guide to creating AI Pages: Using AI Features for Pages.Related ArticlesCreate and Use Report TemplatesCreate and Use Project TemplatesCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamUsing AI Features for PagesDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Use Report Templates | Help Center
content: Create and Use Report Templates | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Use Report TemplatesReports are an important part of CompanyCam and new Report Templates make it easy to have masters for the Report types you create the most. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesTo create Report Templates, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.All User roles can use existing Report Templates. Platform AvailabilityReport Templates can only be managed on the web app. On the webCreate a Report TemplateLog in to CompanyCam.Open the Report you'd like to create a Template from. To do this, start by clicking Reports on the left side panel. You can also access the Reports from the Reports tab inside a Project. To create the Template, click Manage in the upper right corner of the Report, then Save as Template. Here, you will add a Template Name and a Template Description. Click Save to create the Template. Using a Report TemplateOpen the Project you'd like to build a Report for and click on the Reports Tab or open Reports on the left side panel.​ Click on the blue Create Report button, and select Use Existing Template. Click on the Template you'd like to use and it will be added to your Project. Need to make edits to your existing templates? Here is how to manage those templates: How to Manage Templates. FAQsDo I need to create a Report before it can be saved as a Template? No, you don't need to create a Report first to save it as a template. You can create a Report Template from scratch in the Template Manager. What content is saved when I save a Report as a Template? The Report's structure and Section Summaries will be saved as a Template; however, other content, such as photos, will not be saved.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + SumoQuoteCreate and Use Project TemplatesHow to Manage TemplatesCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesCreate and Use Pages TemplatesDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using AI Features for Checklists | Help Center
content: Using AI Features for Checklists | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing AI Features for ChecklistsUse AI to build, complete, and improve your Checklist workflow! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on specific plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.The number of AI Actions available varies by plan.Permission RolesAll User Permission Roles have access to AI Features for Checklists.Platform AvailabilityAI Features for Checklists are only available in the mobile app. In the appCreate a ChecklistTap into the Project you'd like to make a Checklist for.Open the To-Dos Tab within the Project.Next, tap on the + Icon.Choose the Checklist from Voice option.Talk out loud and describe what needs to get done. We'll build a Checklist based on what you describe. You can tap on the blue Pause Button to pause your recording.After you've paused your recording, you can tap Done if you're finished, and we'll then build your Checklist. Complete a Checklist using Voice to TextTap into the Project that contains the Checklist you're working on.Tap on the To-Dos Tab. Open the Checklist you're looking to complete.Tap the Microphone Icon to Talk to Complete the Checklist.Talk out loud and describe what you've completed so far. You can tap the Camera Icon to the right of each Field and take photos while recording. You can tap the Pause Icon to pause the recording, then tap Done when you are finished. When you're done, we'll mark those described sections as complete.To see how AI can help beyond Checklists, check out AI Features for Pages and our Best Practices When Using AI Tools in CompanyCam!Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Export Project and Checklist DataCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesUsing AI Features for PagesBest Practices when Using AI Tools in CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Manage Project Groups | Help Center
content: Create and Manage Project Groups | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Manage Project GroupsWant a better way to organize your Projects? Create Project Groups to bundle related Projects into one unified job. April 17, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:Admins and Managers can create, edit, delete, and view all Project Groups activities. Standard Users can view the Project Groups, view the progress within the Group, view the details page, and navigate to individual projects from the Group view. Restricted Users can only access Projects that they're assigned to within the Project Group. Platform Availability:Creating and managing Project Groups is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webCreating a Project GroupOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab. Click + Create at the top of the Project Groups View or click the + Icon at the top of the left navigation menu and select New Project Group. Name the Project Group and select the Projects you'd like to include. When selecting Projects, you can include Existing Projects as well as create New Projects for the Project Group. After you've chosen your Projects, click Review. Once you've reviewed your Project Group, click Submit to create the Group. Add/Remove Existing Projects from a Project GroupOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab. Click on the Project Group you'd like to edit. Scroll down to the Projects section and click Edit Projects. Select/deselect the Projects you'd like to add or remove to the Project Group. Once those changes have been made, click Save. Create a New Project within a Project GroupOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab. Click on the Project Group you'd like to edit. Click on the Three Dots Icon next to the Project Group's Title. To create a new Project for the Project Group, click Add New Project to Group. Name your Project, add an address, and click Create Project. Deleting a Project GroupOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab. Click on the Project Group you'd like to delete. Click the Three Dots Icon to the right of the page. Click Delete Group Project. View Group ActivityOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab. Click on the Project Group you'd like to view the activity for. You'll see the Project Group activity on the right-hand side of the page. You can use the filtering tools at the top of the page to view activity by Time Frame and Activity Type. ​Share a Project Group Timeline LinkOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab.​ Click on the Project Group you'd like to share.​ Click the Share Icon to the right of the page. ​ Select Enable Timeline Link. Disable a Project Group Timeline LinkOpen the Project Feed and click on the Project Groups Tab. Click on the Project Group that has a Timeline Link enabled. Click the Share Icon. Select Disable Timeline Link. In the app Creating a Project GroupOpen CompanyCam, tap on the + Button.Select Create Project Group.From here, create a title for your Project Group, select the Projects you'd like to include, and select Save.From there, you can see your new Project Group and select a Project to view it. View Project GroupsUnder the Workspace section on the homescreen, tap Project Groups.Tap on the Project Group you'd like to view.Once within the Project Group, you can tap on a Project to open it.Add/Remove Projects from a Project GroupUnder the Workspace section on the homescreen, tap Project Groups.Tap on the Project Group you'd like to manage.Tap on the Three Dots Icon at the top of the page.Tap Edit Project Group.Tap the Checkbox next to each Project you'd like to add or remove.After you've selected your Projects, tap Save. Create a New Project within a Project GroupUnder the Workspace section on the homescreen, tap Project Groups.Tap on the Project Group you’d like to add a Project to.Click the Three Dots Icon at the top of the page. Select Add New Project to a Group.Deleting a Project GroupUnder the Workspace section on the homescreen, tap Project Groups.Tap on the Project Group you'd like to manage.Tap on the Three Dots Icon at the top of the page.Tap Delete Project Group.Tap Delete to confirm.Share a Project Group Timeline LinkUnder the Workspace section on the homescreen, tap Project Groups.Tap on the Project Group you'd like to share.Tap the Share Icon at the top of the page.Tap Enable Timeline.Disable a Project Group Timeline LinkUnder the Workspace section on the homescreen, tap Project Groups.Tap on the Project Group that has a Timeline Link enabled.Tap the Share Icon at the top of the page. Tap Disable Timeline. FAQsCan I add a Collaborated Project to a Project Group? Yes, you can add a Collaborated Project to a Project group, but it depends on whether you're the owning or the invited company within the collaboration.Owning Company: The company that created the collaboration invitation can add the Project to a Project Group.Invited Company: The company that was invited to the collaboration cannot add the Project to a Project Group. Related ArticlesCreate and Modify User GroupsCreate a New ProjectUsing Project LabelsCreate and Use Project TemplatesHow to Create and Edit Project TasksDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Building an Advanced Checklist Template | Help Center
content: Building an Advanced Checklist Template | Help CenterSkip to main contentBuilding an Advanced Checklist TemplateLearn how to create an Advanced Checklist Template with conditional logic and drag-and-drop sections. Updated this weekTable of contentsAdvanced Checklists give your team a smarter way to standardize job-site work. You can build Templates with conditional logic that show or hide fields based on what's entered, drag and drop to restructure sections, and require photos to complete any field.This feature is available on select plans and can only be created on the web app.Creating an Advanced Checklist TemplateNote: Only Admins and Managers can create Checklist Templates.Log in to CompanyCam > go to Templates > Checklists in the left sidebar.Click on the Checklists Tab, then click + Create Checklist Template.In the drop-down menu, click Advanced Checklist. Add a Template Name and Description.Click + Add Section to create a new Section.Click + Add Field to create a new Field.Give the Field a title with the task that needs to be completed.Once you've given the Field a title, choose 1 of the following question types:Yes/No (ask for a yes/no answer)Rating (ask for a 1-5 rating)Multi-Select (ask a question where multiple answers can be selected)Single-Select (ask a question where a single answer can be selected)Text (Ask for a text response) Use the drag and drop option to reorder Fields and Sections at any point as you build.Adding conditional logicConditional logic lets you build follow-up questions that only appear when a crew member selects a specific answer. Crews only see what's relevant to their job, nothing more.Select a field within your Template.To add conditional logic to a Field, hover your cursor over the answer field, and click + Add Conditional Field. Requiring photos and videosTo ensure crews capture the right documentation at the right step, you can require photos/videos to be taken before a Field can be completed. To require photos/videos for a Field, click on the Camera Icon in the Template Builder. ​Duplicating Fields and SectionsTo duplicate a Field or Section, hover over the area and click on the Duplicate Icon. FAQsWhat's the difference between Classic and Advanced Checklists? Classic is the original Checklist experience you've always used. Advanced is the new experience, built with conditional logic and a redesigned Template builder. Can I convert an existing Classic Checklist Template to an Advanced Checklist Template? Classic and Advanced Templates are separate at this time. If you have an existing Classic Template and would like to transform it into an Advanced Checklist, you'll want to rebuild your Template in the Advanced builder to use the new features. Who can create and edit Templates? Only Admins and Managers can create and edit Advanced Checklist templates. All roles can complete checklists in the field. Can I duplicate an existing Template? Yes. You can duplicate an existing Template by navigating to the Template > clicking the 3 Dots Icon in the right corner > Duplicate. Can I build an Advanced Checklist Template in the mobile app? No. Building Advanced Checklist Templates is not available on the mobile app right now. What is the difference between a Section and a Field? Sections are organizational containers that group related fields together. Think of them like phases, categories, or even rooms of a job (for example, "Pre-Work," "Kitchen," or "Final Walkthrough"). Fields are the individual items crew members actually respond to, like a question, a photo prompt, or a text input. Every field lives inside a section, so you'll set up your sections first, then build out the fields within each one. 💡 Need more help?Contact our Support Team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Manage TemplatesCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesGetting Started with CompanyCamUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Requesting Payments | Help Center
content: Requesting Payments | Help CenterSkip to main contentRequesting PaymentsLearn how to set up Payments and request a payment from a customer using a payment link, Tap to Pay, or manual card entry in CompanyCam. Updated this weekTable of contentsPayments lets you collect money from customers without leaving CompanyCam. Set up Stripe once, then send a Payment Request your customer can pay by link, by Tap to Pay, or by card you enter for them.Note: Payments are available on Pro, Premium, and Elite plans. It isn't available on Basic. Upgrade your plan to turn it on. Payments are only available in the United States. Accounts in a trial can set up Payments, but can't create or send Payment Requests until the trial ends and the account upgrades.On the webSetting Up PaymentsNote: You need the Admin or Manager role to set up Payments. If you don't have it, ask an Admin to update your permissions.Log in to CompanyCam and click Payments in the Sidebar.Click Set Up Stripe and follow the prompts. We use Stripe to process Payments and keep everything secure.If Stripe needs more information, you'll see a Finish Setup option. For the full walkthrough, see Setting Up Stripe for Payments. Once Stripe is connected, you can start requesting Payments. You can also reach this from inside a Project: open the Project, then click the Sales tab. Requesting a PaymentNote: All permission roles can create Payment Requests. Standard and Restricted Users can only view, edit, and delete the requests they created.Click Payments in the Sidebar.Click Request Payment.Give the Payment a title, total, and memo, then click Create.You can also request a Payment from inside a Project: open the Project, click the Sales tab, then select + Request Payment. In the appRequesting a PaymentNote: All permission roles can create Payment Requests. Standard and Restricted Users can only view, edit, and delete the requests they created.Open the Project you want to request a Payment for and tap the Sales section.Tap + Payment Request.Enter the amount, add an optional title and memo, then tap Create.Choose how to collect: Enter Card Info, Tap to Pay, or Share a Link.For Tap to Pay, tap Allow to give CompanyCam access to Bluetooth. Without Bluetooth access, Tap to Pay won't work.Tap the customer's card or phone on your device to collect the payment.Tap Done once the payment is received.You can view the completed Payment in the Sales section of the Project. Note: Tap to Pay isn't available on Legacy plans. Upgrade your plan to use it. FAQs What's the minimum payment amount? The minimum Payment amount is $0.50. Is there a transaction limit? Payment Requests have a $5,000 limit per transaction. Can I request a higher limit? You can request a higher limit from the Payments page in your account. Our team reviews each request and follows up with you. How long do Payments take to process? Processing time depends on the payment method:Credit card payments take up to 1 business day to land in your Stripe account.ACH payments take up to 5 business days.How will I know when a payment is complete? All Admins and Managers get a notification once Stripe confirms the payment is fully complete. What devices support Tap to Pay? Tap to Pay works on iPhone XS or later running iOS 18.6 or newer, and on a range of Android devices. What are the processing fees? Fees are charged per transaction:Tap to Pay: 2.5% + $0.30Online and premium cards: 3% + $0.30ACH: 1%, up to $10Can I add sales tax or line items? Payment Requests can't include sales tax or line items. Each request is a single dollar amount. How do I set up a Stripe account? See Setting Up Stripe for Payments for the full setup. What if I'm having issues with Stripe? Payments are powered by Stripe, so their team can best help. See their help guide or contact Stripe Support. How do customers pay a Payment Request? Customers can pay by a mobile-friendly web link, by Tap to Pay, or by card you enter for them. On their end, they can use a credit card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, or ACH. See How to Complete Payments Using CompanyCam. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesAdd or Update Billing InformationHow to Complete Payments Using CompanyCamIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineAccessing Payments and Payout Information in StripeDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Complete Payments Using CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Complete Payments Using CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Complete Payments Using CompanyCamNeed to make a payment to a contractor or company you're working with? Make payments efficiently through CompanyCam! March 31, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startOnce a Contractor requests a Payment, you can complete the payment request through a web link (mobile-friendly), Tap to Pay, or you can manually enter your payment information. Payments can be completed via Credit Card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, or ACH (powered by Stripe). Completing Payments is only available in the United States. How to complete a Payment RequestComplete using a Payment LinkClick on the link for the Payment Request (contractors will be able to share a link with you via email and/or text). From here, you can make a Payment.OR using the web. Add your email address, and then select to pay by Card, Apple/Google Pay, or ACH. Enter your card or bank information into the fields. Click on the purple Pay Button to confirm the Payment. Complete using Tap to PayTap on the Tap to Pay option. If prompted, Allow CompanyCam to find Bluetooth devices.Tap your compatible payment method on the device. The collection screen will look different depending on whether you're on an iOS or Android device. Once complete, you will see a message saying that the payment has been received. Need help requesting payments? Check out our helpful article on How to Request Payments. Related ArticlesAdd or Update Billing InformationRequesting PaymentsIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamIntegrate CompanyCam + BolsterDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Setting Up Stripe for Payments | Help Center
content: Setting Up Stripe for Payments | Help CenterSkip to main contentSetting Up Stripe for PaymentsLearn how to create and connect a Stripe account to start collecting payments through CompanyCam. Updated this weekTable of contentsCompanyCam Payments lets you collect Payments from customers directly through the app. To get started, you'll need to create a Stripe account and complete their business verification process. This article walks you through each step.Regarding Fees:Customers can pay via a web link (that's mobile-friendly), Tap to Pay, or you can manually enter their card information.Tap to Pay is not available on Legacy subscriptions. Upgrade your plan to take advantage of this feature.Customers can pay via credit card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, or ACH (powered by Stripe).Processing fees include:Tap to Pay: 2.5% + $0.30 per transactionOnline & Premium cards: 3% + $0.30 per transactionACH: 1% or up to $10 fee per transactionNo Sales Tax or Line Items can be added. Each Payment Request will be a single dollar amount.Payments are powered by Stripe. If you're having issues with your Stripe account, check out their help guide or contact Stripe Support.Before you set up StripeTo set up Stripe for Payments, you need the Admin or Manager role.You'll need the following information on hand to complete Stripe's business verification:Business profile:Business typeEINBusiness addressIndustryBusiness representative:Legal name and emailDate of birthHome addressLast 4 digits of Social Security numberNote: The business representative should be someone authorized to sign into the Stripe account on your organization's behalf.Setting up your Stripe accountLog in to CompanyCam and click on the Payments Tab in the left-hand navigation menu, and click Start Setup. You'll be prompted to gather some key information that'll be helpful when creating your Stripe account. Once you've gathered this information, click OK, I'm Ready.Next, you'll be routed to Stripe's setup page. If you have an existing Stipe Express account, you'll be prompted to log in. Otherwise, enter your email address to create an account. Next, you'll follow the prompts on screen and add your Business Type. After you've entered your Business Type information, the business representative will be asked to verify their personal details. This information should be for the business representative who has the authorization to sign in to the Stripe Account on your organization's behalf. The information collected during this step is there to verify your identity, keep your account safe, and meet legal and regulatory requirements. Make sure the dedicated person is filling out the form with their legal name, email address, date of birth, home address, phone number, and the last four digits of their Social Security number. Once filled in, click Continue. Next, you'll be asked to add some additional Business Details. Once complete, click Continue. Now that your Business Details have been added, you'll be able to add your Banking Information. Select or search for your bank. Once found, enter the account details you'd like to have Payments sent to, and then click Continue. After your Banking Information has been added, you'll next add Public Details for Customers. Once entered, click Continue. Finally, you'll review the information you provided and click Agree and Submit. FAQsCan I set up Stripe for Payments during a free trial? Accounts that are in trial can set up the Payments feature, but cannot create or send Payment Requests until their trial ends and they upgrade to a paid subscription. Can I request Payments outside of the United States? Payment processing is only available in the United States. If your business is based outside of the US, you will not be able to use CompanyCam Payments.Want to walk through it? Check out our video on how to connect to Stripe below!💡 Need more help?*Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.* The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusRequesting PaymentsHow to Complete Payments Using CompanyCamAccessing Payments and Payout Information in StripeConnecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitanDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Accessing Payments and Payout Information in Stripe | Help Center
content: Accessing Payments and Payout Information in Stripe | Help CenterSkip to main contentAccessing Payments and Payout Information in StripeLearn how to view payment request statuses in CompanyCam and access your Stripe Express Dashboard for payout details. Updated this weekTable of contentsPayments in CompanyCam lets you request payments from customers and track them through to deposit. This article walks you through viewing payment statuses in CompanyCam and accessing your payout information in Stripe.Accessing payment and payout information is only available on the web app. Payment processing is only available in the United States.Viewing payment requestsNote: Only Admin and Manager roles can access Payment Requests. If you don't have access, ask an Admin on your account.Log in to CompanyCam > click Sales in the left-hand navigation menu > click Payments. Here you'll see all Payment Requests across your Projects, along with each request's status, amount, and payment method. Payment Request StatusesEach payment request displays a status label so you know exactly where things stand. Here's what each one means:Draft: The Payment Request was created but hasn't been sent to the customer yet.Sent: The Payment Request has been sent to the customer and is waiting for payment.Payment Processing: The customer submitted a payment, and it's currently being authorized by Stripe. This is common with ACH payments and some card transactions and usually resolves within a few business days.Processing: Payment confirmed by Stripe. The deposit is on its way to your bank account, but hasn't arrived yet. You'll see an estimated deposit date below this label.Paid: The payment has fully settled, and the deposit is in your bank account.Payment Failed: The payment attempt failed. The customer will need to retry.Refund Pending: A refund has been started but hasn't settled yet.Refunded: The refund is complete, and the funds have been returned to the customer.Note: When a payment moves to Processing status, a customer confirmation email is sent automatically. ACH payments may take a bit longer in this stage.Accessing your Stripe Express DashboardTo view your payout details, click Bank Account Settings in the upper-right corner of the Sales screen. Your Stripe Express account will open in a new tab. Log in if prompted. Dashboard viewsOnce you're in your Stripe Express Dashboard, you'll see 3 views. Home viewThe Home view shows a high-level overview of your payment activity:Recent earnings show a graph of payments collected over time.Recent payments lists your most recent payments and their statuses.Total balance displays the available balance ready to be paid out.Click See Details to view a detailed breakdown of your balance. You can also update your bank information from here. Payments viewThe Payments view gives you a detailed look at your payment activity in Stripe, including transaction dates, statuses, payment amounts, and customer names. Balance viewThe Balance view shows your available balance along with your transaction and payout history.Payout scheduleInitial Payout: Your first payout typically takes 7–14 days after successfully receiving your first payment. Subsequent payouts follow your account's payout schedule. Settlement Timeline: This is the time between when a payment is made and when funds become available in your Stripe account.Credit and debit cards: ~2 business daysACH: ~4 business daysPayout Options:Manual payouts: Choose when to send payouts and how much to transfer. Funds typically arrive same-day or within 1–4 business days.Instant payouts: If available, an instant payout option will appear in your Stripe dashboard.Weekly or monthly payouts: Schedule payouts on a recurring basis from within your Stripe dashboard.Note: If your scheduled payout day doesn't exist in a given month (for example, the 31st in a 30-day month), Stripe moves the payout to the last day of that month. If a payout falls on a weekend or holiday, funds arrive the next business day.FAQs Who can access payout information? Only Admin and Manager roles can access Payment Requests and payout information in CompanyCam. If you don't have access, ask an Admin on your account to adjust your permissions. Can I use CompanyCam Payments outside the United States? Payment processing is only available for businesses based in the United States. What does the deposit ETA mean under Processing? The deposit ETA is the estimated date your funds will arrive in your bank account. Stripe calculates this based on your payout schedule. ACH payments may take a bit longer than card payments. What should I do if a payment shows "Payment Failed"? The customer will need to retry the payment. You can resend the payment request from the Sales tab in CompanyCam. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesAdd or Update Billing InformationRequesting PaymentsHow to Complete Payments Using CompanyCamCreate a ProposalDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents
