title: Create a Proposal | Help Center
content: Create a Proposal | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a ProposalLearn how to build and send a Proposal to a customer directly from a Project using the Payments feature. Updated yesterdayTable of contentsProposals is a document builder built into CompanyCam Payments. Use it to outline your scope of work, break down line items, and collect a deposit before the job starts — all from inside a Project.To create Proposals, you'll need to have Payments set up on your account first. See How to Request Payments to get started. This feature is in Early Access; plan availability is subject to change.On the webNote: Only Admin, Manager, and Standard roles can create Proposals.There are two ways to start a new Proposal. Follow the steps for your preferred starting point, then continue from Step 3 onward. Create a Proposal within a ProjectLog in to CompanyCam and open the Project > click the Sales Tab.Click the + Create Proposal Button. Jump to Step 3 below to complete your Proposal.Create a Proposal within the Global Proposals MenuLog in to CompanyCam and click Proposals in the left-hand navigation menu.Click + Create Proposal, choose a Project, and click Create. ​Basic DetailsHere, you can add a Title for the Proposal. You can also click on the address field to edit the Contact Info for the person you're creating the Proposal for, then click Done.​Scope & TimelineDescribe the work you're planning in the Scope & Timeline Section. You can also utilize the AI Assistant to draft your Scope & Timeline. To use the AI Assistant, type in your notes, and the AI will generate a draft based on your Project details and past Proposals.​Line ItemsAdd your line items. For each item, enter a Name, Quantity, Price, and an optional Description, then click Add Item.You can pull items from your Price Book by clicking Add from Price Book, selecting the item, entering the quantity, and clicking Add Item. To remove a line item, click on the Trash Can Icon to the right of the item.After you've added your Line Items, you'll review your subtotal. You can add a tax rate here as well.​DepositAfter reviewing the subtotal, you can decide if you'd like to include a Deposit for the Proposal and select the Payment Options you'd like to accept. If accepting a Credit or Debit Card payment, you can also decide if you'd like to pass the processing fee to your Customer.​Add OnsPhotos: To add photos from your Project to the Proposal, click on the + Button.Files: To add files to the Proposal, click on the + Add files Button.Terms and Conditions: The Terms and Conditions section allows you to enter any terms, conditions, or additional details you'd like your customer to review before approving the Proposal. Note: Your Proposal includes a default Terms & Conditions section. You can edit or remove this text directly in the Proposal editor.E-Signature: If you'd like to collect a signature from your client in the Proposal, enable the Sign and collect e-signature from client toggle. Important: If this option is selected, you must check the box agreeing to sign the document electronically and enter your full name in the text box in order to share the Proposal. ​Once you've finished editing, click Preview to review your Proposal, then Share it with your client. ​ In the appNote: Only Admin, Manager, and Standard roles can create Proposals.Open the Project and tap on the Sales Section.Tap Create Proposal. You can use the AI Assistant to draft your Proposal. Type or speak your notes, and the AI will generate a draft based on your Project details and past Proposals. If you prefer to create your Proposal manually, you can tap anywhere outside of the AI Assistant to dismiss it.Basic DetailsThe Basic Details section will auto-populate a Proposal Title, Proposal Number, and Status. To edit the Proposal, tap into the Basic Details Section > update the title > Save.​CustomerTap the text box under Customer, then enter the Contact Info for the person you're creating the Proposal for, then tap Save.​ScopeTap Add scope of work to open a text box where you can describe the work you'll perform. Once complete, tap Save. ​Line ItemsTap + Add Item to manually add a Line Item or tap + Pricebook to add a Line Item from your Pricebook. When manually adding a Line Item, you'll give the item a Name, Quantity, Unit Cost, and Unit Type. Once complete, tap Save Item.​​Optional: If you prefer not to show line item information in the Proposal, you can toggle off Hide line item prices from customer.​DepositAfter reviewing the subtotal under your Line Items, you can decide if you'd like to include a Deposit for the Proposal and select the Payment Options you'd like to accept. If accepting a Credit or Debit Card payment, you can also decide if you'd like to pass the processing fee to your Customer.​Add OnsPhotos:To add photos from your Project to the Proposal, tap + Add photos. Terms and Conditions:This section allows you to enter any terms, conditions, or additional details you'd like your customer to review before approving the Proposal. Your Proposal includes a default Terms & Conditions section. You can edit or remove this text directly in the Proposal editor.Files:To add Files from your Project, tap + Add files.E-Signature:Enable Sign and collect e-signature from client if you want to collect a signature from your client in the Proposal. If this option is selected, you must check the box agreeing to sign the document electronically and type your full name in the text box in order to share the Proposal.​Tap the View Button to preview the Proposal and share it with your customer by tapping the Share Icon in the top-right corner.FAQsCan I create a Proposal without setting up Payments first? Proposals require Payments to be active on your account. See Setting Up Payments to get started. Can Restricted roles create Proposals? Restricted roles cannot create Proposals. Admin, Manager, and Standard roles have access. Can I add photos from my phone's camera roll to a Proposal? Proposals pull photos from within the Project. You'll need to add photos to the Project first before selecting them for a Proposal. Does the deposit amount have to be a percentage? The deposit can be any dollar amount — it just can't be $0. A Proposal won't be approvable until a deposit amount is entered. Are CompanyCam Payments available outside the United States? Payment processing is only available in the United States. If your business is based outside of the US, you will not be able to use CompanyCam Payments. 💡 Need more help?Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse. Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosRequesting PaymentsHow to Create and Edit Project TasksCreate a Price Book for ProposalsGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create a Price Book for Proposals | Help Center
content: Create a Price Book for Proposals | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a Price Book for ProposalsCreate a centralized Price Book to create consistent Proposals across your Projects and streamline your workflow. Updated yesterdayTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is currently in Early Access; pricing and plan availability are subject to change.Permission Roles:You will need to have the Admin or Manager permissions role to edit and manage Price Book line items.Standard and Restricted Users can access Line Items when building a Proposal.Platform Availability:Creating and modifying Price Book line items is only available on the web app. Please NoteTo access this feature, you must have the Payments feature set up within your account. If you haven't onboarded into Payments, here's how.On the webCreate Line ItemsClick on the Payments Tab within the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the Price Book Tab.To add an item to your Price Book, click + Add Item.Next, add a Name, Description, Unit Price, and Unit Type. Once you're done, click Add Item to add it to the Price Book. Edit and Manage Line ItemsClick on the Payments Tab within the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the Price Book Tab. Find the Line Item you want to manage and click on the Three-Dots Icon to the right of the item. From here, you can click Edit to update the Line Item or click Delete to delete it. Using a CSV Import to create a Price BookClick on the Price Book Tab within the left-hand navigation menu. Click Import from CSV on the right side of the screen. Drag & drop or browse for the CSV you'd like to add to CompanyCam. Select the file and then click on Open to begin uploading the CSV. Once the CSV has been uploaded, click Import from CSV. After you've created your Price Book, it's time to create a Proposal!Related ArticlesUsing Project LabelsCreate a ProposalCreate and Manage Project GroupsUser Permission Roles ExplainedUsing Project BoardsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Creating and Managing Customers | Help Center
content: Creating and Managing Customers | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreating and Managing CustomersLearn how to create and manage Customer records in CompanyCam to track project history, contact details, and new work opportunities. Updated this weekTable of contentsThe Customers directory gives you a central place to manage every customer relationship. See all past Projects tied to a customer, add multiple contacts, and pick up where you left off — no more scrambling through emails or asking a repeat customer to remind you what you talked about last year. On the webView the Customers directoryLog in to CompanyCam, then click Customers in the left navigation bar. Add a new CustomerIn the Customers directory, click + New Customer.Enter the Customer's first name (required) and any additional details — last name, phone, email, and company.Click Save Customer. Note: First name is the only required field. All other contact details are optional.Add a Customer to a ProjectOpen the Project, expand the right-hand side panel if needed, then click the + Button next to Customer. From there, search for an existing Customer or create a new one. Note: Restricted users can view Customer details, but can't add a Customer to a Project.Add additional contacts to a Customer recordOpen a customer record from the Customer directory, then click + Add Contact. Each Customer can have a primary contact and as many additional contacts as needed. Manage a Customer recordClick any Customer in the directory to open their record. All Projects linked to that customer appear here in one view. From the record, click the 3 Dots Icon > Edit Customer to change details or Delete Customer to completely remove the record. To unlink a Customer from a project, open the Project > click the Pencil Icon next to the customer name > scroll down and select Unlink customer from Project. Note: Restricted users can't edit or delete a Customer record. In the appView the Customers directoryOpen CompanyCam, then tap Customers from the home screen. Add a new CustomerFrom the Customer directory, tap + New Customer.Enter the customer's first name and any additional details.Tap Save.Add a Customer to a ProjectOpen the Project, tap + Customer below the Project title.Search for an existing customer or select Create New Customer.If creating a new Customer, select Save.Note: Restricted users can view customer details on mobile, but cannot add a Customer to a Project.Add additional contactsOpen the customer record and tap on the + Icon next to Contacts.Manage a Customer recordOpen the customer record and tap the 3 Dots Icon. From there, you can edit Customer details or delete the record.FAQsCan a Project have more than one Customer contact? Yes, Customers support multiple contacts per Customer record. Each customer has a primary contact, and you can add as many additional contacts as needed. Can I track where a customer came from? You can record a lead source on the Customer record under Additional Details. You can add this information as you create the Customer or after the fact from the Customers directory.In the Customers directory, click the 3 Dots Icon to the right of the Customer record.Select Edit Customer.Add source under Additional Details.Click Save Customer.How does Customers relate to Project Boards? Customers is the who — the relationship and contact details behind each job. Project Boards is the where — where a Project sits in your workflow. The two work together: when you create or edit a Project, you can tie a Customer record to it. Can I see all projects tied to one Customer? Every Customer's detail page shows all linked Projects in one place, so repeat customers and follow-up work are easy to find. 💡 Need more help?Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineCreate a ProposalGetting Started with CompanyCamUsing Project BoardsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using Project Boards | Help Center
content: Using Project Boards | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing Project BoardsLearn how to use Project Boards to track where every project stands across your team's workflow from first contact to completed job. Updated this weekTable of contentsProject Boards give you a visual pipeline to see exactly where every project stands — from a fresh lead to a job in progress to a closed deal. Boards are built on Labels you already use in CompanyCam, so you can shape your pipeline to match how your team actually works, without migrating to a separate app or rebuilding your process from scratch.On the webOpen Project BoardsLog in to CompanyCam, click Projects in the left navigation bar, and select the Boards tab. Understand Boards and LabelsEvery column on a Board is a Label. The Sales board comes with the following labels:Lead (default for all new projects)Proposal SentScheduledActiveCompleteLostUnqualifiedYou can rename, reorder, or customize these Labels to match your workflow. Labels work the same way they always have in CompanyCam — Project Boards are just another way to view your Projects, organized by their Labels.Note: Each Label belongs to one board. If a Project has multiple Labels applied, it appears in the column for the most recently applied Label.Move a Project to a different phaseDrag and drop any project card to a different column to update its Label and move it through your pipeline. Start a new Project from a Board columnClick + at the top of any column. The Project is created with that column's Label already applied. Reorder or add additional Board LabelsNote: Admins and Managers can create, edit, and delete Labels. Standard users can move Projects between columns and view the Board. Restricted users can view the board, but only see projects they have access to.From the Boards Tab, select Manage Board.From there, you can drag and drop your Labels into your preferred order.Select Manage Labels to create a new Label, add an existing Label, or remove a Label from your Board.Click Save. In the appOpen Project BoardsOpen CompanyCam, then tap Boards on the home screen.Move a Project to a different phaseWithin the Boards View, tap on the Project Card.Once inside the Project, tap on the Label Field to select a different phase. The Project will then move to the corresponding column on the board. Start a new Project from a Board ColumnNote: Admins and Managers can create and edit Labels. Standard users can move projects between columns. Restricted users can view the board, but only see the Projects they have access to.Tap + Icon at the top of any column. The Project will be created with that column's Label automatically applied.FAQsCan I rename or change the board Labels?You can rename, reorder, and customize Labels to match your workflow. To rename or change the color of a Label: Log in to CompanyCam > click Resources in the left-hand navigation > select Labels.Locate the Label you want to edit.To update the title, hover over the Label and click the Pencil icon.To update the color, click the Color Box and select a new color. What happens if a Project has more than one Label?The Project appears in the column for the most recently applied label. Can I sort or reorder Projects within a column?Projects within a column are ordered by last updated by default. Custom ordering within a column isn't supported yet.How do Project Boards relate to the Customers directory?Project Boards show the where — where each project sits in your workflow. Customers show the who — the relationship and contact info behind each job. The two work together: a project can have both a customer tied to it and a board label tracking its stage. Who can see what on a Board?Permission roles are respected across boards. You only see Projects you already have access to. Admins and Managers can edit Labels and Board structure. Standard users can move projects between columns. Restricted users can view the board and its projects, but can't edit labels or board settings. 💡 Need more help?Contact our Support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse.Related ArticlesHow to Use the Project MapUsing Project LabelsIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetUsing the CompanyCam + Jobber IntegrationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create a Report | Help Center
content: Create a Report | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a ReportNeed to send photos and information in a way that tells a story and communicates your work? Reports are a great way to do that! Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:Creating Reports is available to all permission roles.Restricted Users can only create and view Reports in Projects that they created or are assigned to. Platform Availability:Users can create Reports in both the web and mobile apps.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Find the Project you want to build a Report for.Select photos by hovering over each photo thumbnail and clicking the Square Icon (upper left corner of each photo) to select individual photos or select photos by the date header. Choose Reports from the menu that appears. Select Create New Report. Add a title to your Report, select the page layout (this can be changed later), and click Create Report. You'll be directed to Section 1 of your Report. You can rename the section by deleting the Section 1 text and typing in your own title. Add any information, descriptions, or info in the section summary. Text Snippets are available by clicking the Text Snippet icon below the text box. Click Photo Options to the right of the Section title to load photo descriptions and load photo comments for the photos in this section, rearrange photos, move multiple photos, or delete Section. To load a single photo description or delete an entry, click the Three Dots Icon in the upper right corner of the photo. You can also add Text Snippets or add text of your choice in the space provided. To add a new Section, click + New Section in the upper left of your Report page. Your new Section will have the option to add photos, rename the Section, and add a Section Summary. Edit Report SettingsTo customize your Report settings, click on Manage located in the top right of the Report, and then click Settings. You can edit your Report Title or add a Subtitle, add Page Header information, change your Page Layout, and toggle Photo Details on and off. Note: Page Footers are not customizable at this time. To edit your Cover Page, click on Cover Page in the upper left of your screen above your Report Sections. Here, you can toggle on/off your Cover Photo, Company Logo, and Cover Page details you wouldn't like displayed. Share your ReportsTo share your Report, click on Manage located in the top right of the Report, and then select Share Report from the drop-down menu. This will generate a link that can be shared via email, or click Copy Link to share your Report another way. Note: Shared Report Links will expire after 30 Days. If the recipient hasn't downloaded the Report within that timeframe a new link will need to be generated and shared. To download your Report, click on Generate PDF located in the upper right of your screen. The Download PDF icon should appear in the upper right corner of the generated Report. OR To download your Report, click on Manage located in the top right of the Report, select Share Report then select View Report from the window. Download PDF should appear in the upper right corner of the generated report. You can also share your report from this page by clicking Share Report. Adding Photos from Multiple ProjectsOpen the Report you'd like to add Photos to.Select the + Icon to add Photos to the Report. Select the Projects Filtering Icon and select the Projects you'd like to add photos from. Select the photos you'd like to add to the Report, and then click Add Photos to the Report.In the appOpen a Project and tap on the Documents Section.Once in the Documents Section, tap on the + Icon at the bottom of the page. From the menu, tap Report. Choose + New Report.Add a title to your Report, select the page layout (this can be changed later), and tap Create Report. Tap Create First Section to create a Section of the Report. After you've created the Section you can edit the Section name by tapping the Pencil Icon. From here you can add a Section Summary and use Text Snippets. Tap Add or Take Photos to load photos into the Section. You can add photos from your Camera or from the Project.Tap the Circle on the photo to select it individually or tap the date to select from a date. After you have your photos chosen tap Add Photos. **Note: If you select by date and have not scrolled down to load all the photos for the date you will see a message asking you to scroll down.** After your photos have loaded in you can use the + Icon to add more photos in a given spot. You can also add a description for the photos by tapping into the Add a description area. Use the back arrow to view the Sections or add a new one. Repeat the steps to create new Sections for more photos.To add content or change the Section in bulk tap the Three Dot Icon to the right of the Section title. This will bring up the Options menu for the Section. In Options, you're able to:Load All Photo DescriptionsLoad All Photo CommentsReorder ItemsMove Items (to a different Section)Remove ItemsDelete Section Repeat steps to make changes in all your Sections. Edit Report SettingsIf you need to edit other parts of the Report use the back arrow to back out to the Report outline.Tap the Three Dots Icon to the right of the Report name to bring up Report Options. You'll have options to Reorder Sections, Share Report, and Delete Report. Tap Report Settings to change the Title Information, Page Layout, and Photo Details. Tap Done to Save. Tap Cover Page to edit the Cover Page of the Report. You're able to change the Cover Photo of the Report as well as toggle on/off Cover Page details. Tap Done to save.Share ReportsFrom within the Report Outline tap on the Three Dots Icon to the right of your Report name. Tap Share Report from the options and choose how you want to share the Report from your device's options.Adding Photos from Multiple ProjectsOpen the Report you'd like to add Photos to.Within a Report Section, select the + Icon to add Photos to the Report.Select Add Photos from Project. Tap on the Filters tool and select the Projects you'd like to select photos from, then Save. Select the photos you'd like to add to the report, then Add Photos.FAQsWhere can I find my existing Reports?In the web app, you can find your Reports within the Reports Tab in the left-hand navigation menu or by opening a Project and clicking into the Reports Tab.In the mobile app, you can find your Reports by opening a Project, tapping on the Documents Section, and selecting the Reports Tab. I accidentally deleted some text that was added to my Report. Can I recover it? If you accidentally deleted some text within a report, there isn't a way to recover that deleted information. Text fields within a Report will auto-save after you've made changes. If I update my company's logo, will the logo backfill onto old Reports? No. If you update your company's logo, the logo will not backfill for previously created content. After updating your logo, the change will reflect on all newly created content moving forward. Can I add a video to a Report? No. At this time, videos cannot be added to a Report, only photos. Can I save frequently used text so I can quickly insert it into my Reports? Yes! Our Text Snippets feature allows you to save your commonly used statements so that they can be quickly added to your Report without needing to type it out manually. Can Restricted Users create and view Reports? Yes. Restricted Users can create new Reports within a Project, but they can only view the Reports they’ve created within Projects they are assigned to. Do shared Report links expire? Yes. Shared Report links expire after 30 days. If the recipient hasn’t downloaded the Report within that timeframe, a new link will need to be generated and shared.Related ArticlesCreate a Report with TagsCreate a Before & After PhotoCreating a ChecklistHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create a Report with Tags | Help Center
content: Create a Report with Tags | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a Report with TagsAdd more customization to your Report-Tags are a great way to show customers how you have been organizing information throughout a Project! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:Creating Reports and adding Tags to photos is available to all permission levels.Restricted Users can create a new Report within a Project, and can only see Reports they created within the assigned Project.Platform Availability: Creating Reports and adding Tags to photos is only available on the web app.On the webSign in to CompanyCam.Open the Project you'd like to create a Report for.Select one photo that you're going to use for the Report. Select the Reports option at the top of the page and choose Create New Report. Make sure to add a title for the Report so you can find it easily later. Go back to the Project and select the photo(s) (click on the Square Icon in the upper left corner of the photo) that you want to use for the first group of photos in your Report.Once the photos have been selected, click Actions at the top, and then click Tag. Once selected, add the Tags or create a new Tag you'd like to add to the photos.Once added, click on the Tags filter tool within the Project, then search or select the Tag(s) you'd like to filter by. Select all photos by clicking the Square Icon by the day if all photos were taken on the same day. If they weren't, you can select the first photo, hold down shift, and select the last photo and all the photos will be selected. Once the photos are selected, click Reports at the top of your screen > Add to Existing Report > find the Report that you just created. Those photos have now been added to the end of the Report. Reorder the photos in the Report if needed. Repeat the process for the next group of photos until your Report is complete! Looking for a faster way to add text to your reports? Check out how to create text snippets to speed up your Report-making process! Related ArticlesUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosUsing Tags in CompanyCamOrganize Photos Using TagsCreate a ReportHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Install a Showcase Gallery on your Webpage | Help Center
content: Install a Showcase Gallery on your Webpage | Help CenterSkip to main contentInstall a Showcase Gallery on your WebpageUse the photos you take for your Projects to display a job from start to finish so prospective clients can see how you work. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesTo access the Showcase Gallery feature, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have this permission, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission. Platform AvailabilityAccess to the Showcase Gallery code is available only on the web app.Please NoteShowcased Projects are the Projects that will be displayed on your Portfolio Site, Showcase Gallery, and Showcase Map. If you embed the Showcase Gallery on your website and also have a Portfolio Site set up, each Showcase you publish will appear on both.On the webInstall the Showcase Gallery​*You must create at least 1 Showcase to install the Showcase Gallery on your website. You only have to publish the Showcase Gallery on your website once for it to display your active Showcases*Click on the Showcases Section within the left-hand navigation menu. From the Showcase page, click <> Add Gallery to Website.​ ​Note: Keep this screen up for reference and open your website in another window or tab.​Next, click on the Install It Yourself option. Optional: If you need help installing the Website Gallery on your webpage, choose the Schedule Free Installation option. We'll get you connected with our third-party installation partner, Virtual Vision. Select your Grid Layout and copy the Code Snippet using either the Copy to Clipboard button or by highlighting the snippet and manually copying it to the clipboard. Please keep in mind that the code in the screenshot below is an example code. You'll need to copy your unique code within your account.Paste the Code Snippet into the body of your website within your website builder. FAQsHow can I install the Showcase Gallery using WordPress or Squarespace? You can install the Showcase Gallery on both of these platforms. Here's how:Add Code using WordPressAdd Code using Squarespace Will I need to install a Showcase Gallery for each Project I'd like to display on my website? No, the Showcase Gallery only needs to be installed once. After installation, you can publish additional projects by following the instructions laid out in Step 1 of this article. Can I add or delete Project Type labels when creating a Showcase? When adding a Project Type label, you can choose from the list of pre-existing Project Types or add your own. Once a Project Type has been added to the list, it will be there for all future Showcases and cannot be deleted from the list. Can I add or delete Materials Used labels when creating a Showcase? You can add Materials Used labels when creating a Showcase. Once the Materials Used label has been added to your list, it will be there for all future Showcases and cannot be deleted from the list. Can I hide Showcases from being displayed on my Showcase Gallery? To hide a Showcase, you can unpublish it. To do this, open the Showcases Tab > disable the Published Toggle. Can I add this custom code to any website? Depending on your website provider, you may need to upgrade to a higher plan to add custom code. Related ArticlesHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusInstall a Website Map on your WebpageCreate and Use Showcases for your Portfolio SiteUsing Project BoardsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Install a Website Map on your Webpage | Help Center
content: Install a Website Map on your Webpage | Help CenterSkip to main contentInstall a Website Map on your WebpageAdd a Map of recent work to your webpage and highlight your activity in the local area for potential clients. April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:To access the Showcase Map feature, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Installing a Website Map on your Webpage is only available on the web app.Please NoteShowcased Projects are the Projects that will be displayed on your Portfolio Site, Showcase Gallery, and Showcase Map. Depending on your website provider, you may need to upgrade to a higher plan to add custom code.On the webCustomize and embed your Showcase MapLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Showcases Section within the left-hand navigation menu. Click < > Add Map to Website to access the code to embed on your website. Next, click on the Install It Yourself option. Optional: If you need help installing the Website Map on your webpage, choose the Schedule Free Installation option. We'll get you connected with our third-party installation partner, Virtual Vision. Optional: Select your Pin Color. Copy the Code Snippet (this is an example code, go to your account for your unique code) using either the Copy to Clipboard button or by highlighting the snippet and manually copying the snippet to the clipboard. Paste the snippet into the body of your website. Create your Portfolio SiteLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Showcases Section in the menu on the left side of the screen. Click Publish Portfolio Site to set up your Portfolio. Use the Google Business Search tool to find your business, then click Continue. Add your company's information and click Continue. Add your Service Areas and the Industries you serve, then click Create. Once created, you can click on Portfolio Site at the top of your Portfolio page to see it live. ​ Website Map FAQsCan a Showcase Map be installed on multiple webpages, and can I create more than one? Yes, you can install a Showcase Map on multiple webpages, however at this time a CompanyCam account can only have one Showcase Map. Does the Showcase Map work with GoDaddy/Google Sites/Wix? Yes! Wix, GoDaddy, and Google Sites support adding widgets like this. For example, Wix has documentation on how to add these in, as do GoDaddy and Google Sites. Is there a fast way to add all of my Projects to the Showcase Map instead of having to create a Showcase for each one? No. At this time, you will need to create a Showcase for each Project you want to display on the Showcase Map. Why are some of my Showcases not appearing on the Showcase Map? The Project that is used to build a Showcase must have GPS coordinates attached. During Project creation or editing, searching for an address or clicking on the map will satisfy that requirement and successfully plot any corresponding Showcase on the Map. Related ArticlesInstall a Showcase Gallery on your WebpageIntegrate CompanyCam + Project Map ItHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionCreate and Use Showcases for your Portfolio SiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Download a Project QR Code | Help Center
content: How to Download a Project QR Code | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Download a Project QR CodeDownload a QR code for a Project so that when scanned, your Project will immediately open within the CompanyCam mobile app! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesAll permission roles can download a Project QR Code.Platform AvailabilityGenerating and downloading a Project QR Code is only available on the web app.On the webLog into CompanyCam.Open the Project you'd like to download a QR Code for.Click on the Three Dots Icon in the top right-hand corner of the Project and select Download QR Code. The Project QR Code will immediately download from your web browser onto your device for you to share with your team.FAQsWhy can’t I open a Project QR Code someone shared with me?Users must be on the same CompanyCam account to scan and open Project QR Codes.How should I scan a Project QR Code?QR Codes must be scanned using the device's native camera, not the camera tool within the CompanyCam mobile app. Related ArticlesHow to Use the Project MapUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamHow to Export Project and Checklist DataGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamYour photos live on the cloud in CompanyCam, but if you need to save them else where you can always download the photos to save them! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: Downloading photos from CompanyCam is an account-level feature and is available to all permission levels. Platform Availability: Downloading photos can be done on both the web and mobile apps. If you're downloading photos on the web, all multi-photo downloads will be packaged in a zipped file on your device.​On the webDownload all photos in the Project:Sign in to CompanyCam.Open the Project you want to download photos from.Click on the Three Dots in the top right of the project and choose Download All Photos from the drop-down menu. Enter the email address you wish to send the downloadable files to.Choose what kind of files you want if they have been edited. Note: If photos aren't edited, you won't see this option.Original will be the photos of how they were originally taken or uploaded into CompanyCam.Modified will download the photos with any photo edits/markups. Your download will go to the email you entered. Also, you can click the blue Download Photos in the browser.Save multiple photos:Select the first photo by clicking on the Square Icon on the photo.Click on each additional photo's square icon you would like to save. Or you can hold the Shift Key and select the checkmark on the last photo to select all photos in between. Click Actions from within the menu at the top of the page. Then click Download. Enter the email address you wish to send the downloadable files to.Choose the type of files you want if they have been edited. Note: If photos aren't edited, you won't see this option.Original will be the photos of how they were originally taken or uploaded into CompanyCam.Modified will download the photos with any photo edits/markups. Your download will go to the email you entered. Also, you can click the blue Download Photos in the browser. Save photos from one day:Click the Square Icon next to the date to select the photos. Click the Actions Button within the menu at the top of the page. Click Download. Enter the email address you wish to send the downloadable files to.Choose what kind of files you want if they have been edited. *If photos aren't edited, you won't see this option.*Original will be the photos of how they were originally taken or uploaded into CompanyCam.Modified will download the photos with any photo edits/markups. Your download will go to the email you entered. Also, you can click the blue Download Photos in the browser. Save a single photo:Click the Three Dots Icon in the upper right corner of the photo thumbnail.Click Download.​ Choose what kind of files you want if they have been edited. Note: If photos aren't edited, you won't see this option.Original will be the photos of how they were originally taken or uploaded into CompanyCam.Modified will download the photos with any photo edits/markups. The photo will download in your browser window, and you can go to your computer's Downloads folder to access them.In the appAuto-save all photos taken in-app to your device*Photos will auto-save to your device when taken in the app when this is turned on. *This feature is user-specific. Individual users will need to turn this on or off within their own accounts. Tap on your User Icon at the top of the page.Next, tap the Settings Button.Once within settings, tap on the Camera option. Scroll down to Save Images to Device and tap the toggle on the right to On/Off to enable or disable (if the toggle is blue it's On).Select photos to save to your deviceOpen a Project and scroll down to the Photos Section.Tap the Select Icon within the Photos section.Tap the photos you want to download to select them. A blue circle with a checkmark should appear in the upper left corner of the photo thumbnail to show they're selected. After you've chosen your photos, tap on the Three Dots Icon at the bottom of the screen.Next, choose Save to Device to save the photos to your camera roll.Other optionsLooking for a shorter way to rename your photos in CompanyCam? Learn how you can use tags to rename downloaded photos!Need hard copies of your photos? Check out our help guide on how to print photos from the CompanyCam website!Related ArticlesUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Share Photos in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Share Photos in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamNeed to send photos to a customer? We have a few ways for you to share photos, depending on what details you're looking to share! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles: All User Permission Roles can share photos.Platform Availability:Photos can be shared from both the web and mobile apps.On the webShare a GalleryThis link allows anyone to view or download only the photos you selected to share. They will have the option to download all the photos at once. Log in to CompanyCam.Hover over each photo thumbnail you want to include in the Gallery and click the Square Icon (upper left corner of each photo) to select the photos.Click Share from within the menu at the top of the page.Choose Share Gallery of Selected Photos. Decide if you'd like to share photo details and/or descriptions with the Gallery. Click Get Link. Click Copy Link and then paste the link into an email, text message, etc.Share all photos from a ProjectOpen a Project.Click the Share in the upper right corner > Share All Images. Decide if you'd like to share photo details and/or descriptions with the Gallery. Click Get Link. Click Copy Link or use the quick share buttons for Facebook or Email. Share a Project TimelineA Project Timeline is a LIVE link! Share this link with a customer and they will be able to see each new photo as it is taken in the Project.Click on a Project that you'd like to share.Click Share > Share Timeline Link. Decide if you'd like to include Photo Descriptions within the Timeline Link. Click Copy Link and paste it into an email, text message, etc. This link allows your customer to see every photo from this Project in real time. Hide Photos from a TimelineSelect the photo(s) you'd like to exclude from your Project Timeline.Click Actions in the banner that appears at the top of the screen. Click Hide in Project Timeline. You will now see the Hidden Icon at the top right of each hidden photo. Disable a Timeline LinkClick on a Project that has a Timeline link that you'd like to disable.Click Share > Disable Timeline Link. No further action is needed. The link will no longer allow access to the photos. In the appShare a single photoOpen any photo and tap the Three Dots Icon in the lower right. Choose Share. Select a method to share the photo (text, email, etc.).Share a GalleryOpen a Project and scroll down to the Photos Section.Tap the Select Icon. Choose the Photos you'd like to share and then tap on the Three Dots Icon at the bottom of the page.From the slide-up menu, choose Share Gallery.Decide if you'd like to share photo details and/or descriptions with the Gallery, then tap Generate Link. Choose an app to share with when the dialog box opens or tap Copy, then open a text message or email, and Paste the link to send!Share a Project Timeline A Project Timeline is a LIVE link! Share this link with a customer, and they will be able to see each new photo as it is taken in the project.Open a Project.Tap on the Share Icon.Select Share Project Timeline. Choose an app to share with when the dialog box opens, or tap Copy, then open a text message or email and Paste the link to send!Hide Photos from your TimelineOpen a Project and scroll down to the Photos Section.Tap the Select Photos Icon. Choose the Photos you'd like to hide and then tap on the Three Dots Icon at the bottom of the page.From the slide-up menu, choose Hide in Project Timeline.You will now see the Hidden Icon at the bottom right of each hidden photo.Other ways to shareCreate and share a ReportA Report allows you to create a more formal way of sharing photos and has the added ability to include Photo Comments, Photo Descriptions, or other information you'd like to relay to your customer. Here's how you can Create a Report. Publish a Portfolio SiteThe Portfolio Site is a public-facing website where you can display your Project Showcases. The Portfolio Site acts as a curated online portfolio for your business to share your work with potential clients and visitors. Here's how you can Publish a Portfolio Site. Need to disable a Gallery Link?Gallery Links can not be disabled on the User's end. If you were looking to deactivate your Gallery Link, please reach out to our Support team. Constantly sharing photos with a contractor or other collaborator? Get them added to your team by inviting them to your CompanyCam account! Here's how to invite new users!Related ArticlesCreate a Before & After PhotoHow to Delete Photos and VideosUpload Existing PhotosCreate a ReportGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Print Photos in the Web App | Help Center
content: Print Photos in the Web App | Help CenterSkip to main contentPrint Photos in the Web AppNeed to print photos off the web? Here's how: April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All permission roles will have access to Print Photos.Platform Availability:Printing is only available on the web app. Please NoteWhen you are selecting photos, be sure not to select any video files. If a video is selected, the print option won't appear.On the webLog in to CompanyCam. Open the Project you'd like to print photos from.Select the photos you want to print by clicking the Square Icon in the top left corner of the photo. Click Actions in the menu at the top of your screen and then Print. Choose your desired photo layout option and decide if you want to include photo details. Photo details include:the place where the photo was takenthe name of the User who took the photothe time and date the photo was taken Click Print Photos.Related ArticlesSave Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera RollUsing Tags to Rename Downloaded PhotosUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Request Reviews in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Request Reviews in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Request Reviews in CompanyCamBoost your online reputation! Select photos to share with your customers and prompt them to leave your business a Google Review. April 13, 2026Table of contents Before you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options. Permission Roles: To connect your CompanyCam account to your Google Business Profile, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Requesting Reviews is an account-level feature and is available to all permission levels.Platform Availability: Connecting your CompanyCam account to your Google Business Profile must be done through the web app.Once set up, Requests for Reviews can be made on both the web and mobile app.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Open the Project that you would like to request a Review for.In the upper right-hand corner, click Request Review. Optional: Click into the Text Box to edit the prefilled message and/or add photos from your Project to the request by clicking + Photos. Select Copy Review Link to copy the unique URL to share with your customer. In the appOpen a Project and tap on the Menu Button.Tap Request Review.Optional: Click into the Text Box to edit the prefilled message and/or add photos from your Project to the request by clicking + Add.Select Scan QR to share with your client in person, OR select Send Link and share the link via text message or email.Customer PreviewCustomers who access your Review Link or QR code will have the option to download and save any photos you attach. They can then select "Review Us on Google" to be directed to leave a review. ** Photos added to the Review Request will not automatically attach to the Google Review. If the customer would like to include the photos in their Review, they must download and attach the photos to the Review. ** Connecting to your Google Business ProfileLog in to CompanyCam.From the left-hand navigation bar, click Reviews. Click + Connect Google Business. Use the Search Bar to search for your business and click on your business name. Verify your company information and click Connect. Note: This may prompt you to sign in to Google and verify your Google Business account. Want to share your Reviews with your Customers? Reviews submitted through CompanyCam will automatically show up on your CompanyCam Portfolio, an easy-to-share site where you can show off your best work. Check out how to set one up.​​FAQCan Project Collaborators send Review Requests?No, Project Collaborators can not send a Review Request. They must be sent from an established User on the company's account. Can I set up Review Requests without having a Google Business account?To request Reviews in CompanyCam, you must have a Google Business profile. Will photos that are added to the Review Request be automatically attached to the Google Review?Photos added to the Review Request will not automatically attach to the Google Review. If the customer would like to include the photos in their Review, they must download and attach the photos to the Review. Can I use completed Reviews on my Portfolio site?Completed reviews will appear on your Portfolio site, if you have it set up. Am I able to confirm reviews before they are posted to Google?All customer reviews submitted through CompanyCam, whether positive or negative, will be posted to Google. This ensures transparency and authenticity in the feedback shared by your customers. Related ArticlesHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamIntegrate CompanyCam + RealWorkGetting Started with CompanyCamResponding to Reviews in Marketing SuiteManaging your Google Business Profile in Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Use Showcases for your Portfolio Site | Help Center
content: Create and Use Showcases for your Portfolio Site | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Use Showcases for your Portfolio SiteThe Portfolio Site is an easy to share site where you can show off your best work. No code or design skills are required! Updated over a week agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission Roles: To access the Showcase and Portfolio features, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Creating and using Showcases for your Portfolio site is available on both web and mobile apps.​Please NoteShowcased Projects are the Projects that will be displayed on your Portfolio Site, Showcase Gallery, and Showcase Map. If you embed the Showcase Gallery on your website and also have a Portfolio Site set up, each Showcase you publish will appear on both.On the webSet up your Portfolio SiteIn the left-hand navigation menu under Marketing, click on the Showcases option. From the Showcase screen, select Create Portfolio. Use the Google Business Search tool to find your business and click Continue. Add your company's information and click Continue. Add your Service Areas and the Industries you serve, then click Create. After creating your Portfolio Site, it will appear at the top of your Portfolio page, where you can view it or copy its link for sharing. Create a ShowcaseLog in to CompanyCam.Select any Project you want to work on and click Showcase This Project in the upper right corner.Once in the Showcase Editor, you must select or populate:Showcase TitleThumbnail PhotoProject Type (type in own or select from the list)Choose photos to includeProducts Used (type in your own)Showcase Description (optional) To add the photos you'd like displayed, click on Select Photos. Click on the Square Icon in the upper left corner of the photos you'd like displayed, or select the Square Icon next to the date to select all photos taken on that day. Once selected, click Confirm. Once all required fields have been filled, click Create. Bulk create ShowcasesClick on the Showcases Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the + Create Showcases option. Choose the Projects you'd like to create Showcases for, then click Add to Portfolio. This action will automatically save your Showcases as Drafts. Before you can publish your Showcases, you'll need to add your Project Type by clicking Select Project Types. Once you've chosen your Project Types, choose which photos appear within the Showcase. To do this, you'll click on the Pencil Icon next to your Showcase and open the editor. In the editor, click Select Photos to decide which photos will appear in the Showcase.You can also update the:Showcase TitleThumbnail PhotoProject Type (type in own or select from the list)Choose photos to includeProducts Used (type in your own)Showcase Description (optional) Once you're done editing the Showcase, click Update. Finally, to publish your Showcase, click on the Publish Toggle. Edit and Manage your ShowcasesTo manage your Showcases, click on the Showcases Tab in your left-hand navigation menu. To enable or disable a Showcase from your website or Portfolio Site, click on the Enable Toggle. To edit or manage an existing Showcase, click on the Pencil Icon to open the editor. Once you've finished editing your Showcase, click Update. In the appSet up your Portfolio SiteOpen the mobile app and tap on the Portfolio Tab.Tap Complete your profile.Use the Google Business Search tool to find your business.Add your company's information, then tap Next.Add your Industry and Service Areas, then tap Done. Create a ShowcaseOpen a Project and tap on the Menu at the top of the page.Choose Showcase this Project.Choose the photos you'd like to include within the Showcase, then tap Next. Next, you'll be brought to the details screen. Here you can change the Cover Photo, add a Title, Project Type, Products Used (optional), and a Description (optional).Once you've finished editing your Showcase, tap Publish. Edit a ShowcaseOpen your Project and tap on the Menu at the top of the page.Within the Menu, choose Edit Showcase.One inside the editor makes any changes you need, then tap Update.FAQsCan I add or delete Project Type labels when creating a Showcase? When adding a Project Type label, you can choose from the list of pre-existing Project Types or add your own. Once a Project Type has been added to the list, it will be there for all future Showcases and cannot be deleted from the list. Can I add or delete Materials Used labels when creating a Showcase? You can add Materials Used labels when creating a Showcase. Once the Materials Used label has been added to your list, it will be there for all future Showcases and cannot be deleted from the list. Why aren't newly uploaded photos displaying within my Showcase? When you upload new photos to a Project, they will be hidden from the Showcase. To edit which photos are displayed, Open the Showcases Tab > Find your Showcase > Edit (Pencil Icon) > click Select Photos > Choose the photos you want to include > click Update. Can I hide Showcases from displaying on my Showcase Gallery or Portfolio Site? To hide a Showcase, you can unpublish it. To do this, open the Showcases Tab > disable the Published Toggle. How can I see all of my published Showcases? To view all of your Showcases, click on the Showcases Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. Here you will see all of your Published and Draft Showcase. Are Showcases impacted or indexed by SEO? No, Showcases don't directly affect SEO, there are many factors at play on each individual website. A recommendation from Google for ensuring JS-injected content can be indexed: To make sure that Google can still see your content after it’s rendered, use the Mobile-Friendly Test or the URL Inspection Tool and look at the rendered HTML. Q: How is the Showcase date determined on the Portfolio Site, Showcase Gallery, and Showcase Map?The Showcase date is based on when the Project was completed. This is approximated by the month when the last photo or video was added to the Project. If you have any additional questions, feel free to reach out to CompanyCam Support at [email protected]!Related ArticlesHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamInstall a Showcase Gallery on your WebpageHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamShowcasing a Project in Marketing SuiteCreating Posts for Socials using Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Check App Version and Update | Help Center
content: How to Check App Version and Update | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Check App Version and UpdateNot sure if the app version of CompanyCam is up to date? Here's how to check that! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you start Plan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: All User Permission Roles can check their app version and update if needed. Platform Availability: Desktop (web app) Requirements: Internet Explorer, Windows 7, and Windows 8 aren't compatible.Mobile Device Requirements: Windows and BlackBerry devices aren't compatible. Supported iOS: 16 and up.Supported Android OS: 7.0 and up.​In the appCheck the app versionTap your User Icon at the top of the home screen.Tap the Settings Icon to access your App Settings.Scroll to the bottom of the page, and the App Version is listed below the red Sign Out button.Update AppIf you have automatic updates turned on for the iOS App Store or Google Play Store, you'll always have the most current version of the app. However, if you prefer not to have this setting turned on, you'll need to check for updates manually and update to keep CompanyCam running as smoothly as possible. iOSOpen the App Store on your mobile device.Tap on your User Icon at the top of the screen.Scroll down to the Updates tab at the bottom of the screen.Check to see if there is one available for CompanyCam.Choose Update.AndroidOpen the Google Play Store on your mobile device.At the top right corner of the store, tap on your User Icon.Tap Manage apps & device. Check to see if there is an update available for CompanyCam.Tap Update. Related ArticlesOpen to the Camera ViewSave Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera RollHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Contact Support | Help Center
content: How to Contact Support | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Contact Support March 20, 2026Table of contentsLooking for a phone number?CompanyCam Support doesn't have a phone number. We're the quickest and most efficient on email and chat. We answer emails within an hour and chats within three minutes during business hours. Give us a try! It's important to us that your questions are answered! How to email SupportEmail us anytime at [email protected]! Our live chat support is available at these times:Monday8:00 am - 5:00 pm CSTTuesday8:00 am - 5:00 pm CSTWednesday8:00 am - 5:00 pm CSTThursday8:00 am - 5:00 pm CSTFriday8:00 am - 4:00 pm CST*Hours and chat availability are subject to change.* On the webHow to Chat with Support Log in to CompanyCam from a web browser.Click Chat with Support at the bottom of the left-hand toolbar. From here, click Send us a message. Access the Help CenterLog in to CompanyCam from a web browser.Click on Chat with Support at the bottom of the left-hand toolbar. From here, click Search for help. You can select a Collection to view the articles for that Collection or use the search bar at the top of the window to look for what you need! ​ In the appHow to Chat with SupportOpen your mobile app and log in.Tap your User Icon at the top left of the home screen. Tap Chat with Support.From here, tap Ask a question.Access the Help CenterOpen your mobile app and log in.Tap your User Icon at the top left of the home screen. Tap Chat with Support. Tap Help. Once inside the Help Center, choose a Collection to view some articles. You can also use the search tool to look for what you need. How to text SupportOur Support Team does not have a phone number to call; however, you can reach us by text! WhatsAppIn WhatsApp, you can send us a message at +1-402-310-5953. SMSYou can send us an SMS message by texting us at +1-866-609-9728. Please keep in mind that only those located in the US can reach us by SMS. Message and data rates may apply. You can also find helpful articles with screenshots at http://help.companycam.com/!Related ArticlesHow to Check App Version and UpdateIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupHow to Request Reviews in CompanyCamHow to Use TranslationsGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Photos and Videos Are Not Uploading on the Mobile App | Help Center
content: Photos and Videos Are Not Uploading on the Mobile App | Help CenterSkip to main contentPhotos and Videos Are Not Uploading on the Mobile AppNo one likes upload issues. But, it's easy to get back on track with these simple steps. April 27, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startIf you have photos or videos that are waiting to be uploaded, do not delete the app. They will be lost if they haven't been saved to your device, as they haven't had a chance to sync to the cloud yet.Tap the Upload Status Queue on the app's home screen and save all photos to your device before deleting the app.Troubleshooting stepsBe sure to keep the mobile app open when uploading photos or videos. As a rule of thumb, some devices can set a limit on the amount of data an app can use when placed in the background. If you are backgrounding the mobile app, it's important to make sure both the mobile app and the device have the necessary permissions.​App Settings:​My Stuff > Uploads > Enable Allow background uploads.​Device Settings:​iOS: Settings > Apps > CompanyCam > Enable Background App Refresh.​Android (Samsung devices): Settings > Battery and Device Care > Battery> Background Usage Limits > Never Sleeping Apps > Add CompanyCam to the list.​Other ​Android devices: Settings > Apps > See All Apps> CompanyCam > App battery usage > Allow background usage > Under Allow background usage, tap Unrestricted.​Try turning your device's Airplane Mode off and on a few times - give a moment each time you turn the mode on and off.If you're using Low Data Mode on your device, try turning this setting off.​iOS: Settings > Cellular > Cellular Data Options > Data Mode.Hit Retry All by tapping the upload status queue > tap the Three Dots in the upper right corner > Retry All (leave the app open while they retry and upload).If this does not work, tap the upload queue > tap the Three Dots in the upper right corner > select Save All.Log out and log back into the app.If that doesn't work, please hard close the app and restart it.If that doesn't work, restart your mobile device.The final thing is to make sure you can find the photos or videos that you've saved to your device. It's now safe to delete the app and reinstall it.Related ArticlesSave Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera RollRecord and Upload VideoUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Best Practices when Using AI Tools in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: Best Practices when Using AI Tools in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentBest Practices when Using AI Tools in CompanyCamHelpful Tips and Tricks for using AI in CompanyCam April 10, 2026Table of contentsHow it worksCompanyCam's AI system operates by taking transcripts or Photo Descriptions from User inputs and then submits them to specialized prompts to generate Outputs. Each AI Action has its own prompt designed to produce outputs based on its own feature. The app processes your photo descriptions and voice inputs to create Daily Logs, Summaries, Checklists, and other documentation. General best practicesVoice InputThe more context and detail you provide within your photo descriptions, the better your results will be. When an AI Feature uses voice input, it allows you to provide extensive context naturally. You can "talk things out" to include all relevant details. When you're using these tools, it's best to be as specific as possible rather than using vague terms. Examples"The living room needs X, Y, Z repairs"."I'm completing work in the living room right now. Next, we're going to work on the dining room". Include directional details: "On the north side of the house".Audio Quality Try to speak as clearly as possible when using AI Tools. A good rule of thumb is to pretend as if you're talking to someone on the phone. You want to minimize background noise as much as possible, avoid muffled speech, and avoid speaking too quickly. It's also helpful to take a moment to gather your thoughts before recording. Best practices by featureWalkthroughThe Walkthrough is the most flexible AI option available. It can create a summary and outline documentation from voice input and photos. With this tool, we generally recommend speaking first, then capturing a photo, and repeating the process. This helps to lay a good foundation for the AI to use when generating your Page. When using this tool, be sure to provide as much context as possible. For example: "I'm in a backyard, and am going to document all the things that need fixing". By providing context, you help to structure the document and create logical sections with appropriate titles. When you're moving between areas or rooms, be explicit about your transitions and organization needs. Checklist AI ToolsThe AI features within Checklists can create and manage Checklists through voice commands. Voice CommandsWhen using these tools, it's helpful to:Use phrases like "mark as done", "rank this as", "fill this out as", etc.Repeat the exact language that the app prompts with for easier matching.Take photos when using the Voice-to-Complete tool to enhance your Checklist progress.Example Voice Commands for Checklist creation"We need a chore to clean the kitchen"."Organize all of the cleaning fields into a section called 'Cleaning Chores'"."Create a new section for the Checklist titled pre-work site inspection. Create a task to inspect the work area for existing damage. Ask a yes/no question: Is there any existing damage?".Example Voice Commands for Checklist CompletionThe chore to clean the kitchen is complete.I inspected the work area for damage, and yes, there is existing damage. There are cracks and scratches. Make a note that it looks like the damage was the result of improper installation".PagesAll Pages features require photos with Photo Descriptions, Comments, and Tags. Adding as much context to your photos will help create the best output. Quick CaptionQuick Caption takes voice input and creates concise, natural-sounding captions. Outputs will be summarized and will not be word-for-word. Important information and limitationsAI AccuracyIt's important to keep in mind that AI is not perfect and can make mistakes. Always review your AI-generated content and edit/correct the output as needed.We're continuously improving how our AI Tools interpret technical language to help improve outputs.Providing comprehensive descriptions and context helps to improve the accuracy of the output you receive.Voice AI Feature RequirementsWhen using a voice-enabled AI Tool, you must:Speak clearly and avoid muffled speech.Minimize background noise and competing voices.Maintain an appropriate speaking pace. Try not to speak too quickly.Be explicit about your intentions. Tell the AI exactly what to do rather than expecting it to infer or connect the dots. Troubleshooting TipsIf you're receiving poor AI output performance, here are some tips and tricks to help increase the performance:Check to make sure you're providing sufficient context/detail for the AI to use.Double-check your audio quality and make sure you're speaking clearly.Make sure you're using clear, specific language.Now that you know more about how our AI Tools work, here's how you can use our AI features for Pages and AI features for Checklists.Related ArticlesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamUsing AI Features for PagesUsing CompanyCam in Spanish: FAQUsing AI Features for ChecklistsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: CompanyCam Mobile App Remodel | Help Center
content: CompanyCam Mobile App Remodel | Help CenterSkip to main contentCompanyCam Mobile App RemodelWe've redesigned our mobile app to help you work faster. Based on your feedback, we've reorganized the app to group related features so that they're easier to discover. April 6, 2026Table of contentsPlease NoteOur redesigned mobile experience is now live for everyone. While things may look a little different, all of your projects, photos, and data are safe and exactly where you left them. What's Changed?The largest change you'll likely notice is that we've redesigned the mobile Home Screen and Project screens. Below are some before-and-after examples for reference:Home Screen:New DesignOld Design Project Screen:New DesignOld Design Features Grouped Together:Home Screen:My Stuff: Allows you to view all your tasks across every Project. Project Screen:To-Dos: The To-Dos Tab groups Checklists and Project Tasks together in one spot.Documents: The Documents Tab groups Pages, Reports, and Files all under one page.Menu: The Project Menu is where you can find important Project Details. This section includes the Project Description, Project Users, Collaborators, Project Contact, and Project Labels.Where can I find my features?PagesPages are now found within the Documents Section at the top of your Project Screen. Here's how it works!ChecklistsChecklists are now found within the To-Dos Section at the top of your Project Screen. Here's how it works!CollaboratorsAdding, managing, and removing Collaborators can now be found by tapping on the Menu Button within the Project Screen. Here's how!LabelsProject Labels can be added and managed in the Project Screen or within the Menu. Here's how!WalkthroughThe Walkthrough feature can now be found in a few places. You can create a Walkthrough within Pages.You can create a Walkthrough from most screens in the mobile app by tapping on the ✨ Sparkle Button.You can create a Walkthrough Note within the app's camera by selecting the Walkthrough mode.My ProfileYour Profile has moved to the top left corner of your home screen.Invite UsersYou can now invite new Users by tapping on the + Button on the Home Screen or through the My Team Section. Here's how!Edit Project DetailsTo edit Project Details (Project Address, Project Description, and Project Contact), you'll open the Menu within the Project Screen. Here's how it works!Chat with SupportTo chat with Support, you can tap on your Profile Icon on the Home Screen and choose 'Chat with Support'. Here's how! FAQsWill I lose any of my work or Projects with the Remodel? Nope! No, you won't lose any of your work. Everything you've created is still here. We've just reorganized how you access it. Can I switch back to the old app version? No, the new mobile app designs will be the experience for all Users going forward. We're confident you'll find it faster and easier to use. If you're having trouble finding something, please let us know! We want to make sure you're set up for success! Related ArticlesSigning in to Multiple Accounts in the Mobile AppIntegrate CompanyCam + Roof.LinkIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupAccess Offline ModeTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents
