title: Change Login Information | Help Center
content: Change Login Information | Help CenterSkip to main contentChange Login InformationEditing your login information, like your email or password, is easy to change in CompanyCam. You can change this on the web and the app! March 27, 2026Table of contentsOn the webLog in to CompanyCam.Click on your User Icon in the top left-hand corner and then click on My Settings from the dropdown menu. In the My Settings section, you can update your email and password by selecting Change Email Address or Password. When updating your email or password, you will need to confirm your current password before saving your changes. Once you've finished making changes, select Save Changes at the top right corner of the screen. In the appTap on your User Icon at the top of the page.Next, tap on Edit Profile.In the Account Info section, tap Edit.Confirm your current password and update your email address and/or password.Tap Save in the upper right corner to save your changes before leaving.*If you don't remember your login or password, click or tap on Forgot My Password on the sign in screen, enter your email address and we'll email you a link to reset your password.*​ Are you needing to change the information on your company account? Go here to find out how to change your Company Information.Related ArticlesEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationEdit or Delete Text SnippetsLogging Out of CompanyCamEdit Company Settings and InformationHow to log in to CompanyCam with SSODid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Deactivate a User | Help Center
content: Deactivate a User | Help CenterSkip to main contentDeactivate a UserIf someone is no longer with your company, you can remove them from your CompanyCam account on the web or in the app. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To deactivate Users, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Managers can deactivate Users with the Manager, Standard, or Restricted role. They cannot deactivate an Admin on the account.There must always be an Admin active on the account. The last or only Admin on the account cannot be deactivated.Platform Availability: You can deactivate Users on both the web and mobile app. ​On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Team Tab from the left-hand toolbar.​ From the list of Users, click on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the User and click Deactivate. In the appTap My Team on the homescreen.Find the User and tap the Three Dots to the right of their name.Tap Deactivate. FAQsDoes Deactivating a User Affect My Billing? Deactivating a User will remove them from your account (they will not be able to log in and view your content), and you will no longer be billed for them. What Happens to a Deactivated User's Content in CompanyCam? When you deactivate a User from CompanyCam, you keep:Photos and videos they addedProjects they createdReports they createdComments on the photos or Projects they made Are you looking to invite a new User to your account? You can learn how to send an invitation here.Related ArticlesView Activity by UserEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationInvite New UsersReactivate a UserAccepting a User InvitationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Edit User's Permissions and Login Information | Help Center
content: Edit User's Permissions and Login Information | Help CenterSkip to main contentEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationEditing someone's permissions or login, like their email or password, is easy to change in CompanyCam. You can change this on the web! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To edit a User's permission role, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission. Managers can update permission roles for other Managers, Standard Users, and Restricted Users, but they cannot assign a role higher than their own.Platform Availability: User permission roles can only be updated on the web app.On the webEdit User's PermissionsLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Team Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. Find a User by typing in their name in the search bar, click on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the User's card you wish to change, and select Edit User.​ Select the User Role that best matches what that person will be doing. Roles are Admin, Manager, Standard, and Restricted. Click Learn More under each role to see more about who that is good for. Scroll to the bottom of the page and click Save Changes.​ OR Click on the dropdown menu in the role column next to a User's name to edit their permissions. Select a new permission role from the dropdown menu.​ Edit a User's login informationFrom the Team Tab, click on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the User's card you wish to change and select Edit User.​ Within the User Settings, click on Change Email Address or Password. Next, you will need to confirm YOUR password before you can update the User's login information. After you've confirmed your password, you can update the User's email address and/or password. Once everything is updated, make sure to click Save Changes at the top of the page.In the appEdit a User's login informationTap My Team on the homescreen.Find a User by typing in their name in the search bar, click on the Three Dots Icon to the right of the User's card you wish to change, and select Edit User.Tap on the Edit Icon to the right of Account Info.Next, you will need to confirm YOUR password before you can update the User's login information.After you've confirmed your password, you can update the User's email address and/or password.Once everything is updated, make sure to click Save at the top of the page.FAQsAre new Users given a default permissions role when they're invited to the account? Yes. When adding a new User to your CompanyCam account, that User, by default, will have the Standard role. What happens when a User's permissions role is changed to Restricted? If Users have contributed to Projects and their permission role is changed to Restricted, the User will need to be assigned to the Projects in order to access them again. This is because Restricted Users are only able to access Projects they're individually assigned to. Here’s how to assign Projects to Users. Now that you've edited the user permissions, you may want to keep track of what they've contributed. Here's how to quickly keep up to date on what each user has been working on. Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserEdit or Delete Text SnippetsChange Login InformationEdit Company Settings and InformationUser Permission Roles ExplainedDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to log in to CompanyCam with SSO | Help Center
content: How to log in to CompanyCam with SSO | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to log in to CompanyCam with SSOMake accessing CompanyCam easier and more secure for your team. Updated this weekTable of contentsBefore you start Plan RequirementsThis feature is available to Enterprise accounts and as an Add-on for all accounts. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesThe Account Owner/Admin will be able to set up SSO (Single Sign On) for the company account. Once set up, SSO is an account-level feature and is available to all permission levels.Platform AvailabilityLogging into CompanyCam with SSO is available on both the web and mobile appsOn the webOpen CompanyCam on a web browser.Click on Single Sign-On. Type in your email address and then click Continue. Once selected, you'll be signed in.​ORLog into your Okta account with your email address and then select Next. Note: Okta is used as an example in this article. Login steps can vary depending on your SSO provider. Type in your password and then click Verify. Once verified, under the My Apps tab, click on CompanyCam or use the search bar at the top of the screen to search your apps for CompanyCam. After selecting, you'll be signed in. In the appOpen the mobile app.Tap on Single Sign-On.Type in your email address and then tap Continue. Once selected, you'll want to confirm logging in by tapping Continue. After confirming, a new mobile browser for Okta will open where you can enter your password.Lastly, enter the code generated by Google Authenticator.Once verified, you'll be signed into CompanyCam!FAQsHow do I use SSO if I have multiple accounts? If you have multiple CompanyCam accounts, you'll want to log in to your SSO account first before signing in to other accounts. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + SuccessWareIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + GroundworkDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Invite New Users | Help Center
content: Invite New Users | Help CenterSkip to main contentInvite New UsersInviting Users to your CompanyCam account allows you to share information, see the content they contribute to the account, and more. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo invite new Users, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityInviting new Users is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Click + Circle Icon at the top of the left sidebar OR click on the Users Tab from the left sidebar.From the Create Button, choose Invite New Users. From the Users Tab, select Invite New Users. Type or paste an email and hit enter. Add 1 or more emails, then hit Review (#) Invites. Select the Permission Role you'd like the User to have from the dropdown menu to the right (Standard is the default role). Once the Permission Roles are selected, click Send (#) Invites.In the appTap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen.Select Add User.Choose Invite from Contacts or Manual.Invite from Contacts: search/tap on any of your contacts from the list. Once you have selected everyone you'd like to invite and their phone number and/or email address, tap the Send Invitation button.Manually Invite User: add the User's information, and they will receive an email/text to complete their account setup.FAQsCan you link two CompanyCam accounts to one email address? No, an email address can only be linked to one CompanyCam account at a time. If you receive an "Email address has already been taken" error when trying to invite a User, that means that the person you are inviting has an existing account, and they will need to reach out to Support to free up their email address before being invited to your account. How does inviting a new user affect my bill?Each user you invite will be added as a billable user on your account. Before sending an invitation, we recommend reviewing your billing page to:Check if you have any empty seats availableSee the cost of adding an additional user What does the User need to do after I invite them? The invited Users will receive a text message and email with a link for them to accept their invitation and complete their account. They will create their own password at that time. Once a User has been added to your CompanyCam account, you can view User's activity by viewing the User's profile. You can also edit User's permission settings at any time to remove or grant access to certain features.Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationHow to log in to CompanyCam with SSOAccepting a User InvitationGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Logging Out of CompanyCam | Help Center
content: Logging Out of CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentLogging Out of CompanyCamIf you need to log out of your CompanyCam account you can do that on both the web and mobile app. Here's how: January 27, 2026Table of contentsOn the webFrom any page in CompanyCam, click your User Icon in the left-hand sidebar. Click Sign Out at the bottom of the drop-down menu.In the appTap your User Icon in the top left corner of the app.Tap Sign Out.Confirm your sign-out on the pop-up by tapping Sign Out again.Are you needing to change your email or password? Here's how to do that!Related ArticlesRefer a Friend to CompanyCamIntegrate CompanyCam + REISIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionHow to log in to CompanyCam with SSODid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Reactivate a User | Help Center
content: Reactivate a User | Help CenterSkip to main contentReactivate a UserIf you have a deactivated User that needs access again, you can quickly give them access, no need to send a new invite! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo reactivate a User, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Managers can reactivate Users with the Manager, Standard, and Restricted roles. They cannot reactivate a deactivated Admin.Platform AvailabilityReactivating a User is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Select the Team Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. Search for the User ID in your User list.Click the Three Dots Icon in the upper right corner of the User's card. Select Reactivate. In the appTap My Team on the homescreen.Tap the Three Dots Icon to the right of the Deactivated User.Select Reactivate.Once you've reactivated them, you may want to double-check their permissions and edit them to ensure they have access to what you need them to have.Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserView Activity by UserEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationInvite New UsersAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: View Activity by User | Help Center
content: View Activity by User | Help CenterSkip to main contentView Activity by UserBecause CompanyCam values truth and transparency, you can see your User's activity and their last upload date. Find out how below! Updated this weekTable of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo view User activity, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityViewing activity by User is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the Team Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. Scroll through your Users to find who you're looking for. You'll see their latest activity on their User Card. If it shows no activity, they have never uploaded an image. Click on the User's Card to see more details on their activity.In the appTap My Team on the homescreen.Search or scroll to find the User you're looking for and tap on their name.From their profile, you can see their last uploaded photos, created Projects, and Reports. Have someone you'd like to invite to a project but don't want/need them added to your account? No problem! You can give guest access to any project, have them upload photos, and set an expiration date for their access. Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + Contractor's CloudIntegrate CompanyCam + HeyProsGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Assign a Checklist to Users | Help Center
content: Assign a Checklist to Users | Help CenterSkip to main contentAssign a Checklist to UsersNeed to work on a Checklist with your teammates? Any User can be assigned a Checklist. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles: You will need to have the Admin, Manager, or Standard role to assign Checklists. If you don't have this permission, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: Assigning a Checklist to Users is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webAssigning ChecklistsOpen any Checklist that you'd like to assign Users to. Click on Assign underneath the Checklist's title. Search and select the Users you'd like to assign to the Checklist. Once assigned to a Checklist, each User will be notified and once they tap on the notification, it will take them to the assigned Checklist.Unassigning ChecklistsOpen any Checklist in CompanyCam that you'd like to unassign Users from. Click the name of the User you'd like to unassign from the Checklist. Select Unassign next to the name of the User you'd like to unassign from the Checklist.​Assigning Checklist FieldsOpen any Checklist that you'd like to assign a User to. Click on the Checklist Field you'd like to assign and select the Assign User Icon. You can either search for the User(s) you'd like to assign or select them from a drop-down menu. The assigned User(s) should now appear under their Task. Unassigning Checklist FieldOpen the Checklist that you'd like to unassign Users from. Click on the Checklist Field you'd like to unassign a User from, and click on the Assign User Icon. Click Unassign. Assigning Checklist SectionsOpen any Checklist in CompanyCam that you'd like to assign Users to. Click the Assign User Icon to the right of the Checklist Section. Select the User(s) you'd like to assign to the Section. You should now see the User(s) assigned to the Checklist Section. Unassigning Checklists SectionsOpen any Checklist in CompanyCam that you'd like to unassign Users from. Click the Assign User Icon to the right of the Checklist Section. Select the User(s) you'd like to unassign to the Section and click Unassign. You should now see that the User(s) are unassigned to the Checklist Section. In the appAssigning ChecklistsLog in to the app and open a Project.Tap on the To-Dos Tab. Open your Checklist.Tap on the Assign Button underneath the Checklist's title.Select the User(s) you'd like to assign to the Checklist.Once you've selected your Users, tap Assign.Unassigning ChecklistsLog in to the app and open a Project.Tap on the To-Dos Tab.Open your Checklist.Tap on the User(s) Icon below the Checklist's title.Select the User(s) you'd like to unassign from the Checklist.Once you've deselected your User(s), tap Assign to unassign.Assigning a Checklist FieldOpen a Project and tap on the To-Dos Tab.Open a Checklist and tap on the Field you'd like to assign a User to. Tap + Assign User and select the User(s) you'd like to assign.After selecting the Users, tap Assign at the top right. You should now see that the User is assigned to the Checklist Field.Unassigning a Checklist FieldOpen a Project and tap on the To-Dos Tab.Open your Checklist and tap on the Field the User is assigned to. Tap the X located next to the User's name to unassign them from the field. Assigning a Checklist SectionOpen a Project and tap on the To-Dos Tab.Open your Checklist and tap on the Three Dots Icon next to the Section you'd like to assign a User to.Tap Assign Section. Select the User(s) you'd like to assign, and then tap Assign at the top right.You should now see that the User is assigned to the Checklist Section.Unassigning a Checklist SectionOpen a Project and tap on the To-Dos Tab.Open your Checklist and tap on the Three Dots Icon next to the Section the User is assigned to. Deselect the User(s) you'd like to unassign, then tap Assign at the top right. You should now see that the User is unassigned to the Checklist Section.FAQsCan Checklists be assigned to Project Collaborators? No, Checklists cannot be assigned to Project Collaborators.Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistAssign a Project to UsersCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesAssign Project Tasks to UsersUsing AI Features for ChecklistsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Assign a Project to Users | Help Center
content: Assign a Project to Users | Help CenterSkip to main contentAssign a Project to UsersNeed to direct work to your teammates? Any User can be assigned a Project. Restricted Users only see Projects they've made or been assigned. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles: Only Admins and Managers can assign and unassign Users. If you do not have one of these roles, reach out to your Admin.Platform Availability:Assigning a Project to Users is available on both the web and mobile apps. Assigning ProjectsOn the webOpen the Project in CompanyCam that you'd like to assign Users to.Click on the expandable Sidebar Icon to the right if it's collapsed.​ Click on Project Users or the Pencil Icon on the right-side panel. Click Assign Users to select Users for the Project. On the Project Users screen, you'll see Users who contributed to the Project or have been assigned to the Project. ​ You can choose to assign one or more Users at a time, or you can select to assign a Group.Once assigned to a Project, each User will be notified, and once they tap on the notification, it will take them to the assigned Project.In the appOpen your Project and tap on the Menu Button.Within the Users Section, tap + Assign.Scroll through your Users and select the User(s) you'd like to assign to the Project.Tap on the Assign option at the top of the page or select the blue Assign User button.Unassigning UsersOn the webOpen any Project in CompanyCam that you'd like to unassign Users from.Click on the expandable Side Bar Icon on the right side if it's collapsed.​ Click on Project Users or the Pencil Icon on the right-side panel. ​ Find the User you'd like to unassign from the Project and click on Unassign on the right side. In the appOpen your Project and tap on the Menu Button.Within the Users Section, tap on the Three Dots Icon to the right side of the User you'd like to unassign.Tap Unassign User.Confirm that you'd like to unassign the user by tapping Unassign.FAQsWhat does assigning a Project actually do? Assigning a Project means designating one or more Users to a specific Project, linking them to it. If your User has the Restricted permission role, assigning them to the Project ensures they will have access to it. Are Users notified when they're assigned to a Project? Yes, once a User is assigned to a Project, they receive a notification. When they tap on that notification, it takes them directly to the assigned Project. What is the difference between an Assigned User and a Project Contributor? The difference between Project Contributors and Assigned Users:Project Contributor: A User who has contributed to the Project by adding photos, comments, or other content.Assigned User: A User who has been manually assigned to the Project by an Admin or Manager. Project Contributors and Assigned Users both appear in the Project Users section of a Project. Assigned Users are identified by a green 'Assigned' Label. If I unassign a Restricted User from a Project, will they maintain access? No. If you unassign a Restricted User from a Project, they will no longer be able to access the Project. Restricted Users only have access to the Projects they create themselves or the Projects they're assigned to. Related ArticlesUsing Project LabelsAssign a Checklist to UsersAssign Project Tasks to UsersHow to Use TranslationsCreate and Manage Project GroupsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Assign Project Tasks to Users | Help Center
content: Assign Project Tasks to Users | Help CenterSkip to main contentAssign Project Tasks to UsersHave some outstanding Project Tasks that need to be completed? Assign Project Tasks to your Users to help get things done! April 14, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesAdmins, Managers, and Standard Users can assign Project Tasks. If you do not have this permission, ask an Admin or Manager on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityAssigning Project Tasks to Users is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webAssigning Users to Project TasksLog in to CompanyCam and open a Project. Click the expandable Sidebar Icon to the right if it's collapsed.​ Within the Tasks Section in the far right panel, click on the Assign Icon. Next, use the Search Bar and type in the name of the User you'd like to assign. Once you've found the User, click on their name to assign. Once assigned, you will see each User's Icon next to the Task. Unassigning Users from Project TasksSign in to CompanyCam and open a Project.Within the Tasks Section in the far right panel, click on the User Icon you'd like to unassign from the Task. Click the Unassign Button to remove the User. In the appAssigning Users to Project TasksLog in to the CompanyCam app.Open the Project you'd like to work in.Tap on the To-Dos Tab within the Project.Next, tap on the Task tab.Tap on the Task you'd like to assign a User to.Once in the Task Editor, select the Assign Icon.Select or use the search function to find the User(s) you want to assign.Once you've selected the User(s), tap Assign.Tap the blue Save Button to save your changes.Unassigning Users from Project TasksLog in to the CompanyCam app.Open the Project you'd like to work in.Tap on the To-Dos Tab within the Project.Next, tap on the Task Tab.Open the Task you'd like to unassign a User from.Tap on the User Icon within the editor.Tap on the 'X' next to the User you want to remove.Once you've removed the User, select the Assign Icon.Tap the blue Save Button to save your changes.FAQsWill Users get a notification when they've been assigned to a Project Task? Yes! Users will receive a notification once they've been assigned to a Project Task. Can I assign more than one User to a Project Task? Multiple Users can be assigned to a Project Task. Who can create Project Tasks? All permission roles can create and complete Project Tasks. Only Admins and Managers can edit and delete Project Tasks. Can Collaborators be assigned Tasks? No, but Collaborators can create, view, and complete Project Tasks. Related ArticlesUsing Project LabelsAssign a Project to UsersAssign a Checklist to UsersHow to Create and Edit Project TasksHow to Use TranslationsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create and Modify User Groups | Help Center
content: Create and Modify User Groups | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate and Modify User GroupsGroups are a great way to organize your users if you have different crews or job positions you want to view all their photos in one place! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesTo manage User Groups, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform AvailabilityCreating and modifying User Groups is only available on the web app.On the webCreate a GroupLog in to CompanyCam and click on the Team Tab located in the left-hand toolbar. ​ Next, click User Groups at the top of the page.​ Click on + Create Group to the right of the screen. Create a name for your Group and select the Users you want to be in this Group from the drop-down box. When you are finished, click Create Group. Now, you can find your new Group under the Groups tab and can view which Users are in the Group, what photos each User has uploaded and to what Project, when the photos were uploaded (date/time), and can filter through the photos as well! Modify a GroupLog in to CompanyCam and navigate to the Team Tab on the left sidebar. Next, click User Groups at the top of the page.​ Find the Group you need to make changes to and click the Three Dots Icon on the Group card. Click Edit Group within the drop-down menu. Edit the Group Name and the members as needed. Click Update Group. You can also view Groups in the mobile app by going to your home page, tapping My Team, and selecting Groups. FAQsIs there a character limit to User Group names? Yes, your Group names cannot exceed 50 characters. How does @mentioning work for a User Group? After a Group has been created, you'll be able to @mention the group within the Project Conversation and on Photo Comments. To do this, in the text box of a Project Conversation or Photo Comments, type '@' and select the group you want to mention. Need to invite more of your team to your CompanyCam account? Here's how to do that: Invite New Users.Related ArticlesCreate a Report with TagsIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusCreate a ReportCommunicate with Your TeamCreate and Manage Project GroupsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Accepting a User Invitation | Help Center
content: Accepting a User Invitation | Help CenterSkip to main contentAccepting a User InvitationYour company can invite you as a User to their CompanyCam account. Once the invitation is sent, here's how to accept it: April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans. Permissions Roles:All User Permission Roles can accept a User Invitation. Platform Availability:Users can accept a User Invitation on both mobile and web. Please NoteTo ensure you are connected to your company's account, wait until you receive your User Invitation before beginning the setup process for your User. On the webIf your invite was sent via email, check your inbox. If you don't see it right away, you may need to check your spam or junk folders.Click Accept Invitation. Clicking the link will redirect you to set up your User.Fill out the rest of your information, create a password, check that you agree to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, and click Finish Account Setup. From here, you'll be logged into your account so you can start taking photos!In the appCheck your email or texts for an invite from CompanyCam and tap on the email.Note: Cell phone carriers may block invitation links when sent via text because the messages are being detected as Spam. If you're having trouble receiving links via Text, we recommend reaching out to the User who sent the invitation initially and asking them to send the invitation via Email as a workaround.Tap Accept Invitation.Selecting the link will redirect you to create your account. Fill out the rest of your information, create a password, check that you agree to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, and click Finish Account Setup.From here, you'll be prompted to Download CompanyCam if you haven't installed the app yet.Once downloaded, open the app and tap Allow Paste if prompted.Now you'll be able to log in to your account!Now that you've successfully been added as a User, you can get to work on a Project! Here's how to start taking photos in the mobile app! Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserInvite New UsersAdd or Update Billing InformationAccepting a Project CollaborationGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Change Your Profile Photo | Help Center
content: How to Change Your Profile Photo | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Change Your Profile PhotoCustomize your profile with a photo to give it a personal touch! April 3, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All User Permission Roles can update their own Profile Photo.Admin and Managers can update the Profile Photo for any User.Platform Availability:Users can update their Profile Photo on both the web and mobile app.Admin and Managers can only update Profile Photos for other Users on the web app.On the webTo change your Profile Photo, click on your User Icon in the top left-hand corner and then click on My Photos from the dropdown menu. Click Change Photo under your current Profile Photo. Drag and drop a photo or click within the blue box to upload a new Profile Photo. Once your new Profile Photo preview appears, click Upload. That's it! Your new Profile Photo will now be saved. In the appTap on your User Icon at the top left of the home screen.Next, tap on Edit Profile.Tap your Profile Photo Icon.From here, you can tap the Capture Button when you're ready to take a photo. When the photo is captured, you can tap Use this Photo to save that photo as your Profile Photo, or you can tap Take another to take a different photo.Do you want to change other account information, like username and password? Go here to edit your login information.Related ArticlesCreate a Before & After PhotoHow to Delete Photos and VideosHow to Use Quick CaptionHow to Use Map This PhotoTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Clock In/Out for Time Tracking (Beta) | Help Center
content: How to Clock In/Out for Time Tracking (Beta) | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Clock In/Out for Time Tracking (Beta)Easily track and document your on-site hours for projects right in the mobile app. Updated over 2 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is currently in a closed Beta. Access to this beta, pricing, and plan availability are subject to change.This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:All User Permission Roles can access Time Tracking in the mobile app and web app. Restricted Users can only access Time Tracking for the Projects they've created or have been assigned to.Platform Availability:Time Tracking is available on both web and mobile apps. On the webCreate a new time entryClick Time Tracking on the left-hand sidebar. Click + New Entry at the top right of the page. Enter the required information, including your Start Time and End Time, then click Create Entry. Delete a time entryClick Time Tracking on the left-hand sidebar. Select the time entry you’d like to update and click the Three Dots Icon next to the entry. Click Delete. Edit a time entryClick Time Tracking on the left-hand sidebar. Select the time entry you’d like to update. Click the Three Dots Icon next to the entry and click Edit. Make updates, then click Save. In the app From the Home screen, tap Time Tracking.Tap the + Icon.Enter the required information, including your Start Time and End Time, then tap Save.Your time entry will appear on your time sheet. Create a time entry with a photoTap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen. Tap Clock In.Tap either Select a Project or No Project, which will prompt you to Take a Clock-in Photo or Skip. After clocking in, the Tracking Time Icon will appear at the top of the Home screen. Tap the Time Tracking Icon to clock out. Tap the Stop Icon.Tap Take a Clock-Out Photo or Skip. View your Time SheetFrom the Home screen, tap Time Tracking.View your recorded time entries.Edit a Time EntryFrom the Home screen, tap Time Tracking.Tap on a recorded time entry.Make your changes and tap Save. Delete a Time EntryFrom the Home screen, tap Time Tracking. Tap on a recorded time entry.Tap the Trash Icon at the top right of the screen. Tap Delete. FAQsWill users get reminders to clock in or out?Yes. Push reminders are sent only to crew members who haven't already clocked in or out. There are also long-running timer alerts to catch forgotten clock-outs, and a midnight auto-stop as a last line of defense. Can an admin fix a missed clock-in for an employee?Yes. Admins and Managers can add, edit, and reassign entries in a few clicks — no need to track the employee down. Can data be exported?Admins and Managers can download a CSV export formatted for payroll handoff. Related ArticlesCreate a New ProjectHow to Upload and Manage FilesIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupLogging Out of CompanyCamHow to Create and Edit Project TasksDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Manage Time Tracking (Beta) | Help Center
content: How to Manage Time Tracking (Beta) | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Manage Time Tracking (Beta)Learn how to view team hours, manage time entries, and export timesheet data from the Time Tracking page. Updated this weekTable of contentsTime Tracking gives Admins and Managers a full view of their crew's hours from the web. Add or fix entries when something goes wrong, configure reminders so the crew stays on top of clocking in and out, and export a clean CSV when it's time to run payroll.This feature is in Beta. Plan availability is subject to change. Managing Time Tracking is only available on the web app.View the Time Tracking ReportNote: Only Admins and Managers can view time entries for other team members. Standard and Restricted Users can view only their own time. Log in to CompanyCam and click on the Time Tracking Option in the left-hand navigation menu. Here, you can use the filtering tools and grouping options to filter data by User, Project, date, or a combination of those. ​Create a Time EntryNote: Only Admins and Managers can create entries on behalf of other Users.Log in to CompanyCam and click on Time Tracking in the left navigation menu.Click + New Entry in the upper-right corner.Enter the time details for the team member, then click Click This User In.Edit a Time EntryNote: Only Admins and Managers can edit entries for other Users. Standard and Restricted Users can edit only their own entries.Log in to CompanyCam > Time Tracking in the left navigation menu.Locate the entry you want to change.Click the 3 Dots Icon next to the entry, then select Edit.Make your changes, then click Save.Delete a Time EntryNote: Only Admins and Managers can delete entries for other Users.Log in to CompanyCam > Time Tracking in the left sidebar.Locate the entry you want to remove.Click the 3 Dots Icon next to the entry, then select Delete. Export Time DataNote: Only Admins and Managers can export time tracking data.Log in to CompanyCam > Time Tracking in the left sidebar.Apply any filters to adjust the data you want to export.Click Export at the top of the page. Your entries download as a CSV based on the current view.Set up Time Tracking RemindersNote: Only Admins and Managers can manage reminder settings.Reminders are push notifications that go out only to crew members who haven't clocked in or out yet — so no one gets a nudge they don't need. Log in to CompanyCam > Time Tracking in the left sidebar.Click Reminders in the upper-right corner. Toggle Clock-In Reminders and Clock-Out Reminders on or off, then set the time and days that match your crew's schedule. Click Save.FAQs Can Standard Users see other people's time entries? Standard users can view only their own time entries. They can't see or edit anyone else's data. Can Restricted Users access time tracking reports? Restricted users can clock in and out and view their own entries, but they can't access reports or time tracking settings. What happens if someone forgets to clock out? A few safeguards are in place. A configurable clock-out reminder goes out to anyone still clocked in at a set time. If a timer runs 9+ hours, a long-running timer alert fires. At midnight, the system stops the timer automatically. Admins and Managers can also edit the entry manually after the fact. Can I reassign an entry to a different project? Admins and Managers can reassign any entry to a different user or project. Locate the entry, click the three dots icon, select Edit, update the project field, and click Save. Can I track time that isn't tied to a project? Time entries don't require a project. Crew can clock in without selecting one, and that time still shows up in reports. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday. The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateHow to Export Project and Checklist DataIntegrate CompanyCam + SmartsheetCreating and Managing CustomersUsing Project BoardsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Getting Started with CompanyCam | Help Center
content: Getting Started with CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentGetting Started with CompanyCamLearn how to download CompanyCam, set up your account, invite your team, create a Project, and share photos with clients. Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsCompanyCam keeps every job photo organized by Project the moment it's taken. No more hunting through texts or camera rolls. Your whole team stays aligned from the field to the office.Download the appOpen the App Store (iPhone) or Google Play Store (Android).Search for CompanyCam.Tap Get or Install.Tap Open once it's finished.You can also log in at app.companycam.com from any desktop browser. Create your accountOpen the app or visit companycam.com > tap Sign Up > enter your company name, work email, and a password > follow the prompts to finish. Invite your teamCompanyCam works best when your whole crew is involved. Invite employees as Users so they can upload photos and access Projects from day one. Go to Team > Invite Users > enter their email addresses and assign a permission role.Not sure which role to assign? See User Permission Roles Explained for a full breakdown. Create your first ProjectA Project keeps every photo, note, and update for a single job in one place. Create a Project for each job site, property, or customer.Tap + Create on the Project feed and select + New Project.Enter a project name, address, and any other details.Tap Create.Take and upload photosInside your Project, tap the camera icon to take a photo or tap (+) then Upload Photos to add existing photos from your device. Once photos are in, you can add descriptions, comments, or markup directly on them.Turn on date and time stamps in your settings if you want them applied automatically to every photo taken through the app. Share with customersThis is where CompanyCam earns its keep. Send clients a link to their job progress and cut the "can you send me an update?" calls for good. OptionWhat it doesBest forGalleryA curated set of photos shared via a static link. Clients can download photos from their end.Sending a finished phase or closeout documentation.TimelineA live link that updates as new photos are added. You control what's visible and can deactivate it at any time.Keeping clients in the loop throughout a job.Photo ReportA structured, formatted document with photos, comments, and descriptions organized into sections.Formal deliverables, insurance, or anything that needs to look polished.PageFlexible, formatted content similar to a Report, with optional AI tools to help organize and write faster.Client-facing summaries with more creative control.Guest AccessA shared link that lets clients view updates, leave comments, and upload their own photos. No CompanyCam account required.Homeowners or new clients you want to keep closely involved.Connect your other toolsConnect CompanyCam with the tools you already use to keep your business running smoothly. Check out our integration opportunities. Looking to build something custom? CompanyCam's open API and documentation make it easy to create custom integrations. FAQsCan I use CompanyCam on a desktop?CompanyCam is fully accessible at app.companycam.com from any desktop browser. The mobile app is available on iOS and Android. Do my clients need a CompanyCam account to view shared photos?Customers don't need an account to view a Gallery, Timeline, Report, or Page. Guest Access also requires no account. How many photos can I store in a Project?CompanyCam does not have a limit on the number of photos that can be stored within a project. You can continue uploading and organizing photos in the same project as needed. Why can't my team member see a Project I created?Your team member likely has Restricted permissions on your CompanyCam account. Users with the Restricted permission role can only view projects they are assigned to or projects they create themselves. Can I share a Timeline link and then hide certain photos later?Yes, you can share a Timeline link and later hide certain photos from that Timeline. To do this:Select the photo(s) you'd like to exclude from your Project Timeline.Click Actions in the banner that appears at the top of the screen.Click Hide in Project Timeline. Need Help?We’re here to help you get the most out of CompanyCam. Whether you have a quick question, need troubleshooting assistance, or want to learn more about features, we’ve got resources available for you.Help Center. Explore our Help Center for step-by-step articles, setup guides, feature overviews, and best practices. It’s the fastest way to find answers and learn how to get more value from CompanyCam.Pre-recorded Classes and Demos. Learn on your own schedule with pre-recorded training sessions and product demos. These resources are great for onboarding new team members, learning best practices, and discovering ways to use CompanyCam more efficiently in the field and office.Contact Support. We're the quickest and most efficient on email and chat. We answer emails within an hour and chats within three minutes during business hours. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + JobNimbusIntegrate CompanyCam + NiceJobIntegrate CompanyCam + HeyProsIntegrate CompanyCam + IssueIDConnecting CompanyCam to ServiceTitanDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: User Permission Roles Explained | Help Center
content: User Permission Roles Explained | Help CenterSkip to main contentUser Permission Roles ExplainedHave questions about the four permission roles in CompanyCam? This article breaks down what each role can and can't do. Updated yesterdayTable of contentsNot every feature referenced in this article is available on every subscription plan. OverviewCompanyCam has four internal permission roles. When you add someone to your account, you assign them one of these roles based on what they need access to. Admin Commonly used for: Company President/Owner, Controller, or Office AdminComplete control of the account, including:Access to billing and subscription detailsAccess to all Projects and featuresAbility to manage UsersAbility to create and manage TemplatesAbility to connect, disconnect, and manage integration settingsManager Commonly used for: Crew Leads, Foremen, or SupervisorsAccess to all Projects and featuresAbility to manage UsersAbility to create and manage TemplatesAbility to connect, disconnect, and manage integration settings for select integrationsStandard Commonly used for: Crew Members, Full-time Employees, or TechniciansAccess to all ProjectsUnable to manage Users, billing, or company-wide settingsRestrictedCommonly used for: Subcontractors, Part-time Employees, or Employees on a "need to know" basisAccess only to Projects they create or are assigned to by an Admin or ManagerUnable to manage Users, billing, or company-wide settings Permissions break downAccount and Billing AdminManagerStandardRestrictedView/Edit subscription details✅❌❌❌Add/Update payment method✅❌❌❌Manage Company Settings✅✅❌❌ User and Group Management AdminManagerStandardRestrictedView User Details✅✅✅❌Invite new Users✅✅❌❌Reactivate Users✅✅❌❌Deactivate Users✅✅❌❌Manage User Groups✅✅❌❌Project Management AdminManagerStandardRestrictedView all Projects✅✅✅❌Create new Projects✅✅✅✅Edit Project details✅✅✅❌Delete Projects✅✅❌❌Archive Projects✅✅❌❌Assign a Project to Users✅✅❌❌View Project Groups✅✅✅❌*Manage Project Groups✅✅❌❌ View Project Boards✅✅✅✅**Move Projects within Project Boards✅✅✅❌Add/Remove Project Collaborators✅✅✅❌Add Guest Access on Project✅✅✅❌*Restricted Users can only view a Project Group if they are assigned to a Project within that group. However, they will still only be able to view the Projects within that Group if they are assigned to the Project. **Restricted Users can view the Project Board, but only see Projects they have access to. Media (Photos, Videos) AdminManagerStandardRestrictedView photos✅✅✅✅*Capture/Upload photos✅✅✅✅*Capture/Upload videos✅✅✅✅*Move photos✅✅✅✅*Share photos✅✅✅✅*Delete ANY photos/videos✅✅❌❌Delete OWN photos/videos✅✅✅✅Restore photos/videos from Project Trash✅✅❌❌*Within their own or assigned Projects only. Documents (Pages, Files, Reports) AdminManagerStandardRestrictedView Pages/Files/Reports✅✅✅✅*Create Pages✅✅✅✅*Upload Files✅✅✅✅*Manage File Access✅✅❌❌Create Reports✅✅✅✅*Edit Pages✅✅✅✅*Edit Reports✅✅✅✅**Delete Pages/Files✅✅✅✅*Delete Reports✅✅✅✅***Within their own or assigned Projects only.**Restricted Users can only view, edit, or delete Reports that they create themselves. To-Dos (Checklists, Tasks) AdminManagerStandardRestrictedCreate a Checklist✅✅✅✅*Assign a Checklist to Users✅✅❌❌Edit ANY Checklist Fields✅✅❌❌Edit OWN Checklist Fields✅✅✅✅Complete Checklist Fields✅✅✅✅*Create a Task✅✅✅✅*Edit Tasks✅✅❌❌Assign a Task to Users✅✅❌❌*Within their own or assigned Projects only. Customers AdminManagerStandardRestrictedView the Customer directory✅✅✅✅Create a new Customer✅✅✅❌Add a Customer to a Project✅✅✅❌Edit/Delete a Customer record✅✅✅❌Payments AdminManagerStandardRestrictedSet up Payments✅✅❌❌Request Payments✅✅✅✅*View ANY Payment requests✅✅❌❌View OWN Payment requests✅✅✅✅Edit ANY Payment requests✅✅❌❌Edit OWN Payment requests✅✅✅✅*Within their own or assigned Projects only. Proposals and Price Book AdminManagerStandardRestrictedCreate a Proposal✅✅✅❌View Proposals✅✅✅❌View Price Book line items✅✅✅✅*Create/Edit/Delete line items✅✅❌❌*Within their own or assigned Projects only. Public presence AdminManagerStandardRestricted Create Showcases✅✅❌❌Set up Showcase Gallery for your webpage✅✅❌❌Set up Showcase Map for your webpage✅✅❌❌Connect to Google Business Profile✅✅❌❌Request Reviews✅✅✅✅**Within their own or assigned Projects only. Other features AdminManagerStandardRestricted Manage Integrations✅✅❌❌Create/Edit Page Templates✅✅❌❌Create/Edit Checklist Templates✅✅❌❌Create/Edit Report Templates✅✅❌❌Use Templates✅✅✅✅**Within their own or assigned Projects only. FAQsWhat are the four permission roles in CompanyCam? The four permission roles within CompanyCam are Admin, Manager, Standard, and Restricted. Where can I view the permission roles of other Users on my account? Admins, Managers, and Standard Users can view all Users' permission roles. The roles can only be viewed within the web app. On the web, they can click on the Team Tab in the left-hand navigation menu. The roles are listed next to the User's name. Restricted Users don't have access to the Team Tab. Can permission roles be edited? Yes. Admins and Managers can edit Users' permission roles at any time; here's how. However, only Admins can edit a User to the Admin role. If a User's permission role changes from Standard to Restricted, will they still have access to Projects they created or are assigned to? If the User creates a Project as a Standard User and then becomes a Restricted User, they don't retain access.If they are assigned a Project as a Standard User and then are made a Restricted User, they do retain access. Can all permission roles access all Projects on the account? Admin, Manager, and Standard Users have access to all Projects on the account. Restricted Users only have access to their own Projects (Projects they create themselves) or Projects they are assigned to. What features can be assigned to Users? Users can be assigned to Projects, Checklists, or Project Tasks. 💡 Tip: Need more help? Contact our support team — we're here Monday through Friday.The CompanyCam Community has real job-site examples, templates, and tips from crews using this every day. Jump in with a question or just browse. Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationReactivate a UserCreate Email RecapsAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Add Your Company's Logo | Help Center
content: Add Your Company's Logo | Help CenterSkip to main contentAdd Your Company's LogoKeep your company's brand front and center by adding your logo to CompanyCam! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:In order to add your logo, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Adding your company's logo is only available on the web app.On the webSign in to CompanyCam using a web browser.Click on the User Icon on the upper left side, and choose Company Settings from the dropdown menu. If a Logo hasn't been added yet, click Upload from Computer. If a Logo is already in place, click the existing Logo to update it. Click Save Changes. FAQsWhat file types can I use for my Company Logo? Logo files must be a .jpg or .png file type. What size should my Company Logo be? You Company Logo should be at least 300 px wide. If it is below the minimum size requirements, the logo will not appear on your content. Related ArticlesDeactivate a UserCancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionEdit Company Settings and InformationIntegrate CompanyCam + JobTreadIntegrate CompanyCam + ContractorFlowDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create Email Recaps | Help Center
content: Create Email Recaps | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate Email RecapsNeed a summary of your activity in CompanyCam? With Admin permissions, generate activity reports to be sent to your or another user's email! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To create Email Recaps, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Email Recaps cannot be sent to Users with the Restricted role. Platform Availability: Creating Email Recaps is only available on the web app. ​On the webLog in to CompanyCam. Click on your User Icon on the left sidebar and then click on Company Settings from the dropdown menu. On the Company Settings page, click on Email Recaps. To create a new recap, click New Email Recap. From the Email Recap window, you can select who the email should go to from your Users list, what kind of recap, and how often it should be delivered. Click Create once you've selected all the options. If you need to edit an existing Email Recap, click the Pencil Icon to the right of the one you need to change. If you need to delete an Email Recap, click the Trash Icon to the right of the Email Recap. FAQsWhen are Email Recaps sent out? Recap emails will be delivered: Daily Recap: 6 pm local timeWeekly Recap: Saturday 6 pm local timeMonthly Recap: 1st of the month, 6 pm local time To get a complete list of all activity in your account, including names of all projects and project information, user contribution, photo count, and much more, check out how to export a Project List!Related ArticlesEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationIntegrate CompanyCam + REISHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + Job-DoxHow to Export Project and Checklist DataDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Export Project and Checklist Data | Help Center
content: How to Export Project and Checklist Data | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Export Project and Checklist DataExporting Project and Checklist data allows you to view your Projects and Checklists outside of CompanyCam in a spreadsheet. April 22, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:To export Project and/or Checklist data, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform Availability:Exporting Project and/or Checklist data can only be done on the web app. On the webLog in to the web version of the app at CompanyCam.Click on your User Icon in the left-hand sidebar and click Company Settings. Click the Exports Tab under Company Settings. From here, you will be able to select a time frame and the data you'd like to export. Select the duration and data from the Menu Icon. Once selected, click on Export .CSV. Check the email that is linked to your CompanyCam account. Keep an eye out for an email from [email protected]. If you don't see an email in your inbox, make sure to check your Spam and Junk folders.While you're here, one other thing that you should check out is Email Recaps. They give you daily or weekly Project and User reports delivered automatically to your inbox! FAQsWhat all is included in a Project Export? A Project export will include:Project IDNameFull AddressLatitudeLongitudeDate CreatedPhoto countDocument countVideo countLabelsUsers Last ActivityWhat all is included in a Checklist Data Export? A Checklist export will include:Checklist IDChecklist NameProject IDProject NameAccount IDAccount NameProject AddressChecklist Template IDChecklist Template NameTasks CountCompleted Tasks CountSkipped Tasks CountPercent CompleteContributorsAssigned ToLatest Contribution At Related ArticlesHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + GAF ProjectAssign a Checklist to UsersCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesIntegrate CompanyCam + Google CalendarDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Edit Company Settings and Information | Help Center
content: Edit Company Settings and Information | Help CenterSkip to main contentEdit Company Settings and InformationWe know that sometimes your business information changes. You can update your CompanyCam account at any time; here's how! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To edit Company Settings, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have this role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: You can edit Company Settings in both the web and mobile apps. On the webSign in to CompanyCam.Click your User Icon on the left sidebar, then click Company Settings. Company Info will be the tab you land on. Update the information that you'd like to change and then select Save Changes at the bottom of the page.​In the appTap your User Icon at the top of the home screen.Next, tap Edit Profile. On the My Info page, tap Edit in the Company Info section.Edit the information you need to change and then tap Save in the top right corner.Related ArticlesEdit Project DetailsEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationAdd Your Company's LogoIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupChange Login InformationDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Access the Company Dashboard | Help Center
content: How to Access the Company Dashboard | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Access the Company DashboardAt a glance, the Company Dashboard is an awesome tool you can use to see a high-level overview of the productivity within your account! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesTo access the Company Dashboard, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission; here's what they'll need.Platform AvailabilityThe Company Dashboard is only available on the web app.Please NoteAdmins and Managers will be able to view:For the whole company: Total Projects, Photos, Checklist Fields completed, Reports created, Documents added, and active Project Collaborations. For each User: User Role, Photo Counts, Checklist Fields completed, Reports created, Documents added, and Last Activity.It's important to note that the top-level, Overview metrics are pulled from Projects created in the selected timeframe. The User metrics reflect any activity for a specific User within the selected timeframe, regardless of when the Project was created. As such, the User metrics will not necessarily "add up" to the company's Overview metrics. On the webAccessing the Company DashboardLog in to CompanyCam.Click on the User Icon on the upper left side, and click on Company Dashboard from the drop-down menu. How to Filter the Company DashboardNow that your Company Dashboard is open, you’ll be able to see an overview of your whole company, individual Users, and add new Users. Totals can be filtered by Yesterday, 1 Week, 30 Days, 60 Days, 1 Year, or All Time. Company OverviewTo filter the whole company, click on the drop-down menu next to Overview to select the timeframe you’d like to sort by. Once selected, the Dashboard will automatically update to reflect the selected range. UsersTo filter by Users, click on the drop-down menu next to Users to select the timeframe you’d like to sort by and/or type in the User's name by clicking into the search bar. Once selected, the Dashboard will automatically update to reflect the selected range and/or User you've searched for. To add a new User, click on + Add User. FAQsHow often is the Company Dashboard updated? Data in the Company Dashboard is refreshed every 24 hours, occurring each evening. Related ArticlesHow to Access Community Checklist TemplatesHow to Access the Corporate PortalAccepting a User InvitationGetting Started with CompanyCamSetting Up Marketing SuiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Access the Corporate Portal | Help Center
content: How to Access the Corporate Portal | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Access the Corporate PortalAt a glance, the Corporate Portal is a tool you can use to see a high-level overview of the accounts within your organization. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available to Enterprise accounts and as an Add-on for all accounts. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:You must be an Organization Admin to access the Corporate Portal. Platform Availability:The Corporate Portal is only available on the web app.On the webAccessing the Corporate PortalLog in to CompanyCam on a web browser.In the left-hand navigation menu, click Company Selector. Click Switch to Org Admin. ORClick on your User Icon at the top of the screen.In the drop-down menu, click Corporate Portal. Using the Corporate PortalDashboard ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Dashboard Tab. Once inside the Dashboard, you will be able to see an overview of the Account Metrics that have taken place within your Organization. These metrics include: ProjectsPhotosChecklistsReportsDocumentsPages Within the Dashboard View, you can also see your Checklist Snapshot. The Checklist Snapshot is where you can view the overall Checklist completion progress across your accounts. If you click View All, you'll see a more in-depth Checklist Completion view across all of your accounts. If you'd like to see the Checklist Progress for a specific account, you can click on the Account Name to access an in-depth list of an account's Checklists. Accounts ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Accounts Tab. Once inside this section, you'll see an Activity Overview graph. This graph can display up to 5 of your accounts and will show a 12-month rolling calendar. The graph helps to showcase an account's activity over time. Below the Activity Overview graph, you can view your User Activity. The User Activity section is where you can view the status of the Users across your Organization. Following the User Activity section, you'll see a breakdown of all the activity between each of the accounts within your Organization, along with: Account NameProjects CountPhotos CountChecklists CountReports CountDocuments CountPages Count Photo Feed ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Photo Feed Tab. Once inside the Photo Feed Tab, you'll be able to see a high-level overview of the photos that are being uploaded across the Organization. You will also have several filtering tools that can be used to narrow the feed down by Accounts, Dates, Projects, Users, and Tags. Resources View In the left-hand menu, click on the Resources Tab. Once inside the Resources Tab, you will be able to select a time frame and account and then generate a .CSV file of all of your Checklist Data. Select the time frame and account(s) from the Menu Icon. Once selected, click Export .CSV. We'll generate a .CSV file with all your Checklist data and send it straight to your inbox once the file is ready to download. Templates ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Templates Tab. Once inside the Templates View, you can create organization-wide Checklist Templates. Once created, these Templates will be accessible across your organization. To create a new Checkist Template, click + Create Template. Next, you'll enter the Template editor. Here you'll title your Template, add a description, and build your Checklist Template. Any edits will save automatically. If you need to duplicate a checklist, you can click Duplicate Template at the top of the page. If you need to edit the Template in the future, you can click on the Template Title to reopen the editor. FAQsCan I switch between different accounts in my organization? Yes! To switch between different accounts within your organization, you can use the account switcher located in the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the Company Selector at the bottom of the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the account you'd like to switch into. Is the Corporate Portal a read-only dashboard? Yes! The Organization Admin will not be able to comment on or share content within the Portal. Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Export Project and Checklist DataHow to Manage TemplatesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamHow to Access the Company DashboardDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Access the Corporate Portal | Help Center
content: How to Access the Corporate Portal | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Access the Corporate PortalAt a glance, the Corporate Portal is a tool you can use to see a high-level overview of the accounts within your organization. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available to Enterprise accounts and as an Add-on for all accounts. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Roles:You must be an Organization Admin to access the Corporate Portal. Platform Availability:The Corporate Portal is only available on the web app.On the webAccessing the Corporate PortalLog in to CompanyCam on a web browser.In the left-hand navigation menu, click Company Selector. Click Switch to Org Admin. ORClick on your User Icon at the top of the screen.In the drop-down menu, click Corporate Portal. Using the Corporate PortalDashboard ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Dashboard Tab. Once inside the Dashboard, you will be able to see an overview of the Account Metrics that have taken place within your Organization. These metrics include: ProjectsPhotosChecklistsReportsDocumentsPages Within the Dashboard View, you can also see your Checklist Snapshot. The Checklist Snapshot is where you can view the overall Checklist completion progress across your accounts. If you click View All, you'll see a more in-depth Checklist Completion view across all of your accounts. If you'd like to see the Checklist Progress for a specific account, you can click on the Account Name to access an in-depth list of an account's Checklists. Accounts ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Accounts Tab. Once inside this section, you'll see an Activity Overview graph. This graph can display up to 5 of your accounts and will show a 12-month rolling calendar. The graph helps to showcase an account's activity over time. Below the Activity Overview graph, you can view your User Activity. The User Activity section is where you can view the status of the Users across your Organization. Following the User Activity section, you'll see a breakdown of all the activity between each of the accounts within your Organization, along with: Account NameProjects CountPhotos CountChecklists CountReports CountDocuments CountPages Count Photo Feed ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Photo Feed Tab. Once inside the Photo Feed Tab, you'll be able to see a high-level overview of the photos that are being uploaded across the Organization. You will also have several filtering tools that can be used to narrow the feed down by Accounts, Dates, Projects, Users, and Tags. Resources View In the left-hand menu, click on the Resources Tab. Once inside the Resources Tab, you will be able to select a time frame and account and then generate a .CSV file of all of your Checklist Data. Select the time frame and account(s) from the Menu Icon. Once selected, click Export .CSV. We'll generate a .CSV file with all your Checklist data and send it straight to your inbox once the file is ready to download. Templates ViewIn the left-hand menu, click on the Templates Tab. Once inside the Templates View, you can create organization-wide Checklist Templates. Once created, these Templates will be accessible across your organization. To create a new Checkist Template, click + Create Template. Next, you'll enter the Template editor. Here you'll title your Template, add a description, and build your Checklist Template. Any edits will save automatically. If you need to duplicate a checklist, you can click Duplicate Template at the top of the page. If you need to edit the Template in the future, you can click on the Template Title to reopen the editor. FAQsCan I switch between different accounts in my organization? Yes! To switch between different accounts within your organization, you can use the account switcher located in the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the Company Selector at the bottom of the left-hand navigation menu. Click on the account you'd like to switch into. Is the Corporate Portal a read-only dashboard? Yes! The Organization Admin will not be able to comment on or share content within the Portal. Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Export Project and Checklist DataHow to Manage TemplatesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamHow to Access the Company DashboardDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Manage Templates | Help Center
content: How to Manage Templates | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Manage TemplatesCreate, edit, and save your Checklist, Report, and Project Templates all in one place! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on specific plans. Permission Roles:To create Templates, you need to have the Admin or Manager role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission. Platform Availability:Managing templates is only available on the web app. On the webLog in to CompanyCam.From the left-hand navigation bar, select Templates. Project TemplatesTo view all of your Project Templates, click on the Projects Tab. To create a new Project Template, click on + Create Project Template in the upper right corner. To edit a Project Template, click on the Title of the Template you'd like to edit > and then make any changes needed. From here, you can edit the title and description. You'll also be able to add Checklist Templates, Report Templates, and/or Project Documents. Checklist TemplatesTo view all of your Checklist Templates, click on the Checklists Tab. To create a new Checklist Template, click on + Create Checklist Template in the upper right corner. To edit a Checklist Template, click on the Title of the Template you'd like to edit > and then make any changes needed. From here, you can edit the title, description, sections, and fields. Any updates will automatically save on the Template and will not affect previously created lists. Report TemplatesTo view all of your Report Templates, click on the Reports Tab. To create a new Report Template, click on + Create Report Template in the upper right corner. To edit a Report Template, click on the Title of the Template you'd like to edit > and then make any changes needed. From here, you can edit the title, description, sections, cover page, and report settings. Any updates will automatically save on the Template and will not affect previously created Reports. Project Files TemplatesTo view all of your Files Templates, click on the Files Tab. To upload a File Template, click on + Upload File in the upper right corner. To delete a File Template, click the Three Dots Icon to the right of the file name, then select Delete. Pages TemplatesTo view all of your Pages Templates, click on the Pages Tab. To create a Pages Template, click on + Create Page Template in the upper right corner. To edit a Pages Template, click on the Title of the Template you'd like to edit > and then make any changes needed. From here, any updates you make will automatically save on the Template and will not affect previously created Pages.Need more information about Templates? Check out our helpful articles for Checklist Templates, Report Templates, and Project Templates!Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistCreate and Use Report TemplatesHow to Access Community Checklist TemplatesCreate and Use Checklist TemplatesCreate and Use Pages TemplatesDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

To create a Pages Template, click on + Create Page Template in the upper right corner. To edit a Pages Template, click on the Title of the Template you'd like to edit > and then make any changes needed. From here, any updates you make will automatically save on the Template and will not affect previously created Pages.Need more information about Templates? title: How to Use Notifications | Help Center
content: How to Use Notifications | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use NotificationsNever miss a comment on a photo with notifications! March 24, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startUsers can @ mention individual Users or @ mention Groups from within the Project or when leaving Comments on photos.When @ mentioning a Group, every User in the Group will receive a Notification.Users can set a reminder notification for comments they want to return to.On the webHow to send a Notification: Open any photo or Project and in the comment field use the @ symbol to mention a User, tag multiple Users, or tag Groups. Click Post to save the Comment, and the tagged User (or Group Users if @ mentioning a Group) will receive an immediate Notification.How to view a Notification:Click the Notification Bell to view Notifications.​ Select a message to open and respond.How to set a Reminder Notification:Open a Project Conversation or Photo and find the Comment you'd like to set a Reminder for. Hover over the Comment and click on the Notification Icon. Select when you would like to receive the Reminder Notification. After setting the Reminder Notification, a pop-up will appear. Optional: Click View to access and manage your Reminder in your Notification Settings. How to set your Notification Settings:Change Notification Settings by clicking on your User Icon in the left-hand toolbar and selecting Notification Settings. Choose how you would like to be notified. When the toggle is blue, this setting is enabled. Note: You will continue receiving Notifications in the in-app Notification Center (Bell Icon), even if push notifications and email notifications are disabled; this cannot be turned off. In the appHow to send a Notification:Open any Project and tap the Comment Icon.Use the @ Symbol to mention another User or tag multiple Users, then tap Post.ORTap on any photo and then tap the Comment Icon at the bottom left corner.Once in the Photo Comment, tap on the @ Symbol to mention another User, then tap on Post.Note: @Mentioned Users and Groups will receive an immediate notification. How to view a Notification:Tap on the Inbox Icon to view your Notifications.Tap on the message to open and respond.How to set a Reminder Notification:Open a Project Conversation or Photo and find the Comment you'd like to set a Reminder for. Long-press the Comment you would like to set a Reminder for. Choose when you'd like to receive the Reminder Notification. After setting the Reminder Notification, a pop-up will appear. Optional: Tap View to access and manage your Reminder in your Notifications.How to set your Notification Settings:Tap on the Inbox Icon.Once within your Inbox, tap on the Gear Icon in the upper right corner to access your Notification Settings.Once in the Notification Settings, you can enable/disable depending on how you would like to receive Notifications in the app. When toggled to blue, the setting is enabled.Note: You will continue receiving Notifications in the in-app Notification Center, even if mobile push and email notifications are disabled; this cannot be turned off.Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Use Map This PhotoCommunicate with Your TeamHow to Use TranslationsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents
