Optional: Tap View to access and manage your Reminder in your Notifications.How to set your Notification Settings:Tap on the Inbox Icon.Once within your Inbox, tap on the Gear Icon in the upper right corner to access your Notification Settings.Once in the Notification Settings, you can enable/disable depending on how you would like to receive Notifications in the app. When toggled to blue, the setting is enabled.Note: You will continue receiving Notifications in the in-app Notification Center, even if mobile push and email notifications are disabled; this cannot be turned off.Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Use Map This PhotoCommunicate with Your TeamHow to Use TranslationsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Use Notifications | Help Center
content: How to Use Notifications | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use NotificationsNever miss a comment on a photo with notifications! March 24, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startUsers can @ mention individual Users or @ mention Groups from within the Project or when leaving Comments on photos.When @ mentioning a Group, every User in the Group will receive a Notification.Users can set a reminder notification for comments they want to return to.On the webHow to send a Notification: Open any photo or Project and in the comment field use the @ symbol to mention a User, tag multiple Users, or tag Groups. Click Post to save the Comment, and the tagged User (or Group Users if @ mentioning a Group) will receive an immediate Notification.How to view a Notification:Click the Notification Bell to view Notifications.​ Select a message to open and respond.How to set a Reminder Notification:Open a Project Conversation or Photo and find the Comment you'd like to set a Reminder for. Hover over the Comment and click on the Notification Icon. Select when you would like to receive the Reminder Notification. After setting the Reminder Notification, a pop-up will appear. Optional: Click View to access and manage your Reminder in your Notification Settings. How to set your Notification Settings:Change Notification Settings by clicking on your User Icon in the left-hand toolbar and selecting Notification Settings. Choose how you would like to be notified. When the toggle is blue, this setting is enabled. Note: You will continue receiving Notifications in the in-app Notification Center (Bell Icon), even if push notifications and email notifications are disabled; this cannot be turned off. In the appHow to send a Notification:Open any Project and tap the Comment Icon.Use the @ Symbol to mention another User or tag multiple Users, then tap Post.ORTap on any photo and then tap the Comment Icon at the bottom left corner.Once in the Photo Comment, tap on the @ Symbol to mention another User, then tap on Post.Note: @Mentioned Users and Groups will receive an immediate notification. How to view a Notification:Tap on the Inbox Icon to view your Notifications.Tap on the message to open and respond.How to set a Reminder Notification:Open a Project Conversation or Photo and find the Comment you'd like to set a Reminder for. Long-press the Comment you would like to set a Reminder for. Choose when you'd like to receive the Reminder Notification. After setting the Reminder Notification, a pop-up will appear. Optional: Tap View to access and manage your Reminder in your Notifications.How to set your Notification Settings:Tap on the Inbox Icon.Once within your Inbox, tap on the Gear Icon in the upper right corner to access your Notification Settings.Once in the Notification Settings, you can enable/disable depending on how you would like to receive Notifications in the app. When toggled to blue, the setting is enabled.Note: You will continue receiving Notifications in the in-app Notification Center, even if mobile push and email notifications are disabled; this cannot be turned off.Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Use Map This PhotoCommunicate with Your TeamHow to Use TranslationsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Refer a Friend to CompanyCam | Help Center
content: Refer a Friend to CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentRefer a Friend to CompanyCamUse your referral link to become a CompanyCam Closer and get paid! April 15, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plansUsers will not have access to referral information when they are in their trial periodPermission Roles:All permission roles have access to this feature. Platform Availability:This feature is available on both the web and mobile apps. On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Click Refer, get $$$ at the bottom of the left sidebar. Click Copy to copy the URL to your clipboard or send it to another platform.​ Paste the link into your message to send it.Make sure your friend uses that link to get signed up, so we know who to send our thanks to!In the appTap on your User Icon at the top of the home screen. Tap Refer, Get Paid.Send your link via text, email, or AirDrop - whatever's easiest.Make sure your friend uses that link to get signed up, so we know who to send our thanks to!When they become a paying User and get billed for the second time, you get paid!FAQsHow do I make sure my referrals are counted? You must share your unique referral link directly with the person or company you're referring. Referrals submitted any other way cannot be counted, and we're unable to add them manually.When will I receive my gift card after referring someone?Once the company you referred has been an active paying customer for two months, you'll be eligible for your gift card. We process gift cards once a month, typically toward the end of the month. Looking for more details about the Referral Program? Check out our Referrals page to learn more!Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + GetTheReferralIntegrate CompanyCam + PipedriveIntegrate CompanyCam + NiceJobIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Signing in to Multiple Accounts in the Mobile App | Help Center
content: Signing in to Multiple Accounts in the Mobile App | Help CenterSkip to main contentSigning in to Multiple Accounts in the Mobile AppIf you have multiple CompanyCam accounts, you can easily switch between these accounts when using the mobile app! January 27, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startSigning into multiple accounts is only available on the mobile app.In the appTap on your User Icon at the top of the home screen.Next, tap Switch Account.Tap Add Account and add your login information for your other account.After you've added your other account, tap on the account you want to be logged in to, and you're good to go.Note: When using CompanyCam, the company name shown at the top of the app is the account you're actively using. If you're looking to create a new company account, you can sign up for that new account here! You'll need to have a unique email address for that account.Related ArticlesHow to Check App Version and UpdateIntegrate CompanyCam + TeamupLogging Out of CompanyCamHow to log in to CompanyCam with SSOTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Using CompanyCam in Spanish: FAQ | Help Center
content: Using CompanyCam in Spanish: FAQ | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing CompanyCam in Spanish: FAQIf your device or browser is set to Spanish, CompanyCam will automatically display in that language for a seamless experience! Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startCompanyCam adjusts based on your device or browser's language settings; this is not a setting inside the CompanyCam app.Some features, such as AI Actions, user-generated content, and integration descriptions, are only available in English.Our support team is available to assist you in Spanish via chat or email.FAQsHow do I use CompanyCam in Spanish? To use CompanyCam in Spanish, you'll want to have your device's language settings set to Spanish. CompanyCam will automatically display in Spanish when your browser or device language settings are set to Spanish. To change your language settings:On your device, go to Settings > Language & Region and set your preferred language to Spanish.If using a web browser, update your language settings in the browser preferences. Restart the app or refresh the page to see the changes.Is Customer Support available in Spanish? Yes! Our support team is available to assist you in Spanish. If you need help, you can reach our team via chat or email. We'll be happy to help! Do you offer training in Spanish? Yes, we offer quarterly training sessions in Spanish to help you and your team make the most of CompanyCam. Check out our page for announcements on upcoming training sessions.​We also have a playlist full of Spanish how-to videos.​Additionally, our Help Center is also available in Spanish, providing resources to support you and your team. What features are not available in Spanish? While the main CompanyCam interface is available in Spanish, there are a few features only available in English:AI Actions: AI-powered features are not yet available in Spanish.User-generated content: Checklists, Reports, and Documents will remain in the language they were originally created in.Integration descriptions: These have not yet been translated into Spanish.Can I request that AI features be available in Spanish? We're actively improving our Spanish translation and language support options. Please feel free to chat or email the team to share your feedback. Are Spanish Translations available? Yes, Spanish Translations are available. Photo Comments, Project Conversations, and Project Tasks can now auto-translate to English or Spanish when the app detects a language other than the device's or browser's language settings. This feature is available on all current Pro, Premium, and Elite plans, but is not available on Legacy plans. Shared Pages Weblinks can also be translated. These links will not auto-translate based on your browser's language settings. Rather, there will be a Language options toggle to switch between languages. This feature is available on all current Premium and Elite plans, but is not available on Legacy plans. Check out our Help Guide on How to Use Translations to learn more! Can I use Quick Caption in Spanish? Yes! You can use the Quick Caption feature in Spanish. Just keep in mind that it won't automatically translate your speech. It will simply transcribe what you're saying in the language you speak. Want to learn more? Check out our Help Guide on How to Use Quick Caption! Related ArticlesHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + ClientTetherCommunicate with Your TeamIntegrate CompanyCam + DropboxHow to Use TranslationsDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: All Billing articles | Help Center
content: All Billing articles | Help CenterSkip to main contentAll Billing articles5 articlesAnnual Subscription PolicyCompanyCam has an Annual pricing option! Learn how to switch.Cancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionNo longer needing CompanyCam? You have the option to manage your account's billing and subscription 24/7 on our website!How to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamNeed an invoice from earlier in the year? Those invoices live in CompanyCam and are available for you to download on our website!Transition to Direct PayThe Apple Store offers a 3-User subscription to CompanyCam. To manage more users for your account you'll want to switch to Direct Pay.Add or Update Billing InformationNeed to add payment or update the one on file? You can access your billing 24/7 from CompanyCam! Learn how below.

title: Annual Subscription Policy | Help Center
content: Annual Subscription Policy | Help CenterSkip to main contentAnnual Subscription PolicyCompanyCam has an Annual pricing option! Learn how to switch. April 13, 2026Table of contentsWith the annual plan, you pay upfront for the full year. Your cost is based on the number of active seats at the time of subscription. For pricing details, visit your billing portal. Payment dateYour card is charged the full yearly amount on the day you subscribe. To view your payment date, go to the Billing page in the web app. Auto-renewalYour annual subscription will automatically renew on the anniversary of your first payment. To avoid renewal, cancel before that date.You can locate this date in the Billing section of the web version of the app at: https://app.companycam.com/billing.User seatsA user seat represents one active user on your account. Your subscription cost is based on the number of filled seats, so as you add or deactivate users, your billable total adjusts accordingly.Your subscription cost is based on the number of active seats at the time of subscription.New users added during the year are billed at a prorated cost for the remainder of the year, charged on the day they're invited.Deactivating a user frees up that seat for someone new.When your subscription renews, your cost will automatically adjust to reflect the current number of active seats.Additional discountsAnnual subscriptions cannot be combined with any other deals or discounts.Downgrades and refundsAnnual subscriptions are non-refundable. If you cancel, you'll retain access through the end of your current subscription period.Unfilled seats are not refunded or credited.You can switch to a monthly subscription at any time. Any remaining time on your annual plan will be applied as a credit toward your monthly invoices. Need to add a payment method before switching to annual? Here's how to add or update your payment information.Related ArticlesTransition to Direct PayHow to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamInvite New UsersCancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Cancel Your CompanyCam Subscription | Help Center
content: Cancel Your CompanyCam Subscription | Help CenterSkip to main contentCancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionNo longer needing CompanyCam? You have the option to manage your account's billing and subscription 24/7 on our website! April 15, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This functionality is available on all paid plans. If you run into any trouble canceling your subscription, please reach out to our Support Team.Permission Roles:To cancel an account, you need the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Canceling your CompanyCam Subscription is available only in the web app.On the webLog in to CompanyCam from a web browser or mobile browser.Click your User Icon and choose Billing. Once in the Billing section, click on Cancel Plan located on the left side of the Billing page. Once the billing portal is open, click on Cancel plan. You're welcome back at any time! FAQsWill CompanyCam save my content after I cancel? Right now, we don't delete your content if you were to cancel and reactivate later. However, we can't promise that it won't change in the future. Related ArticlesTransition to Direct PayHow to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamAdd or Update Billing InformationIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + MarkateDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamNeed an invoice from earlier in the year? Those invoices live in CompanyCam and are available for you to download on our website! Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This functionality is available for all paid plans. Permission Roles:To view invoices and receipts, you need to have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:This functionality is only available on the web app. On the webFind InvoicesLog in to CompanyCam on a web browser.Click on your User Icon and select Billing. From the Billing page, select View Payment History under your Next Payment information. Click View on the right side of the Invoice you'd like to download. That's it! Your invoice should now be downloaded to your device as a PDF. Find ReceiptsTo access your receipt, you must first download the corresponding invoice. Open your downloaded invoice.Click Pay online.Click on Download receipt. FAQsHow many invoices can I access in my Payment History? The last 10 invoices will be available to view and download as a PDF. If you need an invoice or receipt past the last 10, chat with Support or email [email protected]. Not seeing your past invoices or receipts, but paying for a subscription? Your subscription may be through iTunes. Here's how to transition to direct pay to better manage your account and your users!Related ArticlesTransition to Direct PayHow to install and use the CompanyCam Chrome extensionIntegrate CompanyCam + improveit 360Cancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Transition to Direct Pay | Help Center
content: Transition to Direct Pay | Help CenterSkip to main contentTransition to Direct PayThe Apple Store offers a 3-User subscription to CompanyCam. To manage more users for your account you'll want to switch to Direct Pay. April 15, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPermission Roles:To make Billing changes in CompanyCam, you need the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Transitioning to our Direct Pay plans can only be done on the web app.Please NoteBe sure to follow these instructions in order, starting in CompanyCam. If you cancel the Apple App Store billing first, there will be complications. On the webAdd payment in CompanyCamLog in to CompanyCam from the web or a mobile browser.Click on your User Icon, then choose Billing. Select See Available Plans. Choose which plan you'd like, then confirm your updates.Note: You can toggle between monthly and annual subscription options: Cancel Apple App Store SubscriptionFollow these instructions to cancel your Apple App Store subscription to CompanyCam. Questions? Contact our team through live chat or email [email protected]! Now that you've transitioned to direct pay, here's how you can find and view your monthly invoices through the CompanyCam website!Related ArticlesHow to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamAnnual Subscription PolicyAdd or Update Billing InformationCancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

content: Transition to Direct Pay | Help CenterSkip to main contentTransition to Direct PayThe Apple Store offers a 3-User subscription to CompanyCam. April 15, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPermission Roles:To make Billing changes in CompanyCam, you need the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Transitioning to our Direct Pay plans can only be done on the web app.Please NoteBe sure to follow these instructions in order, starting in CompanyCam. If you cancel the Apple App Store billing first, there will be complications. On the webAdd payment in CompanyCamLog in to CompanyCam from the web or a mobile browser.Click on your User Icon, then choose Billing. Select See Available Plans. Choose which plan you'd like, then confirm your updates.Note: You can toggle between monthly and annual subscription options: Cancel Apple App Store SubscriptionFollow these instructions to cancel your Apple App Store subscription to CompanyCam. Questions? Contact our team through live chat or email [email protected]! Now that you've transitioned to direct pay, here's how you can find and view your monthly invoices through the CompanyCam website!Related ArticlesHow to Find Your Past Invoices and Receipts in CompanyCamAnnual Subscription PolicyAdd or Update Billing InformationCancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Add or Update Billing Information | Help Center
content: Add or Update Billing Information | Help CenterSkip to main contentAdd or Update Billing InformationNeed to add payment or update the one on file? You can access your billing 24/7 from CompanyCam! Learn how below. April 15, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This functionality is available on all plans.Permission Roles:To make billing changes, you must have the Admin role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability:Updating a payment method is only available on the web app. On the webManage your billingLog in to CompanyCam from the web or a mobile browser.Click on your User Profile, then choose Billing. How to add your payment methodIf you haven't added a card yet, click on Add Payment Method to add your card. Continue to link your payment method through the setup pages. How to update your payment methodIf a card is already on file, click Update Payment Method. Continue to update your payment method through the setup pages. If you decide that you'd like to pay through an ACH Payment, you can click US Bank Account. FAQWhat payment methods does CompanyCam accept? We accept both credit card and ACH/Bank payments. If you cannot find your bank, that means it isn't supported by our payment processor, and you'll need to pay via credit card. To stay up to date with your monthly billing, here's how to find your past invoices through the CompanyCam website. Ready to start adding new users to your account? Check out how to invite new users through our website!Related ArticlesCancel Your CompanyCam SubscriptionRequesting PaymentsHow to Complete Payments Using CompanyCamAccessing Payments and Payout Information in StripeDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: All Capture articles | Help Center
content: All Capture articles | Help CenterSkip to main contentAll Capture articles19 articlesAccess Offline ModeHave no connection? Not a problem! You can add photos and videos to projects with our Offline Mode.Create a Before & After PhotoCreating a Before & After in CompanyCam is a great way to showcase your work for clients!Create a New ProjectCreate a new project either on the mobile app or on the web!Create a Project without a Street AddressHere's how to create the project using the GPS coordinates vs. the street address.Duplicate a PhotoIf you need to make markups on a photo but want to keep a copy of the original, create a duplicate to add markups to!Edit and Add Drawings to Your PhotosAnnotate your photos with text, icons, drawings and more!Edit Project DetailsWe know mistakes can happen when you're creating Projects. That's why you're able to edit those details, like name and address, easily.How to Add and Use Signatures in CompanyCamGetting Signatures for your files in CompanyCam is easier than ever! Reduce your costs, workload, and the chance of misplaced files!How to Use Dual Video ModeCreate more engagement with the Dual Video mode by using the front and back cameras to show and tell, but inform at the same time!How to Use Quick CaptionAdd dynamic descriptions to your photos without having to touch a keyboard!How to Scan Text on PhotosNeed to quickly save some text within a photo? Scan and copy the text in and paste them for quick reference!Record and Upload VideoVideos are a great way to show off more of your work and communicate effectively. Learn how below!How to Upload and Manage FilesHave Files on your computer or mobile device you'd like to add to a Project? Here's how:Upload Existing PhotosHave photos on your computer or mobile device you'd like to add to a Project? Here's how:Open to the Camera ViewIf you want CompanyCam to open to Camera View rather than your Home Screen, here's how:Add Date/Time and GPS Coordinates Stamps to PhotosGPS and Date/Time Stamps are a great way to have time and location displayed on every photo taken on your account.Save Each Photo as It's Taken to Your Camera RollIf you'd like to save every photo to your Camera Roll as well as in CompanyCam— here's how:Using LiDAR to Measure PhotosWant to reduce manual measuring and speed up your workflow? Use LiDAR mode to provide more accurate job site documentation.Take Photos in the Mobile AppJust downloaded the app and ready to snap some photos? Here’s how to get started in CompanyCam!

title: Access Offline Mode | Help Center
content: Access Offline Mode | Help CenterSkip to main contentAccess Offline ModeHave no connection? Not a problem! You can add photos and videos to projects with our Offline Mode. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All permission roles can access Offline Mode.Platform Availability:Offline Mode is only available in the mobile app.In the appTake photosTo access Offline Mode in the app, click on the Camera Icon at the bottom of the home screen.Select the Offline Tab and choose which Project you'd like to work on.Begin taking photos or recording a video. When finished, select Done. You will then notice a banner on the home screen of your app that says, "(#) items waiting to upload - waiting for connection". Once you reestablish a connection, those photos or videos will begin to upload. Complete ChecklistsIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the To-Dos Section. Open the cached Checklist you'd like to work on.Take photos and complete fields as normal. Once you're back online, the app will upload your progress.Complete Project TasksIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the To-Dos Section. Now you can view and complete existing Tasks, and they'll be updated once you're back online.Create a PageIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the Documents Section.Tap + Icon to create a Page.Tap Page.Tap + New Page. Once you're back online, the app will upload your Page.Create a WalkthroughIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the Documents Section.Tap the + Icon.Tap Page.Tap Walkthrough. Create your Walkthrough as you normally would.Once you're back online, the app will upload your Walkthrough to Pages.FAQsCan I create a new Project while in Offline Mode? Yes! You can create a new Project when in Offline Mode. Once you regain signal, it will sync with your CompanyCam account. Can I access all of my Projects in Offline Mode? No, you won't be able to access all of your Projects when in Offline Mode. Only up to 60 recent, starred, or assigned Projects will be cached for Offline Mode. What happens when I take photos and videos in Offline Mode? When you take photos and videos in Offline Mode, they will be placed in an upload queue and automatically upload once a connection is restored. Can I create or edit a Checklist in Offline Mode? No, you cannot create or edit Checklists in Offline Mode. You can only complete the Checklist Fields that were accessed before going offline. Can I edit or delete Checklist Fields while in Offline Mode? No, you cannot edit or delete Checklist Fields while offline. Can I complete Checklist Fields while in Offline Mode? Yes, Checklist Fields can be completed in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Checklist when you go offline. Can I complete Project Tasks while in Offline Mode? Yes, Project Tasks can be completed in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Project when you go offline. Can I create new Pages or a Walkthrough in Offline Mode? Yes, you can create new Pages or a Walkthrough in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Project when you go offline. Once a connection is reestablished, it will process and upload them. Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosHow to Create and Edit Project TasksUsing AI Features for PagesHow to Use Quick CaptionCompanyCam Mobile App RemodelDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

Yes, Project Tasks can be completed in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Project when you go offline. Can I create new Pages or a Walkthrough in Offline Mode? Yes, you can create new Pages or a Walkthrough in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Project when you go offline. Once a connection is reestablished, it will process and upload them. title: Access Offline Mode | Help Center
content: Access Offline Mode | Help CenterSkip to main contentAccess Offline ModeHave no connection? Not a problem! You can add photos and videos to projects with our Offline Mode. April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All permission roles can access Offline Mode.Platform Availability:Offline Mode is only available in the mobile app.In the appTake photosTo access Offline Mode in the app, click on the Camera Icon at the bottom of the home screen.Select the Offline Tab and choose which Project you'd like to work on.Begin taking photos or recording a video. When finished, select Done. You will then notice a banner on the home screen of your app that says, "(#) items waiting to upload - waiting for connection". Once you reestablish a connection, those photos or videos will begin to upload. Complete ChecklistsIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the To-Dos Section. Open the cached Checklist you'd like to work on.Take photos and complete fields as normal. Once you're back online, the app will upload your progress.Complete Project TasksIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the To-Dos Section. Now you can view and complete existing Tasks, and they'll be updated once you're back online.Create a PageIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the Documents Section.Tap + Icon to create a Page.Tap Page.Tap + New Page. Once you're back online, the app will upload your Page.Create a WalkthroughIf you're within a Project when you go offline, tap on the Documents Section.Tap the + Icon.Tap Page.Tap Walkthrough. Create your Walkthrough as you normally would.Once you're back online, the app will upload your Walkthrough to Pages.FAQsCan I create a new Project while in Offline Mode? Yes! You can create a new Project when in Offline Mode. Once you regain signal, it will sync with your CompanyCam account. Can I access all of my Projects in Offline Mode? No, you won't be able to access all of your Projects when in Offline Mode. Only up to 60 recent, starred, or assigned Projects will be cached for Offline Mode. What happens when I take photos and videos in Offline Mode? When you take photos and videos in Offline Mode, they will be placed in an upload queue and automatically upload once a connection is restored. Can I create or edit a Checklist in Offline Mode? No, you cannot create or edit Checklists in Offline Mode. You can only complete the Checklist Fields that were accessed before going offline. Can I edit or delete Checklist Fields while in Offline Mode? No, you cannot edit or delete Checklist Fields while offline. Can I complete Checklist Fields while in Offline Mode? Yes, Checklist Fields can be completed in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Checklist when you go offline. Can I complete Project Tasks while in Offline Mode? Yes, Project Tasks can be completed in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Project when you go offline. Can I create new Pages or a Walkthrough in Offline Mode? Yes, you can create new Pages or a Walkthrough in Offline Mode as long as you are in the Project when you go offline. Once a connection is reestablished, it will process and upload them. title: Using AI Features for Pages | Help Center
content: Using AI Features for Pages | Help CenterSkip to main contentUsing AI Features for PagesUse AI to quickly pull together information to share with your team! Updated over a month agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:These features are available on specific plans. If you're interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.The number of AI Actions available varies by plan.Permission Roles:All permission roles have access to using AI Features for Pages.Platform Availability:Using AI Features for Pages is available on both the web and mobile apps.Walkthrough Note is only available on the mobile app.What will each AI feature do?SummaryThe Summary feature allows you to add a quick summary for your photos. This tool will add an AI-generated headline callout to complement and provide context to a Page of photos from a Project. You can quickly add context to any photo selection without typing out a single word! Daily LogThe Daily Log allows you to log a day's activity all in one place. This tool will pull together your work into an easy-to-read and editable Page. This way, you can quickly share daily updates back to the office or store a log of your progress. This feature will organize photos and summarize notes into a snapshot Page of information. The Daily Log will only pull information from photos and captions from the selected date range inside your Project. Progress RecapThe Progress Recap will help you organize and summarize your Project's progress over time. This tool will put together a recap from a selection of photos and their captions into an easy-to-read, editable Page. This way, you can quickly send progress updates to managers, customers, and partners. WalkthroughThe Walkthrough feature will allow you to build a Page while you walk, talk, and take photos. This feature will help you write and format your Walkthrough automatically. This way, you can quickly take photos and notes while conducting a Walkthrough without having to type. This feature will automatically organize the photos you're taking and notes you're speaking, then convert them into an easy-to-read Page that can be shared with others.On the webSummaryOpen a Project and click on the Pages Tab. Click + Create Page. Select Summary.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Summary, then click Generate. Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Summary for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab of the Project.Daily LogOpen a Project and click on the Pages Tab. Click + Create Page. Select Daily Log. Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Daily Log, then click Generate. Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Daily Log for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab of the Project. Progress RecapOpen a Project and click on the Pages Tab. Click + Create Page. Select More Templates.Navigate to AI Templates and select Progress Recap. Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Progress Recap, then click Generate. Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a Progress Recap for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab of the Project. In the appSummaryFrom the Project screen, tap on the Documents Tab.Tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose More Templates from the available options.Navigate to the AI Templates section and select Summary.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Summary, then tap Generate.Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Summary for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab in the Documents Tab in the Project.Daily LogFrom the Project screen, tap on the Pages Tab.Under the Pages Tab, tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose Daily Log from the available options.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Daily Log, then tap Generate.Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick Daily Log for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab in the Documents Tab in the Project.Progress RecapFrom the Project screen, tap on the Documents Tab.Under the Pages Tab, tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose More Templates from the available options.Navigate to the AI Templates section and select Progress Recap.Next, select the photos you'd like to include within your Progress Recap, then tap Generate.Once you've generated the Page, the tool will read the Photo Description on each photo and generate a quick recap for you. This can be accessed from the Pages Tab in the Documents Tab in the Project.WalkthroughFrom the Project screen, tap on the Documents Tab.Under the Pages Tab, tap the + Icon at the bottom of the screen and tap Page.Choose Walkthrough from the available options.Tap Start Walkthrough to take photos while completing your dictation.When you've finished your work, tap Done.Your Walkthrough will now be saved in the Pages Tab of your Project.FAQsCan I create a Walkthrough from my desktop? No, Walkthroughs can only be created in the mobile app. Can AI Features for Pages be used in Offline Mode? The Walkthrough feature is compatible with Offline Mode. If a Project is cached for Offline Mode, a User can create a new Page using Walkthrough, and it will process and upload once the User regains an internet connection. The Summary, Daily Log, and Progress Recap features are not compatible with Offline Mode. How long can I use a Walkthrough Note for at a time? The Walkthrough feature can record up to 15 minutes per note. Can I convert a Walkthrough Note into a Checklist? Yes, you can convert a Walkthrough Note into a Checklist. Once your Walkthrough has been saved in your Project, you can convert it to a Checklist by opening the Page > tapping on the Three-Dots Icon at the top of the screen > and selecting Create Checklist. Looking for more ways to use AI? Explore AI Features for Checklists and our Best Practices When Using AI Tools in CompanyCam. Related ArticlesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamCreate and Use Pages TemplatesUsing AI Features for ChecklistsBest Practices when Using AI Tools in CompanyCamGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create a Before & After Photo | Help Center
content: Create a Before & After Photo | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a Before & After PhotoCreating a Before & After in CompanyCam is a great way to showcase your work for clients! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Permission Roles:All User Permission Roles can create a Before & After photo. Platform Availability:Before & After Photos can be created on both the web and mobile apps. ​On the webSelect any two (2) photos. Click the Square Icon (upper left corner of the photo) to select a photo. After choosing any two photos, click Create Before & After from the menu at the top of the screen. Edit, Choose a Template & Save. On this next screen: edit photos, swap Before/After position of each photo, and/or choose a template from the bottom of the screen. Once edits are complete, click the Save button (upper right). In the appTake a Before & After in the Camera Screen:Open the Project you would like to create the Before & After photo in.If you already have your Before photo taken, tap on the photo, then tap on the Three Dots Icon in the lower right corner to open the menu.Choose Take After Photo (if you haven't taken your Before photo yet, go ahead and tap the blue Plus Sign in the project and choose Take Photos Here to capture your first photo, then follow the next steps to take your After Photo).The camera will open for you to take your After photo and display an overlay of your first photo. You can line your photo up by using this overlay image or you can change it with the menu below the capture photo button to be hidden or displayed as an outline. Image Overlay (will appear blurry): Outline Overlay:Once you line up your photos, tap the Capture Button, select the best template to showcase your work, edit the images if needed, and then tap Save.Share your Before & After or create another one!Create a Before & After with Existing PhotosOpen the Project you would like to create the Before & After photo in.Tap on the Select Icon.Choose your Before Photo, then tap on the Three Dots Icon in the lower right corner to open the menu. Select Choose After Photo.The page will open for you to select your After photo. Once you've selected that, you'll want to tap Choose in the upper right corner.Select the template that best showcases your work, edit the images if needed, and then tap Save.After the photo has been saved, you can then share it or create another one!* All Before & After photos should automatically be tagged with the "Before and After" tag. Now that you've created your Before & After photos, you can share them with your customers with a gallery or timeline link. Check out the different ways to share photos from CompanyCam!Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistCreate a ReportCreate and Use Project TemplatesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

In the appTake a Before & After in the Camera Screen:Open the Project you would like to create the Before & After photo in.If you already have your Before photo taken, tap on the photo, then tap on the Three Dots Icon in the lower right corner to open the menu.Choose Take After Photo (if you haven't taken your Before photo yet, go ahead and tap the blue Plus Sign in the project and choose Take Photos Here to capture your first photo, then follow the next steps to take your After Photo).The camera will open for you to take your After photo and display an overlay of your first photo. You can line your photo up by using this overlay image or you can change it with the menu below the capture photo button to be hidden or displayed as an outline. Image Overlay (will appear blurry): Outline Overlay:Once you line up your photos, tap the Capture Button, select the best template to showcase your work, edit the images if needed, and then tap Save.Share your Before & After or create another one!Create a Before & After with Existing PhotosOpen the Project you would like to create the Before & After photo in.Tap on the Select Icon.Choose your Before Photo, then tap on the Three Dots Icon in the lower right corner to open the menu. Select Choose After Photo.The page will open for you to select your After photo. Once you've selected that, you'll want to tap Choose in the upper right corner.Select the template that best showcases your work, edit the images if needed, and then tap Save.After the photo has been saved, you can then share it or create another one!* All Before & After photos should automatically be tagged with the "Before and After" tag. Now that you've created your Before & After photos, you can share them with your customers with a gallery or timeline link. Check out the different ways to share photos from CompanyCam!Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistCreate a ReportCreate and Use Project TemplatesHow to Create and Use Pages in CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create a New Project | Help Center
content: Create a New Project | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a New ProjectCreate a new project either on the mobile app or on the web! May 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles:All User Permission Roles can create a new Project. Restricted Users can only see Projects they've created or have been assigned to. Platform Availability:New Projects can be created on both the web and mobile apps. ​On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Within the Project Feed, click on the + Create Button. Select + New Project or Use a Template. Use the Project Address field on the left to search for your Project’s address or business name, or enter it manually.​ If you do not know the address or business name, choose to Place a Pin and place a map pin or GeoFence. A map pin is perfect for defining a residential home or a small commercial Project. ​For larger Projects or for longitudinal construction, you would be better suited to use a GeoFence. To create a GeoFence, click on the GeoFence Icon. Next, click on each point where you'd like to draw the GeoFence. Once the GeoFence is created, you'll see a shaded area on the map. Edit the Project information fields to the left, then click Create Project.In the appOpen CompanyCam, tap on the + Button.Next, choose Create Project. If you are standing at your Project location, tap the blue Confirm Button. If you are creating a Project elsewhere, use the search box to search for your Project's specific address or business name.After confirming, you can edit the Project details as needed, then tap Create Project on the bottom of the screen.You can now start taking or uploading Project photos!Now that you've created the project, you may want to upload photos that have already been taken! Here's how to do that: Upload Existing Photos. FAQWhat User Permission Roles can see all Projects within a company's account? Admin, Manager, and Standard Users can see all Projects on the company account. If you need one of these Roles, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission. Can I create a new Project from a Template? Using Project Templates is available on select plans. Upgrade your plan to take advantage of this feature. Can I remove a Project's cover photo? No, there isn't a direct option to remove a Project's cover photo, but you can replace it with another photo. Here's how:Select the photo you'd like to set as the Project's Cover Photo.Click Actions at the top of the screen. Choose Set as Cover Photo from the drop-down menu. Related ArticlesHow to Use the Project MapCreate a Project without a Street AddressUpload Existing PhotosCreate and Use Project TemplatesHow to Create and Edit Project TasksDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Create a Project without a Street Address | Help Center
content: Create a Project without a Street Address | Help CenterSkip to main contentCreate a Project without a Street AddressHere's how to create the project using the GPS coordinates vs. the street address. April 17, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans. Using Project Templates is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission RolesAll User Permission Roles can create a Project without a Street Address.After you create a Project using GPS Coordinates, Admin, Managers, and Standard Users can arrive at the job site and see this Project in the Nearby Projects list. Restricted Users must be assigned to the Project to see it.Platform AvailabilityCreating a Project without a street address is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Within the Project Feed, click on the + Create Button. Select + New Project or Use a Template. Use the Project Address section at the left to get into the general area - search a highway name, intersection, nearby business, etc.​ Drag the pin to the exact position for the Project.Edit the Project information fields on the right, and then click Create Project.In the appOpen CompanyCam, tap on the + Button.Next, choose Create Project.Move the Map around until the Pin is exactly where the Project is located and tap the Confirm Button.After creating the Project, you can edit the Project details as needed, then tap Create Project on the bottom of the screen.You can now start taking or uploading Project photos!Now that you've created the Project, you may want to upload photos that have already been taken. Here's how to do that: Upload Existing Photos.Related ArticlesCreate a New ProjectUpload Existing PhotosAccepting a Project CollaborationCreate and Use Project TemplatesGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Duplicate a Photo | Help Center
content: Duplicate a Photo | Help CenterSkip to main contentDuplicate a PhotoIf you need to make markups on a photo but want to keep a copy of the original, create a duplicate to add markups to! July 16, 2025Table of contentsOn the webOpen a Project and find the photo you want to duplicate.Hover your cursor over the photo you want to duplicate. Click on the Square Icon in the top left corner of the photo to select it. Click on Actions. Select Duplicate. In the appOpen a Project and find the photo you want to duplicate.Tap the Three Dots Icon in the bottom right corner of the screen.Select Duplicate. Once your photo has been duplicated, you can edit your photo and add drawings. Accidentally duplicated a photo? Here's how you can delete a photo through the website.Related ArticlesPrint Photos in the Web AppMove PhotosHow to Delete Photos and VideosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamEdit and Add Drawings to Your PhotosDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Edit and Add Drawings to Your Photos | Help Center
content: Edit and Add Drawings to Your Photos | Help CenterSkip to main contentEdit and Add Drawings to Your PhotosAnnotate your photos with text, icons, drawings and more! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsAdding annotations is available on all plans.The Logo Sticker feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesAll User roles can edit and add drawings to their own photos.Admin, Manager, and Standard Users can edit photos in any Projects they have access to.Restricted users can edit photos in the Projects they are assigned to. Platform AvailabilityEditing and adding Drawings is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webOpen the photo you want to edit by clicking on it.Then, click on the Curved Line Icon at the top of the screen to open the Editor. All tools are listed on the left side of your screen. The annotation options are located in the upper 2/3; the editing options are located in the lower 1/3. In descending order, they are:SelectDrawPoly LineColorThicknessArrowCircleSquareTextTimestampStickerRotateCropEdit (Brightness, Contrast, & Saturation)Clear AllUndoRedo When you're done, click the Green Checkmark to save in the upper right corner to save your edits!In the appOpen the photo you want to edit by tapping on it.Then, click on the Curved Line Icon in the bottom left of the screen to open the Editor.All tools are listed on the right side of your screen. The annotation options are located in the upper 2/3, and the editing options are located in the lower 1/3. In descending order, they are:Line ThicknessColorDrawPoly LineArrowCircleSquareTextTime StampStickerRotateCropEdit (Brightness, Contrast, & Saturation)When you're done, tap Save in the bottom right corner.Want to use your edited photos in a before/after picture? Check out our help guide on creating Before & After photos through the website & app! These edited images are also ready to drop into Reports. If you click on the photo as you create or edit a report you can also bring up this edit tool! Related ArticlesUsing LiDAR to Measure PhotosDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Edit and Add Drawings to Your Photos | Help Center
content: Edit and Add Drawings to Your Photos | Help CenterSkip to main contentEdit and Add Drawings to Your PhotosAnnotate your photos with text, icons, drawings and more! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsAdding annotations is available on all plans.The Logo Sticker feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission RolesAll User roles can edit and add drawings to their own photos.Admin, Manager, and Standard Users can edit photos in any Projects they have access to.Restricted users can edit photos in the Projects they are assigned to. Platform AvailabilityEditing and adding Drawings is available on both the web and mobile apps.On the webOpen the photo you want to edit by clicking on it.Then, click on the Curved Line Icon at the top of the screen to open the Editor. All tools are listed on the left side of your screen. The annotation options are located in the upper 2/3; the editing options are located in the lower 1/3. In descending order, they are:SelectDrawPoly LineColorThicknessArrowCircleSquareTextTimestampStickerRotateCropEdit (Brightness, Contrast, & Saturation)Clear AllUndoRedo When you're done, click the Green Checkmark to save in the upper right corner to save your edits!In the appOpen the photo you want to edit by tapping on it.Then, click on the Curved Line Icon in the bottom left of the screen to open the Editor.All tools are listed on the right side of your screen. The annotation options are located in the upper 2/3, and the editing options are located in the lower 1/3. In descending order, they are:Line ThicknessColorDrawPoly LineArrowCircleSquareTextTime StampStickerRotateCropEdit (Brightness, Contrast, & Saturation)When you're done, tap Save in the bottom right corner.Want to use your edited photos in a before/after picture? Check out our help guide on creating Before & After photos through the website & app! These edited images are also ready to drop into Reports. title: Edit Project Details | Help Center
content: Edit Project Details | Help CenterSkip to main contentEdit Project DetailsWe know mistakes can happen when you're creating Projects. That's why you're able to edit those details, like name and address, easily. April 14, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements: This feature is available on all plans. Permission Roles: To edit Project Details, you need to have the Admin, Manager, or Standard role. If you don't have that role, ask an Admin on your company's account for permission.Platform Availability: Editing Project Details can be done on both the web and the mobile app. ​On the webLog in to CompanyCam.Find the Project you need to edit and click to open it.Click on the Three Dots Icon toward the top right of the Project and select Edit Project from the menu. Edit the necessary details, then click Save Project. ​ To add a Project Contact (your client/customer information), click on the expandable Sidebar Icon on the right side if it's collapsed.​ Click Contact Info on the right-side panel. Add their name, phone number, and email address, then click Add Contact. In the appOpen the Project you're working on.Tap Menu in the top right of the Project.Once within the menu, select Edit Details.Edit the necessary details, then tap Save Changes toward the bottom or Save in the top right corner.To add a Project Contact (your client/customer information), tap + Contact below the Project name. Add their name, phone number, and email address, then tap Add Contact.Related ArticlesCreate a Project without a Street AddressEdit User's Permissions and Login InformationEdit or Delete Text SnippetsCreate Text SnippetsHow to Create and Edit Project TasksDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Add and Use Signatures in CompanyCam | Help Center
content: How to Add and Use Signatures in CompanyCam | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Add and Use Signatures in CompanyCamGetting Signatures for your files in CompanyCam is easier than ever! Reduce your costs, workload, and the chance of misplaced files! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission RolesRequesting Signatures is an account-level feature and is available to all permission levels.Platform AvailabilityRequesting Signatures is only available on the web app.Adding Signatures directly to a File is available on both the web and mobile apps.Please NoteThe Signatures feature functionality works differently depending on whether you're working on a web browser or mobile app.On the web, you'll create Signature Requests and send them via email.In the app, you'll collect a Signature directly in the app without sending a request via email.On the webRequest a File SignatureLog in to CompanyCam. Open a Project and click on the Files Tab at the top.Select a File or choose + Add Files.Once you've added or selected a File, click Request Signatures. Add the signer's name and email. You can click Add Another Signer to add multiple. Click the box next to Set Signer Order to move the order of the signers using the 6 dots to the left of their name. Click Next.Choose a Signature field or an Auto-fill field from the menu on the left. Then, use your cursor to place where you'd like their signature. *Switch between signers by clicking their name from the dropdown menu under Signer.* Click Next.Add any CC recipients, and add a subject line and message to your email. Click Send for Signature. The status will show on your File as Signature Pending. Recipients will receive an email from [email protected] for them to review and sign your File. Once all parties have signed, the stats will show on your File as Signed. *If they do not see the email, have them check their Spam folder* Navigate to the page where you placed the Signature and or/auto-fill field, and you'll see their signature! In the appHow to Add File SignaturesIn the app, open a Project and tap on the Documents Tab. Navigate to the Files section.Open the PDF File and then select Fill & Sign.Tap the Editor Icon to mark up your File.Select the Text Icon to add text or the Signatures Icon to add a Signature. Draw your signature, upload it as an image, or type it out. Once completed, select Add.Drag your signature to your preferred position and resize as needed. Add additional signatures if necessary by repeating steps 4-5. Once all text and signatures are added to your File, select Save.Select Yes, I'm Done to complete the process. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + REISIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

Open a Project and click on the Files Tab at the top.Select a File or choose + Add Files.Once you've added or selected a File, click Request Signatures. Add the signer's name and email. You can click Add Another Signer to add multiple. Click the box next to Set Signer Order to move the order of the signers using the 6 dots to the left of their name. Click Next.Choose a Signature field or an Auto-fill field from the menu on the left. Then, use your cursor to place where you'd like their signature. *Switch between signers by clicking their name from the dropdown menu under Signer.* Click Next.Add any CC recipients, and add a subject line and message to your email. Click Send for Signature. The status will show on your File as Signature Pending. Recipients will receive an email from [email protected] for them to review and sign your File. Once all parties have signed, the stats will show on your File as Signed. *If they do not see the email, have them check their Spam folder* Navigate to the page where you placed the Signature and or/auto-fill field, and you'll see their signature! In the appHow to Add File SignaturesIn the app, open a Project and tap on the Documents Tab. Navigate to the Files section.Open the PDF File and then select Fill & Sign.Tap the Editor Icon to mark up your File.Select the Text Icon to add text or the Signatures Icon to add a Signature. Draw your signature, upload it as an image, or type it out. Once completed, select Add.Drag your signature to your preferred position and resize as needed. Add additional signatures if necessary by repeating steps 4-5. Once all text and signatures are added to your File, select Save.Select Yes, I'm Done to complete the process. Related ArticlesIntegrate CompanyCam + REISIntegrate CompanyCam + RoofrIntegrate CompanyCam + HOVERIntegrate CompanyCam+ QuickBooks OnlineIntegrate CompanyCam + BeamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Use Dual Video Mode | Help Center
content: How to Use Dual Video Mode | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use Dual Video ModeCreate more engagement with the Dual Video mode by using the front and back cameras to show and tell, but inform at the same time! April 9, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Role:All User Permissions Roles can access and utilize the Dual Video mode feature. Platform Availability:Dual Video mode is only available on IOS. Dual Video mode is only available on the mobile app for select iPhone models (devices with the Apple A12 chip or newer). In the appTap on the Camera Icon as you normally would when taking a photo. Select the Project you'd like to add a video to.Once within the capture tool, tap on Dual Video.Select the Record Button in the middle of your screen to begin recording.Once you've finished recording your video, select Done to save and upload the video. FAQsCan Android Devices access Dual Video mode? No. Android Devices cannot access Dual Video mode. This feature is only available for select IOS devices. ​What IOS Devices have access to Dual Video Mode? This feature is only available for IOS devices with the Apple A12 chip or newer. Related ArticlesRecord and Upload VideoAccess Offline ModeUsing LiDAR to Measure PhotosDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Use Quick Caption | Help Center
content: How to Use Quick Caption | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Use Quick CaptionAdd dynamic descriptions to your photos without having to touch a keyboard! April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on all plans.Quick Caption does not count towards your AI Action limit (if applicable). Permission Role:All User Permissions Roles can access this feature. Platform Availability:Quick Caption is only available in the mobile app.This feature is compatible with Offline Mode. However, the transcription will not be shown. Once you're back online, the transcription will be processed and saved as a description on the photo. In the appHow to enable or disable AI Quick CaptionNavigate to the Camera View from the Home Screen or within a Project.Tap on the Settings Icon. Open All Camera Settings.Scroll down to the More Section and toggle Quick Caption on/off.How to use AI Quick CaptionNavigate to the Camera View from the Home Screen or within a Project.Confirm that AI Quick Captions is enabled within your app settings.Tap the Capture Button to take a photo. Once the photo has been taken, tap on the Microphone Icon and dictate a description of the photo.Once finished, tap Done to save your Quick Caption. When your work is complete, tap Done within the Camera View.​Now that you've created a quick caption, here are some other ways you can interact with CompanyCam using dictation: Using AI Features for Pages.Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamUsing Tags in CompanyCamTake Photos in the Mobile AppGetting Started with CompanyCamDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Scan Text on Photos | Help Center
content: How to Scan Text on Photos | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Scan Text on PhotosNeed to quickly save some text within a photo? Scan and copy the text in and paste them for quick reference! April 10, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission RolesText Scanning is available to all Permission Roles.Platform AvailabilityText Scanning is only available in the mobile app.In the appOpen a Project and scroll down to view your photos.Tap on a photo that has the text you'd like to copy.Tap on the Scan Icon to enable the Text Scanner.Once you've enabled the Text Scanner, tap on the text you'd like to copy.Once you've copied your text, you can paste it within your Photo Description, save it as a Comment, or place it in a Page for quick reference.Related ArticlesHow to Delete Photos and VideosHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamCreate a ReportHow to Use Map This PhotoDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

If you’re interested in access, our team can help walk you through your options.Permission RolesText Scanning is available to all Permission Roles.Platform AvailabilityText Scanning is only available in the mobile app.In the appOpen a Project and scroll down to view your photos.Tap on a photo that has the text you'd like to copy.Tap on the Scan Icon to enable the Text Scanner.Once you've enabled the Text Scanner, tap on the text you'd like to copy.Once you've copied your text, you can paste it within your Photo Description, save it as a Comment, or place it in a Page for quick reference.Related ArticlesHow to Delete Photos and VideosHow to Share Photos in CompanyCamHow to Download / Save Photos from CompanyCamCreate a ReportHow to Use Map This PhotoDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: Record and Upload Video | Help Center
content: Record and Upload Video | Help CenterSkip to main contentRecord and Upload VideoVideos are a great way to show off more of your work and communicate effectively. Learn how below! Updated over 3 weeks agoTable of contentsBefore you startPlan Requirements:This feature is available on select plans. If you’re interested in access, our team is available to help walk you through your options.Permission Role:All User Permissions Roles can record and upload Videos. Platform Availability:Recording and uploading videos is available on both the web and mobile apps. On the webGo into the Project you'd like to add a video to.Select the + Upload Photos Button. Make sure the video you'd like to upload is less than 5 minutes if you're on Pro and less than 10 minutes if you're on Premium or Elite.Drag and drop or click within the box, choose the file on your computer's hard drive, and select Open.Once you have the video uploaded, you and your team can tag and comment on it here as well! In the appRecord in CompanyCamHead to the Camera View as you normally would when taking a photo.Select the Project you'd like to add a video to.Tap Video at the bottom of the screen.Allow CompanyCam to use your device's microphone if this is the first time using the video feature.Press the Record Button in the middle of your screen to capture video.Go between capturing videos and photos as needed.Once you're done capturing photos and videos, select Done.Upload from camera rollGo into the Project you'd like to add a video to.Tap the + Icon within the Photos Section of the Project. Select Upload Photos.Next, choose Select Photos Manually.Find the video you’d like to upload (Make sure the video falls within the criteria listed above).Once you've selected the video you need, tap Done.On the Confirm Upload page, Tag your video (if needed) and then Upload Photos.FAQs What video resolution is supported? All plans support 4K resolution videos. How is the date determined for videos uploaded from outside the app? External videos uploaded from your device to CompanyCam are organized within your Project by the date the video was captured. If original date metadata is missing, the video will default to the current upload date. Related ArticlesCreating a ChecklistHow to Use Dual Video ModeUpload Existing PhotosCreate a ReportCreate and Use Showcases for your Portfolio SiteDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

title: How to Upload and Manage Files | Help Center
content: How to Upload and Manage Files | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Upload and Manage FilesHave Files on your computer or mobile device you'd like to add to a Project? Here's how: April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesAll User permission roles can upload Files to a Project.To manage File Access, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role.Platform AvailabilityUploading Files is available on both the web and mobile apps.Managing File Access is only available in the web app.Scanning Files is only available in the mobile app.On the webUpload FilesSign in to your CompanyCam account.Select the Project you'd like to add the File to.Once you are in the Project, click Files.Once you have opened the Files Tab, click + Add Files. Click on the From Computer drop-down option. Next, drag and drop or click and select the File from your computer.Manage File AccessSelect the Project you'd like to manage File Access in.Once you are in the Project, click on the Files Tab.Once you have clicked the Files Tab, click the Three Dots Icon next to the File you'd like to manage access to.Click Manage Access. Select a File Access Level and then click Save. Sort FilesYour Files are automatically ordered from most recently added to least recently added. On the web, you can change the way your Files are sorted within a Project. This is currently not available in the mobile app. Note: Re-ordering your Files list will only affect your view on the web. The view for other Users on your account will not be affected.Sign in to your CompanyCam account.Select the Project you'd like to organize your Files in.Once you are in the Project, click on the Files Tab.To sort your Files, click File Name, Date Added, OR File Type. If the arrow points up in that section, your Files are sorted in ascending order (A to Z, earliest to latest). If the arrow points down, the order is descending (Z to A, newest to oldest). If both up and down arrows are present, the Files are not being sorted by that section. In the appUpload FilesOpen the Project you'd like to add a File to.Tap the Documents Tab. Next, tap on the + Button at the bottom of the page.Tap Add Files.Tap Upload or Scan.Scanning your FileHover your camera over the File you'd like to scan and rename the file if needed. The camera will show a blue rectangle over the File on your screen as it analyzes it. The rectangle will turn green once the Document image has been captured.Tips for scanning a File: Make sure you have good lighting and that the File is placed on a flat surface. If possible, have the File on a surface that is of a darker color to help enhance the light quality of the File's image. Edit the File as needed and tap Save.Scan additional Files or tap Save PDF.Your File should now show up under the Files Tab within your Project's Documents section.Manage Document accessOpen your Project and tap on the Documents Section. Tap Files, then open the File you'd like to manage access to. Tap on the Three Dots Icon in the top right-hand corner.Within the slide-up menu, tap Manage Access.Select a Document Access Level and tap Save.Now that you've uploaded Documents to your Project, check out how to convert your Document to a Checklist. FAQs What file types can I upload and what are the file size limits? Files must be one of the following types: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .csv, .txt, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, or .numbers. Each File must be 100 MB or less. Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download a Project QR CodeHow to Upload and Use Project FilesHow to Create and Edit Project TasksAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

content: How to Upload and Manage Files | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Upload and Manage FilesHave Files on your computer or mobile device you'd like to add to a Project? Here's how: April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesAll User permission roles can upload Files to a Project.To manage File Access, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role.Platform AvailabilityUploading Files is available on both the web and mobile apps.Managing File Access is only available in the web app.Scanning Files is only available in the mobile app.On the webUpload FilesSign in to your CompanyCam account.Select the Project you'd like to add the File to.Once you are in the Project, click Files.Once you have opened the Files Tab, click + Add Files. Click on the From Computer drop-down option. Next, drag and drop or click and select the File from your computer.Manage File AccessSelect the Project you'd like to manage File Access in.Once you are in the Project, click on the Files Tab.Once you have clicked the Files Tab, click the Three Dots Icon next to the File you'd like to manage access to.Click Manage Access. Select a File Access Level and then click Save. Sort FilesYour Files are automatically ordered from most recently added to least recently added. On the web, you can change the way your Files are sorted within a Project. This is currently not available in the mobile app. Note: Re-ordering your Files list will only affect your view on the web. The view for other Users on your account will not be affected.Sign in to your CompanyCam account.Select the Project you'd like to organize your Files in.Once you are in the Project, click on the Files Tab.To sort your Files, click File Name, Date Added, OR File Type. If the arrow points up in that section, your Files are sorted in ascending order (A to Z, earliest to latest). If the arrow points down, the order is descending (Z to A, newest to oldest). If both up and down arrows are present, the Files are not being sorted by that section. In the appUpload FilesOpen the Project you'd like to add a File to.Tap the Documents Tab. Next, tap on the + Button at the bottom of the page.Tap Add Files.Tap Upload or Scan.Scanning your FileHover your camera over the File you'd like to scan and rename the file if needed. The camera will show a blue rectangle over the File on your screen as it analyzes it. The rectangle will turn green once the Document image has been captured.Tips for scanning a File: Make sure you have good lighting and that the File is placed on a flat surface. If possible, have the File on a surface that is of a darker color to help enhance the light quality of the File's image. Edit the File as needed and tap Save.Scan additional Files or tap Save PDF.Your File should now show up under the Files Tab within your Project's Documents section.Manage Document accessOpen your Project and tap on the Documents Section. Tap Files, then open the File you'd like to manage access to. Tap on the Three Dots Icon in the top right-hand corner.Within the slide-up menu, tap Manage Access.Select a Document Access Level and tap Save.Now that you've uploaded Documents to your Project, check out how to convert your Document to a Checklist. FAQs What file types can I upload and what are the file size limits? Files must be one of the following types: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .csv, .txt, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, or .numbers. Each File must be 100 MB or less. Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download a Project QR CodeHow to Upload and Use Project FilesHow to Create and Edit Project TasksAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents

FAQs What file types can I upload and what are the file size limits? Files must be one of the following types: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .csv, .txt, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, or .numbers. Each File must be 100 MB or less. title: How to Upload and Manage Files | Help Center
content: How to Upload and Manage Files | Help CenterSkip to main contentHow to Upload and Manage FilesHave Files on your computer or mobile device you'd like to add to a Project? Here's how: April 13, 2026Table of contentsBefore you startPlan RequirementsThis feature is available on all plans.Permission RolesAll User permission roles can upload Files to a Project.To manage File Access, you'll need to have the Admin or Manager role.Platform AvailabilityUploading Files is available on both the web and mobile apps.Managing File Access is only available in the web app.Scanning Files is only available in the mobile app.On the webUpload FilesSign in to your CompanyCam account.Select the Project you'd like to add the File to.Once you are in the Project, click Files.Once you have opened the Files Tab, click + Add Files. Click on the From Computer drop-down option. Next, drag and drop or click and select the File from your computer.Manage File AccessSelect the Project you'd like to manage File Access in.Once you are in the Project, click on the Files Tab.Once you have clicked the Files Tab, click the Three Dots Icon next to the File you'd like to manage access to.Click Manage Access. Select a File Access Level and then click Save. Sort FilesYour Files are automatically ordered from most recently added to least recently added. On the web, you can change the way your Files are sorted within a Project. This is currently not available in the mobile app. Note: Re-ordering your Files list will only affect your view on the web. The view for other Users on your account will not be affected.Sign in to your CompanyCam account.Select the Project you'd like to organize your Files in.Once you are in the Project, click on the Files Tab.To sort your Files, click File Name, Date Added, OR File Type. If the arrow points up in that section, your Files are sorted in ascending order (A to Z, earliest to latest). If the arrow points down, the order is descending (Z to A, newest to oldest). If both up and down arrows are present, the Files are not being sorted by that section. In the appUpload FilesOpen the Project you'd like to add a File to.Tap the Documents Tab. Next, tap on the + Button at the bottom of the page.Tap Add Files.Tap Upload or Scan.Scanning your FileHover your camera over the File you'd like to scan and rename the file if needed. The camera will show a blue rectangle over the File on your screen as it analyzes it. The rectangle will turn green once the Document image has been captured.Tips for scanning a File: Make sure you have good lighting and that the File is placed on a flat surface. If possible, have the File on a surface that is of a darker color to help enhance the light quality of the File's image. Edit the File as needed and tap Save.Scan additional Files or tap Save PDF.Your File should now show up under the Files Tab within your Project's Documents section.Manage Document accessOpen your Project and tap on the Documents Section. Tap Files, then open the File you'd like to manage access to. Tap on the Three Dots Icon in the top right-hand corner.Within the slide-up menu, tap Manage Access.Select a Document Access Level and tap Save.Now that you've uploaded Documents to your Project, check out how to convert your Document to a Checklist. FAQs What file types can I upload and what are the file size limits? Files must be one of the following types: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .csv, .txt, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, or .numbers. Each File must be 100 MB or less. Related ArticlesUpload Existing PhotosHow to Download a Project QR CodeHow to Upload and Use Project FilesHow to Create and Edit Project TasksAssign Project Tasks to UsersDid this answer your question?😞😐😃Table of contents
